tradeGoods.en: Rice, dried fish, salt, timber, bamboo, lacquer materials, feathers, hides, prestige materials such as ivory, bronze goods, jade, and regional products.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为耕地占有、粮仓储备、牲畜、渔具、船只、青铜礼器及宗族网络，而非统一货币资产。都城与贵族聚落财富较集中。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed chiefly in access to land, grain stores, livestock, fishing gear, boats, bronze ritual vessels, and lineage connections rather than standardized monetary assets. Wealth was concentrated in the capital and noble settlements.

unit.en: shi grain equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以宗族土地控制、贡赋再分配和地方交换为主的农业经济，辅以河网运输与有限的跨区域精英贸易。
economicSystem.en: An agrarian economy based on lineage control of land, tributary redistribution, and local exchange, supplemented by riverine transport and limited long-distance elite trade.

source.en: Labor dues and state workshop output
Fish, salt, timber, and wetland resources

category.en: Rewards to local elites and subordinate integration

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要并非金银储备，而是都城与要邑中的粮仓、青铜礼兵器、贝货、布帛、木材和可供再分配的贡品。储备足以应对一般歉收与边境冲突，但难以长期支撑大规模远征。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted less of precious metals than of grain stores in the capital and major towns, bronze ritual and military objects, cowries, textiles, timber, and tribute goods available for redistribution. Reserves were adequate for ordinary crop failures and border conflict but insufficient for prolonged major campaigns.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视实物征收、仓储积谷与通过赏赐稳固地方首领，对外支出谨慎，符合稳定但仍在整合中的南方封国特征。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, emphasizing in-kind collection, granary accumulation, and the stabilization of local chiefs through rewards, with cautious external spending—characteristic of a stable but still-consolidating southern feudal state.

description.zh: 驻守梅里的核心部队，由公族武士与直属侍从组成，负责宫城警卫、仪仗与关键战时突击。
description.en: The core force stationed at Meili, composed of royal lineage warriors and direct retainers, responsible for palace security, ceremonial duties, and decisive wartime action.

description.zh: 由农户与附属聚落征发，装备戈、矛、弓和木盾，是吴国主要作战兵力，适于河网、平原与湿地环境。
description.en: Levied from peasant households and subordinate settlements, armed with dagger-axes, spears, bows, and wooden shields; they formed Wu’s principal fighting force and were suited to riverine plains and marshland terrain.

description.zh: 规模有限，体现对周式军事制度的采纳，主要用于统帅、仪礼与开阔地带作战，而非吴地全部地形。
description.en: Limited in scale, reflecting adoption of Zhou-style warfare; used chiefly for command, prestige, and combat on open ground rather than in all Wu terrain.

description.zh: 依托江河湖泽活动，负责巡逻、输送、突袭与渡河作战，是吴国区别于北方诸侯的重要军事特色。
description.en: Operating on rivers, lakes, and marshes, these forces handled patrol, transport, raids, and river crossings, forming a key military distinction from northern Zhou states.

commanderInChief.zh: 吴州章亲自统军，辅以司马与诸卿
commanderInChief.en: Zhouzhang of Wu as supreme commander, assisted by the Minister of War and noble officers

technology.zh: 军事技术处于西周晚期水平，以青铜兵器、复合弓、皮甲木盾、少量战车和水上运输为主。铁器在此时若有出现也极为稀少，尚未广泛用于军备。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a Late Western Zhou level, centered on bronze weapons, composite bows, leather armor and wooden shields, a small number of chariots, and riverine transport. Iron, if present at all, was extremely rare and not yet widely used for arms.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南方与西方未完全纳入统治的部族势力、边境冲突，以及未来楚国向东扩张的潜在压力；对北方大国则更多是间接政治压力而非立即军事征服风险。
threats.en: Main threats came from tribal groups to the south and west not yet fully integrated into Wu’s rule, local border fighting, and the longer-term prospect of Chu’s eastern expansion; northern great states posed more indirect political pressure than an immediate conquest threat.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约四十万，在西周诸侯中属中等规模。人口分布高度依赖河流、湖沼边缘和可耕低地，密度低于中原核心区，但资源条件较好。
populationDescription.en: With about 400,000 people, Wu was a middling polity by Western Zhou standards. Population distribution depended heavily on rivers, lake margins, and cultivable lowlands; density was lower than in the Central Plains core but resource conditions were favorable.

name.en: Settlements around the Wu capital district

socialClasses.zh: 上层为君主、公族与卿大夫；其下为武士、邑宰、祭司和工官；中层为自耕农、渔民、船民与手工业者；下层为依附农户、俘获人口与地位较低的部族成员。不同区域间整合程度不一。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the ruler, royal kin, and noble officers; beneath them were warriors, town stewards, ritual specialists, and craft overseers; the middle consisted of peasant farmers, fishers, boat people, and artisans; the lower strata included dependent households, captives, and lower-status tribal populations. Levels of integration varied across regions.

allies.zh: 名义上臣属于周王室，并与部分东南地方部族和邻近小型聚落维持从属或婚姻性质关系；尚无稳固意义上的大型诸侯同盟。
allies.en: Wu was nominally subordinate to the Zhou royal house and maintained tributary or marriage-based ties with some southeastern tribal groups and neighboring small settlements; it had no major durable interstate alliance in the later sense.

enemies.zh: 无持续全面战争中的固定大敌，但与周边未归附部族存在摩擦，并对西方渐强的楚势力保持警惕。
enemies.en: Wu had no single permanent great enemy in continuous total war, but faced friction with unsubdued neighboring tribes and remained wary of the growing power of Chu to the west.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以守成为主：对周保持名义恭顺以获取合法性，对邻近部族采用征抚并用，对远方诸侯则通过间接朝聘维持存在感，优先目标是巩固下游长江地区控制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was primarily defensive and consolidating: maintaining nominal deference to Zhou for legitimacy, combining coercion and appeasement toward nearby tribes, and sustaining limited presence among distant feudal states through indirect embassies, while prioritizing control of the lower Yangtze region.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期过渡阶段（实际仍以晚期青铜技术为主）
era.en: Early Iron Age transitional phase (in practice still predominantly Late Bronze Age technology)

keyInnovations.zh: 湿地稻作扩展、沟渠与堤岸整治、木构舟船制造、青铜兵器与礼器铸造、竹木工具改良以及周式礼制与行政记录技术的南传。
keyInnovations.en: Expansion of wet-rice cultivation, canal and embankment management, wooden boatbuilding, casting of bronze weapons and ritual vessels, improved bamboo and wooden tools, and the southward transmission of Zhou ritual and administrative record-keeping techniques.

strengths.zh: 吴国拥有优良的水网环境、较丰富的稻作与渔盐资源、灵活的舟师能力，以及借用周文化提升政治合法性的优势。其地理位置有利于区域整合与物资转运。
strengths.en: Wu possessed a favorable river network environment, relatively rich rice, fish, and salt resources, flexible riverine military capabilities, and the advantage of borrowing Zhou culture to enhance political legitimacy. Its geography favored regional integration and transport of goods.

weaknesses.zh: 国家整合度仍有限，中央对边缘地区控制较弱；贵族宗族与地方首领权力较重；正规行政和书写记录不发达；军事上尚不足以与中原强国正面对抗。
weaknesses.en: State integration remained limited, with weak central control over peripheral areas; noble lineages and local chiefs retained substantial power; formal administration and written record-keeping were underdeveloped; militarily, Wu was not yet capable of confronting the major Central Plains powers on equal terms.

outlook.zh: 若能持续吸收周礼制度、加强都城对地方的控制并发展水陆军事，吴国有望从边缘南方封国成长为区域强国；但若整合失败，则可能长期停留在松散邦国与部族联盟之间。
outlook.en: If it continued adopting Zhou institutions, strengthened capital control over local elites, and developed both riverine and land warfare, Wu could grow from a peripheral southern feudal state into a regional power; if integration faltered, it might remain suspended between a loose polity and a tribal confederation.

description.zh: 江南早期國家，逐步吸收中原制度並控制太湖流域。
description.en: An early southern state in the lower Yangtze gradually adopting Zhou institutions.

governmentForm.zh: 西周宗法封建诸侯国，君主依礼制统治，并受周王名义上的宗主权约束。
governmentForm.en: A Western Zhou hereditary feudal state ruled by a duke through ritual-based aristocratic authority, under the nominal overlordship of the Zhou king.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以宗法等级为核心，由国君、公族卿大夫、士、国人与庶民、工商与手工业者，以及数量有限的奴婢构成。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on a patrilineal aristocratic hierarchy composed of the ducal house, noble lineages and great officers, shi retainers, commoner communities, merchants and artisans, and a limited servile population.

rulingClass.zh: 姬姓公室及其分支公族卿大夫，辅以掌礼、军事和田赋事务的士阶层。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Ji ducal house and allied aristocratic lineages, supported by shi retainers managing ritual, military, and agrarian affairs.

succession.zh: 原则上实行嫡长继承，由公室嫡系男子继位，但仍受宗族共识、卿大夫支持及周礼规范影响。
succession.en: Succession was in principle agnatic primogeniture within the main ducal line, though in practice it depended on clan consensus, noble backing, and Zhou ritual norms.

structure.zh: 鲁国政体以公室为中心，朝廷由宗庙礼官、军事贵族、田赋管理者与邑宰共同运作；国家事务通过公室与卿大夫家族协商处理，地方由采邑、乡里和附属聚落构成。
structure.en: Lu’s government was centered on the ducal court, where ritual officers, military nobles, agrarian administrators, and settlement stewards operated together; affairs of state were handled through the duke in consultation with major aristocratic houses, while local administration rested on fiefs, rural communities, and subordinate settlements.

function.zh: 主持宗庙祭祀、册命、朝聘礼仪、丧葬礼制与礼器保管，维护鲁国作为周礼重镇的政治合法性。
function.en: Oversaw ancestral sacrifices, investiture and court ceremonies, funerary protocol, and custody of ritual bronzes, sustaining Lu’s legitimacy as a stronghold of Zhou rites.

function.zh: 统筹战车编组、兵员征发、边境警备、武器储备与军事训练。
function.en: Managed chariot organization, troop levies, border security, armament stores, and military training.

function.zh: 管理耕地分配、贡赋征收、劳役调度、仓储与农业季节安排。
function.en: Handled land allocation, collection of produce obligations, labor corvée organization, granary matters, and seasonal agricultural coordination.

function.zh: 负责城郭修筑、道路、沟渠、作坊与大型公共工程。
function.en: Responsible for walls, roads, ditches, workshops, and major public works.

function.zh: 记录册命、盟誓、赋役名册、宗族谱系及外交文书，使用早期汉字书写于简牍与青铜铭文。
function.en: Recorded appointments, oaths, levy registers, clan genealogies, and diplomatic texts, using early Chinese writing on bamboo, wood, and bronze inscriptions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方统治以公室直属区域和卿大夫采邑并存。曲阜周边由公室控制较强，外围地区由贵族家臣、邑宰与族长管理，国家通过贡赋、兵役与礼制维系统合。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined ducal domains with aristocratic fiefs. Areas near Qufu were under stronger ducal control, while outer districts were managed by noble stewards, settlement heads, and lineage elders; the state maintained cohesion through tribute, military service, and ritual order.

legalSystem.zh: 法律尚未形成后世成文法体系，主要依周礼、宗法规范、君命及贵族裁断处理案件。刑罚包括罚贡、劳役、笞击、流放与死刑，贵族与庶民适用标准并不完全相同。
legalSystem.en: Lu did not yet possess a later-style codified legal system; most disputes were judged through Zhou ritual norms, kinship rules, ducal commands, and aristocratic adjudication. Punishments included fines in goods, labor penalties, beating, exile, and execution, with nobles and commoners not always treated by identical standards.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自井田式贡赋观念下的农业实物征收、劳役、附庸进献、战利品分配以及手工业供纳；税制以谷物、布帛、牲畜和劳力为主，而非统一货币税。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from agricultural produce obligations under the idealized well-field order, corvée labor, subordinate tribute, shares of war booty, and craft deliveries; taxation was based mainly on grain, textiles, livestock, and labor rather than a uniform monetary tax.

religion.zh: 鲁国信奉西周宗庙祭祀体系，重视祖先崇拜、社稷祭祀、天命观念及占卜实践，并尊奉周公传统。
religion.en: Lu followed the Western Zhou ritual-religious system, emphasizing ancestor worship, sacrifices to the soil and grain, the doctrine of Heaven’s mandate, divination practices, and reverence for the legacy of the Duke of Zhou.

philosophy.zh: 公元前800年的鲁国尚未形成后世儒家等系统哲学，但政治伦理已高度重视礼、德、宗法秩序、敬祖与君臣名分，这些观念后来成为儒家思想的重要土壤。
philosophy.en: In 800 BCE Lu had not yet produced later systematic schools such as Confucianism, but its political ethics already strongly emphasized ritual propriety, virtue, patrilineal order, ancestral reverence, and correct social hierarchy, forming important groundwork for later Confucian thought.

writingSystem.zh: 使用西周时期早期汉字，见于青铜器铭文、木简竹简与礼仪记录，书写主要服务于册命、祭祀、土地与家族事务。
writingSystem.en: Lu used early Chinese script of the Western Zhou type, seen in bronze inscriptions, wooden and bamboo records, and ritual documentation, mainly for appointments, sacrifice, land matters, and lineage affairs.

culturalAchievements.zh: 鲁国以保存周礼、宗庙制度、青铜礼器传统、乐舞仪式和贵族教育闻名。其文化成就在于对礼制文本、礼乐实践与家族谱系记忆的延续，而非大规模帝国式建筑。
culturalAchievements.en: Lu was noted for preserving Zhou ritual, ancestral institutions, bronze ceremonial traditions, court music and dance, and aristocratic education. Its chief cultural achievement lay in continuity of ritual practice, lineage memory, and courtly norms rather than monumental imperial construction.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系中的古汉语
languageFamily.en: Old Chinese within the Sino-Tibetan language family

gdpDescription.zh: 鲁国经济为典型农业主导的西周诸侯国经济，财富主要体现为粮食产出、青铜器与织物储备、牲畜数量和对劳动力的控制。总量在中等诸侯国中较为稳健，但远不及后世集权国家的货币化经济。
gdpDescription.en: Lu’s economy was a typical agriculture-dominated Western Zhou feudal economy in which wealth was expressed mainly through grain output, bronze and textile stores, livestock, and control over labor. Its total scale was solid for a mid-sized state but far from the monetized economies of later centralized kingdoms.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟黍农业、小麦种植的早期扩展、桑麻纺织、青铜铸造、骨角器制作、陶器、畜牧和礼器制造。
mainIndustries.en: Millet-based agriculture, early expansion of wheat cultivation, silk and hemp textiles, bronze casting, bone and horn crafts, pottery, animal husbandry, and production of ritual vessels.

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、布帛、漆木器、青铜器、盐类、牲畜、玉石制品与手工器物；与邻近诸侯间的交换多带有贡礼和政治性质。
tradeGoods.en: Grain, textiles, lacquered wood goods, bronzes, salt, livestock, jade items, and crafted wares; exchange with neighboring states often had tributary and political features as well as commercial ones.

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富以土地使用权、谷仓储粮、家畜、农具和纺织品为主；贵族财富则集中于采邑收入、战车、青铜礼器、奴婢和附属人口。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households held wealth mainly in land-use rights, stored grain, animals, tools, and textiles, while noble wealth was concentrated in fief revenues, chariots, bronze ritual vessels, servile dependents, and attached populations.

unit.en: shi grain equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以宗法封建关系为基础的农业再分配经济，兼具礼物交换、实物贡赋、劳役征调与有限区域市场交换。
economicSystem.en: An agrarian redistributive economy based on patrilineal feudal relations, combining gift exchange, produce tribute, corvée extraction, and limited regional market exchange.

source.en: Corvée Conversion and Ducal Domain Output

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以实物储备为主，集中在曲阜及若干附属仓廪，包括谷物、布帛、青铜、牲畜与礼器，而非大量铸币金银储藏。财政稳健但抗灾能力有限，需依赖丰年积谷。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was primarily held in kind, concentrated in Qufu and subsidiary granaries, including grain, textiles, bronze, livestock, and ritual vessels rather than large stores of coined precious metals. Finances were stable but disaster resilience remained limited and depended on accumulation in good harvest years.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守，强调维持宗庙礼仪、稳定军赋与仓储，不追求激进征敛。国家通过礼制正当化征收，并力图避免对公族与国人造成过重压力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, emphasizing maintenance of ritual institutions, stable military obligations, and granary reserves rather than aggressive extraction. The state legitimized revenue through ritual duty and sought to avoid excessive pressure on noble lineages and commoner communities.

description.zh: 由公室与卿大夫提供战车、御者、甲士与随车步卒，是鲁军的核心突击与身份象征力量。
description.en: Supplied by the ducal house and noble lineages with chariots, drivers, armored warriors, and accompanying infantry, this was the core strike arm and status symbol of Lu’s army.

description.zh: 由国人与属民征发组成，装备矛、戈、弓箭、短兵器和皮甲，是作战与守城的主体。
description.en: Raised from commoner communities and dependent populations, these troops carried spears, daggers-axes, bows, sidearms, and leather armor, forming the main body for field fighting and defense.

description.zh: 负责曲阜城防、仓廪护卫、交通节点与边境警戒，平时兼具治安与征调职能。
description.en: Tasked with defending Qufu, guarding granaries, securing transport points, and patrolling borders; in peacetime they also performed policing and levy-related duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 鲁真公兼最高统帅，军政日常由司马与诸卿分掌
commanderInChief.en: Duke Zhen of Lu as commander-in-chief, with day-to-day military affairs handled by the Minister of War and leading nobles

technology.zh: 鲁军处于周代战车战争体系，武器以青铜戈、矛、剑、箭镞为主，防具多为皮甲和少量青铜甲片。铁器时代已开始于更广区域出现，但鲁国军备仍以青铜传统为核心。
technology.en: Lu’s army belonged to the Zhou chariot-warfare system, with bronze daggers-axes, spears, swords, and arrowheads as its main weapons; protection was mostly leather armor with limited bronze scale components. Although the Iron Age had begun more broadly, Lu’s armament remained primarily rooted in bronze traditions.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近齐、宋等诸侯的边界摩擦、周王室权威衰弱引发的地区竞争，以及内部贵族家族势力坐大可能造成的政治不稳。
threats.en: Main threats came from border friction with neighboring states such as Qi and Song, growing regional competition under weakening Zhou royal authority, and the possibility of internal instability from overmighty noble lineages.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在曲阜周边平原、河谷耕作区与若干城邑聚落。总人口在周代诸侯中属中等规模，足以维持礼制国家与有限军事动员。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the plains around Qufu, river-valley farming zones, and several walled settlements. This was a medium-sized population for a Zhou state, sufficient to sustain a ritual court and limited military mobilization.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为公室与公族卿大夫，中层为士与掌事家臣，下层为农民、工匠、商贾与附属人口；礼制身份与血缘关系比单纯财富更决定社会地位。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the ducal house and noble lineages, followed by shi retainers and administrative dependents; below them were farmers, artisans, merchants, and subordinate populations. Ritual status and kinship mattered more than wealth alone in determining rank.

allies.zh: 鲁国在名义上依附周王室，并与部分姬姓诸侯维持礼仪性的宗亲关系；与宋等邻国可在特定时期保持务实合作，但联盟多不稳固。
allies.en: Lu was nominally aligned with the Zhou royal house and maintained ritual kinship ties with some fellow Ji-lineage states; it could also cooperate pragmatically with neighbors such as Song at times, though alliances were usually fluid.

enemies.zh: 无长期绝对死敌，但与齐国存在潜在竞争与边境压力；对周边小邦和地方部族也需保持戒备。
enemies.en: It had no single permanent mortal enemy, but faced latent rivalry and border pressure from Qi, while also remaining wary of smaller neighboring polities and local groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 鲁国外交以守礼、尊周、维持边境均势与避免过度扩张为主。其目标是通过朝聘、婚姻、会盟和馈赠维护地位，而非依靠持续征服建立霸权。
foreignPolicy.en: Lu’s foreign policy stressed observance of ritual, respect for Zhou authority, maintenance of border balance, and avoidance of overextension. It sought status through visits, marriage ties, meetings, and diplomatic gifts rather than through sustained conquest.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期，仍深受晚青铜时代周文化影响
era.en: Early Iron Age, still strongly shaped by late Bronze Age Zhou culture

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的青铜铸造、战车军事体系、早期铁器接触、夯土城防、牛耕与多样化农具、礼器铭文书写与仓储管理技术。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced bronze casting, chariot warfare systems, early contact with iron implements, rammed-earth fortification, ox traction and diversified farm tools, inscribed ritual bronzes, and granary management techniques.

strengths.zh: 鲁国最大的优势在于政治合法性强、周礼传统深厚、公室声望较高、农业基础稳定且地处山东交通要冲。其文化保守性有助于内部秩序和贵族认同。
strengths.en: Lu’s chief strengths were strong political legitimacy, deep Zhou ritual tradition, high prestige of the ducal house, a stable agrarian base, and a strategic position in Shandong. Its cultural conservatism reinforced internal order and noble cohesion.

weaknesses.zh: 国家规模不大，财政和兵力有限；对贵族家族的依赖较深，中央集权不足；经济货币化程度低，面对长期战争或灾荒时承压明显。
weaknesses.en: The state was not large, and its finances and manpower were limited; it depended heavily on noble lineages and lacked strong centralization; low monetization also made it vulnerable in prolonged war or major famine.

outlook.zh: 公元前800年的鲁国总体稳定，短期内可继续作为尊周守礼的中等诸侯国存在。但随着春秋时期区域竞争加剧，若不能抑制卿大夫势力并强化军政整合，鲁国将逐渐从文化重镇转为政治弱国。
outlook.en: In 800 BCE Lu was broadly stable and could continue in the near term as a middling state known for upholding Zhou ritual order. However, as regional competition intensified in the Spring and Autumn era, Lu risked declining from a cultural center into a politically weaker state if it failed to restrain noble houses and strengthen military-administrative integration.

description.zh: 山東中部的禮樂文化重鎮，為周宗法制度的重要保存者。
description.en: A central Shandong state known for preserving Zhou ritual and aristocratic traditions.

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制下的封建邦国，名义上臣属于周王室，但在汉水与长江中游地区拥有高度自主权。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary feudal monarchy nominally subordinate to the Zhou royal house, but exercising substantial autonomy in the Han River and middle Yangtze regions.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由楚君与芈姓宗室、受封贵族、地方邑君与武士、祭司与巫觋、工匠与商贾、广大农民与附属部族构成，兼具周式宗法封建秩序与南方部族性结构。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the Chu ruler and Mi clan royal house, enfeoffed nobles, local lords and warriors, priests and shamans, artisans and merchants, and a broad base of peasants and subordinate tribal peoples, combining Zhou-style patrilineal feudal order with southern tribal structures.

rulingClass.zh: 以芈姓王族、斗氏、成氏、屈氏、景氏等贵族宗族为统治核心，凭宗族血缘、军功与封邑控制土地、军队和祭祀权。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the Mi royal clan and major aristocratic lineages such as Dou, Cheng, Qu, and Jing, controlling land, troops, and ritual authority through kinship, military merit, and hereditary fiefs.

succession.zh: 通常由君主嫡长系继承，但在早期楚国，宗室内部亦可能因贵族支持、军事威望与祭祀合法性而出现继承竞争。
succession.en: Succession generally followed the senior legitimate male line, though in early Chu internal competition within the royal house could arise depending on aristocratic backing, military prestige, and ritual legitimacy.

structure.zh: 楚国政体以国君为核心，中央由宗室与贵族辅政，地方则通过封邑、属地与附庸部族间接统治。朝廷尚未形成后世成熟官僚制，但已有负责军政、祭祀、赋役、仓储和对外事务的常设职能集团。
structure.en: The Chu polity was centered on the ruler, with central governance assisted by royal kinsmen and nobles, while local territories were governed indirectly through fiefs, dependent lands, and subordinate tribal communities. A fully developed bureaucracy had not yet emerged, but enduring functional offices already existed for military affairs, ritual, taxation and labor, granaries, and foreign relations.

function.zh: 管理君主近臣、宫室、诏令传达、王族事务与宫廷供给。
function.en: Managed the ruler's attendants, palace establishments, transmission of commands, royal clan matters, and court provisions.

function.zh: 统筹征兵、军队调度、边防驻守、武器储备与战时后勤。
function.en: Oversaw levies, troop deployment, frontier defense, weapons stores, and wartime logistics.

function.zh: 管理耕地分配、屯垦、农业劳役、水利修整与人口编户。
function.en: Managed arable land allocation, agricultural colonization, farm labor obligations, water control works, and household registration.

function.zh: 负责城郭营建、道路、渡口、仓廪、冶炼作坊和大型徭役组织。
function.en: Handled city walls, roads, ferries, granaries, smelting workshops, and the organization of major corvée labor.

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、山川社祀、占卜、礼仪和与巫觋体系的协调。
function.en: Directed ancestral rites, cults of mountains and rivers, divination, ceremony, and coordination with shamanic institutions.

function.zh: 征收谷物、土贡、金属与手工业品，管理府库与军需储备。
function.en: Collected grain, local tribute, metals, and craft goods, and administered treasury and military stockpiles.

function.zh: 处理对周王室、邻近诸侯、边地部族的朝聘、盟约与通婚事务。
function.en: Handled missions, alliances, and marriage diplomacy with the Zhou royal house, neighboring states, and frontier tribes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托丹阳周边直属领地、宗室封邑、贵族采邑及附属部族首领。国君通过宗族婚姻、贡赋义务、军事征召和定期朝觐维系控制；在新扩张地区则设置军镇与屯垦点，逐步加强直接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on crown lands around Danyang, royal and aristocratic fiefs, and subordinate tribal chiefs. The ruler maintained control through clan marriage ties, tribute obligations, military levies, and periodic attendance at court; in newly expanded regions, military settlements and agricultural colonies gradually strengthened direct authority.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、宗法秩序、君主裁断和礼制为主，尚无后世成文法典。案件多由贵族长官、邑君和宗族首领依身份等级、赔偿原则与惩戒性刑罚处理，重视叛乱、盗窃、逃役和对祭祀秩序的破坏。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested chiefly on customary law, patrilineal norms, ruler's judgment, and ritual propriety rather than later codified statutes. Cases were usually handled by noble officials, local lords, and clan heads according to rank, compensation principles, and punitive sanctions, with particular concern for rebellion, theft, evasion of labor service, and violations of ritual order.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自田赋、贡纳、徭役、战利品和附庸贡献。核心农业区按户与田地征收谷物，边地和山林地区多以木材、漆、羽毛、兽皮、铜料等土产输纳，并征调成年男子服兵役和劳役。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from land tax, tribute, corvée, war booty, and contributions from dependent communities. Core farming districts paid grain levies by household and landholding, while frontier and forest regions rendered local products such as timber, lacquer, feathers, hides, and copper materials, along with military and labor service from adult men.

religion.zh: 楚人信仰祖先崇拜、自然神灵、山川社祀与巫术传统并存。其宗教体系既受周礼影响，也保留南方强烈的巫觋、灵媒、歌舞祭祀和地方神祇崇拜。
religion.en: Chu religion combined ancestor worship, nature spirits, cults of mountains and rivers, and shamanic traditions. Its religious system was influenced by Zhou ritual forms while preserving strong southern practices of spirit mediation, ecstatic ritual, song-dance offerings, and local deity cults.

philosophy.zh: 公元前800年的楚国尚未形成系统哲学学派，其思想核心是宗法伦理、敬天祭祖、重卜筮与务实扩张相结合的政治文化；同时兼容多族群传统，具有较强开放性。
philosophy.en: In 800 BCE Chu had not yet developed formal philosophical schools. Its intellectual life centered on patrilineal ethics, reverence for Heaven and ancestors, reliance on divination, and a pragmatic expansionist political culture, while remaining notably receptive to diverse ethnic traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 使用早期汉字书写体系，主要见于青铜器铭文、礼器记事和行政性标记，整体上属于西周文字传统在南方的地方化形态。
writingSystem.en: Chu used the early Chinese writing system, chiefly on bronze inscriptions, ritual vessel records, and administrative markings, representing a southern regional adaptation of the Western Zhou script tradition.

culturalAchievements.zh: 楚文化在青铜礼器制造、漆器工艺、织物装饰、乐舞祭礼、羽旄与兽纹审美方面已显特色。其文化融合周式礼制与长江流域本土艺术传统，为后来高度鲜明的楚风奠定基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Chu culture already showed distinctive strengths in bronze ritual production, lacquer craftsmanship, decorated textiles, musical and dance ceremonies, and aesthetics featuring feathers and animal motifs. It fused Zhou ritual forms with indigenous Yangtze artistic traditions, laying the groundwork for the later highly recognizable Chu style.

languageFamily.zh: 官方与贵族交流主要使用古汉语的楚地方变体，同时境内广泛存在与南方本土族群相关的非华夏语言成分。
languageFamily.en: Official and aristocratic communication primarily used a Chu regional form of Old Chinese, while non-Sinitic linguistic elements connected to indigenous southern populations were also widespread within the realm.

gdpDescription.zh: 楚国在前800年属于中原诸侯体系中的富庶扩张型大国。其经济基础在于长江中游和汉水流域的稻作、粟作与混合农业，并辅以林产、渔猎、铜矿与手工业。虽然货币化程度有限，但以粮食、青铜和劳役衡量，其总量已十分可观。
gdpDescription.en: By 800 BCE Chu was a prosperous expansionary power within the Zhou interstate world. Its economy rested on rice agriculture, millet farming, and mixed cultivation in the middle Yangtze and Han River basins, supplemented by forest products, fishing and hunting, copper resources, and craft production. Monetization remained limited, but in grain, bronze, and labor terms its total output was already substantial.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、林业、渔猎、青铜冶铸、漆器、纺织、木工、制陶、盐与地方矿产采集。
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, forestry, fishing and hunting, bronze smelting and casting, lacquerware, textiles, woodworking, pottery, salt, and extraction of local mineral resources.

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、粟、木材、漆、竹木器、兽皮、羽毛、铜料、青铜器、玉石、织物和地方珍异物产。
tradeGoods.en: Rice, millet, timber, lacquer, bamboo and wooden goods, hides, feathers, copper materials, bronze objects, jade, textiles, and distinctive local products.

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在耕地使用权、粮食储备、家畜、木竹器具和宗族关系；贵族财富则集中于土地、附庸人口、青铜礼器、战车、武器和祭祀资源。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in cultivation rights, grain stores, livestock, bamboo and wooden tools, and clan ties; aristocratic wealth was concentrated in land, dependent populations, bronze ritual vessels, chariots, weapons, and ritual resources.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业为本、贵族控制土地和人口的再分配经济。交换多通过贡赋、礼物、战利品和区域贸易实现，市场存在但尚非经济核心。
economicSystem.en: An agrarian redistributive economy in which nobles controlled land and people. Exchange operated chiefly through tribute, gifts, booty, and regional trade, with markets present but not yet central to economic life.

source.en: Tribute from Dependencies and Local Products

source.zh: 徭役折算与官营作坊产出
source.en: Corvée Value and State Workshop Output

treasuryDescription.zh: 楚国国库以粮仓、青铜、兵器、牲畜、布帛和地方贡品构成，而非单一金属货币。丹阳及周边重镇储备较充足，足以支持边地用兵、灾年调剂和大型祭祀活动。
treasuryDescription.en: The Chu treasury consisted primarily of granary stores, bronze, weapons, livestock, textiles, and tribute goods rather than a single metallic currency stock. Danyang and nearby key strongholds maintained substantial reserves sufficient for frontier campaigns, famine adjustment, and major ritual events.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重积谷备战、以贡赋养军，并通过扩张获取新人口与新土地。对新附地区通常先采取较宽松贡纳，再逐步纳入常规赋役体系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized grain accumulation for war, sustaining armies through tribute, and acquiring new population and land through expansion. Newly incorporated regions were often subjected first to lighter tribute before being absorbed into the normal tax and labor system.

description.zh: 由国君直属的精锐武士与贵族随从组成，负责宫廷警卫、战时中军和重要仪仗。
description.en: An elite force of warriors and noble retainers directly attached to the ruler, responsible for palace security, the central battle line, and important ceremonial duties.

description.zh: 以贵族战车及其附属步卒为核心，是楚军在平原和开阔地带的重要冲击力量，体现周式军事传统。
description.en: Centered on noble chariot teams and their attendant infantry, this arm was a key striking force in open terrain and reflected Zhou military traditions.

description.zh: 由农业人口和附属部族男子征发，装备矛、戈、弓、盾，是楚国作战与守城的主体。
description.en: Raised from the agrarian population and subordinate tribal males, equipped with spears, ge-daggers, bows, and shields, forming the bulk of Chu's campaign and garrison forces.

description.zh: 来自山地、林地和新附地区的辅助兵，擅长山林作战、侦察、伏击和轻装机动。
description.en: Auxiliary troops drawn from mountain, forest, and newly incorporated regions, skilled in rough-terrain warfare, scouting, ambush, and light mobility.

commanderInChief.zh: 楚霄敖兼最高统帅，日常军务由司马与宗室将领分掌
commanderInChief.en: Xiao'ao of Chu served as commander-in-chief, with routine military affairs shared by the Sima and royal clan generals

technology.zh: 军备以青铜兵器为主，包括戈、矛、剑、镞、弓和皮甲木盾。战车仍具象征与实战作用，边地部队更适应湿地、丘陵与林地环境。铁器在此时期尚未成为楚军标准装备。
technology.en: Military equipment remained primarily bronze-based, including ge-daggers, spears, swords, arrowheads, bows, leather armor, and wooden shields. Chariots retained both symbolic and practical battlefield roles, while frontier troops were better adapted to wetlands, hills, and forests. Iron had not yet become standard equipment in Chu at this date.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方与西北方向诸侯的牵制、周王室秩序下的政治压制，以及南方和东南边缘地带尚未完全整合的部族反抗。楚国也需警惕扩张过快带来的补给与内部控制压力。
threats.en: Major threats came from northern and northwestern states seeking to contain Chu, the political constraints of the Zhou order, and resistance among southern and southeastern frontier peoples not yet fully integrated. Chu also had to guard against supply strains and internal control problems caused by rapid expansion.

populationDescription.zh: 楚国人口约一百万，在前800年的诸侯国中属于规模较大的区域性强国。人口主要分布于汉水流域、长江中游及其支流盆地，农业聚落与山林部族区并存。
populationDescription.en: With roughly one million inhabitants, Chu ranked among the larger regional powers of 800 BCE. Its population was concentrated in the Han River basin, the middle Yangtze, and adjacent tributary valleys, with both farming settlements and forested tribal zones.

name.zh: 江汉平原区域中心聚落

socialClasses.zh: 最高层为国君与王族，其次为受封贵族与武士，再次为邑中自由民、工匠、商旅与宗教人员，最广大者为农民、依附者和附庸部族民众。不同族群、宗族与身份等级间差异明显。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the ruler and royal house, followed by enfeoffed nobles and warriors, then free townspeople, artisans, traders, and ritual specialists, with the largest group consisting of peasants, dependents, and subject tribal populations. Distinctions among ethnic groups, clans, and status ranks were pronounced.

allies.zh: 楚国在前800年没有稳定意义上的现代式盟友，主要通过向周王室称臣、与周边小邦通婚、以及吸纳南方部族首领形成松散支持网络。
allies.en: In 800 BCE Chu lacked stable allies in the modern sense, relying instead on nominal submission to the Zhou royal house, marriage ties with nearby minor polities, and loose support networks built by incorporating southern tribal chiefs.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对者主要是警惕楚国坐大的北方诸侯，以及不愿接受楚国控制的边地部族和地方势力。与周王室关系虽未完全决裂，但带有等级与地位上的长期紧张。
enemies.en: Its principal potential enemies were northern states wary of Chu's growth, along with frontier tribes and local powers unwilling to accept Chu authority. Relations with the Zhou royal house had not fully broken down, but they carried long-term tension over rank and status.

foreignPolicy.zh: 楚国外交以承认周名义秩序、争取政治合法性、稳固北方边界、向南和东南扩张、通过婚姻与封赏整合部族为核心。其目标是在不立即挑战周体系的前提下，逐步建立独立的大国地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Chu foreign policy centered on recognizing the nominal Zhou order, seeking political legitimacy, securing its northern frontier, expanding southward and southeastward, and integrating tribes through marriage and rewards. Its aim was to build an increasingly independent great-power position without openly overturning the Zhou system at once.

era.zh: 青铜时代晚期至早期铁器时代过渡
era.en: Late Bronze Age to Early Iron Age Transition

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的青铜铸造、战车运用、复合农业体系、漆器工艺、木竹器制造、地方水利与湿地开发技术。铁器知识可能开始传入东亚更广区域，但在楚国尚未普遍用于生产与军备。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included advanced bronze casting, chariot warfare, mixed agricultural systems, lacquer craftsmanship, bamboo and wooden manufacture, and local water-control and wetland reclamation methods. Knowledge of iron may have begun entering wider East Asia, but it was not yet widely used in Chu production or armament.

strengths.zh: 楚国疆域广阔、人口众多、农业与林泽资源丰富，且能融合周礼制度与南方地方传统，具备强大的扩张潜力。其贵族武装、边地辅助兵和灵活的部族整合能力，使其在区域竞争中极具优势。
strengths.en: Chu possessed a broad territory, large population, and abundant agricultural, forest, and wetland resources, while successfully blending Zhou institutions with southern local traditions. Its noble war forces, frontier auxiliaries, and flexible integration of tribal communities gave it strong expansionary potential in regional competition.

weaknesses.zh: 国家整合程度仍有限，许多地区依赖宗族、封邑和附庸首领间接控制。政治秩序受贵族力量牵制，行政与财政标准化不足，若扩张过快，容易出现边地离心、补给困难和继承争斗。
weaknesses.en: State integration remained limited, with many regions controlled indirectly through clans, fiefs, and subordinate chiefs. Political order was constrained by aristocratic power, and administration and finance were not fully standardized; if expansion proceeded too quickly, frontier fragmentation, logistical strain, and succession struggles could result.

outlook.zh: 前800年的楚国正处于上升期。若能继续整合江汉流域、稳定宗室继承并强化对附属地区的直接控制，楚国有望在未来数世纪成长为挑战中原秩序的超级强国。
outlook.en: In 800 BCE Chu was in a clear phase of ascent. If it could continue integrating the Jianghan region, stabilize royal succession, and strengthen direct control over dependent territories, Chu was well positioned to grow over the coming centuries into a superpower capable of challenging the central Zhou order.

description.zh: 長江中游大國，兼具中原與南方文化特徵，正在迅速擴張。
description.en: A large middle Yangtze state blending Zhou and southern traditions, expanding rapidly.

governmentForm.zh: 西周封建体系下的世袭诸侯国，由姬姓宗室分封统治，并承认周王的宗主权。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary feudal vassal state under the Western Zhou order, ruled by an enfeoffed Ji clan line that acknowledged the suzerainty of the Zhou king.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以宗法贵族秩序为核心，上层为国君与公族、卿大夫及士，下层为庶民农户、工匠与商贾，并有一定数量的家内臣属与战俘奴婢。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on a patrilineal aristocratic order, with the ruler and ducal clan at the top, followed by great nobles and shi retainers, beneath whom were common peasant households, artisans, and merchants, along with smaller numbers of household dependents and captive slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 姬姓国君、晋国公族、受封卿大夫家族及掌兵治邑的士阶层。
rulingClass.en: The Ji-clan ruler, the Jin ducal lineage, enfeoffed noble lineages, and the shi stratum who administered settlements and led troops.

succession.zh: 以父死子继的世袭为原则，通常由嫡长子继位，但在早期诸侯国内部亦可能因公族竞争而出现争位。
succession.en: Succession was hereditary, ideally from father to son and usually through the legitimate eldest son, though struggles among collateral noble branches could still arise in early Zhou states.

structure.zh: 晋国政府以国君为中心，依托宗法封君网络治理。中央没有后世官僚国家那样细密的常设机构，而是由卿、大夫、师、宰、史等贵族官职共同承担军政、祭祀、司法与仓储事务；地方则通过封邑、采地与属邑首领管理。
structure.en: The Jin government was centered on the marquis and governed through a kin-based feudal network. It did not yet possess the dense permanent bureaucracy of later states; instead, aristocratic offices such as qing, dafu, military commanders, stewards, and scribes jointly handled military affairs, ritual, justice, and stores, while local administration relied on fiefs, appanages, and subordinate settlement chiefs.

function.zh: 主持宗庙祭祀、册命礼仪、盟誓与对周礼制的执行，维系国君合法性与贵族秩序。
function.en: Oversaw ancestral rites, investiture ceremonies, oath-making, and the performance of Zhou ritual norms, sustaining the ruler’s legitimacy and noble order.

function.zh: 负责征发兵员、整备战车、管理马匹、武库与边地防务。
function.en: Handled troop levies, chariot preparation, horse management, arsenals, and frontier defense.

function.zh: 统计田土、征收谷物与劳役、管理仓廪，并协调封邑贡纳。
function.en: Recorded agricultural land, collected grain and labor dues, managed granaries, and coordinated tribute from fiefs.

function.zh: 保管盟书、世系、赏罚记录、祭祀记载与外交文告。
function.en: Kept alliance texts, genealogies, records of rewards and punishments, ritual documentation, and diplomatic communications.

function.zh: 管理青铜铸造、兵器制造、礼器生产、车具木工与官营手工业。
function.en: Supervised bronze casting, weapon production, ritual vessel manufacture, chariot carpentry, and state-controlled craft activity.

localAdmin.zh: 地方统治依靠国君直辖区域与贵族封邑并存。核心地区由国君派出的家臣、邑宰与军吏管理，外围则由世袭贵族控制采地，向都城提供兵员、贡物与劳役。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined territories directly controlled by the ruler with aristocratic fiefs. Core regions were managed by stewards, town overseers, and military officers appointed by the court, while outer areas were held by hereditary nobles who supplied troops, tribute, and labor to the capital.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以周礼、宗法规范、盟誓约束和国君裁断为主，尚未形成成文法典化体系。贵族与庶民适用标准并不完全相同，重大案件常由国君与卿大夫共同处置。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested mainly on Zhou ritual norms, kinship obligations, oath-bound conventions, and rulings by the marquis rather than a fully codified law code. Nobles and commoners were not judged by exactly the same standards, and major cases were often handled jointly by the ruler and leading nobles.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政来源主要包括农业贡赋、封邑朝贡、劳役征发、战利品分配以及手工业与牧养产出。税制仍属实物与劳役并重的早期形态，以谷物、牲畜、织物和服役为主。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from agricultural tribute, offerings from fiefs, labor service, distributions of war booty, and output from crafts and herding. Taxation remained an early mixed system of payment in kind and labor obligations, centered on grain, livestock, textiles, and service.

religion.zh: 信仰体系属于西周宗教传统，重视天命、祖先崇拜、土地山川祭祀与国家礼典。国君通过祭祖与奉行周礼来证明其统治正当性。
religion.en: Its religious life followed Western Zhou tradition, emphasizing Heaven’s mandate, ancestor worship, sacrifices to local powers and natural spirits, and state ritual observance. The ruler demonstrated legitimacy through ancestral rites and adherence to Zhou ritual norms.

philosophy.zh: 公元前800年的晋国尚处于诸子哲学兴起之前，主导观念为宗法伦理、礼制秩序、祖先敬奉与政治道德观念的早期形态。
philosophy.en: In 800 BCE, Jin preceded the flourishing of the classical philosophical schools; dominant ideas were those of clan ethics, ritual order, reverence for ancestors, and early conceptions of political morality.

writingSystem.zh: 使用西周时期的古汉字书写系统，主要见于青铜器铭文、盟誓文书与简短行政记录，文字风格接近金文传统。
writingSystem.en: It used the early Chinese script of the Western Zhou period, mainly attested in bronze inscriptions, covenant texts, and short administrative records, in a style belonging to the bronze-script tradition.

culturalAchievements.zh: 晋国文化成就体现在礼器铸造、宗庙礼制、战车贵族文化、青铜工艺与对汾河流域周文化的扩展整合。其贵族社会为后来的晋国政治与春秋霸业奠定了礼制和军事基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Jin’s cultural achievements lay in ritual bronze production, ancestral temple practice, chariot-aristocratic culture, bronze craftsmanship, and the extension and integration of Zhou culture in the Fen River region. Its noble society laid the ritual and military foundations for Jin’s later prominence in the Spring and Autumn era.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系早期汉语支，通行上古汉语的西周地区变体。
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, specifically Early Sinitic; the language in use was a regional Western Zhou form of Old Chinese.

gdpDescription.zh: 晋国经济以农业为绝对主体，辅以畜牧、青铜手工业、木作、骨角器制作和有限区域贸易。汾河流域土地较为适宜耕作，支撑了较强的人口承载力和动员能力。由于时代尚早，经济总量应理解为粮食与实物产出的折算值，而非现代货币意义上的GDP。
gdpDescription.en: Jin’s economy was overwhelmingly agrarian, supplemented by herding, bronze craft production, woodworking, bone and horn manufacture, and limited regional trade. The Fen River basin offered comparatively favorable agricultural land, supporting substantial population density and mobilization capacity. Given the period, total output is best understood as a conversion of grain and in-kind production rather than GDP in the modern monetary sense.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟黍农业、小麦种植初步发展、牛羊马畜牧、青铜冶铸、兵器制造、车马制作、织造与制陶。
mainIndustries.en: Millet agriculture, early wheat cultivation, cattle-sheep-horse pastoralism, bronze metallurgy, weapon production, chariot manufacture, weaving, and pottery.

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、青铜器、兵器、马匹、牛羊、皮革、织物、盐类、木材及礼器交换品。
tradeGoods.en: Grain, bronzes, weapons, horses, cattle and sheep, leather, textiles, salt products, timber, and exchange goods for ritual use.

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在土地使用权、粮食储备、农具、家畜与织物上；贵族财富则集中于封邑收入、青铜礼器、战车、马匹与依附人口。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was expressed mainly in land-use access, grain stores, tools, livestock, and textiles; aristocratic wealth was concentrated in fief income, bronze ritual vessels, chariots, horses, and dependent labor.

unit.en: shi of grain equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系属于早期封建实物经济，农业家庭生产与贵族控制的再分配机制并存。国家通过贡赋、劳役和军事征发抽取资源，市场交换存在但规模有限。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was an early feudal in-kind economy combining household agricultural production with aristocratic redistribution. The state extracted resources through tribute, labor, and military levies, while market exchange existed but remained limited in scale.

source.en: Contributions from fiefs and subordinate settlements

source.zh: 手工业与官营作坊产出

category.en: Noble rewards and maintenance of vassals
Granaries, water control, and route maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 晋国国库主要不是金银货币，而是储存在仓廪、畜群、青铜器、武器、车马和贵族贡纳网络中的实物财富。稳定状态下，国库足以支持数季军役与灾年调剂。
treasuryDescription.en: The Jin treasury was not primarily held in precious-metal money but in stores of grain, livestock, bronzes, weapons, chariots, and obligations embedded in the noble tribute network. In stable conditions, it was sufficient to support several campaigning seasons and cushion poor harvest years.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，以维持宗法秩序、仓储安全和军事动员为重点。国家尽量避免对核心贵族施加过重直接负担，而通过封邑输纳和劳役体系取得资源。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, prioritizing the maintenance of kin-based order, granary security, and military mobilization. The court generally avoided imposing excessive direct burdens on core nobles and instead relied on fief contributions and labor obligations.

description.zh: 由贵族战车及其随从组成，是晋国军队的核心突击和指挥力量，强调车战、弓射与礼制化作战编组。
description.en: Composed of noble chariots and their retainers, this was the core striking and command arm of Jin’s forces, emphasizing chariot warfare, archery, and ritually ordered battle formations.

description.zh: 主要由庶民与属邑人口征发而来，装备矛、戈、弓箭与盾牌，用于主力线列、守城和后勤护卫。
description.en: Raised mainly from commoners and subordinate settlements, they were equipped with spears, dagger-axes, bows, and shields for line combat, settlement defense, and logistical security.

description.zh: 部署于边缘地区与交通要道，兼具警戒、侦察、护送与应对戎狄部族袭扰的任务。
description.en: Stationed in frontier zones and along key routes, they performed watch, reconnaissance, escort, and responses to raids by Rong and Di groups.

description.zh: 负责都城、宗庙和国君人身安全，也在需要时担任精锐后备兵力。
description.en: Protected the capital, ancestral temples, and the person of the ruler, while also serving as an elite reserve when needed.

commanderInChief.zh: 晋穆侯
commanderInChief.en: Marquis Mu of Jin

technology.zh: 军事技术属于西周晚期水平，以青铜兵器、复合弓或强弓、木制战车、皮甲与编队作战为主，铁器尚未成为主流军备。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a late Western Zhou level, centered on bronze weapons, strong bows including composite forms, wooden chariots, leather armor, and formation warfare; iron had not yet become the dominant military material.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自汾河周边及西北方向的戎狄部族袭扰、与邻近诸侯的边界摩擦，以及周王室权威变化带来的地区权力重组。
threats.en: The main threats came from raids by Rong and Di groups around the Fen region and to the northwest, border friction with neighboring states, and shifts in regional power caused by changes in the authority of the Zhou royal house.

populationDescription.zh: 晋国在前800年前后已是西周北方较强的诸侯国之一，七十万人口对于汾河流域而言代表较高的开发程度，其中绝大多数居住于乡里聚落，从事农业与相关劳役。
populationDescription.en: By around 800 BCE, Jin was already one of the stronger northern Zhou states, and a population of 700,000 indicates a relatively developed Fen River basin. The overwhelming majority lived in rural settlements and worked in agriculture and related labor.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括国君与公族、卿大夫贵族、士与家臣、自由庶民农户、工匠商贾、附庸部族居民，以及少量奴婢和战俘。
socialClasses.en: Main social strata included the ruler and ducal clan, qing and dafu nobles, shi retainers and household officers, free commoner farmers, artisans and merchants, populations of subordinate tribal communities, and smaller numbers of slaves and captives.

allies.zh: 名义上与周王室维持宗法从属关系，并与部分北方或中原姬姓诸侯保持礼仪性合作；实际联盟多依具体婚姻、会盟与共同防御需要而定。
allies.en: Formally, Jin maintained a kin-based subordinate relationship with the Zhou royal house and ritual cooperation with some northern and central Ji-lineage states; practical alliances depended on specific marriages, covenants, and common defense needs.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量并非固定大国，而是边地戎狄部族及与晋争夺土地、道路和附庸的邻近诸侯势力。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were not yet fixed great-power rivals but frontier Rong and Di groups and neighboring states competing with Jin for land, routes, and subordinate communities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 晋国对外政策以尊周、固本、扩张汾河流域控制力和压制边地部族为核心，通过婚姻、朝聘、军事征伐与安抚并用来扩大影响。
foreignPolicy.en: Jin’s foreign policy centered on honoring Zhou, consolidating its core, extending control in the Fen River basin, and restraining frontier tribes, using a mix of marriage ties, diplomatic attendance, punitive campaigns, and conciliation.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期过渡阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 技术基础仍以晚西周青铜时代传统为主，包括成熟的战车制造、青铜冶铸、夯土城防、井田式农业组织、骨角器和木工工艺；铁器可能已零星出现，但尚未广泛应用。
keyInnovations.en: The technological base still belonged largely to the late Western Zhou bronze tradition, including mature chariot manufacture, bronze metallurgy, rammed-earth fortification, well-field-style agrarian organization, and advanced bone, horn, and woodworking crafts; iron may have appeared sporadically but was not yet widely used.

strengths.zh: 晋国地处汾河流域，农业基础较强，贵族军事传统成熟，宗法秩序稳固，具备较高的人口与兵员动员能力，并且在西周北方诸侯中地位上升明显。
strengths.en: Jin’s strengths lay in its position in the Fen River basin, solid agricultural foundations, mature aristocratic military traditions, stable kin-based order, and strong capacity to mobilize population and manpower, along with a clearly rising status among northern Zhou states.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于国家结构仍高度依赖贵族封邑网络，中央直接控制有限；边地部族压力持续存在，继承与公族竞争亦可能引发内乱，且经济仍以脆弱的农业产出为主。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a state structure still highly dependent on noble fief networks, limited direct central control, continuing pressure from frontier tribes, the possibility of succession and clan rivalry causing internal disorder, and an economy still based largely on vulnerable agricultural output.

outlook.zh: 在前800年前后的稳定局势下，晋国前景总体积极。若能继续整合汾河流域、约束公族并强化对附庸与边地的控制，未来有望成长为中原政治中的主导性强国。
outlook.en: Under the stable conditions of around 800 BCE, Jin’s outlook was broadly positive. If it continued integrating the Fen River basin, restraining rival noble branches, and strengthening control over dependents and frontier zones, it had the potential to grow into a leading power in the politics of northern China.

description.zh: 河東強藩之一，日後將成為春秋霸主的基礎。
description.en: One of the stronger Zhou vassal states in the Fen River region, later a major hegemon.

governmentForm.zh: 西周封建体系下的姬姓诸侯国，初封于关中，后东迁。国君兼任周王室卿士，地位尊崇。
governmentForm.en: A Ji-surnamed Zhou feudal vassal state originally enfeoffed in the Guanzhong area, later relocated eastward. Its duke also served as minister to the Zhou court, holding a prestigious dual role.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以国君、公族与卿大夫为核心上层，士、农、工、商依次分列。郑处中原枢纽，商贾阶层活跃度较高。
socialStructure.en: The duke, ducal clan, and aristocratic ministers formed the upper tier, followed by shi, farmers, artisans, and merchants. Located at the crossroads of the Central Plains, Zheng had a notably active mercantile class.

rulingClass.zh: 姬姓公室及世卿贵族
rulingClass.en: The Ji ducal house and hereditary aristocratic ministers

succession.zh: 嫡长继承为原则，但公族内部争夺时有发生。
succession.en: Succession followed patrilineal primogeniture in principle, though intra-clan struggles were not uncommon.

structure.zh: 以国君为核心，卿大夫分掌军政、祭祀、司法。郑国君臣关系相对紧密，国小而政治高度集中。
structure.en: Centered on the duke, with aristocratic ministers managing military, ritual, and judicial affairs. Zheng was relatively small but politically concentrated.

function.zh: 管理土地、户籍与农业
function.en: Managed land, population registers, and agriculture

function.zh: 掌军队编练、征伐与边防
function.en: Oversaw military organization, campaigns, and defense

function.zh: 负责城郭、道路、水利工程
function.en: Directed construction of walls, roads, and waterworks

function.zh: 审理案件，维护秩序
function.en: Adjudicated cases and maintained public order

localAdmin.zh: 地方以都城直辖区与封邑并存，核心区由国君家臣直管。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined a directly ruled capital district with aristocratic fiefs; the core area was managed by ducal retainers.

legalSystem.zh: 以周礼与习惯法为基础，刑罚维护等级秩序。郑国后来成为最早铸刑书的诸侯国之一。
legalSystem.en: Based on Zhou ritual norms and customary law, with punishments upholding hierarchical order. Zheng would later become one of the first states to codify law.

taxationSystem.zh: 以田赋、劳役、贡纳为主要财源，商业税收亦有一定比重。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came primarily from land taxes, labor corvée, and tribute, with commercial levies also contributing.

religion.zh: 周礼祭祀体系，祖先崇拜与社稷祭为核心。
religion.en: Zhou ritual system centered on ancestor worship and state altars to soil and grain.

philosophy.zh: 尚处周礼传统阶段，重礼乐与宗法伦理。
philosophy.en: Still within the Zhou ritual tradition, emphasizing rites, music, and lineage ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 使用西周汉字，见于青铜铭文和竹简。
writingSystem.en: Used Western Zhou Chinese characters, found in bronze inscriptions and bamboo slips.

culturalAchievements.zh: 郑国地处中原要冲，文化交流频繁，礼乐文化发达，后世以郑声（音乐）闻名。
culturalAchievements.en: Zheng sat at the heart of the Central Plains, with frequent cultural exchange, a developed ritual-music culture, and later fame for its distinctive music.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系—古汉语中原方言
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Old Chinese Central Plains dialect

gdpDescription.zh: 郑国经济得益于中原交通要道地位，农业与商贸并重，物产交换活跃。
gdpDescription.en: Zheng's economy benefited from its location on major Central Plains routes, with both agriculture and commerce being important.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟黍种植、畜牧、纺织、青铜铸造、手工业与中转贸易
mainIndustries.en: Millet farming, animal husbandry, textile making, bronze casting, handicrafts, and transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、布帛、青铜器、牲畜、玉器
tradeGoods.en: Grain, textiles, bronzes, livestock, jade

expenditureBreakdown.zh: 军费约40%、宫廷与祭祀约25%、工程与仓储约20%、外交与赏赐约15%
expenditureBreakdown.en: Military ~40%, court and ritual ~25%, construction and granaries ~20%, diplomacy and rewards ~15%

treasury.zh: 中等规模粮仓与铜器库藏

branches.zh: 战车部队、步卒、城防戍军

commandStructure.zh: 国君统帅，司马主持日常军务，卿大夫各领战车与封邑部曲
commandStructure.en: The duke held supreme command, the Marshal oversaw daily military affairs, and aristocratic ministers commanded chariot units and fief levies

allies.zh: 周王室（国君兼王室卿士）
allies.en: Zhou royal house (duke served as Zhou minister)

enemies.zh: 周边竞争诸侯
enemies.en: Rival neighboring vassal states

foreignPolicy.zh: 凭借周王室卿士身份参与列国政治，积极斡旋中原事务
foreignPolicy.en: Leveraged its role as Zhou court minister to participate in interstate politics and mediate Central Plains affairs

era.zh: 青铜时代晚期/铁器初期

keyInnovations.zh: 青铜冶铸、车马技术、城郭建筑、礼器工艺
keyInnovations.en: Bronze metallurgy, chariot technology, urban fortification, ritual vessel craftsmanship

strengths.zh: 地处中原交通枢纽，国君兼周王卿士地位尊崇，商贸活跃
strengths.en: Located at the Central Plains crossroads, duke held prestigious Zhou court position, active commerce

weaknesses.zh: 国土狭小，四面受敌，战略纵深有限
weaknesses.en: Small territory, surrounded by rivals, limited strategic depth

outlook.zh: 郑国在西周末年地位显赫，东迁后将迅速崛起为春秋初期的强国，但长远面临大国挤压。
outlook.en: Zheng held a prestigious position in the late Western Zhou and would rapidly rise as a leading Spring and Autumn power after relocating east, though it would face pressure from larger states in the long run.

description.zh: 周王室重臣所领之姬姓诸侯国，处中原枢纽，即将成为春秋初期的领衔强国。
description.en: A Ji-surnamed vassal state led by a prominent Zhou court minister, positioned at the Central Plains crossroads, about to become the leading power of the early Spring and Autumn period.

governmentForm.zh: 姬姓诸侯国，承继殷商故地，以周礼治国，国君世袭。
governmentForm.en: A Ji-surnamed vassal state on former Shang territory, governed by Zhou rites under a hereditary duke.

socialStructure.zh: 贵族世卿体制明显，殷遗民与周人共处，社会结构复合。
socialStructure.en: Prominent hereditary aristocratic system; Yin-Shang remnant populations coexisted with Zhou settlers, creating a composite social structure.

rulingClass.zh: 姬姓卫公室与世卿贵族
rulingClass.en: The Ji ducal house of Wey and hereditary aristocratic ministers

succession.zh: 嫡长继承制，但宫廷内斗频繁。
succession.en: Patrilineal primogeniture, but court intrigues were frequent.

structure.zh: 中央以国君为核心，设卿大夫辅政，地方以封邑为基础行政单元。
structure.en: The duke was the center, assisted by aristocratic ministers; local administration was based on fiefs.

function.zh: 管理户籍、土地与农业
function.en: Managed population registers, land, and agriculture

function.zh: 掌军事征伐
function.en: Oversaw military campaigns

function.zh: 负责工程建设
function.en: Directed construction projects

function.zh: 主持刑法
function.en: Administered criminal law

localAdmin.zh: 都城直辖与封邑并行
localAdmin.en: Capital direct rule combined with aristocratic fiefs

legalSystem.zh: 周礼为本，兼用惯习法
legalSystem.en: Based on Zhou ritual norms, supplemented by customary law

taxationSystem.zh: 田赋、劳役与贡纳
taxationSystem.en: Land tax, corvée labor, and tribute

religion.zh: 周式祭祀体系，融合殷商祭祀传统
religion.en: Zhou ritual system, incorporating Shang sacrificial traditions

philosophy.zh: 周礼传统阶段，重宗法伦理
philosophy.en: In the Zhou ritual tradition, emphasizing lineage ethics

writingSystem.zh: 西周汉字，见于青铜铭文
writingSystem.en: Western Zhou script, found in bronze inscriptions

culturalAchievements.zh: 卫国继承殷商故地文化，礼乐之邦，诗歌传统深厚（《诗经·卫风》）。
culturalAchievements.en: Wey inherited Shang cultural heritage; known for its ritual-music tradition and rich poetic culture (Wey Odes in the Book of Songs).

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系—古汉语
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Old Chinese

gdpDescription.zh: 卫国地处黄河下游平原，农业发达，殷商故地商业传统犹存。
gdpDescription.en: Wey occupied the fertile lower Yellow River plain with developed agriculture and enduring Shang commercial traditions.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟黍种植、畜牧、纺织、青铜铸造、制陶
mainIndustries.en: Millet farming, husbandry, textiles, bronze casting, pottery

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、布帛、青铜器、陶器
tradeGoods.en: Grain, textiles, bronzes, pottery

expenditureBreakdown.zh: 军费约35%、宫廷约30%、工程约20%、其他约15%

commandStructure.en: Duke as supreme commander, Marshal overseeing military affairs

allies.zh: 周王室、鲁国
allies.en: Zhou royal house, Lu

enemies.zh: 北方戎狄部落
enemies.en: Northern Rong-Di tribes

foreignPolicy.zh: 维持中原诸侯关系，抵御北方入侵
foreignPolicy.en: Maintained Central Plains alliances while defending against northern incursions

era.zh: 青铜时代晚期

keyInnovations.zh: 青铜冶铸、纺织技术、城郭建筑
keyInnovations.en: Bronze metallurgy, textile technology, urban fortification

strengths.zh: 殷商故地文化底蕴深厚，位处中原核心
strengths.en: Rich cultural heritage from Shang, centrally located in the Plains

weaknesses.zh: 北方戎狄威胁不断，公族内斗频繁
weaknesses.en: Constant northern tribal threats, frequent intra-clan power struggles

outlook.zh: 卫国在春秋初期仍为有影响力的中等诸侯国，但长远来看将逐步衰弱。
outlook.en: Wey remained an influential mid-tier state in the early Spring and Autumn, though it would gradually decline over time.

description.zh: 殷商故地上的姬姓诸侯国，文化底蕴深厚。
description.en: A Ji-surnamed vassal state on former Shang lands, culturally rich.

governmentForm.zh: 舜帝后裔所建诸侯国，奉周礼世袭
governmentForm.en: A vassal state founded by descendants of Emperor Shun, hereditary under Zhou rites

socialStructure.zh: 以国君、宗族贵族、士、平民、依附人口构成
socialStructure.en: Composed of the duke, clan aristocracy, shi, commoners, and dependent populations

rulingClass.zh: 妫姓公室与世卿
rulingClass.en: The Gui ducal house and hereditary ministers

succession.zh: 嫡长继承，宫廷政变偶有发生
succession.en: Patrilineal primogeniture, with occasional palace coups

structure.zh: 小国政体，国君亲政，少数卿大夫辅佐
structure.en: Small-state governance with the duke ruling personally, assisted by a few ministers

function.zh: 户籍与农业
function.en: Population and agriculture

function.zh: 军务
function.en: Military affairs

localAdmin.zh: 都城为核心，辅以少量封邑
localAdmin.en: Capital-centered with a few fiefs

legalSystem.zh: 周礼惯习法
legalSystem.en: Zhou ritual-based customary law

taxationSystem.zh: 田赋与劳役
taxationSystem.en: Land tax and corvée

religion.zh: 周礼祭祀与舜帝先祖崇拜
religion.en: Zhou ritual worship and veneration of ancestral Emperor Shun

writingSystem.zh: 西周汉字
writingSystem.en: Western Zhou Chinese script

culturalAchievements.zh: 陈国以巫风与歌舞著称，保留较多上古祭祀传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Chen was known for its shamanic traditions and ceremonial dances, preserving many archaic ritual practices.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系—古汉语
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Old Chinese

gdpDescription.zh: 陈国经济以农耕为主，规模较小。
gdpDescription.en: Chen's economy was mainly agrarian and relatively small in scale.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟黍种植、畜牧、制陶
mainIndustries.en: Millet farming, animal husbandry, pottery

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、陶器
tradeGoods.en: Grain, pottery

expenditureBreakdown.zh: 军费约30%、宫廷约35%、工程约20%、其他约15%

allies.zh: 蔡国、周王室
allies.en: Cai, Zhou royal house

enemies.zh: 楚国（后期威胁）
enemies.en: Chu (later threat)

foreignPolicy.zh: 依附中原大国，维持小国生存
foreignPolicy.en: Aligned with Central Plains powers to ensure survival as a small state

era.zh: 青铜时代晚期

keyInnovations.zh: 青铜铸造、农业灌溉
keyInnovations.en: Bronze casting, agricultural irrigation

strengths.zh: 舜帝后裔身份尊贵，文化传统深厚
strengths.en: Noble lineage from Emperor Shun, deep cultural traditions

weaknesses.zh: 国小力弱，易受大国干涉
weaknesses.en: Small and weak, vulnerable to interference from larger states

outlook.zh: 陈国在春秋时期将作为中等小国存续，但最终难逃被大国吞并的命运。
outlook.en: Chen would survive as a minor state through much of the Spring and Autumn period, but ultimately could not escape absorption by a greater power.

description.zh: 舜帝后裔建立的中原小国，文化传统独特。
description.en: A small Central Plains state founded by descendants of Emperor Shun, with distinctive cultural traditions.

governmentForm.zh: 姬姓诸侯国，周武王弟蔡叔度之后，奉周礼世袭。
governmentForm.en: A Ji-surnamed vassal state descended from Cai Shuduo, brother of King Wu of Zhou, hereditary under Zhou rites.

socialStructure.zh: 公室贵族、卿大夫、士、平民农户构成
socialStructure.en: Ducal nobility, aristocratic ministers, shi, and commoner farmers

rulingClass.zh: 姬姓蔡公室
rulingClass.en: The Ji house of Cai

succession.zh: 嫡长世袭
succession.en: Hereditary primogeniture

structure.zh: 小型诸侯国政体，国君亲政
structure.en: Small vassal state governance with direct ducal rule

function.zh: 户籍农业
function.en: Population and agriculture

function.zh: 军务
function.en: Military affairs

localAdmin.zh: 以都城为中心的小规模行政
localAdmin.en: Small-scale administration centered on the capital

legalSystem.zh: 周礼惯习法
legalSystem.en: Zhou ritual-based customary law

taxationSystem.zh: 田赋与劳役
taxationSystem.en: Land tax and corvée

religion.zh: 周式祭祀，祖先崇拜
religion.en: Zhou ritual worship, ancestor veneration

writingSystem.zh: 西周汉字
writingSystem.en: Western Zhou Chinese script

culturalAchievements.zh: 蔡国虽小，但地处南北交汇，文化兼容并蓄。
culturalAchievements.en: Though small, Cai was at the north-south interface and culturally eclectic.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系—古汉语
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Old Chinese

gdpDescription.zh: 以农业为主的小型经济体。
gdpDescription.en: A small, primarily agrarian economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟黍种植、畜牧
mainIndustries.en: Millet farming, animal husbandry

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、布帛
tradeGoods.en: Grain, textiles

allies.zh: 陈国、周王室
allies.en: Chen, Zhou royal house

enemies.zh: 楚国（日后主要威胁）
enemies.en: Chu (future major threat)

foreignPolicy.zh: 依附中原强国自保
foreignPolicy.en: Sought protection from Central Plains powers

era.zh: 青铜时代晚期

keyInnovations.zh: 青铜冶铸、农耕技术
keyInnovations.en: Bronze metallurgy, farming techniques

strengths.zh: 姬姓宗室地位，南北交通要道
strengths.en: Royal Ji lineage, positioned on north-south routes

weaknesses.zh: 国小民寡，夹于大国之间
weaknesses.en: Small population, squeezed between major powers

outlook.zh: 蔡国将在春秋时期长期受到楚国北进压力，生存空间日益狭窄。
outlook.en: Cai would face increasing pressure from Chu's northward expansion throughout the Spring and Autumn period.

description.zh: 姬姓小国，地处中原与楚境交界，命运多舛。
description.en: A small Ji-surnamed state at the boundary between the Central Plains and Chu territory.

governmentForm.zh: 姬姓诸侯国，周武王弟曹叔振铎之后，世袭。
governmentForm.en: A Ji-surnamed vassal state descended from Cao Shu Zhenduo, brother of King Wu of Zhou.

socialStructure.zh: 公室、卿大夫、士、庶民
socialStructure.en: Ducal house, ministers, shi, commoners

rulingClass.zh: 姬姓曹公室
rulingClass.en: The Ji house of Cao

succession.zh: 嫡长世袭
succession.en: Hereditary primogeniture

structure.zh: 小型诸侯国，国君亲政
structure.en: Small vassal state with direct ducal rule

function.zh: 户籍农业
function.en: Population and agriculture

function.zh: 军务
function.en: Military affairs

localAdmin.zh: 都城为核心
localAdmin.en: Capital-centered

legalSystem.zh: 周礼惯习法
legalSystem.en: Zhou ritual-based customary law

taxationSystem.zh: 田赋与劳役
taxationSystem.en: Land tax and corvée

religion.zh: 周式祭祀，祖先崇拜
religion.en: Zhou ritual worship, ancestor veneration

writingSystem.zh: 西周汉字
writingSystem.en: Western Zhou Chinese script

culturalAchievements.zh: 曹国都城陶丘（定陶）日后成为中原重要商业都会。
culturalAchievements.en: Cao's capital Taoqiu (later Dingtao) would become an important commercial center of the Central Plains.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系—古汉语
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Old Chinese

gdpDescription.zh: 小型农业经济，但陶丘地理位置优越，有商业潜力。
gdpDescription.en: A small agrarian economy, but Taoqiu's location offered commercial potential.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟黍种植、制陶、手工业
mainIndustries.en: Millet farming, pottery, handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、陶器
tradeGoods.en: Grain, pottery

allies.zh: 宋国、周王室
allies.en: Song, Zhou royal house

enemies.zh: 无主要敌国
enemies.en: No major enemies

foreignPolicy.zh: 依附邻近大国，维持存续
foreignPolicy.en: Allied with neighboring powers for survival

era.zh: 青铜时代晚期

keyInnovations.zh: 青铜铸造、制陶
keyInnovations.en: Bronze casting, pottery

strengths.zh: 都城陶丘地理位置优越，有商业潜力
strengths.en: Capital Taoqiu had a favorable location with commercial potential

weaknesses.zh: 国小力弱，无战略纵深
weaknesses.en: Tiny and weak, no strategic depth

outlook.zh: 曹国将在春秋时期长期作为附庸小国存在，最终被宋国所灭。
outlook.en: Cao would persist as a minor dependent state through the Spring and Autumn, ultimately destroyed by Song.

description.zh: 姬姓小国，都于陶丘，位处中原腹地。
description.en: A minor Ji-surnamed state based at Taoqiu in the heart of the Central Plains.

governmentForm.zh: 传为夏禹后裔所建诸侯国，带有浓厚部族色彩，非典型中原政体。
governmentForm.en: Traditionally attributed to descendants of Yu the Great, with strong tribal characteristics and a non-typical Central Plains political structure.

socialStructure.zh: 部族首领与贵族构成上层，百越族群分散聚居，与中原社会结构有明显差异。
socialStructure.en: Tribal chiefs and nobles formed the upper class; Baiyue ethnic groups lived in dispersed communities, distinct from Central Plains society.

rulingClass.zh: 姒姓王室与部族首领
rulingClass.en: The Si royal house and tribal chiefs

succession.zh: 世袭为主，部族推举亦有影响
succession.en: Primarily hereditary, with tribal selection also having influence

structure.zh: 部族联盟性质的政权，中央控制力有限
structure.en: A polity with confederate tribal characteristics, with limited central control

function.zh: 统领部族武装
function.en: Led tribal military forces

function.zh: 主持宗教祭祀
function.en: Conducted religious ceremonies

localAdmin.zh: 部族聚落自治为主
localAdmin.en: Primarily autonomous tribal settlements

legalSystem.zh: 部族习惯法与氏族规约
legalSystem.en: Tribal customary law and clan regulations

taxationSystem.zh: 实物贡纳与劳役
taxationSystem.en: In-kind tribute and labor service

religion.zh: 越人崇拜蛇、鸟图腾，重巫祝祭祀，兼有水神与山神信仰。
religion.en: Yue people worshipped snake and bird totems, emphasized shamanic rituals, and venerated water and mountain spirits.

writingSystem.zh: 越人尚无系统文字，以刻符与口传为主。
writingSystem.en: Yue lacked a systematic writing system, relying on carved symbols and oral tradition.

culturalAchievements.zh: 越人善于舟楫水战，铸剑工艺精湛（越王剑闻名后世），断发文身为其特色习俗。
culturalAchievements.en: Yue excelled in watercraft and naval combat, with superb sword-forging craftsmanship (Yue royal swords became legendary); tattooing and short hair were distinctive customs.

languageFamily.zh: 侗台语系/壮侗语系（古越语）
languageFamily.en: Kra-Dai (ancient Yue language)

gdpDescription.zh: 越国经济以稻作、渔业、铜锡矿冶和水上交通为特色，与中原经济模式有别。
gdpDescription.en: Yue's economy was characterized by rice cultivation, fishing, copper and tin mining, and waterborne transport, differing from Central Plains patterns.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作、渔业、铸剑冶铜、造船、采矿
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, fishing, sword-forging, copper smelting, shipbuilding, mining

tradeGoods.zh: 铜器、宝剑、稻米、鱼盐、木材
tradeGoods.en: Bronzes, swords, rice, fish and salt, timber

expenditureBreakdown.zh: 军事约45%、首领供养约25%、造船与冶铸约20%、其他约10%
expenditureBreakdown.en: Military ~45%, chieftain maintenance ~25%, shipbuilding and metallurgy ~20%, other ~10%

treasury.zh: 以铜锡矿藏和粮仓为主要储备
treasury.en: Copper-tin ore reserves and granaries as main reserves

commandStructure.zh: 越王/越侯统帅，部族首领各率本部
commandStructure.en: King/Lord of Yue as supreme commander, tribal chiefs leading their own contingents

allies.zh: 楚国（时有往来）
allies.en: Chu (occasional contact)

enemies.zh: 吴国
enemies.en: Wu

foreignPolicy.zh: 偏安东南，与吴国竞争，较少参与中原事务
foreignPolicy.en: Focused on the southeast, competing with Wu, rarely involved in Central Plains affairs

era.zh: 青铜时代晚期

keyInnovations.zh: 铸剑术、造船技术、稻作农业、水战战术
keyInnovations.en: Sword-forging, shipbuilding, rice farming, naval warfare tactics

strengths.zh: 水战能力强，铸剑工艺精湛，山水地形提供天然屏障
strengths.en: Strong naval capability, superb sword craftsmanship, natural mountain-water barriers

weaknesses.zh: 与中原交流有限，文化技术整体落后于华夏诸国，部族凝聚力不足
weaknesses.en: Limited Central Plains contact, cultural-technological lag compared to Hua-Xia states, weak tribal cohesion

outlook.zh: 越国在公元前800年仍处于发展早期，但将在春秋末期崛起为击败吴国的强权。
outlook.en: Yue was still in early development around 800 BCE but would rise to become the power that defeated Wu in the late Spring and Autumn period.

description.zh: 东南百越之国，铸剑名邦，将在春秋末期崛起。
description.en: A Baiyue kingdom of the southeast, famed for swordcraft, destined to rise in the late Spring and Autumn.

governmentForm.zh: 由区域性村落联盟与酋邦组成的分散部落治理体系，政治权威建立在亲族、仪式威望与资源分配能力之上。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal system composed of regional village alliances and chiefdoms, with authority based on kinship, ritual prestige, and the ability to distribute resources.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以定居或半定居村落为基础，围绕亲族群体组织，存在酋长、祭祀人物、熟练工匠、普通家庭成员与边缘附属者之间的地位差异，但总体阶层分化有限。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on sedentary or semi-sedentary villages organized around kin groups, with status distinctions among chiefs, ritual specialists, skilled artisans, ordinary household members, and marginal dependents, though overall stratification remained limited.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、长老与具有宗教威望的祭祀精英
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly families, elders, and ritual elites with religious prestige

succession.zh: 首领地位多在家族或显赫亲族中继承，但仍需获得村社认可，部分地区可能兼有长老推举。
succession.en: Chiefly positions were often inherited within prominent families or kin groups, but still required community acceptance, with some regions likely also using elder selection.

structure.zh: 无统一国家机构；各地区由村落议事、酋长协调与祭祀中心共同维持秩序。规模较大的聚落可对周边小聚落形成松散支配。
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; order was maintained through village councils, chiefly coordination, and ritual centers. Larger settlements could exercise loose influence over surrounding smaller communities.

function.zh: 协调狩猎采集区域、婚姻关系、冲突调停与集体工程安排。
function.en: Coordinated hunting and gathering zones, marriage relations, conflict mediation, and collective labor projects.

function.zh: 主持祖灵、自然灵与季节性仪式，维持共同体认同与首领合法性。
function.en: Conducted ancestor, nature-spirit, and seasonal rituals, sustaining communal identity and chiefly legitimacy.

function.zh: 管理栗、坚果、干鱼、贝类与其他储藏物资，在歉收或仪式活动时进行再分配。
function.en: Managed stored chestnuts, nuts, dried fish, shellfish, and other supplies, redistributing them during shortages or ceremonies.

function.zh: 维持聚落间贸易、婚姻联络、信息传递与结盟安排。
function.en: Maintained inter-settlement trade, marriage ties, information exchange, and alliance arrangements.

function.zh: 组织房屋修缮、坑储设施、道路小径、木构与贝冢相关工程。
function.en: Organized house repairs, storage pits, pathways, wooden structures, and shell-midden related works.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落为核心，较大聚落可能吸纳周边小村形成区域网络，但不存在标准化行政区划。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was village-centered; larger settlements might integrate surrounding hamlets into regional networks, but there were no standardized administrative districts.

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法，主要依据习惯法、亲族调解、赔偿、共同体舆论与仪式性和解来处理争端。
legalSystem.en: There was no written law; disputes were handled chiefly through customary norms, kin mediation, compensation, communal opinion, and ritual reconciliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制；首领通过礼物交换、贡献食物、劳动义务与仪式馈赠获得资源支持。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; chiefs received support through gift exchange, food contributions, labor obligations, and ceremonial offerings.

religion.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜、生育与丰饶仪式为核心，重视山川、森林、海洋与动物之灵。
religion.en: Religion centered on animistic beliefs, ancestor veneration, and fertility and abundance rites, with strong reverence for the spirits of mountains, forests, seas, and animals.

philosophy.zh: 无系统化成文哲学，世界观强调人与自然循环、共同体互惠、祖先延续与仪式平衡。
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophy; the worldview emphasized cycles of nature, communal reciprocity, ancestral continuity, and ritual balance.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，知识通过口述传统、仪式、器物图像与技艺传承保存。
writingSystem.en: There was no writing system; knowledge was preserved through oral tradition, ritual, imagery on objects, and craft transmission.

culturalAchievements.zh: 延续并发展精美绳纹陶器、土偶、漆器、编织物、骨角器与定居村落景观；部分地区形成复杂的食物储藏与仪式空间布局。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included the continued development of elaborate cord-marked pottery, dogu figurines, lacquerware, woven goods, bone and antler tools, and settled village landscapes; some regions developed complex food-storage systems and ritual spatial layouts.

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不明，可能由多个彼此相关或不相关的前日本列岛语言群组成。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is unknown, likely consisting of multiple related or unrelated pre-Japanese archipelago language groups.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以采集、渔猎、坚果加工、有限园圃种植与地方交换为基础，财富主要体现为食物安全、工具储备、装饰品与社会关系，而非货币积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on gathering, fishing, hunting, nut processing, limited horticulture, and local exchange; wealth was expressed mainly in food security, tool stocks, ornaments, and social relationships rather than monetary accumulation.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、狩猎、采集、栗与坚果加工、陶器制作、骨角器制造、木工、漆器与编织
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, hunting, gathering, chestnut and nut processing, pottery production, bone and antler toolmaking, woodworking, lacquerware, and weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、海贝、鱼干、海藻、盐、漆器、优质陶器、玉石饰物、沥青、骨角器与兽皮
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, shells, dried fish, seaweed, salt, lacquerware, fine pottery, stone ornaments, bitumen, bone and antler tools, and animal hides

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常体现在住屋规模、储藏坑数量、渔猎工具、装饰品、漆器与可动员的亲族协作网络。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was typically reflected in house size, number of storage pits, fishing and hunting equipment, ornaments, lacquerware, and the kin networks a household could mobilize.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、共同体共享资源、季节性劳动协作与礼物交换为核心的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on household production, communal resource sharing, seasonal labor cooperation, and gift exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金属货币，而是分散在各大聚落中的储藏食物、优质器物、海产干货、装饰品与首领可再分配的礼品库存。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not centralized metal money but dispersed stocks of stored food, prestige goods, dried marine products, ornaments, and gifts that chiefs could redistribute.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政原则重在维持储备、平衡季节风险、通过馈赠巩固联盟，并以再分配提升首领威望。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized maintaining reserves, balancing seasonal risks, strengthening alliances through gifting, and enhancing chiefly prestige through redistribution.

description.zh: 由成年男性为主组成，平时从事渔猎与生产，战时使用弓箭、石镞、木矛与棍棒进行防卫或突袭。
description.en: Composed mainly of adult men engaged in fishing, hunting, and production in peacetime, they fought with bows, stone arrowheads, wooden spears, and clubs in defense or raids.

description.zh: 依附于地方首领的常备护卫与精锐狩猎者，负责警戒、护送与在冲突中担任核心战力。
description.en: Retainers attached to local chiefs served as guards and elite hunters, providing security, escort, and core fighting strength in conflict.

description.zh: 在较大规模争端时由多个聚落临时联合征召，用于防御边界、报复性袭击或资源争夺。
description.en: Temporarily raised from multiple settlements during larger disputes, these forces defended frontiers, conducted retaliatory attacks, or contested resources.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各地酋长分别统率
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; each local chief commanded separately

technology.zh: 武装技术以木、骨、石器为主，广泛使用弓箭与矛；尚未形成普遍的青铜或铁制武器体系，防御依靠地形、壕沟与聚落布局。
technology.en: Military technology relied primarily on wood, bone, and stone tools, with broad use of bows and spears; there was not yet a widespread bronze or iron weapon system, and defense depended on terrain, ditches, and settlement layouts.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自聚落间资源竞争、局部冲突、沿海与内陆通道控制争端，以及气候波动引发的食物紧张。
threats.en: The main threats came from inter-settlement competition over resources, localized conflict, disputes over coastal and inland routes, and food stress caused by climatic fluctuations.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于日本列岛多个区域，以本州东北部、关东、东海及部分北方与南方沿海地带的村落网络为主，密度整体不高但地区差异显著。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across multiple regions of the Japanese archipelago, especially in village networks of northeastern Honshu, Kanto, Tokai, and some northern and southern coastal zones; overall density was low but regionally varied.

name.zh: 三内丸山类型大型聚落传统地区

name.en: Large circular settlement clusters of Kanto

name.zh: 北陆与东北沿海中心聚落
name.en: Coastal central settlements of Hokuriku and Tohoku

socialClasses.zh: 社会可分为地方首领家族、祭祀者与长老、熟练工匠和专业渔猎者、普通家户成员，以及依附性较强的边缘群体；但尚未形成严格国家阶级制度。
socialClasses.en: Society can be divided into local chiefly families, ritualists and elders, skilled artisans and specialized fishers-hunters, ordinary household members, and more dependent marginal groups; however, a rigid state class system had not yet formed.

allies.zh: 无固定统一盟友；各地区共同体主要通过婚姻、礼物交换与海陆贸易网络维持友好关系，可能与朝鲜半岛南部及北海道、琉球方向的邻近群体存在间接往来。
allies.en: There were no fixed unified allies; regional communities maintained friendly relations mainly through marriage, gift exchange, and maritime and overland trade networks, with possible indirect contacts toward southern Korea, Hokkaido, and the Ryukyu direction.

enemies.zh: 无持续性的统一外敌，敌对关系多为地方层面的资源争夺者和竞争性邻近聚落。
enemies.en: There was no enduring unified external enemy; hostile relations were mostly local, involving resource rivals and competing neighboring settlements.

foreignPolicy.zh: 整体对外策略以维持区域平衡、避免长期毁灭性冲突、通过交换获取稀缺物资并巩固婚姻联盟为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Overall external policy focused on maintaining regional balance, avoiding long destructive conflicts, obtaining scarce goods through exchange, and reinforcing marriage alliances.

era.zh: 晚期绳文时代，处于东亚铁器时代背景下但自身仍以前金属传统为主
era.en: Late Jomon period, within the broader East Asian Iron Age context but itself still predominantly pre-metal in tradition

keyInnovations.zh: 复杂陶器烧制、漆器工艺、磨制石器与骨角器精制、食物储藏设施、栗树管理与定居村落生态利用技术。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included advanced pottery firing, lacquer technology, refined polished stone and bone-antler tools, food storage facilities, chestnut management, and ecological techniques for sedentary village life.

strengths.zh: 拥有成熟的渔猎采集复合经济、较强环境适应力、稳定村落生活、发达手工艺传统与广泛区域交换网络。
strengths.en: Its strengths were a mature mixed fishing-hunting-gathering economy, strong environmental adaptability, stable village life, developed craft traditions, and broad regional exchange networks.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、军事实力有限、无文字与标准化官僚体系，对气候变化与局部资源衰退较为敏感。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included the absence of unified political authority, limited military power, no writing or standardized bureaucracy, and vulnerability to climate change and local resource decline.

outlook.zh: 在公元前800年前后总体保持稳定，但随着来自朝鲜半岛和东亚大陆的农业、金属器与新型社会组织影响增强，未来数世纪内列岛社会将逐步发生深刻转型。
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE it remained broadly stable, but as influences from the Korean Peninsula and the East Asian mainland—especially agriculture, metal tools, and new social organization—intensified, the archipelago would gradually undergo profound transformation over the following centuries.

description.zh: 日本列島仍以晚繩文聚落與區域首領社會為主，尚未形成統一國家。
description.en: Japan remained dominated by Late Jomon village societies and regional chiefdoms, without a unified state.

governmentForm.zh: 以部族联盟为核心的早期酋邦政体，由强势中心聚落统摄周边部落与氏族。
governmentForm.en: An early chiefdom-based tribal confederation centered on a dominant settlement that exercised authority over surrounding tribes and clans.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由酋长家族、部族首领、战士、祭司或萨满、自由平民农户、工匠、渔猎者以及依附人口构成，血缘、氏族与居地关系共同决定地位。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the chieftain's lineage, tribal leaders, warriors, priests or shamans, free commoner farmers, artisans, fishers and hunters, and dependent populations, with rank shaped by kinship, clan ties, and settlement position.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是中心聚落的酋长家族、与其联姻的地方首领，以及掌握青铜礼器、武器和祭祀权威的贵族集团。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was chiefly made up of the chieftain's lineage at the central settlement, allied local headmen tied by marriage, and aristocratic groups controlling bronze prestige goods, weapons, and ritual authority.

succession.zh: 继承大体为家族世袭，但需获得主要氏族与部族首领承认，因此兼具血统继承与部族共议性质。
succession.en: Succession was broadly hereditary within the ruling lineage, but required recognition from major clans and tribal chiefs, combining dynastic inheritance with confederate consent.

structure.zh: 中央权力有限但逐渐形成层级：酋长居于中心聚落，通过亲族首领、地方寨主和祭祀权威管理农业区、河谷聚落与边缘山地社群。
structure.en: Central authority was limited but increasingly hierarchical: the chieftain at the core settlement governed farming zones, riverine communities, and peripheral upland groups through kinsmen, local headmen, and ritual authorities.

function.zh: 讨论战争、盟约、迁徙、贡赋和重大裁决，由酋长家族长者及主要部落首领组成。
function.en: Discussed war, alliances, migration, tribute, and major judgments, composed of elders of the ruling lineage and leading tribal chiefs.

function.zh: 主持祭天、祖灵祭祀、占卜与季节仪式，并协调农时与集体动员。
function.en: Oversaw heaven worship, ancestor and spirit rites, divination, seasonal ceremonies, and coordination of agricultural timing and communal mobilization.

function.zh: 征收粮食、布帛、兽皮、盐、鱼干及地方特产，并管理中心聚落仓储与再分配。
function.en: Collected grain, cloth, hides, salt, dried fish, and local specialties, and managed storage and redistribution at the central settlement.

function.zh: 统计可征召战士、管理武器储备、组织边境警戒与部族联军集结。
function.en: Counted eligible warriors, managed weapon stores, and organized border watch and confederate war-band mobilization.

function.zh: 维系与周边部落、河谷聚落和辽东据点的关系，处理贡纳、婚盟与争端调停。
function.en: Maintained ties with neighboring tribes, river settlements, and Liaodong outposts, handling tribute, marriage alliances, and dispute mediation.

localAdmin.zh: 地方以氏族与部族为基本单位，由地方首领或寨主自治，向中心酋长承担贡赋、兵役和朝会义务；边远地区控制较松，更多依靠盟约和交换维系统合。
