localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on clans and tribes led by local chiefs or settlement headmen, who owed tribute, military service, and attendance at assemblies to the central chieftain; distant areas were loosely controlled and integrated more by alliance and exchange than direct rule.

legalSystem.zh: 尚无成文法典，主要依习惯法、氏族规范、酋长裁断和祭祀誓约处理案件；常见问题包括土地与水源争夺、婚姻纠纷、盗窃、伤害和血仇赔偿。
legalSystem.en: There was no formal written law code; disputes were handled through customary law, clan norms, the chieftain's judgment, and ritual oaths. Common cases involved land and water disputes, marriage conflicts, theft, injury, and compensation for blood-feud.

taxationSystem.zh: 赋役以实物贡纳和劳役为主，包括谷物、粟、黍、麻布、皮毛、鱼盐、青铜原料和季节性徭役；战争时期另有临时征发。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based chiefly on in-kind tribute and labor service, including grain, millet, cloth, furs, fish products, salt, bronze inputs, and seasonal corvée; emergency levies were imposed in wartime.

religion.zh: 宗教以天神、自然神、山川灵、祖先崇拜与萨满祭祀为核心，祭司或萨满在政治合法性与部族团结中具有重要作用。
religion.en: Religion centered on heaven deities, nature spirits, mountain and river powers, ancestor worship, and shamanic ritual, with priests or shamans playing a major role in political legitimacy and tribal cohesion.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成系统哲学传统，观念上强调祖先延续、部族忠诚、天命征兆、人与自然灵界的互动，以及对农时与祭祀秩序的重视。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical tradition yet; prevailing ideas emphasized ancestral continuity, tribal loyalty, omens of heaven, interaction between humans and the spirit world, and the importance of seasonal and ritual order.

writingSystem.zh: 本地尚无成熟自有文字，行政与记忆主要依口传、刻划符号和器物标记；与中原接触地带可能已知晓早期汉字文化，但未普遍使用。
writingSystem.en: There was no mature indigenous writing system; administration and memory relied mainly on oral tradition, incised marks, and object symbols. In contact zones with the Chinese world, awareness of early Chinese writing may have existed, but it was not in common use.

culturalAchievements.zh: 代表性文化成就包括辽东—朝鲜北部青铜器传统、细形铜剑相关武器文化的前期形态、石支墓与巨石墓葬传统、精细磨制石器与骨角器、纺织与麻布生产，以及结合渔猎与农耕的复合生活方式。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable cultural achievements included the bronze traditions of Liaodong and northern Korea, early forms of the weapon culture later associated with slender bronze daggers, dolmen and megalithic funerary traditions, fine polished stone and bone tools, textile and hemp production, and a mixed economy combining farming with fishing and hunting.

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不明，通常推测为朝鲜半岛北部与辽东地区的前朝鲜语或相关古代语言群。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is uncertain, often hypothesized as involving proto-Koreanic or related ancient languages of northern Korea and Liaodong.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、渔猎、畜养和区域性手工业为主，市场交换有限但已出现中心聚落主导的贡纳与再分配网络。由于缺乏货币经济，产值估算以粮食当量衡量。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, fishing, hunting, animal husbandry, and regional craft production, with limited market exchange but a growing tribute-and-redistribution network led by central settlements. Because monetary circulation was undeveloped, output is best estimated in grain-equivalent terms.

mainIndustries.zh: 主要产业包括粟、黍、稻等农业生产，猪犬等家畜饲养，渔业与采集，青铜冶铸，石器与骨器制造，制陶，纺织，木工和盐产。
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included cultivation of millet, broomcorn millet, and some rice; rearing of pigs and dogs; fishing and gathering; bronze casting; stone and bone tool making; pottery; weaving; woodworking; and salt production.

tradeGoods.zh: 贸易物资包括青铜器、玉石与装饰品、兽皮、毛皮、海产干货、盐、麻布、陶器，以及与辽东和中原边缘交换的金属原料与奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included bronze objects, jade and ornaments, hides and furs, dried marine products, salt, hemp cloth, pottery, and metal inputs and prestige goods exchanged with Liaodong and the Chinese frontier.

unitName.zh: 以粮食、布帛和青铜重量折算
unitName.en: Reckoned in grain, cloth, and bronze weight

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、谷仓储量、牲畜、农具、陶器与纺织品；上层家庭则拥有青铜武器、礼器、马具、附庸人口与更多储粮。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth for commoners was expressed mainly in access to land, grain stores, livestock, tools, pottery, and textiles; elite households possessed bronze weapons, ritual objects, horse gear, dependent labor, and larger reserves.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制属于部族再分配经济与家庭自给生产并存的形态，中心聚落通过贡赋和宴飨维持权威，跨区域交换主要服务于精英消费、金属获取和政治联盟。
economicSystem.en: The economic system combined household subsistence production with a tribal redistributive order in which central settlements sustained authority through tribute and feasting; long-distance exchange mainly served elite consumption, metal acquisition, and political alliances.

source.en: Fish, Salt, Forest Products, and Local Tribute

source.zh: 战利品与附属部落输纳
source.en: War Booty and Subject Tribal Payments

category.en: Chieftain's Household and Central Court Maintenance
Storage, Famine Relief, and Public Labor

category.zh: 手工业与精英器物生产
category.en: Craft Production and Elite Goods Manufacture

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非单一金属储备，而是由中心谷仓、青铜库存、布帛、皮毛、盐、武器和祭器组成的综合储藏体系，兼具政治馈赠与赈济功能。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a single metallic reserve but a composite store of central granaries, bronze stock, cloth, hides, salt, weapons, and ritual objects, serving both political gift distribution and relief functions.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在稳固部族联盟与应对歉收：平时适度积储，丰年加强宴飨与馈赠以巩固忠诚，歉年则以仓储救荒并减轻部分贡赋。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to preserve tribal cohesion and withstand poor harvests: in normal years moderate surpluses were accumulated, in good years feasting and gift-giving reinforced loyalty, and in bad years granary reserves were used for relief while some tribute burdens were eased.

description.zh: 由酋长直属亲兵、贵族战士和常备守卫组成，负责都邑防御、仪仗和战时先发突击。
description.en: Composed of the chieftain's personal retainers, noble warriors, and permanent guards, responsible for defending the capital area, ceremonial display, and leading assaults in war.

description.zh: 主体兵力来自各部落征召的步战民兵，装备长矛、短剑、弓箭、木盾和石骨或青铜兵器。
description.en: The main force consisted of militia infantry levied from constituent tribes, armed with spears, short swords, bows, wooden shields, and stone, bone, or bronze weapons.

description.zh: 马匹数量有限，主要用于侦察、传令与贵族机动，不形成大规模骑兵；少量车乘更多体现身份和运输功能。
description.en: Horses were limited and used chiefly for scouting, dispatch carrying, and elite mobility rather than mass cavalry; a small number of vehicles served status and transport roles more than battlefield shock.

name.en: Frontier Watch and Upland War Bands

description.zh: 驻守边缘山地和通道，熟悉地形，擅长伏击、哨戒和对小规模袭扰的反击。
description.en: Stationed in frontier uplands and passes, these forces knew the terrain well and specialized in ambush, watch duty, and response to small raids.

commanderInChief.zh: 酋长本人
commanderInChief.en: The Chieftain Himself

technology.zh: 军事技术处于青铜向铁器过渡阶段，武器以青铜短剑、矛、弓箭和木盾为主，铁器刚开始零星出现；防御依托木栅、土垒、山城雏形与河谷据点。
technology.en: Military technology stood at the transition from bronze to iron, with bronze daggers, spears, bows, and wooden shields as the main weapons, while iron appeared only sporadically; defenses relied on palisades, earthen works, proto-hillforts, and riverine strongpoints.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方和西方草原及林地游动群体的袭扰、辽东周边势力竞争、部族联盟内部离心，以及与燕等华北边缘政体未来可能加深的接触和冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from raids by northern and western steppe or forest mobile groups, competition from neighboring powers in Liaodong, centrifugal tendencies within the confederation, and the prospect of deeper contact and conflict with northern Chinese frontier states such as Yan.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于辽东与朝鲜半岛北部河谷、沿海平原和若干内陆盆地，聚落密度不均，核心区出现较大的中心聚落，外围仍以分散村社为主。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in river valleys, coastal plains, and some inland basins of Liaodong and northern Korea, with uneven settlement density: larger central settlements existed in the core zone, while peripheral areas remained dominated by dispersed villages.

name.zh: 鸭绿江上游聚落群核心

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致可分为酋长与贵族家族、地方首领、祭司或萨满、武士与随从、自由农户、工匠与渔猎者、依附民和战俘。
socialClasses.en: Social classes can be broadly divided into the chieftain and noble lineages, local chiefs, priests or shamans, warriors and retainers, free farmers, artisans and fishers or hunters, dependent people, and war captives.

allies.zh: 无稳定成文盟友体系，主要依靠与邻近部落、辽东地方集团及若干沿海社群的婚盟、互市与临时军事合作。
allies.en: There was no stable formal alliance system; external relations depended mainly on marriage ties, exchange, and occasional military cooperation with neighboring tribes, Liaodong local groups, and some coastal communities.

enemies.zh: 主要对手并非单一国家，而是边境竞争部落、草原游动群体以及潜在与辽西、燕地扩张势力接触中的敌对集团。
enemies.en: Its enemies were not a single state but competing frontier tribes, mobile steppe groups, and potentially hostile forces emerging through contact with expanding polities in western Liaoxi and the Yan region.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以守护贸易通道、吸纳周边部落、维持辽东与朝鲜北部资源网络为主，倾向通过贡纳、婚盟、馈赠与有限武力巩固影响，而非进行持续远征。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on securing exchange routes, drawing neighboring tribes into its orbit, and preserving the resource networks of Liaodong and northern Korea, relying more on tribute, marriage ties, gift diplomacy, and limited force than on sustained long-distance conquest.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期（青铜向铁器过渡期）

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括发达的青铜铸造传统、巨石墓与石构葬制、磨制石器与骨角器精制、麻布纺织、储粮设施改进，以及铁器知识的初步传入与试用。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included a developed bronze-casting tradition, megalithic and stone-built burial practices, refined polished stone and bone tools, hemp weaving, improved storage facilities, and the initial introduction and experimental use of iron.

strengths.zh: 该政体的优势在于拥有较大人口基础、辽东至朝鲜北部的广阔资源腹地、青铜礼兵器所支撑的精英统合能力，以及通过祭祀、婚盟和再分配维系的部族联盟网络。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in a relatively large population base, a broad resource hinterland spanning Liaodong to northern Korea, elite integrative power supported by bronze prestige weapons and ritual goods, and a tribal alliance network sustained through ritual, marriage, and redistribution.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于中央集权程度有限、文字与常设官僚薄弱、对外围地区控制松散、军事动员依赖季节与部族忠诚，且在铁器竞争和更成熟国家体制面前存在制度性劣势。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited centralization, weak literacy and permanent bureaucracy, loose control over peripheral regions, military mobilization dependent on season and tribal loyalty, and structural disadvantages when facing iron-based competition and more mature state systems.

outlook.zh: 就公元前800年前后而言，朝鲜处于稳定而上升的复杂酋邦阶段；若能继续整合辽东和半岛北部部落、吸收铁器技术并加强中心聚落权威，未来可能向更成熟的早期国家发展，但也容易因外来压力和内部离心而反复分化。
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, Joseon stood as a stable and rising complex chiefdom; if it continued integrating tribes of Liaodong and northern Korea, adopted iron technology, and strengthened authority at the central settlement, it could develop toward a more mature early state, though it remained vulnerable to both external pressure and internal fragmentation.

description.zh: 朝鮮半島北部與遼東一帶的早期複合政體，處於青銅與鐵器過渡期。
description.en: An early complex polity in northern Korea and Liaodong during the bronze-to-iron transition.

capital.zh: 阿桑迪瓦特与哈斯提那补罗等传统中心
capital.en: Traditional centers at Asandivat and Hastinapura

governmentForm.zh: 后吠陀世袭君主制，结合宗族贵族与祭司支持
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Later Vedic monarchy supported by clan aristocracy and priestly authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会以瓦尔那秩序为骨架，王族与刹帝利武士贵族居上，婆罗门祭司掌握礼仪与知识权威，吠舍农牧民与商旅构成生产主体，首陀罗及附属劳动者承担服务与劳役；同时仍保留氏族、家支与乡村共同体结构。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the varna order, with the royal house and Kshatriya warrior aristocracy at the top, Brahmin priests holding ritual and intellectual authority, Vaishya cultivators, pastoralists, and traders forming the productive base, and Shudras and dependent laborers providing service and labor; older clan, lineage, and village community structures also remained important.

rulingClass.zh: 俱卢王族、刹帝利氏族首领与受王室供养的婆罗门祭司集团
rulingClass.en: The Kuru royal family, Kshatriya clan chiefs, and Brahmin priestly groups maintained by the court

succession.zh: 原则上为父系世袭，通常由王子继承，但须得到宗族长老、贵族战士与祭司承认
succession.en: Succession was in principle patrilineal and hereditary, usually passing to a son of the king, but required recognition by lineage elders, warrior nobles, and priests

structure.zh: 政体并非完备官僚国家，而是以宫廷、氏族首领、祭司团与地方村社组成的分层统治体系。国王通过王庭、祭祀网络、贡赋征收与军事召集维持统治，地方上则依靠村长、氏族领袖和附庸贵族行使权力。
structure.en: The polity was not a fully bureaucratic state but a layered system built around the royal court, clan chiefs, priestly circles, and local village communities. The king ruled through the court, ritual networks, tribute collection, and military levies, while local power was exercised by headmen, lineage leaders, and subordinate nobles.

function.zh: 管理王宫、储藏、侍从、使者接待与王室土地
function.en: Managed the palace, stores, attendants, reception of envoys, and royal lands

function.zh: 组织王家祭祀、火祭、加冕礼、盟誓与历法性仪式，维护王权合法性
function.en: Organized royal sacrifices, fire rituals, consecrations, oaths, and calendrical ceremonies, preserving the legitimacy of kingship

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、手工品与劳役，管理储备粮与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, craft goods, and labor dues, and managed reserve stores and redistribution

function.zh: 登记战士、马匹、战车与部族兵役义务，协调出征和防务
function.en: Recorded warriors, horses, chariots, and tribal military obligations, coordinating campaigns and defense

function.zh: 与村长、氏族长和附庸领主联络，处理土地、灌溉与纠纷调停
function.en: Liaised with village headmen, clan leaders, and subordinate lords, handling land, irrigation, and dispute mediation

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠村社、氏族和区域贵族。村长负责组织耕作、征发与治安，较大的聚落可能由王室代理人或贵族驻守。国家对基层的控制较为间接，重在贡赋与兵役而非日常细密管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied primarily on villages, clans, and regional nobles. Village headmen organized cultivation, levies, and order, while larger settlements could host royal agents or noble retainers. State control at the grassroots was relatively indirect, focusing on tribute and military obligations rather than daily close administration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、宗族规范和吠陀礼制为核心，由国王、长老和婆罗门共同裁断。对盗窃、牲畜争夺、婚姻、债务和边界纠纷的处理多依靠赔偿、誓言、见证与宗教净化，而非成文法典。
legalSystem.en: Law was centered on custom, lineage norms, and Vedic ritual order, adjudicated by the king, elders, and Brahmins. Theft, cattle disputes, marriage, debt, and boundary conflicts were usually handled through compensation, oaths, witness testimony, and ritual purification rather than a written legal code.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物和劳役为主，包括谷物分成、牲畜贡纳、祭祀供品、手工制品和季节性徭役。王室亦可从战利品、附庸献纳和过境交换中获益。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mainly in kind and labor, including shares of grain, livestock tribute, ritual offerings, manufactured goods, and seasonal corvée. The court also benefited from war booty, subordinate gifts, and transit exchange.

religion.zh: 后吠陀宗教，以祭火、颂唱和大型王家祭祀为核心，崇奉因陀罗、阿耆尼、苏摩、伐楼那等神祇；同时王权与祭仪深度结合，婆罗门在礼制上地位日益上升。
religion.en: Later Vedic religion centered on fire sacrifice, recitation, and large royal rituals, venerating deities such as Indra, Agni, Soma, and Varuna; kingship was closely tied to ritual, and Brahmins were rising in status through their mastery of sacred practice.

philosophy.zh: 思想仍以吠陀祭祀宇宙观和礼的正确实践为主，但已出现对祭祀意义、言语权力、宇宙秩序与自我本质的更深思考，为奥义书式反思奠定基础。
philosophy.en: Thought remained focused on the Vedic sacrificial worldview and correct ritual performance, but deeper reflection on the meaning of sacrifice, the power of speech, cosmic order, and the nature of self was emerging, laying foundations for later Upanishadic speculation.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无广泛用于国家治理的本土文字系统，知识主要依靠口头传承、韵律背诵与师徒教育保存
writingSystem.en: There was no widely used indigenous writing system for state administration; knowledge was preserved mainly through oral transmission, metrical recitation, and teacher-disciple instruction

culturalAchievements.zh: 俱卢地区是后吠陀礼制规范化的核心地带之一，对王家祭祀、婆罗门传统、梵文前期口传文学和社会等级观念的定型影响深远。与邻近的般遮罗共同构成北印度早期正统吠陀文化中心。
culturalAchievements.en: The Kuru region was one of the key centers in the formalization of Later Vedic ritual culture, exerting lasting influence on royal sacrifice, Brahmanical tradition, early Sanskrit oral literature, and social hierarchy. Together with neighboring Panchala, it formed a major center of early orthodox Vedic culture in northern India.

languageFamily.zh: 印度-伊朗语支下的古印度-雅利安语，礼仪语言为早期梵语/吠陀梵语传统
languageFamily.en: Old Indo-Aryan within the Indo-Iranian branch, with early Sanskrit/Vedic Sanskrit serving as the ritual language tradition

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业和牧业为基础，辅以金属加工、陶器、纺织和区域交换。作为恒河—亚穆纳上游平原的后吠陀王国，俱卢拥有较成熟的定居农业，但其政治衰落使资源动员能力弱于鼎盛时期。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture and pastoralism, supplemented by metalworking, pottery, textiles, and regional exchange. As a Later Vedic kingdom in the upper Ganges-Yamuna plain, Kuru had relatively developed settled agriculture, but its political decline reduced its capacity to mobilize resources compared with its peak.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、乳制品、铁器与铜器加工、陶器制作、木工、车舆制造、纺织
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, herding, dairy production, iron and copper working, pottery, carpentry, vehicle making, and textiles

tradeGoods.zh: 大麦、小麦、稻米、牛马、酥油、皮革、羊毛、棉织品、金属工具、陶器、盐及礼仪用品
tradeGoods.en: Barley, wheat, rice, cattle, horses, ghee, leather, wool, cotton textiles, metal tools, pottery, salt, and ritual goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为耕地使用权、牛群、谷仓储量、青铜或铁制工具、车具和家族劳动力。贵族与祭司拥有更多牲畜、贡纳和礼物，而普通村民财富较低且受收成波动影响明显。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed in access to arable land, cattle holdings, grain stores, bronze or iron tools, carts, and family labor. Nobles and priests possessed larger herds, tribute, and gifts, while ordinary villagers had modest wealth and were highly vulnerable to fluctuations in harvests.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业、牧群财富、贡赋再分配和有限市场交换并存的混合经济；王室与祭司通过礼物、贡纳和宗教仪式在财富流动中占据关键地位。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining village agriculture, herd wealth, tribute redistribution, and limited market exchange; the court and priesthood occupied key positions in the circulation of wealth through gifts, tribute, and religious ritual.

source.zh: 手工业、通行与交换收益

category.zh: 储备、修缮与公共劳役供给
category.en: Reserves, repairs, and public labor provisioning

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非完全以贵金属储藏，而多由粮仓、牲畜、祭祀用品、武备和少量金属财富构成。由于王权衰落和地方贵族自主性增强，中央财政储备有限且易受歉收与战争影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not composed entirely of precious metal but consisted largely of granary reserves, livestock, ritual goods, military equipment, and limited metal wealth. Because royal authority was declining and local nobles were becoming more autonomous, central reserves were modest and vulnerable to poor harvests and conflict.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持王室威望、祭祀正统和军事号召力，而非系统性积累现金性盈余；在衰退阶段，王室更依赖传统贡赋、礼物和再分配维系秩序。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized sustaining royal prestige, ritual orthodoxy, and military mobilization rather than systematically accumulating liquid surpluses; in this period of decline, the court depended increasingly on traditional tribute, gifts, and redistribution to preserve order.

description.zh: 由王室亲兵、贵族战士和战车乘员组成，是象征地位与机动作战的核心力量
description.en: Comprised of royal retainers, noble warriors, and chariot crews, forming the core force for prestige and mobile warfare

description.zh: 主要来自村社与附庸氏族，装备长矛、弓箭、盾牌和简单铁制或木制武器
description.en: Drawn mainly from villages and subordinate clans, armed with spears, bows, shields, and simple iron or wooden weapons

description.zh: 规模较小，用于传令、侦察和追击，尚未形成独立重骑兵体系
description.en: Small in number, used for dispatches, reconnaissance, and pursuit, not yet organized as an independent heavy cavalry arm

description.zh: 由地方首领和氏族贵族率领，平时分散，战时会合，忠诚更偏向各自家族
description.en: Led by local chiefs and clan nobles, dispersed in peacetime and assembled in war, with loyalty leaning more toward their own families

commanderInChief.zh: 罗阇亲自统帅，辅以贵族将领与祭司顾问
commanderInChief.en: The Raja commanded in person, assisted by noble leaders and priestly advisers

technology.zh: 处于印度铁器时代早期阶段，已使用铁制工具和部分兵器，但战车仍具重要地位；军队依赖弓矛、盾牌、皮甲或简易护具，组织方式仍深受氏族动员传统影响。
technology.en: Kuru stood in the early Indian Iron Age, using iron tools and some weapons, though chariots still retained major importance; the army relied on bows, spears, shields, leather armor or simple protection, and its organization remained strongly shaped by clan-based mobilization.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近诸国尤其般遮罗的竞争、地方贵族离心、粮食歉收引发的内部不稳，以及西北通道上更广泛人口流动带来的压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from rivalry with neighboring polities, especially Panchala, centrifugal tendencies among local nobles, internal instability caused by poor harvests, and broader population movements affecting the northwestern approaches.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于恒河—亚穆纳上游流域的村落、农田和牧场，定居农业聚落增多，但大部分居民仍生活在乡村环境中。作为后吠陀核心区之一，俱卢的人口密度在北印度属较高水平。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in villages, farmland, and pasturelands of the upper Ganges-Yamuna basin, with settled agricultural communities increasing, though most people still lived in rural settings. As one of the core Later Vedic regions, Kuru had a comparatively high population density for northern India.

name.zh: 因陀罗波罗伽一带聚落
雅穆纳河沿岸区域中心

socialClasses.zh: 王族与刹帝利、婆罗门、吠舍、首陀罗及依附劳动者构成基本层次；此外还有工匠、车匠、牧人、商旅和边缘部族群体。社会等级正在制度化，但旧有氏族认同仍然强烈。
socialClasses.en: The main social layers were the royal family and Kshatriyas, Brahmins, Vaishyas, Shudras, and dependent laborers; artisans, chariot makers, herders, traders, and marginal tribal groups were also present. Social hierarchy was becoming more institutionalized, but older clan identities remained strong.

allies.zh: 无稳固远距离盟友；与部分俱卢系宗族、地方附庸和偶尔合作的北印度邻邦维持松散关系
allies.en: No firm long-distance allies; Kuru maintained loose relations with related clans, local subordinates, and occasionally cooperative neighboring north Indian polities

enemies.zh: 主要竞争者为般遮罗，此外也需警惕周边新兴王国对俱卢旧有威望和土地的蚕食
enemies.en: Its principal rival was Panchala, and Kuru also had to watch emerging neighboring kingdoms encroaching on its former prestige and lands

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持区域地位、通过婚姻与祭祀联系稳固声望、避免被更强邻国边缘化为主。由于国势衰退，俱卢更倾向于防御性平衡，而非积极扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving regional standing, reinforcing prestige through marriage and ritual ties, and avoiding marginalization by stronger neighbors. Because the kingdom was declining, Kuru favored defensive balancing over active expansion.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具和兵器的逐步采用、较成熟的战车制造、定居农业扩展、祭祀程序的高度规范化、口传知识体系的精细化记诵技术
keyInnovations.en: The gradual adoption of iron agricultural tools and weapons, relatively mature chariot construction, expansion of settled agriculture, highly formalized ritual procedure, and refined oral techniques for preserving knowledge

strengths.zh: 俱卢拥有深厚的礼制传统、较高的文化威望、肥沃的农业基础以及与婆罗门正统紧密结合的政治合法性。在北印度政治文化史上，它仍是重要象征性中心。
strengths.en: Kuru possessed deep ritual traditions, high cultural prestige, a fertile agricultural base, and political legitimacy closely tied to Brahmanical orthodoxy. In the political and cultural history of northern India, it remained an important symbolic center.

weaknesses.zh: 王权资源动员能力下降，地方贵族和氏族势力较强，行政整合有限，缺乏成熟文字官僚体系，面对般遮罗等邻国竞争时显得被动。
weaknesses.en: Royal capacity to mobilize resources was declining, local nobles and clans were strong, administrative integration was limited, and there was no mature written bureaucracy; this left Kuru in a passive position against rivals such as Panchala.

outlook.zh: 短期内俱卢仍会凭借传统威望维持存在，但其政治主导地位难以恢复。更可能的趋势是被邻近强国压制或吸纳，而其真正持久的影响将体现在宗教、礼制与北印度正统文化传统中。
outlook.en: In the short term Kuru would likely survive on the strength of traditional prestige, but its political dominance was unlikely to recover. It was more likely to be overshadowed or absorbed by stronger neighbors, while its enduring importance would lie in religion, ritual, and the orthodox cultural traditions of northern India.

description.zh: 後吠陀時代的重要核心王國之一，但其霸權已逐步減弱。
description.en: One of the central Later Vedic kingdoms, though its earlier prominence was fading.

capital.zh: 阿希恰特拉与坎毗利耶两大传统中心
capital.en: Traditional twin centers at Ahichhatra and Kampilya

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制，受贵族宗族、祭司集团与部族会议传统制约
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy constrained by aristocratic lineages, priestly elites, and surviving tribal assembly traditions

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈后吠陀时期的分层结构，以王族与刹帝利武士贵族、婆罗门祭司、吠舍农牧与商人、首陀罗劳作者为主体，并包含依附部落与边缘森林人群
socialStructure.en: Society followed a stratified Later Vedic order centered on the royal house and Kshatriya warrior nobility, Brahmin ritual specialists, Vaishya farmers-herders and traders, and Shudra laboring groups, alongside dependent tribes and forest-margin communities

rulingClass.zh: 王室、刹帝利氏族贵族与受宠婆罗门祭司共同构成统治核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling core consisted of the royal family, Kshatriya clan nobility, and favored Brahmin priests

succession.zh: 以父系世袭为原则，通常由王子继位，但须得到主要宗族首领与祭司认可
succession.en: Succession was principally patrilineal, usually passing to a royal son, but required acceptance by leading clan chiefs and priests

structure.zh: 般阇罗并无后世意义上的成文官僚国家，而是以宫廷、宗族、祭司和地方首领共同运作的王权体系。国王依靠王室随从、征赋征发网络、婆罗门顾问以及地方聚落首领实施统治。
structure.en: Panchala was not a fully bureaucratic state in the later sense, but a royal system operated jointly through court retainers, clan networks, priestly authority, and local chiefs. The king ruled through household attendants, revenue and levy networks, Brahmin advisers, and settlement headmen.

function.zh: 管理王宫、侍从、储藏、使者接待、御前仪式与日常政令传达
function.en: Managed the palace, retainers, stores, reception of envoys, court ceremony, and transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 主持王家祭祀、加冕礼、火祭、大型王权仪式，并为政治决策提供宗教正当性
function.en: Oversaw royal sacrifices, consecrations, fire rituals, major kingship ceremonies, and supplied religious legitimacy for political decisions

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、手工制品与附庸贡纳，登记仓储并组织再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, craft goods, and dependent tribute, recorded storage, and organized redistribution

function.zh: 编组王家亲兵、召集氏族武装、维护马匹与战车、组织边境防御
function.en: Organized royal retainers, summoned clan levies, maintained horses and chariots, and arranged frontier defense

function.zh: 联络村社首领，协调屯聚、农田、道路、渡口与区域治安
function.en: Liaised with village headmen and coordinated settlements, fields, roads, ferries, and regional security

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠村社首领、氏族长者、附属小首领与王室任命的征赋代理人。北部与南部中心周边控制较强，边缘地区则更依赖盟约与贡纳关系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on village headmen, clan elders, subordinate chiefs, and royal revenue agents. Control was stronger around the northern and southern centers, while peripheral zones depended more on alliances and tribute ties.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命、宗族仲裁与婆罗门所阐释的达摩观念为基础。土地、牲畜、婚姻、债务与祭祀义务的争端多由地方长者和祭司裁断，重大案件可诉至王廷。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on custom, royal command, clan arbitration, and notions of dharma interpreted by Brahmins. Disputes over land, cattle, marriage, debt, and ritual obligation were usually judged by local elders and priests, with major cases appealable to the royal court.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自农业实物征收、牲畜贡赋、附庸献纳、战争掠获和仪式性馈赠。税制并不完全标准化，通常按收成、土地占有、氏族地位与政治服属关系征取。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue came chiefly from agricultural dues in kind, livestock tribute, subordinate offerings, war booty, and ceremonial gifts. Taxation was not fully standardized and was assessed according to harvests, landholding, clan status, and political subordination.

religion.zh: 后吠陀宗教，以祭火、苏摩仪式和对因陀罗、阿耆尼、伐楼那、密多罗等神祇的祭祀为核心，同时王权与大型祭仪紧密结合
religion.en: Later Vedic religion centered on fire sacrifice, soma rites, and worship of deities such as Indra, Agni, Varuna, and Mitra, with kingship closely tied to major sacrificial ceremonies

philosophy.zh: 思想处于从仪式中心主义向更深层宇宙与自我探问过渡的阶段。般阇罗与俱卢地区共同孕育了梵书与早期奥义思想的环境，重视祭仪秩序、言语权威与宇宙—社会对应关系。
philosophy.en: Thought was in a transitional stage from ritual centrality toward deeper inquiry into cosmos and self. Together with the Kuru sphere, Panchala helped foster the milieu of the Brahmanas and early Upanishadic speculation, emphasizing ritual order, verbal authority, and correspondences between cosmos and society.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无广泛行政文字使用，文化主要依靠精确口传保存，以吠陀梵语诵读传统为核心
writingSystem.en: There was no widely used administrative writing system; culture was preserved chiefly through exact oral transmission centered on Vedic Sanskrit recitation

culturalAchievements.zh: 般阇罗是后吠陀时代重要的祭祀、教育与王权文化中心，与俱卢并列为经典礼仪传统的核心区域之一。其成就主要体现在复杂祭礼体系、口头诗文保存、婆罗门学术传统与政治—宗教结合的王室仪式。
culturalAchievements.en: Panchala was a major center of ritual, learning, and kingship culture in the Later Vedic age, standing with Kuru as one of the core regions of classical ritual tradition. Its achievements lay in complex sacrificial systems, preservation of oral poetry, Brahmin scholarly traditions, and royal ceremonies linking politics and religion.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系—印度-伊朗语族—古印度雅利安语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Indo-Iranian, Old Indo-Aryan

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧和实物再分配为主，市场交换处于成长阶段。财富主要体现为谷物、牛群、马匹、金属器、土地控制权和仪式馈赠，而非普遍货币化产值。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based primarily on agriculture, pastoralism, and redistribution in kind, with market exchange still developing. Wealth was expressed mainly in grain, cattle, horses, metal tools, control of land, and ritual gifts rather than fully monetized output.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻、麦、大麦与杂粮种植，牛牧与马畜养殖，铁器与铜器制作，木工、车制作、陶器、纺织、皮革加工与祭祀用品生产
mainIndustries.en: Cultivation of rice, wheat, barley, and millets; cattle and horse husbandry; iron and copper working; carpentry, chariot making, pottery, textiles, leatherwork, and production of ritual goods

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牛、马、酥油、皮革、织物、木器、战车部件、铁制农具与武器、铜器、盐及礼仪用品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, cattle, horses, ghee, leather, textiles, wooden goods, chariot components, iron tools and weapons, copper items, salt, and ritual goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富以耕地使用权、牛只、粮仓、农具和亲属劳力为基础；精英家庭则拥有更多牲畜、马匹、战车、金饰、奴仆与仪式声望。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was based on access to fields, cattle, stored grain, tools, and kin labor; elite households possessed larger herds, horses, chariots, gold ornaments, servants, and ritual prestige.

unit.zh: 日常生计等值银克/人/年
unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室再分配、村社生产、宗族义务和有限区域交换并存的农业—牧业经济
economicSystem.en: An agrarian-pastoral economy combining royal redistribution, village production, clan obligations, and limited regional exchange

category.zh: 祭祀、赠礼与婆罗门供养

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要并非金银铸币，而是谷仓储备、牲畜、金属器、礼物存藏和少量贵金属积存的综合体。王室财富的很大部分以可再分配的实物形式存在。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not mainly coined bullion but a composite of granary reserves, livestock, metal goods, stored gifts, and small holdings of precious metals. Much royal wealth existed in redistributable goods in kind.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健保守，优先保障王室威望、祭祀活动、军力动员与灾年仓储；在丰收年加强实物积累，在冲突或祭仪大年增加征收和馈赠动员。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and stability-oriented, prioritizing royal prestige, ritual performance, military mobilization, and reserve storage for poor harvests; in good years the kingdom accumulated more in kind, while conflict or major ritual years brought heavier extraction and gift mobilization.

description.zh: 由王室随从、武士与受庇护战士组成，装备矛、弓、盾和早期铁制武器，是宫廷和核心地区的主要常备力量
description.en: Composed of royal retainers, warriors, and protected fighting men, armed with spears, bows, shields, and early iron weapons; this was the main standing force for the court and core territories

description.zh: 战时由各氏族、村社和附属首领提供，人数最多但训练和装备不均
description.en: Supplied in wartime by clans, villages, and subordinate chiefs; numerically the largest arm but uneven in training and equipment

description.zh: 由贵族武士和驾车者构成，是声望最高的作战兵种，适合平原机动作战、仪式展示与指挥核心战斗
description.en: Consisting of noble warriors and charioteers, this was the most prestigious arm, suited to plains warfare, ceremonial display, and command-centered combat

description.zh: 主要承担侦察、联络、追击与边境警戒，规模较小但日益重要
description.en: Handled scouting, communications, pursuit, and frontier watch; relatively small in size but increasingly important

description.zh: 负责渡口、道路、谷仓和边缘聚落防卫，平时半农半兵
description.en: Protected ferries, roads, granaries, and frontier settlements, serving part-time as farmer-soldiers

commanderInChief.zh: 国王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The Raja as supreme commander

technology.zh: 军备处于后吠陀铁器化早期阶段，使用弓箭、长矛、盾牌、战斧、皮甲或织物护具，以及由马拉动的轻型战车。铁制兵器和农具正在提升动员能力，但战车贵族仍具重要地位。
technology.en: Military technology stood in the early ironizing phase of the Later Vedic age, using bows, spears, shields, axes, leather or textile protection, and horse-drawn light chariots. Iron weapons and tools were improving mobilization, though chariot nobility still retained major importance.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自俱卢等恒河—亚穆纳地区竞争政体的战争、边缘部族反抗、附庸不稳以及争夺农田与交通线的地区冲突，而非遥远西亚或东亚诸国的直接军事压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from warfare with rival Ganga-Yamuna polities such as Kuru, unrest among peripheral tribes, instability among dependents, and regional conflicts over farmland and routes, rather than direct military pressure from distant West Asian or East Asian states.

populationDescription.zh: 人口在同时代北印度属于较大规模，主要分布于恒河—亚穆纳之间的农业聚落、河流沿岸与林地开垦带。人口增长受益于铁器推广、农业扩展与较成熟的王权组织。
populationDescription.en: Its population was large by contemporary North Indian standards, concentrated in agrarian settlements between the Ganga and Yamuna, along river corridors, and in newly cleared woodland zones. Growth was supported by the spread of iron tools, agricultural expansion, and more developed kingship structures.

name.zh: 帕里夫拉杰卡附近集聚区

socialClasses.zh: 王族与刹帝利贵族居于顶层；婆罗门凭借祭祀与知识享有极高声望；吠舍构成农业和交换的主体；首陀罗及其他依附劳作群体承担重体力生产。另有奴仆、战俘、林地部族与附庸群体处于边缘位置。
socialClasses.en: The royal family and Kshatriya nobility stood at the top; Brahmins enjoyed very high prestige through ritual and learning; Vaishyas formed the main productive agricultural and exchange stratum; Shudras and other dependent laboring groups bore much of the heavy work. Servants, captives, forest tribes, and subordinate peoples occupied more marginal positions.

allies.zh: 与部分恒河平原小首领、宗族性附庸和礼仪往来密切的北印度政体维持松散盟友关系；在某些时期可与俱卢共享文化与祭祀传统，但政治上并不稳定同盟。
allies.en: Panchala maintained loose allied relations with smaller Ganga plain chiefs, clan-based dependents, and North Indian polities linked by ritual exchange; at times it shared cultural and sacrificial traditions with Kuru, though this did not amount to a stable political alliance.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手是北印度邻近竞争王国，尤其是俱卢等争夺地区霸权、祭祀声望与农耕资源的政体；边缘林地部族和不稳附庸也可能成为冲突对象。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were neighboring North Indian rival kingdoms, especially polities such as Kuru competing for regional supremacy, ritual prestige, and agrarian resources; frontier forest tribes and unstable dependents could also become targets of conflict.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以区域均势、婚姻联结、祭祀声望竞争、附庸整合与交通节点控制为核心。般阇罗更关注北印度政治网络，而非与遥远文明进行直接国家层面的接触。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on regional balance, marriage ties, competition for ritual prestige, integration of dependents, and control of transport nodes. Panchala was focused far more on North Indian political networks than on direct state-level contact with distant civilizations.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期的后吠陀阶段
era.en: Early Iron Age, Later Vedic phase

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具与武器逐步普及、林地开垦扩大、战车与马匹组织化运用、精密口传教育体系、较成熟的祭祀仪轨编排，以及更稳定的河谷农业生产方式
keyInnovations.en: Gradual spread of iron tools and weapons, expanded forest clearance, organized use of chariots and horses, a highly precise oral educational system, more developed ritual procedure, and more stable river-valley agriculture

strengths.zh: 般阇罗的优势在于位居恒河—亚穆纳核心地带、农业潜力强、人口众多、祭祀文化权威高，并且在后吠陀政治与学术传统中占据中心位置。其双中心格局有助于维持南北区域控制和资源调配。
strengths.en: Panchala’s strengths lay in its position in the core Ganga-Yamuna zone, strong agrarian potential, large population, high ritual-cultural authority, and central place in Later Vedic political and intellectual tradition. Its dual-center arrangement helped sustain control over northern and southern sectors and coordinate resources.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于国家结构仍偏向宗族化和再分配型，行政整合有限，对地方首领和祭司权威依赖较大；缺乏成熟货币财政与成文官僚体系，王权在继承与外战中易受精英制衡。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a still clan-oriented and redistributive state structure, limited administrative integration, and heavy dependence on local chiefs and priestly authority; it lacked a mature monetary fiscal system and written bureaucracy, and royal power could be constrained by elites in succession struggles or war.

outlook.zh: 在公元前800年前后，般阇罗前景总体积极，仍是北印度最有影响力的王国之一。若能继续扩大农耕、稳固对附属地区的控制并维持与婆罗门传统的结合，其地位可长期延续；但随着更复杂的领土国家兴起，旧式战车贵族与祭祀政治模式将面临调整压力。
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, Panchala’s outlook was broadly favorable, and it remained one of the most influential kingdoms in North India. If it could continue agrarian expansion, secure its dependent territories, and preserve its alliance with Brahmin tradition, its importance could endure; however, the rise of more complex territorial states would place increasing pressure on the older chariot-aristocratic and ritual-political order.

description.zh: 恆河-亞穆納間的重要王國，為後吠陀政治與祭祀中心之一。
description.en: An important kingdom of the Ganga-Yamuna region and a major center of Later Vedic politics and ritual.

governmentForm.zh: 吠陀式世袭君主制，依托宗族贵族、祭司与地方首领治理
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Vedic monarchy governed through clan nobles, priests, and local chieftains

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、村社与正在成形的瓦尔那秩序为基础。王族与武士贵族居于上层，婆罗门祭司掌握宗教与礼仪权威，自由农民、牧民与工匠构成主体，另有依附劳动者、仆役及被征服部族成员处于较低地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on clans, village communities, and an increasingly defined varna order. The royal house and warrior nobility formed the upper stratum, Brahmin priests held religious and ritual authority, free cultivators, pastoralists, and artisans made up the bulk of the population, and dependent laborers, servants, and incorporated tribal groups occupied lower positions.

rulingClass.zh: 毗提诃王族、刹帝利贵族及受王室庇护的婆罗门祭司集团
rulingClass.en: The Videhan royal family, Kshatriya nobles, and Brahmin priestly groups patronized by the court

succession.zh: 以父系世袭为原则，通常由国王之子继承，但须获得王族长老、贵族与祭司的承认
succession.en: Succession was principally patrilineal, usually passing to a king's son, but requiring recognition by royal elders, nobles, and priests

structure.zh: 中央政权以国王宫廷为核心，围绕王室顾问、祭司、军事首领、税赋征收者与地方领主展开。其统治仍带有早期国家特征，行政制度未完全官僚化，更多依赖宗族关系、礼仪权威与贡赋网络。
structure.en: The central government centered on the royal court, with royal advisers, priests, military leaders, tax collectors, and local chiefs around it. Its rule still had the character of an early state: administration was not fully bureaucratized and depended heavily on kinship ties, ritual authority, and tributary networks.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、仓储、使者接待、王室土地与日常政务协调
function.en: Managed the court, granaries, reception of envoys, royal lands, and coordination of daily governance

function.zh: 主持王室祭祀、维护火祭传统、制定吉日并为重大决策提供宗教合法性
function.en: Oversaw royal rituals, maintained fire-sacrifice traditions, determined auspicious dates, and provided religious legitimacy for major decisions

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、手工业品与劳役，管理仓储和再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, craft goods, and labor obligations, and managed storage and redistribution

function.zh: 组织王家卫队、战车贵族、地方征召兵与边境防务
function.en: Organized the royal guard, chariot nobles, local levies, and frontier defense

function.zh: 联络村长、部族首领与封邑贵族，监督地方贡赋和治安
function.en: Liaised with village headmen, tribal chiefs, and landed nobles, supervising local tribute and order

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村社为基本单位，由村长、氏族长老及王室任命或认可的地方首领共同管理。边缘地区常保留被纳入部族的内部首领，只要按时纳贡并服从军事征召即可。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized around village communities, managed jointly by headmen, clan elders, and local chiefs appointed or recognized by the crown. Peripheral regions often retained their own incorporated tribal leaders so long as tribute was paid and military levies obeyed.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要建立在习惯法、氏族仲裁、王命与祭司认可的达摩观念之上。刑罚多为罚牲、罚谷、赔偿、放逐或仪式性惩戒，重罪则可能遭处死。
legalSystem.en: Law rested mainly on customary practice, clan arbitration, royal command, and priestly notions of dharma. Punishments often included fines in cattle or grain, compensation, exile, or ritual penalties, with death possible for grave offenses.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自农业贡赋、牲畜税、战利品分配、附属部族贡物和劳役。征收多以实物为主，谷物、牛只、酥油、纺织品和金属器具均可作为贡纳。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue came chiefly from agrarian tribute, livestock dues, shares of war booty, tribute from subordinate tribes, and labor obligations. Collection was predominantly in kind, with grain, cattle, ghee, textiles, and metal goods all acceptable as payments.

religion.zh: 以早期吠陀宗教为核心，崇奉因陀罗、阿耆尼、苏摩、伐楼那等神祇，重视火祭、牲祭、祭司颂唱与王权神圣化；同时也吸收恒河中游本土信仰与圣地传统。
religion.en: Religion was centered on early Vedic worship, venerating deities such as Indra, Agni, Soma, and Varuna, with strong emphasis on fire sacrifice, animal offerings, priestly recitation, and sacral kingship; it also absorbed local sacred traditions of the middle Ganges region.

philosophy.zh: 思想仍以吠陀祭仪、宇宙秩序与王权正当性为主，但随着东进开发和王廷文化发展，关于祭祀、社会秩序、苦行与知识权威的讨论开始孕育，成为后期奥义书思想兴起的土壤。
philosophy.en: Thought remained focused on Vedic ritual, cosmic order, and royal legitimacy, but as eastern expansion and court culture developed, discussions of sacrifice, social order, asceticism, and the authority of knowledge were beginning to emerge, laying groundwork for later Upanishadic thought.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无成熟、普遍使用的本土书写系统，文化传承主要依靠口诵传统，由祭司以严格音韵方式记忆和传授吠陀文本。
writingSystem.en: There was no mature, widely used indigenous writing system in regular administrative use; cultural transmission relied primarily on oral tradition, with priests memorizing and transmitting Vedic texts through strict phonetic recitation.

culturalAchievements.zh: 毗提诃在东部吠陀世界中是新兴文化中心，擅长祭祀礼仪、口传诗歌、王家赞歌、牛马养殖文化与村社农业组织。其成就体现在将西北吠陀传统成功移植并适应到恒河中游湿润环境之中。
culturalAchievements.en: Videha was an emerging cultural center in the eastern Vedic world, notable for sacrificial ritual, oral poetry, royal hymns, cattle-and-horse culture, and village agrarian organization. Its achievement lay in successfully transplanting and adapting northwestern Vedic traditions to the wetter middle Ganges environment.

languageFamily.zh: 古印度-雅利安语，属印欧语系印度-伊朗语族
languageFamily.en: Old Indo-Aryan, part of the Indo-Iranian branch of the Indo-European language family

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济处于兴盛中的早期农业国家阶段，以稻作、麦类、牧养和林地开垦为支柱。生产仍以自给村社为核心，但在王都和祭祀中心已出现较明显的再分配、专业工匠和区域交换。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was that of a thriving early agrarian state, supported by rice cultivation, cereals, pastoralism, and forest clearance. Production remained centered on self-sufficient villages, but the capital and ritual centers showed clear signs of redistribution, specialized artisans, and regional exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、林地垦殖、木工、陶器、纺织、铜铁器制造、战车与车轮制作
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, forest clearing, carpentry, pottery, textiles, copper and iron working, and chariot and wheel making

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、大麦、小麦、豆类、牛只、酥油、皮革、木材、蜂蜜、盐、铜器、铁器、珠饰、纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, barley, wheat, pulses, cattle, ghee, leather, timber, honey, salt, copper goods, iron tools, beads, and textiles

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以耕地使用权、牛群、谷仓储粮、木屋、农具和家庭成员劳力体现。贵族和祭司拥有更多牲畜、礼器、战车和附属劳动力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through use-rights to arable land, cattle herds, stored grain, wooden dwellings, tools, and family labor. Nobles and priests possessed larger herds, prestige goods, chariots, and dependent laborers.

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业和牧业为基础、由王室贡赋再分配加以统合的混合型实物经济；市场交换存在但规模有限，远未形成铸币货币经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed in-kind economy based on village agriculture and pastoralism, integrated through royal tribute and redistribution; market exchange existed but remained limited, far from a coin-based monetary economy.

source.en: Tribute from subordinate tribes and districts

category.zh: 地方首领赏赐与公共劳役
category.en: Rewards to local chiefs and public labor obligations

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以粮仓、牲畜群、金属器、礼器和储备物资的形式存在，而非统一铸币储备。其价值会随收成、祭祀开销和战争消耗而显著波动。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury existed chiefly as granary stocks, livestock herds, metal objects, ritual vessels, and reserve goods rather than a standardized coin reserve. Its value fluctuated greatly with harvests, ritual expenditure, and war losses.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健保守，注重通过实物征收维持宫廷、军队和祭祀体系，并在丰年积储粮食以应对歉收、迁徙与战事。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly conservative, emphasizing in-kind collection to sustain the court, army, and ritual system, while storing grain in good years against bad harvests, migration pressures, and warfare.

description.zh: 由国王直属的精锐武士组成，负责都城防卫、王室护卫和关键作战任务。
description.en: An elite force directly attached to the king, responsible for defending the capital, protecting the royal household, and carrying out key battlefield duties.

description.zh: 由贵族武士和其随从构成，是传统吠陀军事威望的核心，用于冲击、指挥和仪式展示。
description.en: Composed of noble warriors and their retainers, this was the prestige core of traditional Vedic warfare, used for shock action, command, and ceremonial display.

description.zh: 来自村社和附属部族的主体兵力，装备长矛、弓箭、木盾和少量铁制兵器，适合守卫道路、渡口和田野边界。
description.en: The main body drawn from villages and subordinate tribes, armed with spears, bows, wooden shields, and limited iron weapons, suitable for guarding roads, river crossings, and field boundaries.

name.zh: 边地侦骑与部族辅助兵

description.zh: 由边境牧民、猎手和被纳入部族提供，熟悉森林、河流与沼泽地形，主要承担侦察、袭扰和向导任务。
description.en: Provided by frontier pastoralists, hunters, and incorporated tribes, they were familiar with forests, rivers, and marshy terrain and mainly handled scouting, raiding, and guiding.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The King as Commander-in-Chief

technology.zh: 军事技术处于铁器时代早期水平，仍以木制战车、复合弓或长弓、长矛、铜器与逐渐增多的铁制刀矛为主。防具有限，多为皮革、厚布和木盾。
technology.en: Military technology was at an early Iron Age level, still centered on wooden chariots, composite or self bows, spears, bronze equipment, and increasing numbers of iron blades and spearheads. Armor was limited, consisting mostly of leather, heavy cloth, and wooden shields.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与邻近恒河平原政体的竞争、边缘森林地带未完全纳入的部族抵抗，以及对耕地、牧场和贸易通道的争夺。
threats.en: The main threats came from competition with neighboring Ganges polities, resistance from not yet fully integrated forest tribes on the margins, and struggles over farmland, pasture, and trade routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口在当时东部吠陀世界中属较大规模，分布于弥提罗及其周边村社、河谷聚落和新近开垦地区。人口增长得益于农业扩张、森林清理和西来雅利安人群与本地居民的融合。
populationDescription.en: The population was large by the standards of the eastern Vedic world, spread across Mithila, surrounding villages, riverine settlements, and newly cleared lands. Growth was driven by agrarian expansion, forest clearance, and the blending of incoming Indo-Aryan groups with local populations.

socialClasses.zh: 主要包括王族、刹帝利贵族、婆罗门祭司、自由农民与牧民、工匠商贩、附属部族成员、仆役与依附劳动者。社会分层正在强化，但仍保留较强的氏族与地方共同体色彩。
socialClasses.en: The main social categories included the royal family, Kshatriya nobles, Brahmin priests, free peasants and pastoralists, artisans and traders, incorporated tribal groups, servants, and dependent laborers. Stratification was becoming stronger, but society still retained a pronounced clan and local communal character.

allies.zh: 与部分东部吠陀氏族、姻亲贵族网络及若干已归附的地方首领保持合作关系；与俱卢、般遮罗等西方吠陀传统中心存在文化联系，但未必是正式盟友。
allies.en: It maintained cooperative ties with some eastern Vedic clans, marriage-linked noble networks, and several subordinate local chiefs; it also had cultural connections with western Vedic centers such as Kuru and Panchala, though these were not necessarily formal alliances.

enemies.zh: 无长期固定的世界性宿敌，但与邻近部族、地方首领及竞争中的恒河平原政体存在周期性冲突。
enemies.en: It had no permanent world-scale archenemy, but faced recurring conflicts with neighboring tribes, local chiefs, and rival polities in the Ganges plain.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以区域扩张、巩固边境、通婚结盟和吸纳地方首领为主，目标是在东部建立更稳固的吠陀王权秩序。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on regional expansion, frontier consolidation, marriage alliances, and incorporation of local chiefs, with the goal of establishing a firmer Vedic royal order in the east.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具与兵器的逐步采用、湿润地区农业扩张、木制战车改良、谷物仓储技术、祭司口传记忆体系和河流渡运组织
keyInnovations.en: The gradual adoption of iron tools and weapons, agricultural expansion into wetter lands, improved wooden chariots, grain storage techniques, priestly oral memorization systems, and organized river transport

strengths.zh: 毗提诃的优势在于人口增长、农业扩张潜力、王权与祭司合作带来的合法性，以及其作为东部吠陀前沿国家的文化活力。它能够吸纳新土地和新人口，并逐步形成较稳定的贡赋体系。
strengths.en: Videha's strengths lay in demographic growth, strong potential for agrarian expansion, legitimacy derived from cooperation between kingship and priesthood, and the cultural dynamism of a frontier eastern Vedic state. It was able to absorb new land and new populations while gradually building a stable tribute system.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于行政官僚化不足、对氏族和地方首领依赖较大、经济仍以实物自给为主、边缘地区控制力有限，且气候、洪水和收成波动会直接影响国力。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included limited bureaucratic development, heavy reliance on clans and local chiefs, an economy still dominated by in-kind subsistence, incomplete control over frontier zones, and direct vulnerability to climate, floods, and harvest fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 到公元前800年，毗提诃前景总体乐观。若能继续整合恒河中游土地、强化王室与祭司网络并维持对地方贵族的控制，它有望成长为北印度东部重要的王国与思想中心。
outlook.en: As of 800 BCE, Videha's outlook was broadly favorable. If it could continue integrating the middle Ganges lands, strengthen royal-priestly networks, and maintain control over local nobles, it had the potential to grow into a major kingdom and intellectual center in eastern northern India.

description.zh: 東吠陀世界的重要新興王國，代表雅利安文化向恆河中下游擴展。
description.en: A rising eastern Vedic kingdom reflecting Indo-Aryan expansion into the middle Ganges region.

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭君主为核心、联合地方贵族与部族首领的早期王国政体
governmentForm.en: An early hereditary monarchy centered on a king and supported by local nobles and tribal chiefs

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、武士贵族、祭司与学者、城镇工匠与商人、乡村农民牧民及依附劳作者构成，兼具吠陀社会等级与西北边地部族结构特征。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, warrior nobility, priests and learned specialists, urban artisans and merchants, rural cultivators and pastoralists, and dependent laborers, combining Vedic social hierarchy with northwestern tribal patterns.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、军事贵族、地方酋长及与宫廷结盟的祭司集团
rulingClass.en: The royal family, military nobility, local chieftains, and priestly groups allied to the court

succession.zh: 以父子世袭为主，若王位有争议，则由宗族长老、贵族与重要祭司共同认可继承者
succession.en: Primarily patrilineal hereditary succession, though disputed accessions were likely settled through recognition by clan elders, nobles, and important priests

structure.zh: 王权直接控制都城周边与主要道路节点，边远谷地和部族地区则通过封臣、城邑首领和贡赋关系进行间接统治。行政尚未形成高度文官化体系，但已有宫廷书记、税吏、仓储管理者与军事指挥层。
structure.en: Royal power directly controlled the area around the capital region and key route junctions, while distant valleys and tribal zones were ruled indirectly through vassals, town leaders, and tribute ties. Administration was not yet highly bureaucratized, but court scribes, tax collectors, granary managers, and military commanders already existed.

function.zh: 管理王宫、宗庙、宫廷供给、使者接待及王室财产
function.en: Managed the palace, royal cult, court provisioning, reception of envoys, and royal property

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、手工业制品与道路关税，并保管储备粮与贡品
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, craft goods, and road tolls, and maintained grain reserves and tribute stores

function.zh: 组织王家亲兵、征召部族武装、守卫关隘并协调边防
function.en: Organized the royal guard, mobilized tribal levies, guarded passes, and coordinated frontier defense

function.zh: 维护山道、渡口、驿站与商旅安全，征收通行税和市税
function.en: Maintained mountain roads, river crossings, staging posts, and trade security, while collecting transit tolls and market dues

function.zh: 主持王室祭祀、季节仪式、祖先与诸神崇拜，并为统治合法性提供支持
function.en: Oversaw royal rituals, seasonal ceremonies, ancestor and deity worship, and supported dynastic legitimacy

localAdmin.zh: 地方以城邑、村社和部族领地为基本单位，由王室任命或承认的首领、乡官与税吏管理；都城周边控制较强，外围地区多依赖盟约与纳贡。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through towns, villages, and tribal territories under chiefs, local officers, and tax collectors appointed or recognized by the crown; control was strongest near the capital and looser in outer areas, where alliance and tribute were more important.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依据习惯法、王命、宗族仲裁与祭司传统；涉及土地、水源、牲畜、婚姻、债务与伤害赔偿的纠纷，多由地方长老和王室代表共同裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law rested mainly on customary norms, royal commands, clan arbitration, and priestly tradition; disputes over land, water, livestock, marriage, debt, and injury compensation were usually judged by local elders together with royal representatives.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入以实物征收为主，包括谷物份额、牲畜、劳役、工艺品进贡以及道路与关隘税；商路贸易为王室提供额外收入。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily collected in kind, including grain shares, livestock, labor service, craft tribute, and tolls at roads and passes; trade along the routes brought additional income to the crown.

religion.zh: 以早期吠陀宗教为主体，祭火、祭天神与祖先崇拜并存，同时吸收西北地方山川神灵、蛇神与部族保护神信仰。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly early Vedic, featuring fire ritual, worship of heavenly deities, and ancestor cults, while also absorbing local northwestern mountain, river, serpent, and tribal protective deities.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成后世系统哲学学派，但祭司传统、颂诗记忆、礼仪思维与关于王权、秩序、祭献和宇宙循环的观念已相当重要。
philosophy.en: Later formal philosophical schools had not yet emerged, but priestly tradition, recited hymnic learning, ritual thought, and ideas about kingship, order, sacrifice, and cosmic cycles were already important.

writingSystem.zh: 书写使用有限，行政和宗教知识多依赖口传；若有记录，多借助外来书写实践或临时记号，尚无后世犍陀罗地区普遍定型的地方文字体系。
writingSystem.en: Writing was limited, and most administrative and sacred knowledge was transmitted orally; where records existed, they likely relied on imported writing practices or temporary marks, as the region had not yet developed the later standardized local scripts associated with Gandhara.

culturalAchievements.zh: 健驮逻在800 BCE的主要文化成就在于其作为交通与交流节点的地位：融合印度河上游、阿富汗山地与草原边缘文化；擅长口述诗歌、祭祀传统、马车与骑乘文化、金属工艺、珠饰制作以及城镇贸易生活。
culturalAchievements.en: Gandhara’s main cultural achievement in 800 BCE lay in its role as a crossroads: it blended upper Indus, Afghan highland, and steppe-edge influences; it was notable for oral poetry, ritual traditions, chariot and riding culture, metalworking, bead production, and urban trade life.

languageFamily.zh: 主要使用古印度-雅利安语支的西北方言，并夹杂地方底层语言与跨山地贸易中使用的多语交流
languageFamily.en: The main speech belonged to northwestern Old Indo-Aryan dialects, alongside local substrate languages and multilingual communication used in trans-montane trade

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农牧结合为基础，辅以山地贸易和交通税收。按铁器时代地区性王国标准，健驮逻经济相对活跃，尤其受益于连接喀布尔河谷、印度河上游和平原诸国的通道地位。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on mixed farming and pastoralism, supplemented by upland trade and transit taxation. By Iron Age regional kingdom standards, Gandhara had a relatively active economy, benefiting especially from its position connecting the Kabul valley, the upper Indus, and the plains polities.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、马匹与役畜饲养、青铜与早期铁器加工、木工、陶器、纺织、珠饰制作、长途贸易与关隘运输服务
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, horse and draft-animal breeding, bronze and early iron working, carpentry, pottery, textile production, bead-making, long-distance trade, and pass transport services

tradeGoods.zh: 大麦、小麦、牲畜、羊毛、皮革、木材、盐、半宝石珠饰、金属器、马匹，以及来自西方和山地的奢侈品中转
tradeGoods.en: Barley, wheat, livestock, wool, leather, timber, salt, semi-precious stone beads, metal goods, horses, and transshipped luxury items from western and highland regions

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、谷物储备、牛羊、马匹、青铜铁器工具与家族网络。城市商人和贵族家庭财富明显高于普通农户。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly held in land-use rights, grain stores, cattle, sheep, horses, bronze and iron tools, and kinship networks. Urban merchants and noble households were substantially wealthier than ordinary farming families.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业村社为基础、由王室和地方贵族抽取剩余，并由商路节点推动交换的再分配型混合经济
economicSystem.en: A redistributive mixed economy based on agrarian village communities, surplus extraction by the crown and local nobles, and exchange driven by trade-route nodes

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物、牲畜、金属块、奢侈品和可再分配贡品共同构成，并非全部为可流通贵金属。稳定时期可维持边防、赈济与外交馈赠。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of grain, livestock, metal by weight, prestige goods, and redistributable tribute rather than fully monetized precious metal alone. In stable years it was sufficient to sustain frontier defense, relief, and diplomatic gifting.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视储粮、维持山口守备和通过赏赐巩固地方忠诚；在歉收或边境紧张时，会增加劳役与临时贡赋。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, emphasizing grain storage, maintenance of mountain-pass defenses, and rewards to secure local loyalty; in poor harvests or frontier crises, labor dues and extraordinary tribute could be increased.

description.zh: 由都城周边与直属领地提供，是军队主干，装备长矛、盾牌、弓和少量铁制兵器。
description.en: Raised from the capital region and royal domains, these formed the core of the army, armed with spears, shields, bows, and some iron weapons.

description.zh: 由贵族与其随从组成，具有威望与机动性，多用于突击、追击和仪式性展示。
description.en: Composed of nobles and their retainers, this force offered prestige and mobility and was used for shock action, pursuit, and ceremonial display.

description.zh: 依托西北山地与通道环境发展而来，擅长侦察、传讯和袭扰，反映区域较早使用骑乘作战的特色。
description.en: Developed from the northwestern upland and corridor environment, they excelled in scouting, dispatch riding, and harassment, reflecting the region’s relatively early use of mounted warfare.

description.zh: 由附庸部族和外围谷地在战时提供，装备不一，但熟悉地形，适合守卫山道与伏击作战。
description.en: Provided in wartime by tributary tribes and outer valleys, these troops were unevenly equipped but knew the terrain well and were effective in pass defense and ambush fighting.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The King as Commander-in-Chief

technology.zh: 军备处于青铜向铁器过渡阶段，使用复合弓或强弓、长矛、短剑、战斧、皮甲和木盾；山地作战经验丰富，机动性强于许多平原王国。
technology.en: Military equipment was in transition from bronze to iron, using strong bows, spears, short swords, axes, leather armor, and wooden shields; experience in upland warfare gave the kingdom greater mobility than many plains polities.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西北山口竞争者、游动部族、边疆反叛以及控制商路节点的冲突；来自印度平原强邻的直接大规模入侵在此时并非常态，但区域争霸始终存在。
threats.en: Main threats came from rivals near the northwestern passes, mobile tribes, frontier revolts, and conflicts over trade-route nodes; large direct invasions from powerful plains neighbors were not the norm at this time, but regional competition remained constant.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约五十万，主要分布在普什卡拉瓦蒂、塔克西拉周边聚落、河谷农耕区和山麓牧养地。人口构成多元，既有定居农民，也有半游牧部族与商旅人群。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 500,000, concentrated around settlements near Pushkalavati and the Taxila region, river-valley farming zones, and piedmont pastoral lands. It was socially mixed, including settled cultivators, semi-pastoral tribes, and mobile trading communities.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族居于顶层；其下为祭司、学者和书记；城市中有商人、工匠、车马经营者；乡村主体为农民与牧民；另有依附劳工、仆役与战俘。部族身份与血缘关系在社会地位中仍十分重要。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal family and nobles; below them were priests, learned specialists, and scribes; towns contained merchants, artisans, and transporters; the rural majority consisted of cultivators and pastoralists; beneath them were dependent laborers, servants, and captives. Tribal identity and kinship remained highly important in determining status.

allies.zh: 与上印度河谷及旁遮普西北部若干小邦、部族首领和商路共同体保持松散联盟或互市关系
allies.en: Maintained loose alliances or reciprocal trade relations with smaller polities, tribal chiefs, and route communities in the upper Indus and northwestern Punjab

enemies.zh: 主要对手是争夺山口、河谷和商贸控制权的邻近部族与地方政权，而非单一长期宿敌
enemies.en: Its main opponents were neighboring tribes and local polities competing for passes, valleys, and commercial control rather than one single long-term archenemy

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以务实平衡为主：对内整合部族与城邑，对外维护交通安全、交换网络和边疆缓冲；通过联姻、馈赠、纳贡与武力威慑并用来维护影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic and balanced: internally it aimed to integrate tribes and towns, while externally it sought to secure routes, exchange networks, and frontier buffers; marriage alliances, gifts, tribute, and military deterrence were all used to maintain influence.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 早期铁器工具与武器的逐步采用、改良农具、山地道路与渡口组织、马匹与车辆运用、青铜铸造、珠饰与手工业生产技术
keyInnovations.en: Gradual adoption of early iron tools and weapons, improved agricultural implements, organized upland roads and river crossings, use of horses and vehicles, bronze casting, and craft technologies for beads and other manufactured goods

strengths.zh: 地处上印度河与阿富汗通道之间，战略位置极佳；农牧并行增强韧性；贸易和关税收入可观；人口结构多元，具备较强边地机动与军事适应力。
strengths.en: It occupied an excellent strategic position between the upper Indus and Afghan corridors; mixed agriculture and herding increased resilience; trade and toll revenues were significant; and its diverse population gave it strong frontier mobility and military adaptability.

weaknesses.zh: 王权对外围地区控制有限，依赖地方贵族和部族忠诚；文书与行政体系尚不成熟；经济仍受收成、交通安全和地区局势波动影响较大。
weaknesses.en: Royal control over outer regions was limited and depended on local nobles and tribal loyalty; written administration was still underdeveloped; and the economy remained highly vulnerable to harvests, route security, and regional instability.

outlook.zh: 在800 BCE的稳定局势下，健驮逻有望继续作为西北交通枢纽而繁荣，并逐步加强都城地区与商路控制；但若西方高地势力增强或内部贵族分裂，其统一性将面临考验。
outlook.en: Under the stable conditions of 800 BCE, Gandhara was well placed to prosper further as a northwestern transit hub and gradually strengthen control over its capital region and trade routes; however, stronger western highland powers or internal noble fragmentation could test its cohesion.

description.zh: 位於印度河上游與喀布爾通道之間的重要山口王國。
description.en: A key upland kingdom linking the upper Indus with routes to Afghanistan.

governmentForm.zh: 由多个区域性酋邦组成的分散部落政治体系，权力建立在亲族、战士追随者、土地控制与祭祀威望之上。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal-political system of multiple regional chiefdoms, with authority based on kinship, warrior followings, control of land, and ritual prestige.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、宗族与村落共同体为基础，上层为酋长家族与武士随从，中层包括牧民、农民、工匠与商贩，下层为依附者、劳作者及战俘。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on clans, lineages, and village communities, with chief families and warrior retainers at the top, herders, farmers, artisans, and traders in the middle, and dependents, laborers, and war captives below.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、战士贵族、祭祀首领与控制铁器和牲畜资源的部族上层。
rulingClass.en: Regional chief families, warrior nobles, ritual leaders, and tribal elites who controlled iron tools and livestock resources.

succession.zh: 多为世袭但不绝对固定，通常在酋长家族内传承，并需获得宗族长老与武士集团认可。
succession.en: Usually hereditary but not absolutely fixed, typically passing within the chief's family and requiring recognition from lineage elders and warrior groups.

structure.zh: 无统一国家官僚机构，各地酋邦由酋长、亲族长老、武士首领和祭祀人物共同治理；公共事务主要围绕土地分配、放牧路线、争端调解、防御和葬祭礼仪展开。
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; local chiefdoms were governed by chiefs, lineage elders, warrior leaders, and ritual specialists together. Public affairs centered on land allocation, grazing routes, dispute settlement, defense, and funerary rites.

function.zh: 协助酋长处理继承、盟约、资源分配与部落间关系。
function.en: Assisted the chief in succession, alliances, resource distribution, and inter-tribal relations.

function.zh: 调解婚姻、土地、牲畜与血仇纠纷，维护习惯法。
function.en: Mediated disputes over marriage, land, livestock, and blood-feuds, preserving customary law.

function.zh: 负责护卫酋长、征收贡赋、守卫要地与组织战斗。
function.en: Protected chiefs, collected tribute, guarded key sites, and organized fighting.

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、葬俗、立石纪念与神圣场所管理。
function.en: Conducted ancestor rites, funerary customs, memorial stone setting, and management of sacred sites.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村落、氏族聚落和小型酋邦为单位，没有标准化行政区划；酋长通过亲属网络、贡赋关系和个人威望进行统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized through villages, clan settlements, and small chiefdoms, without standardized districts; chiefs ruled through kinship networks, tribute ties, and personal prestige.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，重视赔偿、和解、牲畜补偿、婚姻约定与血亲责任；重大冲突可诉诸决斗、集体仲裁或复仇。
legalSystem.en: Law was mainly customary, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, livestock payments, marriage agreements, and kin responsibility; serious conflicts could lead to duels, collective arbitration, or vengeance.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一税制，主要以贡赋、劳役、牲畜、谷物、铁器与战利品分配为财政基础。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system; finances rested mainly on tribute, labor service, livestock, grain, iron goods, and distribution of war booty.

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、英雄纪念、自然神灵、山丘与巨石圣地崇拜为主，葬俗与祭祀密切相关，常通过石棺墓、石圈和墓葬随葬品体现信仰观念。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, hero commemoration, nature spirits, and reverence for hills and megalithic sacred places. Beliefs were closely tied to funerary customs, expressed through cist burials, stone circles, and grave goods.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系哲学传统，世界观主要体现为宗族伦理、勇武荣誉、祖先记忆、土地与牲畜价值，以及仪式维系共同体秩序的观念。
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophical tradition had yet emerged; the worldview was expressed through clan ethics, martial honor, ancestral memory, the value of land and cattle, and the belief that ritual sustained communal order.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本土成熟文字系统，信息传播依赖口述传统、记忆、符号性标记及物质文化表达。
writingSystem.en: There was no mature indigenous writing system; transmission of information relied on oral tradition, memory, symbolic marks, and material culture.

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出显著的巨石墓葬传统、熟练的铁器制造、黑红陶工艺、珠饰制作、区域性长距离交换网络，以及后来南印度和早期泰米尔政治文化的社会基础。
culturalAchievements.en: The culture developed a notable megalithic funerary tradition, skilled ironworking, black-and-red ware pottery, bead production, regional long-distance exchange networks, and the social foundations of later South Indian and early Tamil polities.

languageFamily.zh: 主要为早期达罗毗荼语系诸语言，可能并存部分其他地方语言。
languageFamily.en: Primarily early Dravidian languages, with some other local languages likely coexisting.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业和畜牧业为基础，辅以铁器制造、陶器、珠饰和区域贸易。由于缺乏统一市场与货币体系，总量估算只能以实物生产和劳役动员能力折算。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture and pastoralism, supplemented by ironworking, pottery, bead craft, and regional trade. Because there was no unified market or currency system, total output can only be estimated through material production and labor mobilization.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作与杂粮种植、牛牧与羊牧、铁器冶炼与锻造、陶器烧制、珠饰制作、林产采集、盐和石材交换。
mainIndustries.en: Rice and millet cultivation, cattle and sheep herding, iron smelting and forging, pottery production, bead-making, forest product gathering, and exchange in salt and stone.

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、黑红陶、半宝石珠饰、食盐、牲畜、谷物、木材、蜂蜜、象牙、皮革与森林产物。
tradeGoods.en: Iron tools, black-and-red ware, semi-precious stone beads, salt, livestock, grain, timber, honey, ivory, leather, and forest products.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在牲畜数量、储粮、铁器、陶器、珠饰和对土地及水源的使用权，而非统一货币存量。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in livestock holdings, stored grain, iron tools, pottery, beads, and access rights to land and water rather than in standardized money.

economicSystem.zh: 以自给型村落经济为主体，配合酋邦贡赋、礼物交换、婚盟馈赠和有限的长距离贸易，属于分散的部落—酋邦混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed tribal-chiefdom economy dominated by self-sufficient village production, combined with chiefdom tribute, gift exchange, marriage payments, and limited long-distance trade.

source.zh: 战利品与附属部落供奉

category.en: Paths, water sources, and settlement maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中于单一王室金库，而是分散储存在酋长粮仓、牲畜群、金属贮藏、珠饰财富和祭祀储备之中。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not concentrated in a single royal vault but dispersed among chiefly granaries, herds, metal stores, bead wealth, and ritual reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持酋长威望、保障武士追随者、组织祭祀和积累实物储备为主，政策偏向稳定贡赋与再分配，而非扩大市场税收。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining chiefly prestige, supporting warrior retainers, organizing rituals, and building material reserves, favoring stable tribute and redistribution rather than expanded market taxation.

description.zh: 由酋长直属随从组成，装备较好的铁制武器，用于护卫、突袭和维持权威。
description.en: Composed of the chief's direct retainers, equipped with better iron weapons and used for protection, raids, and enforcing authority.

description.zh: 战时由各村落和氏族征集，使用长矛、标枪、弓箭、刀剑和木盾，是主要作战力量。
description.en: Raised from villages and clans in wartime, they used spears, javelins, bows, swords, and wooden shields and formed the main fighting force.

description.zh: 依托熟悉地形的牧民和年轻战士，擅长侦察、伏击、追击和牲畜掠夺战。
description.en: Based on herders and young fighters familiar with the terrain, they specialized in scouting, ambushes, pursuit, and cattle-raiding warfare.

commanderInChief.zh: 各地最高酋长与部族联军首领
commanderInChief.en: Leading regional chiefs and confederate war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术处于早期铁器时代，广泛使用铁矛头、铁剑、斧、弓箭和木盾；战争方式以步兵冲突、伏击、掠袭和地方防御为主，尚未形成成熟战车或大规模骑兵体系。
technology.en: Military technology was at an early Iron Age stage, with widespread use of iron spearheads, iron swords, axes, bows, and wooden shields. Warfare relied on infantry clashes, ambushes, raiding, and local defense, without a mature chariot or large cavalry system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦之间的竞争、牲畜掠夺、资源争夺、干旱导致的迁徙冲突，以及海岸和内陆贸易路线控制权之争。
threats.en: Main threats came from rivalry among neighboring chiefdoms, cattle raiding, competition for resources, migration conflicts caused by drought, and struggles over control of coastal and inland trade routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布广泛，集中于德干高原南部、泰米尔地区、卡纳塔克和安得拉部分地区的村落、牧营和小型聚落，整体密度不高但聚落网络已相当稳固。
populationDescription.en: The population was widely distributed across southern Deccan, Tamil regions, Karnataka, and parts of Andhra in villages, pastoral camps, and small settlements. Overall density was modest, but settlement networks were already fairly stable.

name.zh: 阿迪恰纳卢尔地区聚落

socialClasses.zh: 上层为酋长家族、战士贵族和祭祀人物；中层为自由农民、牧民、工匠和交换者；下层为依附者、雇工、债务从属者和战俘。社会分层存在，但尚未形成后世成熟国家的严格等级官僚制度。
socialClasses.en: The upper layer consisted of chiefly families, warrior nobles, and ritual specialists; the middle included free farmers, herders, artisans, and exchangers; the lower included dependents, laborers, debt-bondsmen, and war captives. Social stratification existed, but not the rigid bureaucratic hierarchy of later states.

allies.zh: 无固定统一盟友；各酋邦常与邻近部落、沿海交换群体及部分内陆聚落结成临时婚盟、贸易盟约或军事同盟。
allies.en: There were no fixed unified allies; chiefdoms often formed temporary marriage alliances, trade compacts, or military coalitions with neighboring tribes, coastal exchange groups, and inland settlements.

enemies.zh: 无统一国家级敌人；主要对手是竞争性酋邦、掠夺性部落集团及争夺盐源、牧场和贸易节点的邻近势力。
enemies.en: There were no unified state-level enemies; the main opponents were rival chiefdoms, raiding tribal groups, and neighboring powers contesting salt sources, pasture, and trade nodes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以地方化、务实和网络化为特征，重视婚姻联结、贡礼交换、互不侵犯安排与贸易通路安全，而非领土帝国式扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign relations were localized, pragmatic, and network-based, emphasizing marriage ties, gift exchange, non-aggression arrangements, and security of trade routes rather than imperial territorial expansion.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器冶炼与锻造技术的扩展、黑红陶烧制、石棺墓和石圈墓建造、珠饰加工、农业工具改进以及区域交换网络的巩固。
keyInnovations.en: Expansion of iron smelting and forging, black-and-red ware firing, construction of cist and stone-circle burials, bead-working, improved agricultural tools, and consolidation of regional exchange networks.

strengths.zh: 铁器应用提升了农业和战争能力，氏族网络与酋邦联盟具有较强地方适应性，巨石文化和祖先祭祀增强了共同体凝聚力，区域贸易正在扩展。
strengths.en: The use of iron improved agriculture and warfare, clan networks and chiefdom alliances gave strong local adaptability, megalithic culture and ancestor rituals strengthened communal cohesion, and regional trade was expanding.

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一王权、文字行政和稳定税制，难以长期协调大规模工程或持续远征，且易受环境波动和内部争斗影响。
weaknesses.en: Politics were highly fragmented, lacking unified kingship, written administration, and a stable tax system, making it difficult to coordinate large-scale works or sustained campaigns and leaving the society vulnerable to environmental fluctuation and internal conflict.

outlook.zh: 在公元前800年前后总体稳定，并将继续作为南印度早期复杂社会发展的重要基础；随着铁器普及、贸易增长和酋长权力加强，未来可能逐步演化为更集中的早期历史时期区域政体。
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE the culture was broadly stable and would continue to serve as a major foundation for the development of complex societies in South India; with wider iron use, growing trade, and stronger chiefly authority, it could gradually evolve into more centralized regional polities of the early historic period.

description.zh: 南印度由多個農牧結合的巨石文化酋邦組成，為後來泰米爾諸國的前身。
description.en: South India consisted of multiple megalithic chiefdoms that formed the background to later Tamil polities.

name.zh: 東南亞青銅—鐵器酋邦網絡
name.en: Bronze-Iron Age Chiefdom Networks of Southeast Asia

governmentForm.zh: 由河谷、海岸与岛屿聚落组成的分散式酋邦网络，权力建立在亲族关系、仪式威望、贸易控制与对稻作劳力的组织之上。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of chiefdoms based in river valleys, coasts, and islands, with authority grounded in kinship ties, ritual prestige, control of exchange, and the organization of labor for rice agriculture.

socialStructure.zh: 社会通常分为首领家族、祭司或仪式专家、战士与随从、工匠与航海贸易者、普通农民渔民，以及少量依附人口或战俘。不同地区的等级化程度不一，但普遍存在以馈赠、婚姻联盟和祖先崇拜维系的上下关系。
socialStructure.en: Society was commonly divided among chiefly lineages, ritual specialists, warriors and retainers, artisans and maritime traders, ordinary farmers and fishers, and a small dependent or captive population. Degrees of hierarchy varied by region, but vertical ties were widely maintained through gift exchange, marriage alliances, and ancestor veneration.

rulingClass.zh: 地方首领家族、仪式精英与控制长途交换路线的港口或河口贵族。
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly lineages, ritual elites, and port or estuary aristocracies that controlled long-distance exchange routes.

succession.zh: 多为世袭，但常受长幼次序、母系或父系亲属支持、婚姻联盟与地方会议认可影响；若首领威望不足，也可能被支系取代。
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary, but often shaped by seniority, support from maternal or paternal kin, marriage alliances, and recognition by local councils; weaker chiefs could be displaced by rival branches.

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构。各酋邦以首领居地为核心，周边村落通过贡纳、劳役、祭祀参与和军事追随与中心结合；较强节点位于红河三角洲、湄公河下游与群岛海峡航道附近。
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus. Each chiefdom centered on a chief's seat, with surrounding villages tied to it through tribute, labor service, ritual participation, and military following; stronger nodes were located near the Red River delta, the lower Mekong, and island straits along sea routes.

function.zh: 管理仓储、贡物分配、宴飨馈赠与附属村社义务。
function.en: Managed storage, distribution of tribute goods, feast-gifts, and the obligations of dependent villages.

function.zh: 主持季节祭祀、葬仪、祖先崇拜与巫觋活动，以巩固首领合法性。
function.en: Oversaw seasonal rites, funerary ceremonies, ancestor veneration, and shamanic practices that reinforced chiefly legitimacy.

function.zh: 组织河运、海运、转口交换、外来商人接待与关键物资征收。
function.en: Organized riverine and maritime transport, entrepôt exchange, reception of foreign traders, and collection of key trade dues.

function.zh: 召集战士、守卫聚落、护送商旅并处理部族冲突与掠夺报复。
function.en: Mobilized warriors, guarded settlements, escorted trade movements, and handled inter-polity conflict and raiding reprisals.

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理由村社长老、宗族领袖与首领任命的代理人共同承担。河谷稻作区管理较集中，岛屿与山地则更依赖盟约和馈赠关系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled jointly by village elders, lineage heads, and agents appointed by chiefs. Management was more concentrated in rice-growing river valleys, while island and upland zones relied more on alliance and gift-based ties.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为主，强调赔偿、婚姻义务、土地与水域使用权、血亲调解和仪式宣誓。严重争端可由首领裁决，辅以神判、誓言或祖灵见证。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, emphasizing compensation, marriage obligations, rights to land and waters, kin mediation, and ritual oath-taking. Serious disputes could be judged by chiefs, sometimes with ordeals, sworn pledges, or ancestral witness.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有标准化货币税制，主要通过稻米、渔获、盐、林产、珠贝、金砂、布匹、劳役和贸易过境贡纳实现资源汲取。
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized monetary tax system; resources were extracted mainly through tribute in rice, fish, salt, forest products, shells, gold dust, textiles, labor service, and transit dues on trade.

religion.zh: 以万物有灵、祖先崇拜、自然神灵和地方圣地祭祀为核心，常见对山岳、河流、巨石、祖墓与海洋力量的敬畏；部分地区已有铜鼓、玉石与高等级墓葬相关的仪式传统。
religion.en: Religion centered on animism, ancestor veneration, nature spirits, and rituals at local sacred places, with reverence for mountains, rivers, megaliths, ancestral tombs, and maritime forces; some regions already showed ritual traditions associated with drums, prestige jade, and high-status burials.

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系书面哲学，观念主要体现为祖先伦理、互惠馈赠、航海与季风知识、土地水域禁忌以及通过仪式维持宇宙与社群平衡的世界观。
philosophy.en: There was not yet a formal written philosophy; ideas were expressed instead through ancestral ethics, reciprocal gift relations, knowledge of sailing and monsoons, taboos governing land and waters, and a worldview that upheld cosmic and communal balance through ritual.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本地通行文字体系，信息依靠口传、记忆专家、结绳式记忆法、器物纹样与仪式表演保存；与外部文明接触尚不足以形成普遍书写行政。
writingSystem.en: There was no broadly used local writing system; knowledge was preserved through oral transmission, memory specialists, cord-based mnemonic practices, object patterns, and ritual performance, while contact with literate civilizations had not yet produced regular written administration.

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出适应热带河口与群岛环境的稻作、渔猎与舟船技术；形成精致的编织、木作、骨角器、石器晚期传统与早期金属工艺；大规模宴飨、墓葬与海上交换推动了区域性的身份象征物传播。
culturalAchievements.en: The region developed rice farming, fishing, and watercraft technologies suited to tropical estuaries and island environments; it also produced refined weaving, woodworking, bone and antler tools, late stone traditions, and early metallurgy. Large feasts, burials, and maritime exchange spread prestige goods across the region.

languageFamily.zh: 语言极为多样，主要包括南亚语系、南岛语系以及部分侗台与汉藏边缘语言社群的早期形态。
languageFamily.en: Languages were highly diverse, including early forms of Austroasiatic, Austronesian, and some communities at the edges of Tai-Kadai and Sino-Tibetan linguistic spheres.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业为主，但较一般部落社会更深地嵌入区域交换网络。红河与湄公河流域提供稻米与人口基础，海岸与群岛节点则通过贝、盐、金属原料、木材、香料前体和海产建立跨区域流通。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was primarily agrarian and subsistence-based, but more deeply integrated into regional exchange than a typical tribal society. The Red River and Mekong zones supplied rice and population, while coastal and island nodes enabled interregional circulation of shells, salt, metal inputs, timber, proto-spice products, and marine goods.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、渔业、采集林产、盐业、舟船制造、纺织编织、陶器制作、石器与早期青铜及铁器加工、珠饰与贝器生产。
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice agriculture, fishing, forest gathering, salt making, boatbuilding, textile weaving, pottery production, stoneworking, early bronze and ironworking, and manufacture of beads and shell ornaments.

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、盐、鱼干、贝壳、龟甲、珠饰、玉石与半宝石、金砂、铜锡原料、铁器、木材、藤料、树脂、香木、羽毛、象牙、兽皮与织物。
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, dried fish, shells, turtle shell, beads, jade and semi-precious stones, gold dust, copper and tin inputs, iron tools, timber, rattan, resins, fragrant woods, feathers, ivory, hides, and textiles.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在稻田使用权、舟具、渔具、家畜、陶器、布匹与亲族支持网络；精英则积累珠贝、金属器、礼器和可用于宴飨与婚盟的库存。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of access to rice land, boats, fishing gear, livestock, pottery, textiles, and kin support networks; elites accumulated shells, metal goods, prestige items, and stores usable for feasts and marriage alliances.

economicSystem.zh: 混合型酋邦经济：以家庭生产和村社互助为基础，上层通过贡纳、转口贸易、仪式再分配和季节性劳役集中资源。
economicSystem.en: A mixed chiefly economy: rooted in household production and village reciprocity, with elites concentrating resources through tribute, entrepôt exchange, ritual redistribution, and seasonal labor obligations.

category.zh: 首领家政与宴飨再分配
地方调解与附属村社赏赐
category.en: Local Mediation and Rewards to Dependent Communities

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非统一中央金库，而是分散储存在主要酋长居地、谷仓、港口库房和精英墓葬体系中的粮食、盐、金属、珠贝与礼器库存。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a single central reserve but a dispersed stock of grain, salt, metals, shells, and prestige goods held in major chiefly residences, granaries, port storehouses, and elite ritual complexes.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政倾向稳健保守，以维持首领威望与联盟稳定为先。资源更多用于馈赠、婚盟、祭祀和护商，而非固定官僚薪给。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice was cautious and conservative, prioritizing chiefly prestige and alliance stability. Resources were directed more toward gift distribution, marriage alliances, ritual, and trade protection than toward salaried bureaucracy.

description.zh: 由首领直属随从、勇士和护卫组成，负责核心聚落守卫、对外展示威望和关键冲突中的突击。
description.en: Made up of direct retainers, champions, and guards serving chiefs, responsible for defending core settlements, displaying prestige, and striking in major clashes.

description.zh: 战时由附属村落征发，装备矛、盾、弓、石制或金属刃器，主要用于守寨、伏击和短程征讨。
