description.en: Raised from dependent villages in wartime, armed with spears, shields, bows, and stone or metal blades, mainly used for settlement defense, ambush, and short-range expeditions.

description.zh: 以独木舟和木板船为基础，承担河道巡逻、海峡护航、袭扰与快速机动，是贸易网络安全的重要力量。
description.en: Based on canoes and plank-built boats, these forces conducted river patrols, strait escort, raiding, and rapid movement, making them vital to trade-network security.

commanderInChief.zh: 各地大酋长或联盟首领
commanderInChief.en: Regional Paramount Chiefs or Alliance Leaders

technology.zh: 武器技术处于青铜向铁器过渡阶段，常用矛、戈形刃器、短剑、弓箭、木盾与藤甲；舟船、浅水机动与湿地作战经验比重甲和攻城技术更重要。
technology.en: Military technology was in transition from bronze to iron, with common use of spears, halberd-like blades, short swords, bows, wooden shields, and rattan armor; boats, shallow-water mobility, and wetland warfare were more important than heavy armor or siegecraft.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦间对河口、矿产、盐场和贸易通道的竞争，以及海上袭扰、山地部族冲突和季风灾害导致的资源紧张。
threats.en: Main threats came from competition among neighboring chiefdoms over estuaries, mineral zones, salt fields, and trade routes, as well as maritime raiding, upland tribal conflict, and resource stress caused by monsoon disruptions.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布高度分散，集中在红河三角洲、湄公河支流平原、马来半岛沿岸、越南中部海岸带以及印尼群岛部分肥沃海岸与河口。多数居民居住于村社或半永久聚落。
populationDescription.en: Population was highly dispersed, concentrated in the Red River delta, lower Mekong tributary plains, the coasts of the Malay Peninsula, the central Vietnamese littoral, and fertile coasts and estuaries of parts of the Indonesian archipelago. Most people lived in villages or semi-permanent settlements.

name.zh: 红河三角洲大型聚落群
name.en: Large Red River Delta Settlement Cluster

name.zh: 马来半岛西岸贸易据点

socialClasses.zh: 首领与贵族家族、祭司和巫觋、战士随从、工匠与商旅、自由农民渔民、依附人口与战俘构成主要社会层次。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata consisted of chiefs and noble lineages, priests and shamans, warrior retainers, artisans and traders, free farmers and fishers, and dependent people or captives.

allies.zh: 无固定统一盟友体系；常见的是相邻河谷、海岸与岛屿酋邦之间通过通婚、联合祭祀、互市与共同护航形成的临时联盟。
allies.en: There was no fixed unified alliance system; more common were temporary alliances among neighboring riverine, coastal, and island chiefdoms formed through marriage ties, joint ritual, reciprocal markets, and cooperative escort of trade.

enemies.zh: 没有单一外敌，敌对关系主要表现为与竞争性酋邦之间围绕港口、河口、山口、矿产和人口资源的冲突。
enemies.en: There was no single external enemy; hostile relations mainly took the form of conflicts with rival chiefdoms over ports, estuaries, mountain passes, mineral zones, and population resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持贸易通道畅通、巩固婚姻网络、吸引外来交换者和避免被更强区域势力边缘化为目标。对外政策务实灵活，多以礼物、通婚和共同航运安排代替正式条约。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to keep trade routes open, strengthen marriage networks, attract outside exchange partners, and avoid marginalization by stronger regional powers. It was pragmatic and flexible, relying more on gifts, marriage ties, and shared shipping arrangements than on formal treaties.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期

keyInnovations.zh: 湿地稻作改良、舟船与季风航行经验、独木舟和木板船结合技术、盐业与渔产保存、早期铁器使用、铜与锡资源整合、织造与竹木建筑适应热带环境。
keyInnovations.en: Improved wet-rice cultivation, seafaring and monsoon-navigation knowledge, combined canoe and plank-boat traditions, salt production and fish preservation, early use of iron, integration of copper and tin resources, and weaving and bamboo-wood architecture adapted to tropical conditions.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于地理位置连接中国南方、印度洋边缘与群岛海域，兼具河运与海运能力；社会结构弹性较强，能通过婚盟和馈赠迅速重组政治关系；资源种类丰富，特别是稻米、海产、林产与矿物。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths lay in its geography, linking southern China, the fringes of the Indian Ocean, and the archipelagic seas, with both riverine and maritime transport capacity. Social structures were flexible enough to reorganize political ties through marriage and gift exchange, and the region possessed abundant rice, marine goods, forest products, and minerals.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、文字行政与稳定税制，限制了大规模动员与长期工程能力；政治依赖个人威望，联盟容易分裂；热带疾病、洪水、风暴和地理破碎性提高了统治成本。
weaknesses.en: The absence of unified rule, written administration, and a stable tax system limited large-scale mobilization and long-term public works. Politics depended heavily on personal prestige, making alliances fragile, while tropical disease, floods, storms, and fragmented geography increased the cost of rule.

outlook.zh: 前景总体稳定且具有上升潜力。随着铁器扩散、海上交通经验累积和区域交换扩大，这些酋邦中的部分强势节点可能在未来数世纪发展为更具层级性的早期国家和港口政体。
outlook.en: The overall outlook was stable and carried upward potential. As iron spread, maritime knowledge deepened, and regional exchange expanded, some of these stronger nodes were likely over coming centuries to develop into more hierarchical early states and port polities.

description.zh: 紅河、湄公河與群島地區分布多個早期酋邦與海陸交換網絡。
description.en: The Red River, Mekong, and island zones contained multiple early chiefdoms tied by overland and maritime exchange.

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心的安纳托利亚君主制，国王依靠宫廷贵族、地方领主与宗教权威共同统治。
governmentForm.en: A central Anatolian monarchy centered on kingship, in which the king ruled through court nobles, local lords, and religious authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族战士、神庙人员、自由农民、工匠与依附人口组成。高地与河谷地区的聚落围绕要塞、宫殿和仓储设施分布。乡村人口占多数，但贵族与神庙对土地、牲畜和贡赋拥有较强控制力。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, noble warriors, temple personnel, free farmers, craftsmen, and dependent populations. Settlements in the uplands and river valleys clustered around fortresses, palaces, and storage facilities. Most people lived in the countryside, but nobles and temples exercised strong control over land, herds, and tribute.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是王室家族、地方贵族与掌握大型祭祀中心的神职者。宫廷精英通过土地占有、战车武力和宴飨网络维系统治。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was made up chiefly of the royal family, regional nobles, and clergy attached to major cult centers. Court elites maintained power through landed wealth, chariot-based force, and networks of feasting and patronage.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部世袭传承，多偏向父子继承，但也可能受到贵族拥立与宫廷斗争影响。若继承不稳，地方贵族与神庙可能成为决定性力量。
succession.en: The throne was normally hereditary within the royal line, tending toward father-to-son succession, though noble backing and court struggles could affect the outcome. In unstable transitions, regional nobles and temples could become decisive.

structure.zh: 王国以戈耳迪翁宫廷为政治中心，通过宫廷官员、仓储管理者和地方首领执行统治。中央对核心地带控制较强，对边缘山区则更多依赖附庸关系与贡赋。神庙在合法性、财富保管与地方协调中发挥重要作用。
structure.en: The kingdom was centered politically on the court at Gordion, with authority exercised through palace officials, storehouse managers, and local chiefs. Central control was stronger in the core territory and more dependent on tributary relations in the uplands and border zones. Temples played an important role in legitimacy, wealth storage, and local coordination.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、粮仓、贡赋登记与宫廷工坊。
function.en: Managed the court, granaries, tribute records, and palace workshops.

function.zh: 负责征召贵族武装、守备要塞与组织出征。
function.en: Oversaw noble levies, fortress garrisons, and campaign organization.

function.zh: 协调王室祭祀、神庙财产与重大节庆。
function.en: Coordinated royal rites, temple estates, and major festivals.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由贵族领主、要塞长官与村社首领共同管理。核心谷地可能直接受王室监督，外围地区则以纳贡与服兵役维系关系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled jointly by noble lords, fortress commanders, and village heads. Core valleys were more directly supervised by the crown, while outer districts were tied by tribute and military service.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要建立在习惯法、王室裁断与宗教誓约之上。涉及土地、牲畜、婚姻与赔偿的纠纷通常由地方首领或宫廷仲裁。
legalSystem.en: Law rested mainly on customary practice, royal judgment, and religious oath-taking. Disputes over land, livestock, marriage, and compensation were usually settled by local chiefs or the court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以谷物、牲畜、纺织品、金属制品和劳役为主，货币化程度很低。附庸与地方贵族还需在战争时提供兵员、车辆和补给。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was collected chiefly in grain, livestock, textiles, metal goods, and labor service, with little monetization. Vassals and regional nobles were also expected to provide men, vehicles, and supplies in wartime.

religion.zh: 多神教（库柏勒崇拜）
religion.en: Phrygian polytheism (Cybele worship)

culturalAchievements.zh: 弗里吉亚以木工、青铜器、纺织品与巨型墓冢传统著称。戈耳迪翁展示出复杂的宴飨文化、精工家具制作与王权象征性建筑。
culturalAchievements.en: Phrygia was noted for woodworking, bronzes, textiles, and the tradition of monumental tumuli. Gordion shows a complex culture of feasting, fine furniture production, and symbolic royal architecture.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系，弗里吉亚语
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Phrygian language

philosophy.zh: 以王权神授、祖先传统与大母神崇拜为核心的宗教—礼仪世界观。
philosophy.en: A religious-ritual worldview centered on sacral kingship, ancestral tradition, and the cult of the Great Mother.

writingSystem.zh: 约前8世纪后期开始出现早期弗里吉亚字母，受腓尼基字母体系影响；行政实践仍大量依赖口述与记忆。
writingSystem.en: An early Phrygian alphabet, influenced by the Phoenician script tradition, began to appear in the later 8th century BCE; much administration still relied on oral practice and memory.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以谷物农业、畜牧、木材加工和区域性手工业为基础。作为安纳托利亚内陆的重要节点，弗里吉亚也从连接爱琴海、叙利亚与高原腹地的陆路贸易中获益。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on grain farming, herding, timber working, and regional craft production. As a major inland Anatolian node, Phrygia also benefited from overland trade linking the Aegean, Syria, and the plateau interior.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、木工、纺织、青铜冶铸、陶器
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, woodworking, textiles, bronze metallurgy, pottery

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、羊毛、纺织品、木器、青铜器、马匹、染料、蜂蜜
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wool, textiles, wooden goods, bronze goods, horses, dyes, honey

name.en: Weighed silver and payment in kind

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备和织物。贵族家庭则拥有更大牧群、金属器、战车装备与宴飨器具。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, and textiles. Noble households possessed larger herds, metal goods, chariot equipment, and feasting vessels.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室再分配、地方贡赋和家庭生产并存的混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining royal redistribution, local tribute, and household production.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由贵金属、成批谷物、织物、青铜器和牲畜共同构成，实际储备多分散于宫廷仓储与神庙。贵族墓葬与宴飨器具显示王室能够集中相当可观的剩余资源。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metal, bulk grain, textiles, bronzes, and livestock, much of it stored across palace depots and temples. Elite burials and feasting equipment indicate that the monarchy could concentrate substantial surplus resources.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向积累实物储备、维持宫廷威望并确保战时动员能力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored accumulating goods in kind, maintaining court prestige, and preserving wartime mobilization capacity.

description.zh: 由长矛兵、盾兵和部分轻装投射兵组成，是守城与野战主力。
description.en: Composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, and some light missile troops; they formed the core of both garrison and field forces.

description.zh: 以贵族精英为核心，用于冲击、侦察和指挥展示。
description.en: Centered on noble elites, used for shock action, reconnaissance, and command display.

description.zh: 分驻边境据点和交通节点，负责警戒与仓储防御。
description.en: Stationed in frontier strongholds and route nodes, responsible for watch, storage protection, and local defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王
commanderInChief.en: The King

technology.zh: 军备处于铁器早期成熟阶段，广泛使用长矛、铁剑、青铜盔甲部件、复合弓与战车。要塞建筑和木石结合防御工事较发达。
technology.en: Military equipment stood at an early mature Iron Age level, using spears, iron swords, bronze armor components, composite bows, and chariots. Fortress construction and mixed timber-stone defenses were relatively developed.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自安纳托利亚诸邻邦竞争、边缘山地部族袭扰，以及未来来自西方和北方流动武装的压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival Anatolian states, raiding by upland tribes, and the growing future pressure of mobile armed groups from the west and north.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约八十万，主要分布在中安纳托利亚高原的农业谷地与牧场地带。戈耳迪翁是最大政治与经济中心，其他人口多居于村落、要塞附属聚落和小型市镇。
populationDescription.en: The population of about 800,000 was concentrated in the agricultural valleys and pasture zones of central Anatolia. Gordion was the largest political and economic center, while most others lived in villages, fortress-dependent settlements, and small towns.

name.zh: 佩西努斯前身圣地聚落

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、神职者、自由农民、工匠商人、依附民与奴役人口。
socialClasses.en: Royalty and nobles, clergy, free farmers, artisans and traders, dependents, and enslaved people.

allies.zh: 主要依靠与安纳托利亚内陆诸地方领主和附庸群体的松散同盟，而非固定条约体系。
allies.en: It relied mainly on loose alliances with inland Anatolian lords and vassal groups rather than on a fixed treaty system.

enemies.zh: 与吕底亚前身势力、叙利亚方向商路竞争者及山地部族存在潜在冲突。
enemies.en: Potential adversaries included proto-Lydian powers, competitors along Syrian trade routes, and upland tribal groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交重在控制内陆交通线、吸纳边缘首领并通过贡礼、婚盟与军事威慑维持区域霸权。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling inland routes, incorporating peripheral chiefs, and maintaining regional hegemony through tribute, marriage alliances, and military deterrence.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期至中期安纳托利亚文化圈
era.en: Early to Middle Iron Age Anatolian cultural sphere

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器武器与工具扩散、木构与土石混合防御建筑、精细纺织、青铜铸造和字母书写的初步采用。
keyInnovations.en: Spread of iron tools and weapons, mixed timber-earth-stone defensive building, fine textile production, bronze casting, and early adoption of alphabetic writing.

strengths.zh: 地处交通枢纽、农业与牧业兼备、王权象征强、手工业和宴飨文化发达。
strengths.en: It held a strategic crossroads position, combined farming with pastoralism, projected strong royal symbolism, and possessed advanced craft and feasting culture.

weaknesses.zh: 政权对外围控制有限，制度仍高度依赖贵族忠诚与个人王权，易受外来冲击影响。
weaknesses.en: Control over outer regions was limited, institutions depended heavily on noble loyalty and personal kingship, and the state was vulnerable to external shocks.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍可保持安纳托利亚内陆强国地位，但若遭遇大规模外侵或继承危机，稳定性将迅速下降。
outlook.en: In the short term it could remain a major inland Anatolian power, but large-scale invasion or a succession crisis would quickly reduce its stability.

description.zh: 安納托利亞中部新興強國，承繼後赫梯時代的權力真空。
description.en: A rising central Anatolian kingdom that filled part of the post-Hittite power vacuum.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为核心的军事-官僚帝国，国王通过总督、省份与附庸体系统治广大领土。
governmentForm.en: A monarch-centered military-bureaucratic empire in which the king ruled extensive territories through governors, provinces, and a vassal system.

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈严格等级化结构，顶层为王室与大贵族，其次为军政官员、祭司与抄写员，再下为自由农民、工匠、商人、附庸民与被迁徙人口，底层包括奴隶与战俘。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly hierarchical, with the royal house and great nobles at the top, followed by military and administrative officials, priests, and scribes, then free peasants, artisans, merchants, dependent populations and deportees, with slaves and captives at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 亚述王室、宫廷贵族、高级将领、省总督与大神庙祭司共同构成统治阶层，但最终权力集中于国王及其近臣。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Assyrian royal family, court nobles, senior generals, provincial governors, and major temple clergy, though ultimate authority was concentrated in the king and his close entourage.

succession.zh: 实行世袭君主制，通常由王子继位，但继承安排依赖国王指定、宫廷支持与军事力量，王位争夺并不罕见。
succession.en: The monarchy was hereditary, usually passing to a royal son, but succession depended on royal designation, court backing, and military strength, and disputes over the throne were not uncommon.

structure.zh: 中央政府围绕国王、宫廷、大神庙与军政官僚系统运作；帝国核心地区被划为行省，由总督管理，边缘地区则通过附庸王、公侯与条约体系加以控制。
structure.en: The central government operated around the king, court, major temples, and a military-administrative bureaucracy; core imperial lands were organized into provinces under governors, while peripheral regions were controlled through vassal kings, local rulers, and treaty arrangements.

function.zh: 负责王令起草、文书档案、外交书信、贡赋记录与省份报告汇总。
function.en: Handled drafting royal decrees, maintaining archives, diplomatic correspondence, tribute records, and the collection of provincial reports.

function.zh: 监督各省总督、统计户口与土地、征收粮赋与实物税，并管理附庸的贡纳义务。
function.en: Oversaw provincial governors, recorded population and land, collected grain and in-kind taxes, and managed tribute obligations from vassals.

function.zh: 组织常备军、征召部队、军械储备、战马与战车调配，以及远征后勤。
function.en: Organized the standing army, troop levies, weapon reserves, horse and chariot allocation, and campaign logistics.

function.zh: 管理主要神庙财产、祭祀供奉、宗教节庆以及与王权合法性相关的神谕与仪式。
function.en: Managed major temple properties, offerings, religious festivals, and oracles and rituals tied to royal legitimacy.

function.zh: 负责宫殿、城墙、道路、运河、军需仓库和被迁徙劳工的调度。
function.en: Oversaw palaces, city walls, roads, canals, military storehouses, and the deployment of deported labor.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以省为核心，省总督统辖税收、司法、治安与征兵；边疆和新征服地区常驻军政官员并与附庸王并行治理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was province-centered, with governors controlling taxation, justice, security, and recruitment; frontier and newly conquered zones often had military-administrative officers operating alongside vassal rulers.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系基于王令、地方习惯法与早期美索不达米亚法传统，司法由王室官员、地方长官与神庙机构共同执行，刑罚严厉并强调对叛乱、逃税和违抗的震慑。
legalSystem.en: The legal system drew on royal decrees, local customary law, and older Mesopotamian legal traditions; justice was administered by royal officials, local authorities, and temple institutions, with severe punishments emphasizing deterrence against rebellion, tax evasion, and disobedience.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以粮食、牲畜、金属、纺织品和劳役等实物征收为主，核心省份负担常规赋税，附庸国则缴纳贡物、军役与特殊供奉。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was chiefly collected in kind through grain, livestock, metals, textiles, and labor service; core provinces bore regular taxes, while vassal states paid tribute, military service, and special offerings.

religion.zh: 亚述宗教属于古代美索不达米亚多神信仰体系，以国家主神亚述神为中心，同时崇拜伊什塔尔、阿达德、纳布、辛、沙马什和马尔杜克等诸神。
religion.en: Assyrian religion belonged to the ancient Mesopotamian polytheistic tradition, centered on the state god Ashur while also venerating Ishtar, Adad, Nabu, Sin, Shamash, Marduk, and other deities.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成后世意义上的系统哲学，思想核心体现为王权神授、宇宙秩序、占卜与天象解释，以及通过仪式维系国家与神明之间的契约关系。
philosophy.en: There was not yet philosophy in the later systematic sense; the intellectual core lay in divinely sanctioned kingship, cosmic order, divination, celestial interpretation, and ritual maintenance of the covenantal relationship between the state and the gods.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿卡德语楔形文字，宫廷和行政文书高度依赖抄写员；阿拉米语在帝国内部的日常交流和地方行政中也日益重要。
writingSystem.en: Akkadian cuneiform was the principal writing system, heavily used in court and administration by trained scribes; Aramaic was also becoming increasingly important for everyday communication and local administration across the empire.

culturalAchievements.zh: 亚述文化成就体现在宫殿浮雕、巨型守护神像、王室铭文、军事与狩猎图像叙事、城市规划，以及对巴比伦与更早美索不达米亚学术传统的继承与整理。
culturalAchievements.en: Assyrian cultural achievements included palace reliefs, colossal guardian figures, royal inscriptions, narrative military and hunting imagery, urban planning, and the preservation and organization of Babylonian and earlier Mesopotamian scholarly traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 统治语言阿卡德语属于闪米特语系东闪米特支；帝国内广泛使用的阿拉米语属于闪米特语系西北闪米特支。
languageFamily.en: The ruling language, Akkadian, belonged to the East Semitic branch of the Semitic language family; the widely used Aramaic of the empire belonged to the Northwest Semitic branch.

gdpDescription.zh: 帝国经济以高产农业、王室再分配、战争掠夺、贡赋和区域贸易相结合为特征，在公元前800年前后处于近东最强盛和最具动员力的经济体之一。
gdpDescription.en: The imperial economy combined productive agriculture, royal redistribution, war plunder, tribute, and regional trade, making it one of the strongest and most mobilizable economies in the Near East around 800 BCE.

mainIndustries.zh: 主要产业包括灌溉农业、畜牧业、金属加工、青铜与铁兵器制造、纺织、宫殿建筑、石材雕刻、木材加工和长途商贸。
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included irrigated agriculture, pastoralism, metalworking, bronze and iron arms production, textiles, palace construction, stone carving, woodworking, and long-distance trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 贸易品包括大麦、小麦、枣椰、羊毛织物、染色布、金银、铜、锡、铁器、木材、马匹、象牙、香料、葡萄酒和奢侈工艺品。
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included barley, wheat, dates, wool textiles, dyed cloth, gold and silver, copper, tin, iron goods, timber, horses, ivory, aromatics, wine, and luxury crafted items.

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富以土地使用权、谷物储备、牲畜、工具和纺织品形式保存；城市精英与神庙则持有贵金属、奴隶、仓储资产和跨地区贸易收益。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was held in land-use rights, grain stores, livestock, tools, and textiles; urban elites and temples held precious metals, slaves, storehouse assets, and profits from interregional trade.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为宫廷、神庙和私有生产并存的再分配型农业帝国经济，国家通过税赋、贡纳、劳役和军事掠夺对资源进行高度集中。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a redistributive agrarian imperial economy in which palace, temple, and private production coexisted, while the state concentrated resources through taxation, tribute, labor obligations, and military plunder.

source.zh: 核心省份粮赋与实物税
source.en: Core provincial grain and in-kind taxation

source.zh: 王室庄园、神庙收益与关卡征收
source.en: Royal estates, temple income, and transit levies

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由贵金属、战利品、贡物、武器储备、粮仓与王室工坊库存共同构成，集中于卡尔胡等核心王城及主要神庙设施之中。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metals, war booty, tribute goods, weapon reserves, granaries, and stocks from royal workshops, concentrated in core royal cities such as Kalhu and in major temple facilities.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重高强度动员与战争维持，通过征服、重组行省、迁徙人口和强化贡赋来扩大税基，同时将剩余资源投入军队、宫殿和基础设施。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized intense mobilization and the maintenance of warfare, expanding the tax base through conquest, provincial reorganization, deportation of populations, and stronger tribute extraction, while investing surplus resources into the army, palaces, and infrastructure.

description.zh: 步兵是亚述军队核心，包括持矛兵、弓箭手、盾兵与攻城部队，训练较系统，常配合工程兵实施围城战。
description.en: Infantry formed the core of the Assyrian army, including spearmen, archers, shield-bearers, and siege troops, with relatively systematic training and frequent cooperation with engineers in siege warfare.

description.zh: 骑兵在新亚述时期日益重要，用于侦察、追击、侧翼机动和远程压制，是战车之外更灵活的打击力量。
description.en: Cavalry was becoming increasingly important in the Neo-Assyrian period, used for reconnaissance, pursuit, flanking movement, and ranged harassment, offering a more flexible striking force than chariots.

description.zh: 战车仍具王权象征和战场冲击作用，多用于平坦地带的精锐作战、指挥与护卫。
description.en: Chariots still carried strong royal symbolism and battlefield shock value, serving in elite combat, command, and escort roles especially on open ground.

description.zh: 包括破城槌操作手、筑垒工、工匠和运输队，擅长修路、架桥、构筑围攻坡道和突破城防。
description.en: Included ram crews, sappers, craftsmen, and transport units skilled in road-building, bridging, siege ramps, and breaching fortified defenses.

description.zh: 来自附庸国和被征服地区，提供轻步兵、弓箭手、投石兵、骆驼兵和地方向导，增强帝国远征适应性。
description.en: Drawn from vassal states and conquered regions, these forces provided light infantry, archers, slingers, camel troops, and local guides, increasing the empire’s campaign adaptability.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿达德-尼拉里三世国王
commanderInChief.en: King Adad-nirari III

technology.zh: 亚述军队装备铁制兵器、复合弓、战车、攻城槌、云梯与工程器械，拥有近东最先进的围城技术、军队调度与道路后勤体系之一。
technology.en: The Assyrian military employed iron weapons, composite bows, chariots, battering rams, ladders, and engineering devices, possessing one of the Near East’s most advanced systems of siegecraft, troop coordination, and road logistics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方的乌拉尔图、南方巴比伦的不稳定局势、东部米底与伊朗高地部族、叙利亚与黎凡特的反叛，以及草原游动部族的袭扰。
threats.en: Major threats came from Urartu in the north, instability in Babylonia to the south, the Medes and Iranian highland tribes in the east, rebellions in Syria and the Levant, and raids by steppe mobile peoples.

populationDescription.zh: 帝国人口约五百万，涵盖亚述本土居民、巴比伦与上美索不达米亚城市人口、叙利亚和黎凡特诸族、被迁徙的战俘群体及众多附庸地区臣民，是高度多族群、多语言的帝国社会。
populationDescription.en: The empire’s population was about five million, including Assyrian heartland inhabitants, populations of Babylonia and Upper Mesopotamian cities, peoples of Syria and the Levant, deported captive communities, and subjects from many vassal lands, making it a highly multiethnic and multilingual imperial society.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室与贵族、高级官员与将领、祭司与抄写员、商人和工匠、自由农民、附庸民与被迁徙人口，以及奴隶和战俘。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the royal house and nobility, high officials and generals, priests and scribes, merchants and artisans, free peasants, dependent populations and deportees, and slaves and war captives.

allies.zh: 亚述在公元前800年前后真正意义上的平等盟友很少，更多依靠叙利亚、黎凡特和安纳托利亚边缘诸邦的附庸关系与朝贡体系维持影响力。
allies.en: Assyria had few true equal allies around 800 BCE, relying instead on vassal relationships and tributary arrangements with states on the Syrian, Levantine, and Anatolian margins to maintain influence.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手为乌拉尔图、反复动荡的巴比伦势力、部分阿拉米诸邦、叙利亚和黎凡特的反叛城邦，以及东部伊朗高地的米底和其他山地部族。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were Urartu, unstable Babylonian forces, certain Aramean polities, rebellious city-states in Syria and the Levant, and the Medes and other highland tribes of the eastern Iranian zone.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以威慑、远征、索贡、册封附庸和必要时直接吞并为核心，目标是控制贸易通道、压制潜在挑战者并维持近东霸权。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on deterrence, campaigning, tribute extraction, vassal installation, and direct annexation when necessary, aiming to control trade routes, suppress challengers, and preserve Near Eastern hegemony.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括铁兵器的大规模应用、专业化攻城机械、帝国道路与驿传网络、复杂灌溉工程、标准化行政文书和多区域军需调度体系。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included large-scale use of iron weaponry, specialized siege machinery, imperial road and courier networks, complex irrigation works, standardized administrative documentation, and multi-regional military logistics.

militaryEngineering.zh: 军事工程古代世界最先进：新亚述帝国拥有古代最强大的攻城技术——攻城锤、移动攻城塔、坑道掘进和引水淹城等手段系统化运用。铁制武器大规模装备军队。亚述战车和骑兵编制是古代最完善的军事组织。
militaryEngineering.en: Most advanced military engineering in the ancient world: the Neo-Assyrian Empire possesses antiquity's most powerful siege technology — battering rams, mobile siege towers, tunneling, and water diversion systematically employed. Iron weapons equip the army at scale. Assyrian chariots and cavalry represent the ancient world's most refined military organization.

hydraulicEngineering.zh: 水利工程宏大：塞纳赫里布在尼尼微修建的供水渠道长约80公里，包含世界已知最古老的水渠桥。灌溉系统支撑美索不达米亚肥沃的农业。
hydraulicEngineering.en: Grand hydraulic engineering: Sennacherib's water supply channel to Nineveh stretches ~80 km, including the world's oldest known aqueduct bridge. Irrigation systems sustain Mesopotamia's fertile agriculture.

overallAssessment.zh: 公元前800年的新亚述帝国拥有古代世界最先进的军事技术和工程能力。铁制武器的大规模使用、系统化的攻城术和完善的军事组织使亚述成为古代近东最令人畏惧的军事力量。但亚述的技术优势高度集中在军事领域，其暴力统治方式最终将导致帝国崩溃。
overallAssessment.en: The Neo-Assyrian Empire in 800 BCE possesses the ancient world's most advanced military technology and engineering. Large-scale iron weapons, systematized siege warfare, and refined military organization make Assyria the most feared military force in the ancient Near East. But Assyria's tech advantage is highly concentrated in the military domain; its brutal ruling style will ultimately lead to imperial collapse.

strengths.zh: 亚述的优势在于极强的军事实力、成熟的省制和贡赋体系、有效的人口迁徙与资源集中能力、发达的围城技术，以及对近东核心农耕区和交通线的控制。
strengths.en: Assyria’s strengths lay in its formidable military power, mature provincial and tribute system, effective ability to relocate populations and concentrate resources, advanced siege technology, and control over the core agrarian zones and communication routes of the Near East.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于统治高度依赖持续征服与军事威慑，边疆过长、被征服地区反叛频繁、财政对贡赋和战利品依赖明显，且宫廷政治与继承危机可能削弱中央权威。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were that rule depended heavily on continued conquest and military intimidation, frontiers were overstretched, conquered regions rebelled frequently, finances relied noticeably on tribute and booty, and court politics and succession crises could weaken central authority.

outlook.zh: 在公元前800年前后，亚述正处于强势扩张与制度巩固阶段，短期前景极佳，有望继续压制巴比伦、叙利亚和黎凡特诸邦；但若未来外部强敌联合、内部反叛叠加宫廷失序，帝国也将面临严重的结构性压力。
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, Assyria was in a phase of strong expansion and institutional consolidation, with an excellent short-term outlook and strong prospects for further domination over Babylonia, Syria, and the Levant; yet in the longer run, coalitions of external enemies, internal revolts, and court disorder could place the empire under severe structural strain.

description.zh: 近東最強大的軍事帝國，控制美索不達米亞並干預敘利亞、伊朗與黎凡特。
description.en: The dominant military empire of the Near East, controlling Mesopotamia and projecting power into Syria, Iran, and the Levant.

ideology.zh: 亚述神授权的帝国扩张主义，通过持续征服、人口迁徙和恐怖威慑建立近东霸权，大王是神在人间的代理人
ideology.en: Imperial expansionism mandated by the god Ashur, establishing Near Eastern hegemony through continuous conquest, population deportation, and terror-based deterrence, with the Great King as the god's earthly agent

relationship.zh: 国王是帝国的绝对中心，掌控军事、行政和宗教权力。王廷通过总督体系和情报网络对各省实施严密控制。然而继承问题和宫廷权斗始终是潜在威胁——太后、宦官和高级将领都可能在王位更替中发挥决定性作用。
relationship.en: The king was the absolute center of the empire, controlling military, administrative, and religious power. The court exercised tight control over provinces through the governor system and intelligence networks. However, succession disputes and court power struggles remained persistent threats—queen mothers, eunuchs, and senior generals could all play decisive roles in royal transitions.

description.zh: 新亚述王廷以卡尔胡宏伟的宫殿建筑群为中心，集中了帝国最高的决策权力。大王亲自统帅远征军，巡视各省，接受附庸朝贡，并在宫殿浮雕中展现王权的绝对威严。阿达德-尼拉里三世统治时期，帝国正处于制度巩固阶段：行省体制日趋成熟，人口迁徙政策有效瓦解了被征服地区的抵抗力量，军事后勤和道路网络支撑着远距离征服。然而王廷政治并非铁板一块——太后萨姆穆拉马特（塞米拉米斯的历史原型）据传在此时期拥有异常的政治影响力，显示出王廷内部权力分配的复杂性。大王需要不断通过军事胜利来维系自身权威和贵族的忠诚。
description.en: The Neo-Assyrian royal court, centered on the magnificent palace complex at Kalhu, concentrated the empire's supreme decision-making power. The Great King personally commanded expeditionary forces, inspected provinces, received vassal tribute, and displayed absolute royal majesty in palace reliefs. Under Adad-nirari III, the empire was in a phase of institutional consolidation: the provincial system was maturing, population deportation policies effectively dismantled resistance in conquered territories, and military logistics and road networks supported long-distance conquest. Yet court politics were not monolithic—Queen Mother Sammuramat (the historical basis for Semiramis) reportedly wielded extraordinary political influence during this period, revealing the complexity of power distribution within the court. The Great King needed continuous military victories to sustain his authority and noble loyalty.

role.zh: 帝国统治者，推动制度巩固与领土扩张
role.en: Imperial ruler driving institutional consolidation and territorial expansion

role.zh: 拥有异常政治影响力的王太后，塞米拉米斯传说的历史原型
role.en: Queen mother with extraordinary political influence, historical basis for the Semiramis legend

leaderTitle.zh: 图尔塔努（大将军兼高级总督）

ideology.zh: 在帝国框架内扩大行省自主权和个人权力，通过控制地方税收、军队和人口迁徙获取利益，在王位更替中发挥造王者角色
ideology.en: Expanding provincial autonomy and personal power within the imperial framework, profiting through control of local taxation, armies, and population deportation, playing kingmaker roles in royal transitions

relationship.zh: 行省总督是帝国行政体系的支柱，但也是王权最危险的潜在挑战者。图尔塔努（大将军）和其他高级总督在各自管辖区内拥有巨大的军事和经济权力，部分总督的实力已接近半独立状态。他们在名义上执行王令，但在继承危机中常成为决定性力量——既可以支持国王，也可能成为叛乱的策划者。
relationship.en: Provincial governors were the pillars of the imperial administrative system but also the most dangerous potential challengers to royal power. The Turtanu (Commander-in-Chief) and other senior governors wielded enormous military and economic power within their jurisdictions, with some approaching semi-independent status. They nominally executed royal orders but often became decisive forces during succession crises—capable of supporting the king or orchestrating rebellion.

description.zh: 新亚述帝国的行省制度是古代世界最高效的领土管理体系之一，但也孕育了威胁中央权力的离心力量。帝国核心地区被划分为多个行省，每位总督在辖区内负责征税、征兵、司法和公共工程。高级官员如图尔塔努（大将军）和拉布沙奇（大酒政）等不仅掌控军事力量，还管理着广阔的行省领地。部分总督利用人口迁徙政策在辖区安置忠于自己的族群，建立个人势力基础。阿达德-尼拉里三世时期，太后萨姆穆拉马特据传不得不借助某些总督的支持来维持其政治影响力，反映出中央权力与地方权力之间复杂的博弈关系。随着帝国版图不断扩大，远方行省的总督越来越难以被有效监控——他们截留贡赋、私自扩军、建立独立的外交联系，在事实上形成了帝国体制内的权力集团。这种结构性矛盾在后来的帝国历史中反复引发内战和分裂危机。
description.en: The Neo-Assyrian provincial system was one of the most efficient territorial management systems in the ancient world, but it also bred centrifugal forces threatening central power. The imperial core was divided into multiple provinces, with each governor responsible for taxation, conscription, justice, and public works within their jurisdiction. Senior officials like the Turtanu (Commander-in-Chief) and Rab-shakeh (Chief Cupbearer) not only controlled military forces but also managed vast provincial territories. Some governors used population deportation policies to settle groups loyal to themselves in their jurisdictions, building personal power bases. During Adad-nirari III's reign, Queen Mother Sammuramat reportedly had to rely on certain governors' support to maintain her political influence, reflecting the complex power dynamics between central and local authority. As the empire's territory continued expanding, governors of distant provinces became increasingly difficult to effectively monitor—they intercepted tribute, privately expanded their armies, and established independent diplomatic contacts, effectively forming power blocs within the imperial system. This structural contradiction would repeatedly trigger civil wars and fragmentation crises in later imperial history.

role.zh: 帝国最高军事长官兼高级行省管理者，权力仅次于国王
role.en: Supreme military commander and senior provincial administrator, second only to the king in power

role.zh: 管理帝国边缘行省，掌控地方驻军和贡赋征收
role.en: Governed frontier provinces, controlling local garrisons and tribute collection

ideology.zh: 亚述神为宇宙主宰，帝国扩张是执行神意的神圣事业；通过占星术、肝脏占卜和文献收集保存文明知识
ideology.en: Ashur as cosmic sovereign, imperial expansion as sacred duty executing divine will; preserving civilizational knowledge through astrology, hepatoscopy, and archival collection

headquarters.zh: 亚述城亚述神庙与尼尼微抄写学堂
headquarters.en: Temple of Ashur at Assur and scribal academies at Nineveh

relationship.zh: 祭司集团为帝国扩张提供宗教合法性——每次远征都被定义为亚述神的旨意。大祭司在王位继承确认和重大决策中通过神谕发挥关键作用。神庙控制可观的土地和财富，尼尼微的书吏和占星师则建立了古代世界最重要的图书馆传统。
relationship.en: The priestly establishment provided religious legitimacy for imperial expansion—each campaign was defined as the will of the god Ashur. The high priest played a key role through oracles in confirming royal succession and major decisions. Temples controlled considerable land and wealth, while Nineveh's scribes and astrologers established one of the ancient world's most important library traditions.

description.zh: 亚述祭司集团以亚述城的亚述神庙为精神中心，在帝国政治中扮演着不可或缺的角色。大祭司通过占卜和神谕为国家重大决策提供神意指引，包括军事远征的时机选择和王位继承的确认。占星术在新亚述时期达到高度发展，祭司们系统记录天象变化并将其与政治事件相关联，形成了复杂的预兆学说。尼尼微的抄写学堂收集和整理了大量巴比伦和亚述的文学、科学和宗教文献，为后来亚述巴尼拔图书馆的建立奠定了基础。神庙系统同时是重要的经济实体，拥有土地、牲畜和手工作坊。祭司们在维护宗教正统的同时也积极吸收被征服地区的神灵崇拜，展现了帝国宗教政策的包容性和战略性。
description.en: The Assyrian priestly establishment, centered on the Temple of Ashur at the city of Assur, played an indispensable role in imperial politics. The high priest provided divine guidance for major state decisions through divination and oracles, including the timing of military campaigns and confirmation of royal succession. Astrology reached a high level of development in the Neo-Assyrian period, with priests systematically recording celestial phenomena and correlating them with political events, creating a complex omen doctrine. The scribal academies of Nineveh collected and organized vast quantities of Babylonian and Assyrian literary, scientific, and religious texts, laying the foundation for the later Library of Ashurbanipal. The temple system was also a major economic entity, owning land, livestock, and craft workshops. Priests maintained religious orthodoxy while actively incorporating the deity cults of conquered regions, demonstrating the inclusiveness and strategic nature of imperial religious policy.

role.zh: 帝国最高宗教权威，确认王位继承与重大军事决策
role.en: Supreme religious authority of the empire, confirming succession and major military decisions

role.zh: 解读天象预兆，为国家决策提供占星学指导
role.en: Interpreted celestial omens, providing astrological guidance for state decisions

ruler.zh: 马尔杜克-扎基尔-舒米一世

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心、由神庙与城市精英共同支撑的两河下游世袭君主制
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy centered on royal authority and supported by temples and urban elites in southern Mesopotamia

socialStructure.zh: 社会以国王、神庙祭司、书吏、贵族地主、城市自由民、佃农、工匠、商人以及奴隶构成；乡村与沼泽边缘地区还存在半自治的阿拉米人与迦勒底部落群体。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the king, temple priesthood, scribes, noble landholders, urban free citizens, tenant farmers, artisans, merchants, and slaves; semi-autonomous Aramean and Chaldean tribal groups also existed in the countryside and marsh fringes.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、大神庙祭司集团、总督与高级书吏家族、主要城市的地主与商人精英
rulingClass.en: The royal house, major temple priesthoods, governors and senior scribal families, and the landed and mercantile elites of the principal cities

succession.zh: 原则上为父系世袭，由王室内部确认；但在政局动荡时，亚述干预、宫廷政变或地方强人拥立亦可能左右继承。
succession.en: Succession was in principle patrilineal within the royal house, but in unstable periods it could be shaped by Assyrian intervention, palace intrigue, or the backing of powerful regional figures.

structure.zh: 巴比伦王国政务围绕王宫与大神庙展开。国王通过宫廷官员、各城总督、神庙管理人和书吏体系治理各地。巴比伦、波尔西帕、西帕尔、乌鲁克、尼普尔等古老城市保留较强地方传统与宗教威望，中央权力在核心灌溉区较强，在南部沼泽与部落边缘地带较弱。
structure.en: The Babylonian state was organized around the palace and major temples. The king governed through court officials, city governors, temple administrators, and a scribal apparatus. Ancient cities such as Babylon, Borsippa, Sippar, Uruk, and Nippur retained strong local traditions and religious prestige; central authority was stronger in the core irrigation zone and weaker in the southern marshes and tribal frontiers.

function.zh: 起草敕令、保存泥版档案、处理外交文书、登记土地与赋役
function.en: Drafted decrees, maintained clay-tablet archives, handled diplomatic correspondence, and registered land and obligations

function.zh: 监督大神庙财产、祭祀供给、神职任命协调及神庙劳役征发
function.en: Oversaw major temple estates, ritual provisions, coordination of priestly appointments, and temple labor obligations

function.zh: 组织河道疏浚、堤坝修缮、分水调度与农业灌溉维护
function.en: Organized dredging of waterways, repair of levees, water allocation, and maintenance of agricultural irrigation

function.zh: 征收谷物、枣椰、牲畜、银与劳役，管理王室仓储与转运
function.en: Collected grain, dates, livestock, silver, and labor dues, and managed royal storage and transport

function.zh: 负责城防守备、道路巡查、征召地方武装并监控部落边境
function.en: Handled urban defense, road patrols, mobilization of local forces, and monitoring of tribal frontiers

function.zh: 记录诉讼、担保契约、债务文书、婚姻继承与财产裁断
function.en: Recorded lawsuits, guarantees, debt instruments, marriage and inheritance matters, and property judgments

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以城市为基本单位，由总督、城长、神庙管理人及地方长老共同运作。农业村社承担灌溉与赋役义务，南部迦勒底和阿拉米部落地区则多通过盟约、贡纳和军事镇压并用的方式维系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was city-based, operated by governors, city heads, temple managers, and local elders. Rural communities bore irrigation and labor duties, while southern Chaldean and Aramean tribal districts were managed through a mix of agreements, tribute, and coercion.

legalSystem.zh: 法律传统延续古巴比伦与加喜特时期惯例，依赖成文契约、法庭证词、见证人与神庙誓言。王令、城市习惯法及神庙法并存，土地、债务、婚姻、奴隶和租佃案件最为常见。
legalSystem.en: The legal system continued Old Babylonian and Kassite traditions, relying on written contracts, court testimony, witnesses, and temple oaths. Royal edicts, urban customary law, and temple law coexisted, with land, debt, marriage, slavery, and tenancy cases being most common.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物为主，包括谷物、枣椰、羊毛、牲畜和劳役，也征收按重量计的银。神庙地产、王室庄园和城市税站构成财政基础；战时常增派特别贡纳与军需征收。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily in kind, including grain, dates, wool, livestock, and labor service, with silver also assessed by weight. Temple lands, royal estates, and urban tax points formed the fiscal base; in wartime, special tribute and military requisitions were often added.

religion.zh: 以美索不达米亚多神信仰为核心，崇拜马尔杜克、纳布、沙马什、伊什塔尔、恩利尔与辛等神。巴比伦新年祭典与《埃努玛·埃利什》传统强化了马尔杜克的至尊地位，神庙是宗教、经济与政治权威中心。
religion.en: Religion was centered on Mesopotamian polytheism, with worship of Marduk, Nabu, Shamash, Ishtar, Enlil, Sin, and other gods. The Babylonian New Year festival and the tradition of the Enuma Elish reinforced Marduk's supreme position, while temples served as religious, economic, and political centers.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成后世意义上的系统哲学，思想以神意、王权正当性、宇宙秩序、占卜智慧和书吏伦理为主。学术传统重视通过天象、祭祀和古代文本理解神与人的关系。
philosophy.en: There was not yet a systematic philosophy in the later sense; thought was dominated by divine will, royal legitimacy, cosmic order, divinatory wisdom, and scribal ethics. Intellectual life emphasized understanding relations between gods and humans through celestial signs, ritual, and ancient texts.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿卡德语楔形文字，书面语保留高度传统化形式，同时苏美尔语仍作为宗教与学术语言在祭司和书吏教育中延续。
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was Akkadian cuneiform. Written usage preserved highly traditional forms, while Sumerian continued as a liturgical and scholarly language in priestly and scribal education.

culturalAchievements.zh: 巴比伦保持了古代两河文明最成熟的书吏、天文占卜、法律契约和宗教文学传统。泥版档案、词汇表、祭仪文本、史诗与祈祷文持续抄写流传，城市神庙建筑与运河景观也体现出高度文明连续性。
culturalAchievements.en: Babylonia preserved the most mature scribal, astral-divinatory, legal-contractual, and religious literary traditions of ancient Mesopotamia. Clay tablet archives, lexical lists, ritual texts, epics, and prayers continued to be copied, while temple architecture and canal landscapes reflected a high degree of civilizational continuity.

languageFamily.zh: 以东闪米特语支的阿卡德语巴比伦方言为行政与高文化语言，民间亦广泛使用西闪米特的阿拉米语方言
languageFamily.en: The Babylonian dialect of Akkadian, an East Semitic language, was the administrative and high-cultural language, while West Semitic Aramaic dialects were also widely spoken in everyday life

gdpDescription.zh: 巴比伦经济在铁器时代西亚仍属高水平农业—神庙—城市复合经济体，依靠密集灌溉农耕、枣椰园、羊毛纺织、神庙地产与区域贸易运作。但在公元前800年前后，长期战乱、部落侵扰和亚述压力削弱了财政稳定与生产连续性。
gdpDescription.en: The Babylonian economy remained one of the more advanced agro-urban temple economies of Iron Age Western Asia, operating through intensive irrigation farming, date orchards, wool textiles, temple estates, and regional trade. Around 800 BCE, however, chronic warfare, tribal incursions, and Assyrian pressure undermined fiscal stability and continuity of production.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、枣椰种植、羊毛与纺织、神庙与王室庄园经营、陶器、金属加工、建筑砖业、河运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, date cultivation, wool and textile production, temple and royal estate management, pottery, metalworking, brickmaking, and riverine trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口谷物、枣椰、羊毛、纺织品、皮革、陶器和手工业品；输入木材、石材、金属、马匹、奢侈品以及来自伊朗高地和叙利亚方向的原料。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included grain, dates, wool, textiles, leather, pottery, and manufactured goods; imports included timber, stone, metals, horses, luxury items, and raw materials from the Iranian highlands and the Syrian direction.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。城市精英和神庙承租人可拥有房屋、奴隶、银、牲畜和枣椰园份额；普通农户多以小块耕地、租地权、农具和家庭劳力为主要资产，部落边缘居民则更依赖牲畜与流动财产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly. Urban elites and temple leaseholders could possess houses, slaves, silver, livestock, and shares in date orchards, while ordinary farmers mainly held small plots, tenancy rights, tools, and family labor; frontier tribal households relied more on herds and mobile property.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室与神庙大地产、城市市场交换和家庭生产并存的再分配—市场混合经济。银按重量充当价值尺度，但大量交易仍以实物和劳役结算。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a redistributive-market hybrid combining royal and temple estates, urban exchange, and household production. Silver by weight served as a unit of value, but many transactions were still settled in kind and labor.

source.zh: 贡纳、附庸供给与非常征收
source.en: Tribute, dependent contributions, and extraordinary levies

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非全为铸币现金，而是由银重储备、谷仓、牲畜、纺织品、神庙库存及可动用贡品共同构成。由于战争和地方离叛，实际可支配储备常低于名义账面。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a cash reserve in coined money, but a combination of weighed silver, grain stores, livestock, textiles, temple inventories, and mobilizable tribute. Because of war and local unrest, actually usable reserves were often lower than nominal accounts suggested.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守维持：尽量保障神庙祭祀和运河系统，同时在危机时提高特别征收、要求城市承担军需，并依赖神庙和地方精英预支物资。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservatively stabilizing: it sought to preserve temple rites and the canal system while, in crises, increasing extraordinary levies, requiring cities to support military logistics, and relying on temples and local elites to advance supplies.

description.zh: 由持矛、盾牌、弓箭与短剑的步兵构成，是守城、河道防御和会战的主力。
description.en: Composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, archers, and short-sword infantry, they formed the main force for city defense, canal security, and field engagements.

description.zh: 由城市与乡村按赋役征发，在战时加强守备、运输和工程任务，战斗力不及常备军。
description.en: Raised from cities and countryside through obligation, they reinforced garrisons, transport, and engineering duties in wartime, though they were less effective than regular troops.

description.zh: 数量有限，由王室和贵族提供，用于指挥、冲击和象征威望，但在这一时期相对步兵与骑乘侦察的重要性已下降。
description.en: Limited in number and supplied by the king and nobles, they served command, shock, and prestige roles, though by this period they were declining in importance relative to infantry and mounted scouting.

description.zh: 主要用于侦察、传令、追击和监视阿拉米与迦勒底部落活动，包含部分骑乘弓手和轻装部队。
description.en: Used mainly for reconnaissance, dispatch riding, pursuit, and monitoring Aramean and Chaldean movements, including some mounted archers and light troops.

description.zh: 负责船运、浮桥、城墙修补、补给和运河障碍设置，在两河下游作战环境中具有关键作用。
description.en: Responsible for transport by boat, pontooning, wall repair, supply, and canal obstruction works, they were crucial in the lower Mesopotamian theater.

commanderInChief.zh: 巴比伦之王兼统帅
commanderInChief.en: The King of Babylon as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军备处于铁器时代早期成熟阶段，使用铁制武器与青铜装备并存，复合弓、长矛、盾牌、战车、攻城器具和河运后勤体系均较发达，但整体军事组织与攻城效率逊于同时代亚述。
technology.en: Military technology was at an early mature Iron Age stage, combining iron weapons with bronze equipment. Composite bows, spears, shields, chariots, siege tools, and river logistics were well developed, but overall organization and siege effectiveness were inferior to contemporary Assyria.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方强大的亚述帝国，其次是南部和西部活动频繁的迦勒底与阿拉米部落，以及东面的埃兰影响。内部城市离心与部落叛乱亦严重削弱国防。
threats.en: The chief threat came from the powerful Assyrian state to the north, followed by active Chaldean and Aramean tribes in the south and west, as well as Elamite influence to the east. Internal centrifugal tendencies among cities and tribal revolts also seriously weakened defense.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在幼发拉底河和底格里斯河下游灌溉带及古老城市周边。虽然巴比伦尼亚仍是人口稠密的农业文明区，但因战乱、运河失修和部落迁徙，部分边缘地区人口流动显著。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated mainly in the lower Euphrates-Tigris irrigation belt and around the ancient cities. Although Babylonia remained a densely settled agrarian civilization, warfare, canal neglect, and tribal migration caused significant movement in some frontier zones.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、祭司、大地主和高级书吏；中层为城市自由民、商人、工匠和中小地主；下层为佃农、雇工、债务依附者与奴隶。阿拉米人与迦勒底部落成员部分融入定居社会，部分保持独立身份。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, priests, large landholders, and senior scribes; the middle strata included urban free citizens, merchants, artisans, and middling landholders; the lower strata comprised tenant farmers, laborers, debt dependents, and slaves. Some Aramean and Chaldean tribal members were integrated into settled society, while others retained separate identities.

allies.zh: 缺乏稳固同盟；偶尔与反亚述的南部部落、部分阿拉米集团或埃兰势力形成临时配合
allies.en: It lacked stable allies; at times it formed temporary alignments with anti-Assyrian southern tribes, some Aramean groups, or Elamite interests

enemies.zh: 亚述为首要敌对强权，部分迦勒底与阿拉米部落亦时常叛服无常并威胁王国控制
enemies.en: Assyria was the primary hostile great power, while some Chaldean and Aramean tribes also alternated between submission and rebellion, threatening royal control

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以求存为主：在承认现实压力与维护王权尊严之间反复摇摆，采取纳贡、结盟、镇压部落、争取南部忠诚城市和借助宗教合法性的综合策略。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was primarily one of survival: wavering between acknowledging political realities and preserving royal dignity, it used tribute, alliances, tribal suppression, efforts to secure loyal southern cities, and appeals to religious legitimacy.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制工具与武器逐渐普及；复杂运河灌溉技术、泥砖建筑、楔形文字档案管理、天文观测与占卜体系、标准化重量衡量及成熟的契约书写传统仍为其核心技术优势。
keyInnovations.en: Iron tools and weapons were becoming increasingly common; complex canal irrigation, mudbrick construction, cuneiform record keeping, astronomical observation and divinatory systems, standardized weights, and mature contractual writing remained its core technological strengths.

strengths.zh: 拥有深厚的城市文明传统、宗教威望、发达灌溉农业、成熟书吏体系和重要区域贸易地位；巴比伦作为宗教与文化中心具有超越单纯军事实力的影响力。
strengths.en: It possessed a deep urban tradition, religious prestige, developed irrigation agriculture, a mature scribal apparatus, and an important place in regional trade; Babylon itself held influence beyond mere military power as a religious and cultural center.

weaknesses.zh: 军事组织弱于亚述，地方分裂严重，对部落边缘地带控制不稳，财政因战争和非常征收而承压，中央王权常依赖神庙与地方精英妥协。
weaknesses.en: Its military organization was weaker than Assyria's, local fragmentation was serious, control over tribal frontier zones was unstable, finances were strained by war and emergency levies, and central kingship often depended on compromise with temples and local elites.

outlook.zh: 短期前景不稳。若无法重建南部秩序并抵御亚述压力，王国将继续陷于附庸化、内乱和王位更替；但巴比伦的宗教与文化中心地位使其即使在政治衰弱时仍能长期保持历史重要性。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was unstable. Unless it could restore order in the south and resist Assyrian pressure, the kingdom would remain vulnerable to vassalization, internal disorder, and dynastic turnover; yet Babylon's religious and cultural centrality ensured enduring historical importance even in political weakness.

description.zh: 南美索不達米亞古老王國，受亞述壓力並受迦勒底與阿蘭勢力牽制。
description.en: The ancient kingdom of southern Mesopotamia, under Assyrian pressure and challenged by Chaldean and Aramean groups.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为核心的集权领土王国，依托王室、总督与要塞体系治理。
governmentForm.en: A centralized territorial monarchy centered on the king and administered through royal officials, governors, and a fortress network.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与军事贵族、神庙祭司、地方总督与书记官、自由农民与牧民、工匠商人以及战俘和被迁徙人口构成。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and military nobility, temple priests, provincial governors and scribes, free farmers and pastoralists, artisans and merchants, and war captives or resettled populations.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、军事贵族、要塞指挥官、高级祭司与受王权任命的地方总督构成统治阶层。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was formed by the royal family, military aristocracy, fortress commanders, senior priests, and provincially appointed governors loyal to the crown.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王室内部世袭，由国王指定继承人；在实践中常见父子共治或王子提前参与军政事务。
succession.en: Succession was generally hereditary within the royal house, with the king designating an heir; in practice, father-son co-rule or early princely participation in rule was common.

structure.zh: 乌拉尔图政府以国王为最高权威，兼具军事统帅、最高祭祀保护者与土地分配者身份。中央宫廷设有负责贡赋、仓储、工程、军务与宗教事务的官员，地方则由总督管理省区，并以密集要塞、仓库与灌溉据点维持控制。
structure.en: The Urartian government placed the king at the apex as supreme military commander, protector of the cult, and allocator of land. The central court included officials responsible for tribute, granaries, construction, military affairs, and religious institutions, while provincial administration rested on governors supported by a dense network of fortresses, storehouses, and irrigation centers.

function.zh: 记录铭文、贡赋、仓储清单、土地与徭役分配，维护王室文书。
function.en: Recorded inscriptions, tribute, storage inventories, land assignments, and labor obligations, and maintained royal documentation.

function.zh: 征集粮食、牲畜、金属与手工业成品，管理王室仓库与再分配。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, metals, and manufactured goods, and managed royal storehouses and redistribution.

function.zh: 统筹驻军、要塞补给、武器储备、道路联络与边境防御。
function.en: Oversaw garrisons, fortress provisioning, weapons stores, road communications, and frontier defense.

function.zh: 组织修建运河、蓄水设施、宫殿、神庙、城墙与山地道路。
function.en: Organized the construction of canals, reservoirs, palaces, temples, walls, and mountain roads.

function.zh: 管理国家神庙、祭献牲畜、节庆礼仪与神庙地产。
function.en: Managed state temples, sacrificial livestock, festival rites, and temple estates.

function.zh: 监督各地税赋、治安、征发劳役与迁徙人口安置。
function.en: Supervised provincial taxation, security, labor levies, and the settlement of resettled populations.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以省区和要塞为核心。国王任命总督、城堡指挥官与仓储官，统辖周边村社、牧场和灌溉地。许多边远地区以军事殖民点与迁徙居民加强控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration revolved around provinces and fortresses. The king appointed governors, fortress commanders, and storage officials who supervised surrounding villages, pastures, and irrigated lands. In many frontier zones, control was strengthened through military colonies and resettled populations.

legalSystem.zh: 乌拉尔图并无保存完备的成文法典如巴比伦式法典，但实际运作依赖王命、地方习惯、神庙规范以及军事纪律。土地、贡赋、劳役和军需事务通常受王室文书直接规制，刑罚可能包括罚役、没收、拘禁与处决。
legalSystem.en: Urartu does not preserve a complete law code comparable to Babylonian models, but governance operated through royal commands, local custom, temple norms, and military discipline. Matters of land, tribute, labor, and military supply were typically regulated by royal order, with punishments including forced labor, confiscation, detention, and execution.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以实物贡赋为主，包括谷物、葡萄酒、牲畜、金属、木材和手工业品，并辅以徭役、军事义务及附庸地区的定额贡纳。
taxationSystem.en: The taxation system was primarily based on payments in kind, including grain, wine, livestock, metals, timber, and crafted goods, supplemented by labor service, military obligation, and fixed tribute from dependent regions.

religion.zh: 乌拉尔图宗教为国家多神信仰，主神哈勒迪居于核心地位，常与风暴神泰舍巴和太阳神希维尼并列。王权通过献祭、神庙建筑和铭文宣示自己受诸神庇佑。
religion.en: Urartian religion was a state-centered polytheistic system in which the god Haldi held the central place, often alongside the storm god Teisheba and the solar god Shivini. Kings proclaimed divine favor through sacrifice, temple construction, and inscriptions.

philosophy.zh: 乌拉尔图没有形成如后世希腊或中国那样的系统哲学传统，其思想核心体现为王权神授、秩序、征服、农业开发与对神明的仪式性服从。
philosophy.en: Urartu did not develop a formal philosophical tradition like later Greece or China; its intellectual worldview centered on divinely sanctioned kingship, order, conquest, agrarian development, and ritual obedience to the gods.

writingSystem.zh: 官方铭文主要使用改造自亚述楔形文字的乌拉尔图语书写系统，同时地方层面可能并存若干口头语言与未充分记录的书写习惯。
writingSystem.en: Official inscriptions mainly used the Urartian language written in a form of cuneiform adapted from Assyrian models, while multiple spoken local languages and less well-documented writing practices likely coexisted at the regional level.

culturalAchievements.zh: 乌拉尔图以山地要塞建筑、巨石城墙、王室铭文、青铜工艺、兵器制造和大型运河灌溉工程著称，其国家组织能力在高地环境中尤为突出。
culturalAchievements.en: Urartu was notable for mountain fortress architecture, cyclopean walls, royal inscriptions, bronze craftsmanship, weapons production, and large-scale canal irrigation works; its capacity for state organization in a highland environment was especially impressive.

languageFamily.zh: 乌拉尔图语属于胡里—乌拉尔图语系；王国内部还包含多种高地地方语言成分。
languageFamily.en: The Urartian language belonged to the Hurro-Urartian family, while the kingdom also contained diverse local highland speech communities.

gdpDescription.zh: 乌拉尔图经济在公元前800年前后处于高加索—亚美尼亚高原地区的强势水平，以王室主导的农业扩张、牧业、金属开采、青铜与铁器制造以及战争掠夺和贡赋再分配为支柱。由于古代经济缺乏统一货币统计，相关数值仅为现代折算估计。
gdpDescription.en: Around 800 BCE, the Urartian economy was strong by the standards of the Armenian Highlands and adjacent Caucasian world, resting on crown-led agricultural expansion, pastoralism, metal extraction, bronze and iron production, and the redistribution of booty and tribute. Because ancient economies lacked unified monetary accounting, these figures are modern equivalence estimates only.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄栽培与酿酒、牧羊牧牛、铜铁采冶、青铜器制造、武器生产、石木建筑、水利工程与长途贡赋运输。
mainIndustries.en: Cereal cultivation, viticulture and winemaking, sheep and cattle herding, copper and iron mining and smelting, bronze manufacture, arms production, stone and timber construction, irrigation works, and long-distance tribute transport.

tradeGoods.zh: 出口或再分配品包括金属制品、马匹、牲畜、皮革、木材、葡萄酒和工艺青铜器；输入品包括奢侈纺织品、部分精制手工品及来自南方和西方的高价值商品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports or redistributed goods included metal products, horses, livestock, leather, timber, wine, and crafted bronzes; imports included luxury textiles, selected fine manufactures, and high-value goods from southern and western regions.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、谷仓储粮、羊群、牛只、驴马、陶器与金属工具。贵族和神庙则掌握大规模牲畜、兵器、奢侈器物和被征调劳力。
householdWealth.en: Typical household wealth was measured in access to land, stored grain, flocks, cattle, donkeys or horses, pottery, and metal tools. Nobles and temples controlled much larger herds, weapons, luxury goods, and mobilized labor.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为王室主导的再分配经济，结合村社农业、地方贡赋、牧业交换与有限的区域远距贸易。国家通过要塞仓储体系集中资源，再向军队、工匠和边疆殖民点分配。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a crown-dominated redistributive economy combining village agriculture, provincial tribute, pastoral exchange, and limited regional long-distance trade. The state concentrated resources through fortress storehouses and redistributed them to armies, craftsmen, and frontier settlements.

source.en: Tribute from dependent and frontier regions
Transit duties, trade, and miscellaneous receipts

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由贵金属、青铜器、武器、谷物、葡萄酒、牲畜和其他实物储备共同构成，并分散储存在图什帕及若干重要要塞和神庙仓库中，而非单一金属金库。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metals, bronzes, weapons, grain, wine, livestock, and other stores in kind, distributed across Tushpa and several major fortresses and temple depots rather than concentrated in a single metallic vault.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在通过征服、迁民、灌溉开发与要塞建设扩大税基。王室偏好将财政盈余投入防御工事、农业基础设施和象征王权的建筑，而非长期维持纯现金储备。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on expanding the taxable base through conquest, resettlement, irrigation development, and fortress construction. The crown preferred to invest surpluses in defenses, agricultural infrastructure, and monuments of kingship rather than maintain purely monetary reserves.

description.zh: 军队主力，由持矛兵、盾兵、弓箭手和部分重装护卫构成，负责攻城、防守与山地作战。
description.en: The main body of the army, composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, archers, and some heavier guards, used in sieges, defense, and mountain warfare.

description.zh: 乌拉尔图重视马匹和机动力，骑兵地位日益提升，战车仍具威望和冲击用途，但在崎岖地形中受限。
description.en: Urartu valued horses and mobility; cavalry was gaining importance, while chariots retained prestige and shock value though they were limited in rough terrain.

description.zh: 驻守边疆与内地据点，负责粮仓、军械库、道路和水利设施的守备，也是地方征发和治安的核心力量。
description.en: Stationed in frontier and interior strongholds, they guarded granaries, arsenals, roads, and irrigation works and formed the backbone of local security and mobilization.

description.zh: 来自边地部族、迁徙居民和附属地区，承担侦察、山区行动、运输和次要战线任务。
description.en: Drawn from frontier tribes, resettled populations, and subordinate regions, they served in scouting, mountain operations, transport, and secondary theaters.

commanderInChief.zh: 伊什普伊尼国王
commanderInChief.en: King Ishpuini

technology.zh: 乌拉尔图军队处于早期铁器时代高水平，广泛使用复合弓、青铜与铁制兵器、鳞甲或片甲、防御坚固的山地要塞和成熟的攻守城技术。军用道路和仓储网络提升了持续动员能力。
technology.en: The Urartian military stood at a high Early Iron Age level, using composite bows, bronze and iron weapons, scale or lamellar armor, strongly fortified mountain fortresses, and developed siege and defensive techniques. Military roads and storage networks improved sustained mobilization.

threats.zh: 最大威胁来自南方强敌亚述帝国，其军力、攻城能力和战略弹性均十分强大。其他压力包括西方安纳托利亚势力、东部与北部高地部族，以及附庸地区潜在叛乱。
threats.en: The greatest threat came from the powerful Assyrian Empire to the south, whose military strength, siege capability, and strategic flexibility were formidable. Additional pressures came from western Anatolian powers, eastern and northern highland tribes, and potential revolts in dependent regions.

populationDescription.zh: 约九十万人口分布于凡湖周边高地、河谷农业区、牧场地带和一系列王家要塞周边定居点。人口构成复杂，包含乌拉尔图核心居民、胡里安与其他高地族群，以及被迁徙安置的人口。
populationDescription.en: Roughly 900,000 people were distributed across the highlands around Lake Van, river-valley farming districts, pasture zones, and settlements clustered around royal fortresses. The population was mixed, including a Urartian core, Hurrian and other highland groups, and resettled peoples.

name.zh: 埃雷布尼地区前身聚落
特伊舍巴伊尼地区前身聚落

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致可分为王室与军事贵族、祭司与高级官员、地方精英与堡寨军官、自由农民和牧民、工匠与商旅，以及战俘、奴仆和被迁徙居民。阶层流动有限，但军功、王室任命和迁民安置可创造新地方精英。
socialClasses.en: Social strata were broadly divided into the royal family and military nobility, priests and high officials, local elites and fortress officers, free farmers and pastoralists, artisans and traders, and war captives, servants, and resettled populations. Mobility was limited, but military service, royal appointment, and resettlement could create new local elites.

allies.zh: 乌拉尔图的外交更多依赖附庸关系、临时边地同盟和对高地部族的宗主权，而非稳定意义上的平等盟友。某些时期可与反亚述势力形成短暂合作。
allies.en: Urartian diplomacy relied more on tributary relations, temporary frontier alliances, and suzerainty over highland groups than on stable equal allies. At times it could align briefly with other anti-Assyrian forces.

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为亚述；此外与部分安纳托利亚政体、高地部族和不稳附属地区之间也时有冲突。
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was Assyria; it also faced intermittent conflict with certain Anatolian states, highland tribes, and unstable subordinate regions.

foreignPolicy.zh: 乌拉尔图的对外政策旨在控制凡湖周边高地、关键山口、金属与牧业资源区，并通过军事远征、要塞建设和迁徙安置建立缓冲地带，以抗衡亚述北进。
foreignPolicy.en: Urartian foreign policy aimed to dominate the highlands around Lake Van, key mountain passes, and metal- and pastoral-resource zones, using military campaigns, fortress construction, and resettlement to create buffer zones against Assyrian expansion.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 山地石砌要塞体系、大规模运河灌溉、青铜铸造、铁器兵械、王室仓储后勤网络以及适应高原环境的军事与农业工程。
keyInnovations.en: Mountain stone-fortress systems, large-scale canal irrigation, bronze casting, iron weaponry, royal storage-and-logistics networks, and military and agricultural engineering adapted to plateau conditions.

strengths.zh: 乌拉尔图的优势在于强有力的王权、成熟的要塞网络、优良的金属工艺、可观的农业与牧业基础，以及在高地环境中的战略防御能力。其国家组织和迁民政策使资源整合效率较高。
strengths.en: Urartu's strengths lay in strong kingship, a mature fortress network, fine metalworking, a substantial agrarian and pastoral base, and impressive defensive capacity in highland terrain. Its state organization and resettlement policies gave it relatively effective resource integration.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于对王权和军事扩张的高度依赖、经济货币化程度有限、边远地区整合不稳，以及在与亚述长期消耗战中面临更大人口与资源对手的压力。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included heavy dependence on royal authority and military expansion, limited monetization, unstable integration of remote regions, and the pressure of facing a larger and more resource-rich Assyrian rival in prolonged conflict.

outlook.zh: 在公元前800年前后，乌拉尔图正处于兴盛扩张阶段，具备继续巩固高地霸权并与亚述长期竞争的能力。但未来成败将取决于王位继承稳定性、边疆控制以及能否持续抵御南方帝国的军事与外交压力。
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, Urartu was in a flourishing and expansionary phase, capable of consolidating highland hegemony and competing with Assyria over the long term. Its future success, however, would depend on stable succession, frontier control, and its ability to withstand the military and diplomatic pressure of the southern empire.

description.zh: 凡湖周邊強國，為亞述北方最重要的競爭者之一。
description.en: A major kingdom around Lake Van and one of Assyria's principal northern rivals.

ruler.zh: 阿堤阿德王朝君主（姓名不详）

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭君主为核心的早期铁器时代地区王国，结合宫廷统治、地方贵族与附属社群的朝贡关系
governmentForm.en: A hereditary early Iron Age regional monarchy centered on the royal court, combining palace rule, local aristocracies, and tributary ties with subordinate communities

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族与战士精英、祭司与宫廷官员、城市工匠与商人、乡村自由民、小农、牧民以及一定数量的奴仆和依附人口构成
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, aristocratic and warrior elites, priests and court officials, urban artisans and merchants, rural freemen, small farmers, pastoralists, and a limited number of servants and dependent laborers

rulingClass.zh: 以阿堤阿德王族、萨第斯宫廷贵族、地方地主首领和军事随从集团为核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the Atyad royal family, the Sardis court nobility, local landed chiefs, and military retainers

succession.zh: 原则上实行父系世袭，但在早期阶段仍可能受宗族长老、贵族共识和宫廷力量平衡影响
succession.en: Succession was in principle patrilineal and hereditary, though in this early phase it was likely still shaped by clan elders, aristocratic consensus, and court power balances

structure.zh: 政权以萨第斯王宫为中心，国王通过亲族、贵族首领、宫廷管家、军队首领和地方代治者治理核心谷地与周边高地。国家整合程度尚不如后世帝国，更接近由王室核心领地和若干附属地区构成的复合王国。
structure.en: The state was centered on the royal palace at Sardis, where the king governed the core valleys and surrounding uplands through kinsmen, noble chiefs, palace stewards, military leaders, and local deputies. State integration was still less developed than later empires, resembling a composite kingdom of royal core lands and attached districts.

function.zh: 管理宫廷供给、仓储、王室地产、宴飨和贡物登记
function.en: Managed court supplies, storage, royal estates, feasting, and the registration of tribute

function.zh: 统筹王家卫队、征召贵族随从、边地守备和战时动员
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, mobilization of noble retainers, frontier defense, and wartime levies

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、手工业品和金属，管理公共仓廪与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, craft goods, and metals, and managed public granaries and redistribution

function.zh: 组织国家祭祀、维护圣所、协调祭司团与王权合法性仪式
function.en: Organized state cults, maintained sanctuaries, and coordinated priesthoods and rituals of royal legitimacy

function.zh: 联络地方首领、处理附属社群义务、监督道路节点和河谷聚落
function.en: Liaised with local chiefs, managed obligations of dependent communities, and supervised road nodes and valley settlements

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖河谷聚落长、贵族家族和受王室承认的地方首领。核心区由王室控制更强，外围地区多以纳贡、服役和提供士兵来体现臣属关系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on valley settlement heads, noble lineages, and locally recognized chiefs under royal authority. The core zone was more directly controlled by the crown, while outer districts expressed subordination mainly through tribute, service, and the provision of troops.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于习惯法、王命、宗族仲裁和赔偿原则。重大案件由国王或贵族法庭裁决，土地、婚姻、债务和伤害赔偿是常见事务。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on customary practice, royal commands, clan arbitration, and principles of compensation. Major cases were decided by the king or aristocratic courts, with land, marriage, debt, and injury compensation among common matters.

taxationSystem.zh: 赋税以实物和劳役为主，包括谷物、葡萄酒、畜产、纺织品、金属和季节性徭役；附属地区则以朝贡、军役和道路运输义务承担财政负担。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mainly in kind and labor service, including grain, wine, animal products, textiles, metals, and seasonal corvée; subordinate districts bore fiscal burdens through tribute, military service, and transport obligations.

religion.zh: 吕底亚宗教融合安纳托利亚本地传统、山地与生殖崇拜、祖先敬祀以及与卢维—赫梯遗存相关的神祇观念。王权通过祭祀和圣所奉献来巩固合法性。
religion.en: Lydian religion blended local Anatolian traditions, mountain and fertility cults, ancestor reverence, and divine concepts linked to Luwian-Hittite survivals. Royal authority was reinforced through sacrifice and dedication at sanctuaries.

philosophy.zh: 公元前800年的吕底亚尚无成体系哲学传统，思想以神话宇宙观、祭祀秩序、王权神授观念和贵族伦理为主。
philosophy.en: In 800 BCE Lydia had no formal philosophical tradition; thought was dominated by mythic cosmology, ritual order, sacral kingship, and aristocratic ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 此时书写使用尚有限，行政与纪念用途可能借助安纳托利亚和叙利亚影响下的书写实践；成熟的吕底亚文字出现于更晚时期。
writingSystem.en: Writing was still limited at this date, and administrative or commemorative use may have drawn on Anatolian and Syrian-influenced scribal practices; the fully developed Lydian script emerged later.

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就体现在金属工艺、纺织、王室礼仪、山地与河谷聚落的防御建筑，以及连接爱琴海与内陆的混合型安纳托利亚宫廷文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements were seen in metalworking, textiles, royal ceremonial, defensive building in upland and valley settlements, and a hybrid Anatolian court culture linking the Aegean with the interior.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系安纳托利亚语族，吕底亚语与卢维语等近缘
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Anatolian branch; Lydian was related to languages such as Luwian

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业为基础，辅以畜牧、金属加工、木材采集、纺织和区域贸易。萨第斯所在的赫耳摩斯河谷土地肥沃，且连接爱琴沿岸与安纳托利亚腹地，使王国具备高于一般部落联盟的财富组织能力，但尚未达到后期吕底亚的货币化和商业化程度。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, supported by herding, metalworking, timber extraction, textiles, and regional trade. The fertile Hermus valley around Sardis and its position between the Aegean coast and the Anatolian interior gave the kingdom greater wealth-organizing capacity than a typical tribal confederation, though it had not yet reached the monetized and commercialized level of later Lydia.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与酒、橄榄与油、羊毛纺织、畜牧、青铜与早期铁器制作、木材、陶器和地方贸易运输
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, viticulture and wine, olives and oil, wool textiles, herding, bronze and early iron production, timber, pottery, and local trade transport

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、羊毛织物、皮革、牲畜、木材、金属锭、手工器物，以及从沿海希腊人与腓尼基网络间接流通的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, wool textiles, leather, livestock, timber, metal ingots, crafted goods, and luxury items indirectly circulating through Greek coastal and Phoenician networks

unitName.zh: 舍客勒式重量单位与实物计量
unitName.en: shekel-like weight units and commodity measures

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、谷仓储备、牲畜、农具、纺织品和宗族关系；贵族财富则集中于大地产、战车或骑乘装备、金属器、宴饮器具和附庸网络。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held chiefly in land use rights, grain stores, livestock, tools, textiles, and kinship ties; aristocratic wealth was concentrated in larger estates, chariot or riding equipment, metal vessels, banquet goods, and networks of dependents.

economicSystem.zh: 以宫廷再分配、乡村自给和区域交换并存的混合经济。国家通过贡赋和王室地产集中资源，再经宴飨、军需和赏赐将财富重新分配给贵族、祭司和战士。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining palace redistribution, rural self-sufficiency, and regional exchange. The state concentrated resources through tribute and royal estates, then redistributed wealth through feasting, military supply, and rewards to nobles, priests, and warriors.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要由粮食储备、牲畜、金属锭、器物、纺织品和少量贵金属构成，而非后世意义上的铸币现金储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of grain reserves, livestock, metal ingots, vessels, textiles, and limited precious metals rather than coined cash in the later sense.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视积谷、维持王室威望和保障战时动员能力。稳定时期通过储备盈余巩固宫廷与边地关系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, emphasizing grain accumulation, royal prestige, and wartime mobilization capacity. In stable periods, surpluses were stored to strengthen ties between the court and frontier districts.

description.zh: 由王室亲兵、核心区步兵和持矛持盾战士组成，是守卫萨第斯与战时列阵的基础力量。
description.en: Composed of royal retainers, core-area infantry, and spear-and-shield warriors, forming the main force for guarding Sardis and battlefield deployment.

description.zh: 以贵族精英为主，处于战车传统向更灵活骑乘作战过渡阶段，用于冲击、侦察和追击。
description.en: Drawn mainly from aristocratic elites, these forces stood at a transitional stage between older chariot traditions and more flexible mounted warfare, serving in shock action, reconnaissance, and pursuit.

description.zh: 来自乡村和附属聚落的临时征召兵，装备较简陋，主要承担守备、运输和补充作战任务。
description.en: Temporary levies from rural and dependent settlements, more lightly equipped and used chiefly for garrison duty, transport, and reinforcement roles.

description.zh: 负责山口、道路和边境据点，熟悉地形，适合伏击和小规模冲突。
description.en: Responsible for passes, roads, and frontier strongpoints, these troops knew the terrain well and were suited to ambushes and small-scale conflict.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王
commanderInChief.en: The King

technology.zh: 军事技术处于早期铁器时代水平，使用铁制与青铜制兵器并存，装备包括长矛、短剑、弓、圆盾、头盔、皮甲和部分战车或骑乘装备。
technology.en: Military technology was at an early Iron Age level, with both iron and bronze weapons in use, including spears, short swords, bows, round shields, helmets, leather armor, and some chariot or mounted equipment.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自安纳托利亚中西部的竞争性王国与部族，尤其是不断上升的弗里吉亚势力、周边新赫梯—卢维亚拉米国家、山地部族袭扰，以及亚述向小亚细亚延伸影响所带来的战略不确定性。
threats.en: Major threats came from competing kingdoms and tribal groups in central and western Anatolia, especially the rising power of Phrygia, surrounding Neo-Hittite and Luwian-Aramaean polities, highland raiding groups, and the strategic uncertainty created by Assyrian influence extending toward Asia Minor.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于赫耳摩斯河谷、帕克托罗斯河流域、萨第斯周边平原与丘陵农业带，另有较分散的山地聚落和牧业社区。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Hermus valley, the Pactolus basin, the plains and agricultural hills around Sardis, with more dispersed upland settlements and pastoral communities elsewhere.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与高贵族居于顶层，其下为地方首领、战士、祭司和富裕地主；城市有工匠、商人和役工；多数人口为农民、牧民与依附家庭，另有少量奴仆和战俘。
socialClasses.en: The royal family and high nobility stood at the top, followed by local chiefs, warriors, priests, and prosperous landholders; towns contained artisans, merchants, and laborers; most people were farmers, herders, and dependent households, with a smaller number of servants and captives.

allies.zh: 暂无稳定而明确的正式盟友体系，王国更依赖与邻近安纳托利亚社群的通婚、互市与临时协作关系。
allies.en: There was no clearly defined stable alliance system; the kingdom relied more on marriage ties, exchange, and temporary cooperation with neighboring Anatolian communities.

enemies.zh: 未处于与某一单一大国的全面战争状态，但与弗里吉亚扩张势力、部分山地部族及若干竞争性安纳托利亚政体存在持续紧张。
enemies.en: It was not engaged in total war with any single great power, but it faced ongoing tensions with expanding Phrygian power, certain highland tribes, and several competing Anatolian polities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持西安纳托利亚平衡、保卫通往爱琴海与内陆的交通线、吸纳边缘社群入贡，以及避免过早卷入亚述主导的东部大战略为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to maintain the balance in western Anatolia, protect routes to the Aegean and the interior, draw peripheral communities into tribute relations, and avoid premature entanglement in the Assyrian-dominated eastern strategic sphere.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期

keyInnovations.zh: 早期铁器锻造、改进的谷物储存设施、山地与河谷防御工事、畜力运输、纺织工艺和跨区域金属衡量交换实践。
keyInnovations.en: Early iron forging, improved grain storage facilities, upland and valley fortifications, animal-powered transport, textile production techniques, and interregional exchange by weighed metal.

strengths.zh: 吕底亚的优势在于萨第斯周边肥沃河谷、连接爱琴与内陆的地理位置、可动员的贵族战士阶层，以及吸收多种安纳托利亚传统的政治弹性。这些条件为其后续崛起为地区强国奠定了基础。
strengths.en: Lydia’s strengths lay in the fertile valleys around Sardis, its strategic position between the Aegean and the interior, a mobilizable aristocratic warrior class, and political flexibility shaped by multiple Anatolian traditions. These conditions laid the groundwork for its later rise as a regional power.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于国家整合度仍有限、书写与官僚化水平较低、外围控制依赖贵族忠诚和贡赋关系，且易受弗里吉亚、亚述间接压力及内部继承不稳影响。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included limited state integration, low levels of writing and bureaucratization, reliance on aristocratic loyalty and tribute ties for peripheral control, and vulnerability to Phrygian pressure, indirect Assyrian influence, and unstable succession.

outlook.zh: 若能巩固萨第斯核心区、控制东西交通和整合周边社群，吕底亚在未来数世纪极有可能从地区王国发展为横跨西安纳托利亚的重要强权。
outlook.en: If it could consolidate the Sardis core, control east-west communications, and integrate surrounding communities, Lydia was well positioned over the coming centuries to develop from a regional kingdom into a major power spanning western Anatolia.

description.zh: 安納托利亞西部的地方王國，後來將成為愛琴與內陸之間的重要強權。
description.en: A western Anatolian kingdom that would later become a major power between the Aegean and interior.

name.zh: 新赫梯—卢维亚兰诸国联盟

capital.zh: 卡尔凯美什、哈马及其他都城

governmentForm.zh: 由多个城邦王国组成的松散邦联，各国保有主权，在对外战争与朝贡问题上临时协调
governmentForm.en: A loose confederation of multiple city-kingdoms, each retaining sovereignty while coordinating only intermittently on war and tribute matters

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族与战车武士、祭司、书吏、城镇工匠与商人、自由农民以及依附民和奴隶构成；不同国家中卢维、亚兰与残余赫梯传统并存
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of royal households, nobles and chariot warriors, priests, scribes, urban artisans and merchants, free peasants, and dependent laborers and slaves; Luwian, Aramaean, and residual Hittite traditions coexisted across the states

rulingClass.zh: 地方王室、世袭贵族、军政长官与大神庙祭司集团
rulingClass.en: Local royal families, hereditary nobles, military-administrative officials, and major temple priesthoods

succession.zh: 通常实行父子世袭，但在王室分支、贵族支持与外部强权干预下常出现继承争夺
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary from father to son, but disputes were frequent due to rival royal branches, noble backing, and interference by external great powers

structure.zh: 不存在统一中央朝廷，而是由卡尔凯美什、哈马、比特阿古西、古尔古姆、帕提那、米利德等王国各自统治；邦联层面仅在抵御亚述时形成使节往来、盟誓和联军安排
structure.en: There was no single central court; rather, kingdoms such as Carchemish, Hamath, Bit Agusi, Gurgum, Pattin, and Melid ruled themselves independently. At the confederational level, diplomacy, oath alliances, and coalition forces appeared mainly in response to Assyrian pressure

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室地产、仓储、贡品接收与王命传达
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, storage facilities, receipt of tribute, and transmission of royal commands

function.zh: 征召战士、维护战车与马匹、守卫城防并组织联军协作
function.en: Raised troops, maintained chariots and horses, guarded fortifications, and organized cooperation in coalition warfare

function.zh: 主持祭祀、管理神庙土地与祭司网络，并监督宗教合法性
function.en: Oversaw rituals, temple lands, priestly networks, and the religious legitimacy of rule

function.zh: 负责铭文、行政记录、条约文本、税赋清册与外交书信
function.en: Handled inscriptions, administrative records, treaty texts, tax registers, and diplomatic correspondence

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、金属与手工业实物，管理地方仓廪与运输
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, metals, and craft goods in kind, and managed local granaries and transport

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以城邦和周边乡村为单位，由国王任命或认可的总督、城长、堡垒长、税吏及神庙代理人共同管理；边境地区常依附部族首领与藩属酋长
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized around city-states and their rural hinterlands, governed by governors, city chiefs, fortress commanders, tax officials, and temple agents appointed or recognized by the king; frontier zones often relied on tribal leaders and vassal chiefs

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依赖王室裁断、地方习惯法、宗教誓约与赔偿原则；契约、贡赋和土地边界争端由书吏记录，重罪可处死刑、流放或没收财产
legalSystem.en: Law depended chiefly on royal judgment, local customary law, sacred oaths, and compensation principles; contracts, tribute obligations, and land disputes were recorded by scribes, while serious crimes could bring execution, exile, or confiscation

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物为主，包括谷物、橄榄油、葡萄酒、牲畜、纺织品、金属与劳役；附庸地区和商路关卡还征收贡物、通行税与战时特别征发
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily in kind, including grain, olive oil, wine, livestock, textiles, metals, and labor service; vassal regions and trade routes also yielded tribute, tolls, and extraordinary wartime levies

religion.zh: 宗教呈高度混合性，崇拜风暴神、太阳神、库巴巴、雷谢夫、哈达德及地方守护神；王权通过祭祀、神庙捐献和纪念碑铭文获得神圣正当性
religion.en: Religion was highly syncretic, venerating storm gods, sun deities, Kubaba, Resheph, Hadad, and local tutelary gods; kingship derived sacred legitimacy through ritual, temple patronage, and commemorative inscriptions

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成抽象哲学传统，思想核心在于王权合法性、神意、祖先记忆、城邦荣誉与盟誓伦理
philosophy.en: No abstract philosophical tradition had yet emerged; core thought centered on royal legitimacy, divine will, ancestral memory, civic honor, and the ethics of oath-keeping

writingSystem.zh: 使用多种文字并存：卢维象形文字用于纪念碑铭文，阿拉米字母日益普及于行政与外交，部分地区亦继承楔形文字传统
writingSystem.en: Multiple scripts coexisted: Luwian hieroglyphs were used for monumental inscriptions, the Aramaic alphabet was increasingly common in administration and diplomacy, and cuneiform traditions survived in some areas

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要成就包括带浮雕的城门与宫殿装饰、双语或多语王室铭文、石雕狮像与守护神像、铁器与青铜工艺、以及融合赫梯、叙利亚与亚兰元素的城市艺术风格
culturalAchievements.en: Notable achievements included relief-decorated gates and palaces, bilingual or multilingual royal inscriptions, stone lions and guardian statues, iron and bronze craftsmanship, and an urban artistic style blending Hittite, Syrian, and Aramaean elements

languageFamily.zh: 语言环境多元，主要包括安纳托利亚语支的卢维语与闪米特语支的阿拉米语，另受阿卡德语外交传统影响
