languageFamily.en: The linguistic environment was diverse, chiefly including Luwian of the Anatolian branch and Aramaic of the Semitic branch, with additional influence from Akkadian diplomatic traditions

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉与旱作农业、畜牧业、区域手工业和商路中转为基础。整体财富低于亚述等帝国，但在幼发拉底河渡口、叙北平原与安纳托利亚南缘的贸易节点上仍具重要地位。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on irrigated and dry farming, pastoralism, regional craft production, and transit trade. Total wealth was below that of empires like Assyria, yet these states remained important at Euphrates crossings, the northern Syrian plains, and the southern Anatolian trade corridors

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、牧业、纺织、金属加工、石雕、木工、陶器和商路转运
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, wine and olive oil production, pastoralism, textiles, metalworking, stone carving, woodworking, pottery, and caravan transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 输出谷物、葡萄酒、橄榄油、羊毛织物、金属制品、木材、马匹与工艺品；输入锡、奢侈品、象牙、香料、优质金属与外来制成品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included grain, wine, olive oil, woolen textiles, metal goods, timber, horses, and crafted items; imports included tin, luxury goods, ivory, aromatics, fine metals, and foreign manufactured wares

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、谷仓储粮、牲畜、纺织品和工具；城市精英则拥有石屋、金属器、战车装备和更稳定的神庙或王室关系网络
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in land-use rights, stored grain, livestock, textiles, and tools; urban elites possessed stone houses, metalware, chariot equipment, and more stable ties to temples or royal courts

economicSystem.zh: 经济属于宫廷—神庙—乡村复合体系，国家征收实物税并重新分配，市场交换和长途贸易在城镇中活跃，但尚无统一货币经济
economicSystem.en: The economy was a palace-temple-rural composite system in which states collected taxes in kind and redistributed them, while market exchange and long-distance trade were active in towns without a unified monetary economy

treasuryDescription.zh: 金库规模有限，且分散于多个王国的宫廷和神庙仓库中，以银、金器、青铜器、谷物储备与应急牲畜形式保存；在持续战争压力下流动性不足。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited in size and dispersed among the courts and temple storehouses of several kingdoms, held in silver, gold objects, bronze goods, grain reserves, and emergency livestock; under constant war pressure, liquidity was weak

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向短期防务与保城，依赖临时征发、盟友贡礼和商路税收补足常规收入，因此在亚述进攻年份常出现赤字
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized immediate defense and the holding of cities, relying on extraordinary levies, allied gifts, and trade-route tolls to supplement regular income; deficits were therefore common in years of Assyrian attack

description.zh: 由城镇守军、持矛步兵、盾兵和征召农兵组成，是守城与野战的主力
description.en: Composed of urban garrisons, spearmen, shield-bearers, and levied peasants; it formed the main force for siege defense and field warfare

description.zh: 由贵族与其随从操作战车，是传统精锐兵种，用于冲击、指挥和展示王权
description.en: Operated by nobles and their retainers, this was the traditional elite arm used for shock action, battlefield command, and royal display

description.zh: 包括早期骑兵、骑乘信使与边境侦察队，适合巡逻、追击和通报敌情
description.en: Included early cavalry, mounted messengers, and frontier scouts suited to patrol, pursuit, and intelligence reporting

description.zh: 由附庸城邦、山地部族与边境首领提供，战力不均但在防御和地形作战中有用
description.en: Supplied by vassal cities, hill tribes, and frontier chiefs; uneven in quality but useful for defense and terrain warfare

commanderInChief.zh: 各国国王及其首席将领
commanderInChief.en: The kings of each state and their chief generals

technology.zh: 处于铁器时代中期军事水平，广泛使用铁制矛头、刀剑、复合弓、战车、城墙、箭塔和攻守工事，但整体组织和攻城技术逊于亚述
technology.en: Military technology stood at a mid-Iron Age level, with widespread use of iron spearheads, swords, composite bows, chariots, city walls, towers, and defensive works, though organization and siege capabilities were inferior to Assyria

threats.zh: 最大威胁来自亚述帝国的持续扩张与分化瓦解策略；此外还面临乌拉尔图、弗里吉亚势力波动、边境部族袭扰及各国之间缺乏统一指挥的问题
threats.en: The greatest threat came from the relentless expansion and divide-and-subdue strategy of the Assyrian Empire; additional dangers included shifts in Urartian and Phrygian power, frontier tribal raiding, and the lack of unified command among the states

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于北叙利亚平原、幼发拉底河沿岸、阿曼努斯山口与安纳托利亚南缘盆地，以农村聚落为主，城市集中于贸易节点与王都
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed across the northern Syrian plains, the Euphrates corridor, the Amanus passes, and the basins of southern Anatolia, with most people in rural settlements and cities concentrated at trade nodes and royal capitals

socialClasses.zh: 上层为诸王、王族、贵族、祭司与军事首领；中层包括书吏、商人、工匠与较富裕农牧民；下层为普通农民、依附民、劳役人口与奴隶。族群与语言差异并未完全对应阶层，但王室和神庙系统掌握主要资源。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of kings, royal kin, nobles, priests, and military leaders; the middle strata included scribes, merchants, artisans, and wealthier farmers and herders; the lower strata were ordinary peasants, dependents, laborers, and slaves. Ethnic and linguistic differences did not map perfectly onto class, but royal and temple institutions controlled the main resources

allies.zh: 彼此间常有临时同盟，并与部分亚兰城邦、以色列、腓尼基城邦或反亚述势力建立阶段性合作，但盟约脆弱且易受战争形势影响
allies.en: They often formed temporary alliances among themselves and at times cooperated with certain Aramaean states, Israel, Phoenician cities, or other anti-Assyrian forces, though such arrangements were fragile and highly sensitive to military conditions

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为亚述；在特定时期也与乌拉尔图、邻近亚兰诸国及边境山地部族发生冲突
enemies.en: Their principal enemy was Assyria; at particular times they also clashed with Urartu, neighboring Aramaean polities, and frontier hill tribes

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以求存为主，策略包括结盟、输贡、婚姻联系、利用山口与河渡口的战略位置，以及在强邻之间反复平衡；一些国家选择顺服亚述，另一些则周期性反抗
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was primarily survival-oriented, using alliances, tribute, marriage ties, strategic control of passes and river crossings, and repeated balancing between stronger neighbors; some states chose accommodation with Assyria while others resisted periodically

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括铁器锻造、石雕与正门浮雕建筑、复合弓与战车体系、改良城防工事、以字母文字进行更高效的行政记录，以及区域性灌溉与储粮技术
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included iron forging, stone carving and gate-relief architecture, composite bow and chariot systems, improved fortifications, more efficient administrative record-keeping through alphabetic writing, and regional irrigation and grain-storage techniques

strengths.zh: 优势在于地处交通枢纽、城防坚固、文化兼容性强、工艺传统深厚，并能在危机时动员多个城邦与部族资源
strengths.en: Strengths included their position on major transit routes, strong fortifications, high cultural adaptability, deep craft traditions, and the ability to mobilize the resources of multiple city-states and tribes in times of crisis

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、财政分散、联军协调困难、人口与资源总量有限，且在攻城战、常备军制度和帝国级后勤方面明显不如亚述
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses lay in political fragmentation, dispersed finances, difficulty coordinating coalition armies, limited total population and resources, and clear inferiority to Assyria in siege warfare, standing-army systems, and imperial logistics

outlook.zh: 公元前800年前后，这些国家仍能依靠城防、外交与暂时结盟维持独立，但在亚述持续西进的压力下，未来数十年被逐步征服、纳入行省体系的风险极高
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, these states could still preserve independence through fortifications, diplomacy, and temporary alliances, but under continuing Assyrian westward pressure they faced a very high risk of gradual conquest and incorporation into a provincial imperial system over the following decades

description.zh: 敘北與安納托利亞南部多個小王國並存，受亞述持續壓迫。
description.en: A cluster of small states in northern Syria and southern Anatolia facing sustained Assyrian pressure.

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制，兼具宫廷贵族、军事将领与地方属邦网络的区域王国
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy with a regional kingdom structure supported by court nobles, military commanders, and a network of subordinate local polities

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与军事贵族、神庙祭司、城镇自由民、商人手工业者、乡村农牧民以及依附人口组成；城市精英对国家政治与贸易影响显著。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house and military nobility, temple priests, urban freemen, merchants and artisans, rural farmers and pastoralists, and dependent populations; urban elites held major influence over politics and trade.

rulingClass.zh: 王室家族、大马士革宫廷贵族、军队统帅、重要城市长官与大神庙祭司
rulingClass.en: The royal family, Damascene court nobility, army commanders, major city governors, and high temple priests

succession.zh: 通常为父子继承，但在王权衰弱时易受宫廷派系、军队首领与外敌干预
succession.en: Usually patrilineal succession from father to son, though weak kingship could be contested by court factions, military leaders, and foreign intervention

structure.zh: 国家以大马士革宫廷为核心，国王直接统辖都城周边和主要战略城市，并通过总督、城邑长官、附庸首领和神庙体系管理地方。
structure.en: The state was centered on the royal court at Damascus; the king directly ruled the capital region and major strategic cities, while provinces were managed through governors, city officials, vassal chiefs, and temple institutions.

function.zh: 负责国王诏令、宫廷事务、使节接待与档案保管
function.en: Handled royal decrees, palace affairs, reception of envoys, and record keeping

function.zh: 征收粮食、牲畜、金属与附庸贡品，并管理王室仓库
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, metals, and vassal tribute, and managed royal storehouses

function.zh: 管理驻军、战车、征召兵役、边防与军需
function.en: Oversaw garrisons, chariots, levies, frontier defense, and military supply

function.zh: 监督各城镇长官、道路、水源与边境属地关系
function.en: Supervised town officials, roads, water resources, and relations with frontier dependencies

function.zh: 协调王室祭祀、神庙供奉、占卜与宗教合法性
function.en: Coordinated royal cult, temple offerings, divination, and religious legitimacy

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖城邦传统与王室委任相结合；重要城市由忠于王室的长官或军政首领治理，乡村地区则通过村社长老、土地持有者和税吏控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined older city-state traditions with royal appointment; major cities were governed by officials or military-administrative chiefs loyal to the crown, while rural areas were controlled through village elders, landholders, and tax collectors.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于王室裁决、地方习惯法、契约文书和神庙誓约；商业、土地和债务纠纷多由城邑长官与长老审理。
legalSystem.en: Law rested mainly on royal judgment, local customary practice, written contracts, and temple oaths; commercial, land, and debt disputes were often heard by city officials and councils of elders.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以谷物、酒、橄榄油、牲畜、劳役和附庸贡赋为主，边贸关税与商队通行费亦是重要收入。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on grain, wine, olive oil, livestock, corvée labor, and tribute from subordinate territories, with customs dues and caravan transit fees also forming important revenue.

religion.zh: 多神信仰占主导，崇拜风暴神哈达德、月神辛及地方性神祇；王权通过向主神献祭来宣示合法性。
religion.en: Polytheistic religion predominated, centered on the worship of the storm god Hadad, the moon god Sin, and local deities; kingship asserted legitimacy through offerings to major gods.

philosophy.zh: 尚无系统化哲学传统；政治伦理与世界观主要体现在王权神授、神谕、誓约、祖先传统和实用治国经验中。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical school; political ethics and worldview were expressed through divine kingship, oracles, oath traditions, ancestral custom, and practical statecraft.

writingSystem.zh: 使用帝国化趋势日益明显的亚兰字母文字，主要书写于石碑、陶片、木板、皮革和纸草文书上。
writingSystem.en: An Aramaic alphabetic script was used, increasingly important across the Near East, written on stone inscriptions, ostraca, wooden tablets, leather, and papyrus documents.

culturalAchievements.zh: 大马士革王国在亚兰语的传播、石刻铭文、象牙与金属工艺、城防建筑以及连接内陆与地中海贸易文化方面成就显著。
culturalAchievements.en: The kingdom of Damascus was notable for the spread of Aramaic, commemorative inscriptions, ivory and metal craftsmanship, fortified urban architecture, and its role in linking inland and Mediterranean trade cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 西北闪米特语族，主要语言为古亚兰语
languageFamily.en: Northwest Semitic language family, with Old Aramaic as the principal language

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉农业、园圃种植、牧业、手工业和过境贸易为核心。大马士革凭借绿洲农业与商路枢纽地位维持较高区域财富，但持续战争与向亚述纳贡削弱了整体增长。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on irrigated agriculture, orchard cultivation, herding, crafts, and transit trade. Damascus sustained relatively high regional wealth through oasis agriculture and its caravan position, but repeated warfare and tribute pressure from Assyria weakened overall growth.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物与葡萄种植、橄榄油生产、牧羊牧山羊、纺织、青铜与铁器制造、木工、皮革加工、香料与商队服务
mainIndustries.en: Grain and vine cultivation, olive oil production, sheep and goat herding, textiles, bronze and ironworking, woodworking, leather processing, spices, and caravan services

tradeGoods.zh: 葡萄酒、橄榄油、羊毛织物、金属器、木材转运品、香料、染料、马匹、牲畜及来自阿拉伯与两河的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Wine, olive oil, wool textiles, metal goods, transshipped timber, spices, dyes, horses, livestock, and luxury goods from Arabia and Mesopotamia

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人和地权家庭拥有较高财富，乡村家庭则多依赖土地、牲畜和粮仓储备；财富差距因战争与赋役而扩大。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchants and landholding families possessed higher wealth, while rural households depended mainly on land, livestock, and grain stores; inequality widened under war and taxation.

economicSystem.zh: 以宫廷再分配、神庙经济、地方土地所有制与市场交换并存的混合型古代经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed ancient economy combining palace redistribution, temple economy, local landholding, and market exchange

source.en: Agricultural taxes and payments in kind

category.zh: 向亚述纳贡与外交支出
category.en: Tribute to Assyria and diplomatic payments

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由称量银、青铜器、粮储、牲畜和贡品构成。由于持续战争、外交赔款与贸易受阻，储备较先王时代明显减少。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of weighed silver, bronze goods, grain reserves, livestock, and tribute items. Ongoing warfare, diplomatic payments, and disrupted trade had notably reduced reserves compared with the reigns of earlier kings.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向应急与军费优先，通过提高实物征收、强化商路税收和动用王室储备来维持政权，但已显露不可持续性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was increasingly emergency-driven and military-first, relying on heavier in-kind collection, tighter control of transit taxes, and use of royal reserves to sustain the state, but it was becoming unsustainable.

description.zh: 以长矛兵、盾兵和部分弓箭手为主，是守城、列阵和边境驻防的核心兵种。
description.en: Composed mainly of spearmen, shield-bearers, and some archers, forming the core force for garrison duty, field formations, and city defense.

description.zh: 延续叙利亚—黎凡特传统的精锐战车力量，用于冲击、指挥与机动作战，但面对亚述改良军制已逐渐失去优势。
description.en: An elite chariot arm in the Syrian-Levantine tradition, used for shock action, command, and mobility, though it was gradually losing its edge against improved Assyrian military systems.

description.zh: 数量有限，主要承担侦察、袭扰、护送商路和边境警戒任务。
description.en: Limited in number, they mainly performed scouting, raiding, caravan escort, and frontier watch duties.

description.zh: 由各城镇和乡村在战争时期征发，装备不整，适合守城、运输和短期集结作战。
description.en: Raised from towns and villages in wartime, unevenly equipped and best suited for city defense, transport, and short-term mobilization.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The King as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军队处于铁器时代中期水平，装备铁制兵器、复合弓、马拉战车和石土混合城防工事，但在攻城技术、兵种协同和常备军制度上逊于亚述。
technology.en: The army stood at a middle Iron Age level, using iron weapons, composite bows, horse-drawn chariots, and mixed stone-earth fortifications, but lagged behind Assyria in siegecraft, combined arms, and standing-army organization.

threats.zh: 最大威胁来自扩张中的新亚述帝国；此外还需防备以色列、犹大、北叙利亚诸邦的局部冲突，以及附庸离心和边境游牧部族侵扰。
threats.en: The principal threat came from the expanding Neo-Assyrian Empire; additional dangers included local conflict with Israel, Judah, and northern Syrian states, as well as vassal disloyalty and raids by frontier tribal groups.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于大马士革绿洲、巴沙珊高地边缘、主要商路节点和若干设防城市；乡村与半游牧人口比例较高。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Damascus oasis, the margins of Bashan, major caravan routes, and several fortified towns; rural and semi-pastoral populations remained substantial.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、军事贵族和祭司；中层为商人、地主、书记员、工匠；下层为农民、牧民、劳役者与家内依附人口。
socialClasses.en: Upper strata included the royal family, military nobles, and priests; the middle included merchants, landholders, scribes, and artisans; the lower included peasants, herders, laborers, and dependent household populations.

allies.zh: 与部分叙利亚西部城邦、若干阿拉米附属首领及偶发性的反亚述联盟保持松散合作，但联盟关系脆弱且多变。
allies.en: It maintained loose cooperation with some western Syrian cities, certain Aramaean subordinate chiefs, and occasional anti-Assyrian coalitions, though such alliances were fragile and changeable.

enemies.zh: 新亚述帝国是首要敌手；与以色列和犹大长期存在边境与势力争夺，对乌拉尔图也保持警惕。
enemies.en: The Neo-Assyrian Empire was the foremost enemy; long-standing rivalry with Israel and Judah continued over borders and influence, while Urartu was also watched cautiously.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交策略以生存为目标，在纳贡、拖延、结盟与边境施压之间反复调整，试图保住大马士革作为内陆强国和商路中心的地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was survival-oriented, shifting between tribute, delay, alliance-building, and frontier pressure in an attempt to preserve Damascus as an inland power and caravan hub.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制武器与工具、字母文字行政书写、灌溉与绿洲农业管理、复合弓作战、车马军事动员及区域商路组织
keyInnovations.en: Iron weapons and tools, alphabetic administrative writing, irrigation and oasis agriculture management, composite-bow warfare, chariot-horse military mobilization, and regional caravan-route organization

strengths.zh: 大马士革地理位置优越，控制内陆商路与绿洲农业；亚兰语和城市网络增强了行政与贸易联通；王国仍保有传统军事实力与区域影响力。
strengths.en: Damascus held an advantageous position over inland caravan routes and oasis agriculture; Aramaic language use and urban networks strengthened administrative and commercial connectivity; the kingdom still retained traditional military strength and regional influence.

weaknesses.zh: 王权衰落、附庸控制力下降、财政赤字扩大，且军事体制难以匹敌亚述的攻城与常备军优势；长期战争损害了人口与贸易安全。
weaknesses.en: Kingship was weakening, control over dependents was declining, fiscal deficits were widening, and the military system could not match Assyria’s siege capacity and standing-army superiority; long warfare damaged population security and trade.

outlook.zh: 到公元前800年前后，王国虽仍是黎凡特重要强权，但整体已进入下行阶段。若无法建立稳定反亚述联盟或完成军政改革，其独立性很可能在未来数十年内被亚述逐步削弱并最终终结。
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, the kingdom remained a major Levantine power but was clearly in decline. Without a stable anti-Assyrian coalition or meaningful military-administrative reform, its independence was likely to be progressively eroded and ultimately ended by Assyria within the coming decades.

description.zh: 黎凡特內陸的重要阿蘭王國，正遭受亞述軍事壓力。
description.en: A major inland Aramean kingdom in the Levant under growing Assyrian military pressure.

ruler.zh: 约阿施晚期或耶罗波安二世早期
ruler.en: Late Joash or early Jeroboam II

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制，辅以宫廷官僚、地方长老与军事总管
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy supported by court officials, local elders, and military commanders

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、祭司与先知、军事阶层、城市自由民、乡村农民、工匠、商人及奴仆构成；支派认同仍存，但国家行政与土地财富的重要性已高于早期松散支派结构。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobility, priests and prophets, military elites, urban freemen, rural farmers, artisans, merchants, and slaves or bonded dependents; tribal identities remained, but state administration and landed wealth had become more important than the earlier loose tribal structure.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、宫廷贵族、军队将领、地主阶层以及控制主要圣所的祭司集团
rulingClass.en: The royal family, court nobles, army commanders, major landholders, and priestly groups controlling key sanctuaries

succession.zh: 王位通常按王朝世袭传承，但受宫廷政变、军事支持与亚述等强权压力影响，继承并不绝对稳定。
succession.en: The throne generally passed hereditarily within the dynasty, though succession was not fully secure and depended on palace politics, military backing, and pressure from great powers such as Assyria.

structure.zh: 中央政权以国王与王宫为核心，下设掌管财政、军务、贡赋、文书与王室产业的官员；地方由城邑长官、长老与地区军事首领共同治理。
structure.en: The central state was centered on the king and palace, with officials overseeing finance, military affairs, tribute, records, and royal estates; local administration was shared by town governors, elders, and regional military leaders.

function.zh: 负责文书、诏令、外交通信、档案与税赋记录
function.en: Handled documents, royal decrees, diplomatic correspondence, archives, and tax records

function.zh: 征收谷物、油、酒、牲畜与贵金属贡赋，并管理国库
function.en: Collected tribute in grain, oil, wine, livestock, and precious metals, and managed the treasury

function.zh: 管理王室土地、仓储、工坊、建筑工程与劳役征发
function.en: Administered royal lands, storage facilities, workshops, building projects, and labor levies

function.zh: 组织战车、步兵、边防驻军、征召民兵与防御工事
function.en: Organized chariots, infantry, frontier garrisons, militia levies, and defensive works

function.zh: 监督各城邑长官、地方司法、粮仓与交通节点
function.en: Supervised city governors, local justice, granaries, and transport nodes

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托设防城邑网络进行。王室任命或认可城邑长官，地方长老在司法、土地与社群事务上仍有重要作用。乡村地带以宗族和村社为基层单位，向附近行政中心输送赋税与劳役。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through a network of fortified towns. City governors were appointed or recognized by the crown, while local elders retained important roles in justice, land matters, and communal affairs. Rural areas were organized through clans and villages that delivered taxes and labor to nearby administrative centers.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合王室裁断、习惯法、盟约传统与宗教规范。城门长老、地方官与祭司共同参与裁判，重大案件可上诉至王室。财产、债务、婚姻、伤害赔偿与宗教违法均受规制。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal judgment, customary law, covenantal tradition, and religious norms. Elders at the gate, local officials, and priests all participated in adjudication, while major cases could be referred to the crown. Property, debt, marriage, injury compensation, and religious offenses were all regulated.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以实物税和劳役为主，包括谷物、橄榄油、葡萄酒、羊毛、牲畜及地方贡赋；商路关税和战利品分配亦为财政来源。王室对战略城邑及肥沃地区征收较重。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based mainly on payments in kind and labor service, including grain, olive oil, wine, wool, livestock, and provincial tribute; customs dues on trade routes and distribution of war booty also contributed revenue. The crown taxed strategic towns and fertile districts more heavily.

religion.zh: 官方与民间宗教并存。以色列社会以敬奉雅威为核心，但在伯特利、但等圣所进行国家祭祀，同时也存在对巴力、亚舍拉等地方神祇或象征物的混合性崇拜。先知传统持续批评宗教混杂与社会不公。
religion.en: Official and popular religion coexisted. Israelite society centered on the worship of Yahweh, with state cult activity at sanctuaries such as Bethel and Dan, while mixed practices involving Baal, Asherah, and other local deities or symbols also persisted. The prophetic tradition continued to criticize religious syncretism and social injustice.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成后世意义上的系统哲学，思想核心体现为盟约伦理、王权合法性、先知训诫、家族责任与神意历史观。
philosophy.en: There was not yet a systematic philosophy in the later sense; the intellectual core consisted of covenantal ethics, royal legitimacy, prophetic admonition, family obligation, and a view of history governed by divine will.

writingSystem.zh: 使用早期希伯来字母文字，属于西北闪米特字母系统，主要用于行政刻写、陶片记账、铭文与宗教传统的书面保存。
writingSystem.en: An early Hebrew alphabetic script was used, part of the Northwest Semitic alphabetic tradition, mainly for administration, ostraca accounting, inscriptions, and the written preservation of religious traditions.

culturalAchievements.zh: 铁器时代的城防建筑、石砌宫殿与仓储体系、象牙与金属工艺、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产组织、先知文学传统的形成，以及对希伯来宗教记忆与王国史叙事的早期整理，都是这一时期的重要成就。
culturalAchievements.en: Important achievements of the period included Iron Age fortification architecture, stone-built palaces and storage complexes, ivory and metal craftsmanship, organized wine and olive-oil production, the development of prophetic literary tradition, and early compilation of Hebrew religious memory and royal-historical narratives.

languageFamily.zh: 西北闪米特语族，主要语言为古希伯来语，并与亚兰语、腓尼基语保持密切接触
languageFamily.en: Northwest Semitic; the main language was Ancient Hebrew, in close contact with Aramaic and Phoenician

gdpDescription.zh: 王国经济以农业为基础，辅以畜牧、地区贸易、手工业和对交通节点的控制。与同时期大帝国相比规模有限，但在黎凡特内陆具有可观生产能力，特别是橄榄油、葡萄酒和谷物剩余可支持宫廷、军队与城市建筑。
gdpDescription.en: The kingdom's economy was primarily agrarian, supplemented by pastoralism, regional trade, crafts, and control of transport corridors. Its scale was modest compared with the great empires, but it had significant productive capacity within the inland Levant, especially in olive oil, wine, and grain surpluses that supported the court, military, and urban construction.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄油压榨、葡萄酒酿造、牧羊与畜牧、纺织、陶器、金属加工、建筑石料与象牙装饰工艺
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, olive-oil pressing, wine production, sheep herding and livestock raising, textiles, pottery, metalworking, building stone, and ivory decorative craftsmanship

tradeGoods.zh: 出口橄榄油、葡萄酒、羊毛、亚麻制品、陶器及部分金属制品；进口木材、奢侈品、战车相关材料、香料、贵金属和高级手工品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included olive oil, wine, wool, linen goods, pottery, and some metal products; imports included timber, luxury goods, chariot-related materials, aromatics, precious metals, and fine crafted items.

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭拥有少量土地、果树、牲畜与储粮，但财富分布明显不均。贵族与大地主通过兼并土地、债务控制和王室关系积累显著财富，城市精英在奢侈消费方面尤为突出。
householdWealth.en: Most households possessed small plots, orchard rights, livestock, and stored grain, but wealth was distributed unevenly. Nobles and major landholders accumulated considerable wealth through land concentration, debt control, and royal patronage, while urban elites were especially visible in luxury consumption.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业村社为基础、由王室再分配与市场交换并存的混合经济。实物租税、劳役、王室仓储和商旅贸易共同维持国家运转。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agrarian village communities, with both royal redistribution and market exchange. In-kind taxes, labor service, royal storage, and merchant trade all helped sustain the state.

source.en: Agricultural taxes in kind and land dues

source.zh: 地方贡赋与王室产业收入
source.en: Provincial tribute and royal estate income
Customs, trade dues, and transit tolls

source.zh: 战利品、罚金与附属收入

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以贵金属、奢侈品、仓储粮油和可动员牲畜共同构成。其规模足以应对一般边境冲突和宫廷开支，但若面对长期对外纳贡或大规模入侵，将迅速承压。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metals, luxury goods, stored grain and oil, and mobilizable livestock. It was sufficient for ordinary frontier conflict and court expenses, but would come under severe pressure if faced with prolonged tribute payments or major invasion.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体稳健，依赖农业剩余和王室控制节点获取收入。稳定时期会积累仓储与贵金属储备，但在对外强权压力上升时，财政往往转向征收更重贡赋与紧急动员。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly conservative, relying on agricultural surpluses and crown control of key nodes for revenue. In stable years the state accumulated stores and precious-metal reserves, but under growing pressure from foreign powers it tended toward heavier tribute extraction and emergency mobilization.

description.zh: 由长矛兵、持盾兵和部分投射兵组成，是王国防御与攻城行动的主力，平时分驻城防据点与要道。
description.en: Composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, and some missile troops, this was the main force for defense and siege operations, normally distributed among fortified sites and key routes.

description.zh: 规模不大但具有精锐性质，用于机动作战、王室威望展示和关键战场冲击，依赖较高维护成本。
description.en: Small in size but elite in character, it was used for mobile warfare, royal prestige, and decisive battlefield shock, though it required high maintenance costs.

description.zh: 负责守卫亚兰方向、约旦河谷和主要设防城邑，兼管预警、巡逻和补给据点。
description.en: Responsible for guarding the Aramean front, the Jordan Valley, and major fortified towns, while also handling warning, patrol, and supply functions.

description.zh: 战时临时征召，以农村人口为基础，适合守土、防御和补充兵力，但训练与装备参差不齐。
description.en: Raised temporarily in wartime from the rural population, these men were suited to territorial defense and reinforcement, but their training and equipment were uneven.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The King as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军事技术处于黎凡特铁器时代中等偏上水平，使用铁制兵器、复合弓、战车、城墙、城门系统和粮仓支撑的设防网络。尚无亚述式的大规模攻城机器与常备职业化体系。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a moderately advanced Levantine Iron Age level, using iron weapons, composite bows, chariots, walls, gate systems, and granary-supported fortification networks. It did not yet possess the large siege machines or highly professional standing system of Assyria.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自亚述势力的重新介入、北方与东北方的阿兰诸国竞争、与犹大的边界摩擦，以及一旦大国局势变化所产生的附庸化压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from renewed Assyrian involvement, competition with Aramean states to the north and northeast, border friction with Judah, and the danger of being forced into vassal status if great-power conditions shifted.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于撒马利亚山地、耶斯列平原及若干谷地农业区，农村占绝对多数。与沿海商贸城邦相比，人口密度中等，但在内陆王国中已属较具规模。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Samarian highlands, the Jezreel Valley, and several agricultural lowlands, with a strong rural majority. Compared with the commercial cities of the coast, population density was moderate, but for an inland kingdom it was fairly substantial.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、祭司与高级军官；中层为城镇工匠、商人、书记和中等地主；下层为自耕农、佃农、牧民、劳役者与奴仆。债务和土地兼并加剧了阶层差距。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the royal family, nobles, priests, and senior officers; the middle strata consisted of town artisans, merchants, scribes, and middling landholders; the lower strata included small farmers, tenant cultivators, herders, laborers, and slaves. Debt and land concentration widened social inequality.

allies.zh: 没有牢固的大国盟友；在特定时期可能与腓尼基城邦、某些亚兰势力或地方小国保持务实合作，但结盟多属短期与局势性安排。
allies.en: It had no firm great-power allies; at times it could maintain pragmatic cooperation with Phoenician city-states, certain Aramean forces, or local petty states, but alliances were usually short-term and situational.

enemies.zh: 潜在或现实敌对者包括亚述、部分阿兰诸国，以及在特定边境争议中对立的犹大或其他邻邦。
enemies.en: Potential or actual enemies included Assyria, some Aramean states, and, in specific border disputes, Judah or other neighboring polities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持地区自主、平衡周边势力、保护贸易通道和避免过早卷入亚述直接控制为目标。王国倾向于在强邻之间采取灵活现实主义，而非长期意识形态联盟。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve regional autonomy, balance surrounding powers, protect trade routes, and avoid premature absorption into direct Assyrian control. The kingdom tended toward flexible realism among stronger neighbors rather than long-term ideological alliances.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具与兵器的普及、四室民居、压榨设施、设防城门体系、标准化仓储与行政记账、石砌宫殿建筑，以及区域道路与补给节点整合。
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included the spread of iron tools and weapons, four-room houses, pressing installations, fortified gate systems, standardized storage and administrative accounting, stone palace architecture, and the integration of regional roads and supply nodes.

strengths.zh: 王国拥有较稳固的农业基础、相对成熟的城邑网络、可观的油酒产量、有效的地区行政整合，以及在耶户王朝后期逐步恢复的政治稳定。其地理位置使其能参与南北与东西商路交换。
strengths.en: The kingdom possessed a solid agrarian base, a relatively developed network of towns, substantial oil and wine output, effective regional administrative integration, and growing political stability in the later Jehu-dynasty period. Its location allowed participation in north-south and east-west trade exchange.

weaknesses.zh: 国家规模有限，内部存在宗教分裂、社会贫富悬殊和地方精英掣肘；军事虽足以应对邻近冲突，但难与亚述式帝国机器长期抗衡。财政在被迫纳贡时也较脆弱。
weaknesses.en: The state was limited in scale and faced internal religious division, social inequality, and obstruction from local elites; while its military was sufficient for nearby conflicts, it could not long withstand an Assyrian-style imperial machine. Its finances were also vulnerable if heavy tribute were imposed.

outlook.zh: 约公元前800年时，以色列王国处于相对稳定甚至复苏的阶段，具备进一步扩展影响力的条件；但从中长期看，其命运将越来越受亚述西进和黎凡特权力重组支配。若不能有效整合内部并妥善处理对外臣服关系，未来独立性将明显下降。
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, the Kingdom of Israel was in a relatively stable and even recovering phase, with conditions favorable for expanding its influence; yet in the medium to long term its fate would be increasingly shaped by Assyrian westward expansion and the restructuring of Levantine power. Unless it integrated its internal order more effectively and managed foreign submission carefully, its independence would decline markedly.

description.zh: 北方希伯來王國，仍具區域影響力，但深受亞述局勢制約。
description.en: The northern Hebrew kingdom, still regionally significant but increasingly constrained by Assyrian politics.

leader.zh: 约阿施或耶罗波安二世

ideology.zh: 以撒马利亚为中心的中央集权君主制，通过伯特利和但的国家圣所维护王权合法性，推动领土扩张和贸易发展
ideology.en: Centralized monarchy centered on Samaria, maintaining royal legitimacy through state sanctuaries at Bethel and Dan, pursuing territorial expansion and trade development

relationship.zh: 耶户王朝通过军事政变上台，经数代经营已建立稳定统治。国王控制军队、征税系统和国家圣所，积极发展与腓尼基的贸易关系。然而王朝面临先知运动的持续批评、与犹大的边境摩擦以及亚述帝国的日益逼近。
relationship.en: The Jehu dynasty came to power through military coup and after several generations had established stable rule. The king controlled the army, taxation system, and state sanctuaries, actively developing trade relations with Phoenicia. However, the dynasty faced continuous criticism from the prophetic movement, border friction with Judah, and the looming approach of the Assyrian Empire.

description.zh: 耶户王朝在约阿施和即将继位的耶罗波安二世时期正经历复兴。耶户的血腥政变终结了暗利—亚哈家族的统治，但也将北国以色列推入了数十年的政治调整期。到公元前800年前后，王朝已建立起有效的行政体系——撒马利亚的王宫以精美的象牙装饰闻名，米吉多和哈琐的城防设施也经过全面加强。国王通过在伯特利和但设立国家圣所来对抗耶路撒冷的宗教向心力，但这在正统雅威信仰者看来是分裂行为。王室积极发展橄榄油和葡萄酒的出口贸易，与推罗等腓尼基城市保持密切商业往来。耶罗波安二世时代将把以色列推向领土和经济的巅峰，但先知们警告这种繁荣建立在社会不公之上。
description.en: The Jehu dynasty under Joash and the soon-to-succeed Jeroboam II was experiencing revival. Jehu's bloody coup had ended the Omri-Ahab dynasty's rule but also pushed the northern Kingdom of Israel into decades of political adjustment. By around 800 BCE, the dynasty had established an effective administrative system—Samaria's palace was famous for its fine ivory decorations, and fortifications at Megiddo and Hazor had been comprehensively strengthened. The king established state sanctuaries at Bethel and Dan to counter Jerusalem's religious gravity, though orthodox Yahweh worshippers viewed this as divisive. The court actively developed olive oil and wine export trade, maintaining close commercial relations with Phoenician cities like Tyre. The era of Jeroboam II would push Israel to its territorial and economic peak, but prophets warned that this prosperity was built on social injustice.

role.zh: 耶户王朝第四代国王，稳定内政并对外发动有限扩张
role.en: Fourth-generation Jehu dynasty king, stabilizing domestic affairs and launching limited expansion

role.zh: 即将开创以色列最繁荣时代的未来君主
role.en: Future king who would inaugurate Israel's most prosperous era

ideology.zh: 独一的雅威崇拜，反对巴力和亚舍拉等外来神崇拜，呼吁社会正义、保护穷人权利、批判王室的压迫和宗教混杂
ideology.en: Exclusive Yahweh worship, opposing the cults of Baal, Asherah, and other foreign deities, calling for social justice, protecting the rights of the poor, and criticizing royal oppression and religious syncretism

headquarters.zh: 各地先知团体与乡村集会
headquarters.en: Prophetic communities and rural assemblies across the land

relationship.zh: 先知运动与王室之间存在深刻的结构性张力。先知们承认王权但拒绝对国王盲目服从，以“雅威之言”为武器批评王室的宗教混杂政策、土地兼并和对穷人的压迫。以利亚曾公开对抗亚哈和耶洗别的巴力崇拜，以利沙则继续这一传统。先知运动虽无军事力量，但其道德权威能动摇王权的合法性。
relationship.en: The prophetic movement and the royal house maintained a deep structural tension. Prophets acknowledged kingship but refused blind obedience to the king, wielding 'the word of Yahweh' to criticize the court's religious syncretism, land concentration, and oppression of the poor. Elijah had publicly confronted Ahab and Jezebel's Baal worship, and Elisha continued this tradition. Though the prophetic movement lacked military power, its moral authority could shake the legitimacy of kingship.

description.zh: 雅威先知运动是古代以色列最独特的政治—宗教现象之一。先知不是祭司，不依附于固定的神庙体系，而是以“雅威的代言人”身份独立活动。以利亚在迦密山戏剧性地挑战四百五十名巴力先知的故事塑造了先知作为正义卫士的形象。以利沙继承其衣钵，继续在北国行奇迹、传神谕、介入政治。先知运动的核心主张是独一的雅威崇拜——反对与腓尼基传入的巴力和亚舍拉崇拜混合。同时他们强烈批评社会不公：富人兼并穷人的土地、法庭审判不公、债务奴役泛滥。这种社会批判传统将在阿摩司和何西阿等文字先知中达到高峰。先知运动虽然缺乏组织化的政治力量，但其对以色列宗教和道德思想的影响深远，最终塑造了犹太教一神信仰的核心特征。
description.en: The Yahwist prophetic movement was one of the most distinctive politico-religious phenomena in ancient Israel. Prophets were not priests and did not depend on a fixed temple system, but operated independently as 'spokespersons of Yahweh'. The dramatic story of Elijah challenging 450 prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel shaped the image of prophets as champions of righteousness. Elisha inherited his mantle, continuing to perform miracles, deliver oracles, and intervene in politics in the northern kingdom. The movement's core demand was exclusive Yahweh worship—opposing syncretism with the cults of Baal and Asherah introduced through Phoenician influence. They also fiercely criticized social injustice: the rich seizing the poor's land, corrupted courts, and widespread debt slavery. This tradition of social critique would reach its peak in the literary prophets Amos and Hosea. Though the prophetic movement lacked organized political power, its influence on Israelite religious and moral thought was profound, ultimately shaping the core characteristic of monotheism in Judaism.

role.zh: 以利亚的继承者，在北国传播雅威信仰并介入政治
role.en: Successor of Elijah, spreading Yahweh worship and intervening in politics in the northern kingdom

role.zh: 对抗巴力崇拜的传奇先知，先知运动的精神象征
role.en: Legendary prophet who confronted Baal worship, spiritual symbol of the prophetic movement

ideology.zh: 以腓尼基巴力—亚舍拉崇拜取代或融合雅威独尊传统，推行宗教多元共存，借助腓尼基神庙经济模式和国际化祭祀体系提升王权合法性和国家财富
ideology.en: Replacing or syncretizing the Yahwist exclusivist tradition with Phoenician Baal-Asherah worship, promoting religious pluralism, and leveraging the Phoenician temple-economic model and internationalized ritual system to bolster royal legitimacy and national wealth

relationship.zh: 巴力崇拜派系通过推罗公主耶洗别嫁给亚哈王而获得了宫廷层面的强力支持。耶洗别不仅引入巴力和亚舍拉崇拜，还以王后权威供养四百五十名巴力先知和四百名亚舍拉先知，试图在以色列建立腓尼基式的国家宗教体系。这一派系直接威胁了雅威独尊的宗教传统，引发以利亚等先知的殊死对抗——卡密山上的对决成为这场宗教冲突最戏剧性的象征。
relationship.en: The Baal worship faction gained powerful court-level support through the marriage of Tyrian princess Jezebel to King Ahab. Jezebel not only introduced Baal and Asherah worship but used her queenly authority to maintain 450 prophets of Baal and 400 prophets of Asherah, attempting to establish a Phoenician-style state religion in Israel. This faction directly threatened the Yahwist exclusivist tradition, provoking a life-or-death confrontation with prophets like Elijah—the contest on Mount Carmel became the most dramatic symbol of this religious conflict.

description.zh: 巴力崇拜在以色列的扩张是古代近东最引人注目的宗教冲突之一。腓尼基的巴力—美尔卡特是推罗城邦的主神，掌管丰饶、风暴和航海，其崇拜体系伴随着精致的神庙建筑、丰盛的祭祀仪式和国际化的祭司网络。当亚哈王迎娶推罗公主耶洗别时，巴力崇拜获得了前所未有的官方支持——撒马利亚修建了宏伟的巴力神庙，腓尼基祭司涌入以色列，丘坛上的混合祭祀日益盛行。耶洗别以铁腕手段迫害雅威先知，据《列王纪》记载杀害了大量雅威先知，迫使以利亚等人逃亡旷野。巴力崇拜的吸引力不仅在于宗教层面——它代表着与腓尼基地中海贸易网络的深度整合、先进的城市文化和富裕的寺庙经济。对于以色列的都市精英和商人阶层而言，拥抱巴力崇拜意味着融入更广阔的国际体系。然而，雅威独尊的先知们将此视为对西奈盟约的根本性背叛，最终耶户政变将以血腥方式终结暗利家族和巴力祭司的统治。
description.en: The expansion of Baal worship in Israel constituted one of the most dramatic religious conflicts in the ancient Near East. The Phoenician Baal-Melqart was the patron deity of Tyre, governing fertility, storms, and seafaring, with a worship system accompanied by elaborate temple architecture, lavish ritual ceremonies, and an internationalized priestly network. When King Ahab married the Tyrian princess Jezebel, Baal worship gained unprecedented official support—a magnificent Baal temple was built at Samaria, Phoenician priests flooded into Israel, and syncretic worship at high places became increasingly prevalent. Jezebel persecuted Yahwist prophets with an iron hand; according to the Book of Kings, she killed many prophets of Yahweh, forcing Elijah and others to flee into the wilderness. The appeal of Baal worship was not merely religious—it represented deep integration with the Phoenician Mediterranean trade network, advanced urban culture, and wealthy temple economies. For Israel's urban elite and merchant class, embracing Baal worship meant joining a broader international system. However, the Yahwist exclusivist prophets viewed this as a fundamental betrayal of the Sinai covenant, and ultimately Jehu's coup would violently end the rule of the Omri family and the Baal priests.

role.zh: 推罗公主，巴力崇拜在以色列的最高推动者，以利亚的头号敌人
role.en: Tyrian princess, supreme promoter of Baal worship in Israel, Elijah's arch-nemesis

role.zh: 撒马利亚巴力神庙的最高祭司，主持巴力祭祀仪式
role.en: Chief priest of the Baal temple at Samaria, presiding over Baal worship rituals

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制，以耶路撒冷宫廷、圣殿祭司体系与地方长老共同维持统治
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy sustained by the Jerusalem royal court, the Temple priesthood, and local elders

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、祭司与利未人、军官与书吏、城市工匠与商人、乡村自由农、佃户、债役者及少量奴仆构成；宗族与氏族纽带依然重要
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobles, priests and Levites, officers and scribes, urban artisans and merchants, rural free farmers, tenant cultivators, debt-servants, and a small number of slaves; kinship and clan ties remained important

rulingClass.zh: 大卫家王室、耶路撒冷贵族、圣殿高级祭司、地方氏族首领
rulingClass.en: The Davidic royal house, Jerusalem nobility, senior Temple priests, and leading clan chiefs in the countryside

succession.zh: 父死子继的王朝世袭为主，但需宫廷、祭司与地方精英承认；摄政与共治在政治过渡期可能出现
succession.en: Primarily dynastic father-to-son succession, though acceptance by court, priesthood, and local elites was important; regencies and co-rule could occur during political transitions

structure.zh: 中央政府规模不大，围绕国王、王室家政、书记官体系、圣殿机构与地方城邑长老运转；国家控制力在耶路撒冷及其周边最强，在丘陵乡村多依赖宗族领袖与地方官吏
structure.en: The central government was modest in size, organized around the king, royal household administration, scribal offices, Temple institutions, and local town elders; state control was strongest in Jerusalem and its environs, while the hill-country countryside relied more on clan leaders and local officials

function.zh: 辅佐国王处理政务、外交、王室地产与日常行政
function.en: Assisted the king in governance, diplomacy, royal estates, and daily administration

function.zh: 负责文书、征税记录、敕令传达、契约与仓储清册
function.en: Managed documents, tax records, royal orders, contracts, and storage inventories

function.zh: 主持献祭、管理圣殿财物、宗教节期与部分司法裁断
function.en: Oversaw sacrifices, Temple property, religious festivals, and certain judicial decisions

function.zh: 征收谷物、油、酒与畜产，并管理再分配与军需储备
function.en: Collected grain, oil, wine, and livestock products, and managed redistribution and military reserves

name.zh: 地方城门司法与长老议事机构
name.en: Local Gate Courts and Council of Elders

function.zh: 处理地方争讼、地产边界、债务与婚姻继承事务
function.en: Handled local disputes, land boundaries, debts, and marriage and inheritance matters

localAdmin.zh: 地方以设防城镇、乡村聚落与宗族网络为基础，由城邑长老、地方官、军队指挥者和税赋管理员共同管理；王权通过驻军、仓储中心和道路传令维系统合
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on fortified towns, village settlements, and clan networks, managed jointly by town elders, local officials, military commanders, and tax overseers; royal authority was maintained through garrisons, storage centers, and road-based communication

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断与雅威信仰传统为基础，反映早期以色列—犹大法制；城门审判、长老仲裁、祭司解释与王室干预并存
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, royal judgment, and Yahwistic religious tradition, reflecting the early Israelite-Judahite legal order; gate adjudication, elder arbitration, priestly interpretation, and royal intervention coexisted

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以实物征收为主，包括谷物、葡萄酒、橄榄油、畜产、劳役与战时特别贡赋；圣殿也收取献祭相关供物与宗教性奉献
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily in kind, including grain, wine, olive oil, livestock products, labor service, and special wartime levies; the Temple also received sacrificial dues and religious offerings

religion.zh: 国家核心信仰为对雅威的崇拜，以耶路撒冷圣殿为象征中心；但地方高处、家族祭坛以及与周边迦南传统相关的混合宗教实践仍然存在
religion.en: The core state religion was the worship of Yahweh, symbolically centered on the Jerusalem Temple; however, local high places, family shrines, and mixed practices linked to surrounding Canaanite traditions still persisted

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成后世意义上的系统哲学，思想以圣约、王权合法性、祖先传统、智慧箴言与伦理训诫为主
philosophy.en: There was not yet a formal philosophical system in the later sense; thought centered on covenant, royal legitimacy, ancestral tradition, wisdom sayings, and moral instruction

writingSystem.zh: 使用早期希伯来文字，属西北闪米特字母体系，主要见于行政标记、铭文、短文书和宗教用途
writingSystem.en: An early Hebrew script was used, belonging to the Northwest Semitic alphabetic tradition, mainly appearing in administrative marks, inscriptions, short documents, and religious contexts

culturalAchievements.zh: 犹大文化成就在于耶路撒冷作为宗教与王权中心的巩固、圣殿礼仪传统的延续、早期希伯来书写的使用、山地农业聚落网络的发展，以及大卫王朝记忆对集体身份的塑造
culturalAchievements.en: Judah’s cultural achievements included the consolidation of Jerusalem as a religious and royal center, the continuation of Temple ritual tradition, the use of early Hebrew writing, the development of a hill-country agricultural settlement network, and the shaping of collective identity through the memory of the Davidic house

languageFamily.zh: 西北闪米特语族，主要语言为希伯来语，并与亚兰语、腓尼基语等近缘语言接触
languageFamily.en: Northwest Semitic language family, with Hebrew as the main language and close contact with Aramaic and Phoenician

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性农业为主，辅以葡萄酒、橄榄油、牧业、地方手工业和区域贸易。与大帝国相比规模很小，但对一个山地小国而言相对稳定。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on subsistence agriculture, supplemented by wine, olive oil, pastoralism, local crafts, and regional trade. It was very small compared with great empires, but relatively stable for a small hill-country kingdom.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄栽培、橄榄种植、牧羊与牧山羊、陶器制作、纺织、石工、金属小规模加工
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, viticulture, olive cultivation, sheep and goat herding, pottery production, textiles, stoneworking, and small-scale metalworking

tradeGoods.zh: 橄榄油、葡萄酒、谷物、羊毛、皮革、陶器、香料转运品、铜铁制工具、牲畜
tradeGoods.en: Olive oil, wine, grain, wool, leather, pottery, transported aromatics, copper and iron tools, and livestock

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现在耕地使用权、梯田、葡萄园、橄榄树、蓄群、陶罐储粮和家族劳动力，而非大量贵金属
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was embodied in rights to cultivated land, terraces, vineyards, olive trees, herds, stored grain in jars, and family labor rather than large holdings of precious metal

economicSystem.zh: 以家户农业和宗族土地控制为基础的再分配型经济，王室与圣殿通过贡赋、仓储和劳役调动资源，市场交换存在但规模有限
economicSystem.en: A redistributive economy grounded in household agriculture and clan-based landholding, in which the crown and Temple mobilized resources through tribute, storage, and labor service; market exchange existed but remained limited in scale

source.zh: 农业实物税与王室贡赋
source.en: Agricultural taxes in kind and royal tribute

source.zh: 劳役折算与王室地产收入
source.en: Corvée-equivalent value and royal estate income

source.zh: 贸易关税与道路关卡收入
圣殿奉献与祭献相关收入
战利品、罚金与杂项收入

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模有限，主要由贵金属储备、圣殿珍藏、仓储粮油和可迅速征发的实物资源构成；在丰年尚可积蓄，遇到对外纳贡或大战时容易吃紧
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited in scale, consisting mainly of precious-metal reserves, Temple valuables, stored grain and oil, and in-kind resources that could be rapidly requisitioned; it could accumulate in good years but came under strain when external tribute or major war arose

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视粮仓储备、实物税稳定征收和避免过度压榨乡村人口；在王权稳固时期倾向小幅结余以备战乱与歉收
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, emphasizing granary reserves, stable collection of taxes in kind, and avoidance of excessive pressure on the rural population; in stable periods the crown preferred modest surpluses to prepare for war and poor harvests

description.zh: 驻守耶路撒冷与王宫、圣殿周边的核心武装，承担王室保卫与紧急调度任务
description.en: The core force stationed in Jerusalem and around the palace and Temple, responsible for royal security and rapid response

description.zh: 由各宗族与城镇征召的主力，装备长矛、盾牌、短剑、弓箭，战时集结后作山地防御与城防作战
description.en: The main force drawn from clans and towns, equipped with spears, shields, short swords, and bows, assembled in wartime for hill-country defense and garrison warfare

description.zh: 部署于南部、谢非拉与交通节点的守备兵，负责警戒袭扰、保护仓储与控制道路
description.en: Troops deployed in the south, the Shephelah, and key routes to watch for raids, protect storehouses, and control roads

description.zh: 规模较小，主要承担侦察、传令和有限机动任务，非大规模战车兵种
description.en: Small in size, chiefly used for scouting, dispatch carrying, and limited mobility rather than as a large chariot arm

commanderInChief.zh: 犹大王
commanderInChief.en: King of Judah

technology.zh: 处于铁器时代早期至中期，使用铁制工具与部分铁兵器，但青铜仍未完全消失；作战以步兵、城防工事、弓箭和近战武器为主，战车作用有限
technology.en: The kingdom stood in the early to middle Iron Age, using iron tools and some iron weapons though bronze had not disappeared; warfare relied mainly on infantry, fortifications, bows, and close-combat weapons, with only limited use of chariots

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方以色列王国的边境紧张、非利士沿海势力、以东与阿拉伯部族袭扰，以及更远方正在扩张的新亚述帝国未来可能施加的压力
threats.en: Main threats came from border tensions with the northern Kingdom of Israel, Philistine coastal powers, Edomite and Arabian tribal raiding, and the longer-term pressure that the expanding Neo-Assyrian Empire might eventually exert

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于犹大山地、耶路撒冷周边、谢非拉部分城镇与南部边缘地带；整体人口不大，但比更早的定居阶段更集中且政治整合度较高
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly in the Judean hill country, around Jerusalem, in parts of the Shephelah, and in the southern fringe; overall numbers were modest, but settlement was more concentrated and politically integrated than in earlier stages

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族与高级祭司；中层为军官、书吏、地方长老、商人和熟练工匠；底层为多数农民、牧人、佃户、负债劳工与家内奴仆
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal family, nobles, and senior priests; the middle included officers, scribes, local elders, merchants, and skilled artisans; the lower consisted of most farmers, shepherds, tenants, debt laborers, and household slaves

allies.zh: 无稳定的大国盟友；在特定时期可能与地方势力、阿拉伯群体或反以色列派系形成暂时合作
allies.en: No stable great-power allies; at times Judah could form temporary cooperation with local powers, Arabian groups, or factions opposed to Israel

enemies.zh: 主要对手为以色列王国及若干边境敌对群体；同时需警惕非利士、以东及地区霸权势力的介入
enemies.en: Its main rival was the Kingdom of Israel along with certain hostile border groups; Judah also had to watch Philistines, Edom, and interventions by stronger regional powers

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以生存与平衡为核心，优先巩固南方山地腹地、维持贸易通道、避免与更强帝国正面冲突，并在邻近诸国之间灵活周旋
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on survival and balance, prioritizing consolidation of the southern hill-country heartland, maintenance of trade routes, avoidance of direct conflict with stronger empires, and flexible maneuvering among neighboring states

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具与兵器的更广泛使用、山地梯田农业、水窖与储水系统、设防城门与城墙建设、字母文字行政化应用
keyInnovations.en: Wider use of iron agricultural tools and weapons, hill-country terrace farming, cistern and water storage systems, fortified gates and walls, and administrative use of alphabetic writing

strengths.zh: 犹大的优势在于大卫王朝的延续提供了较强的统治合法性，耶路撒冷兼具政治与宗教中心地位，山地地形利于防御，社会整合度在小国中较高，财政与军力虽有限但在稳定时期较为可控
strengths.en: Judah’s strengths lay in the continuity of the Davidic dynasty, which gave relatively strong legitimacy; Jerusalem served as both political and religious center; hill-country terrain favored defense; social cohesion was comparatively high for a small state; and while finances and military power were limited, they were manageable in stable periods

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点是人口和资源有限、城市化程度低、对农业收成高度敏感、常备军规模偏小、边境容易受袭扰，并且面对以色列或新亚述等更强对手时缺乏战略纵深
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited population and resources, low urbanization, high vulnerability to harvest fluctuations, a small standing army, exposure to border raiding, and lack of strategic depth when facing stronger opponents such as Israel or Neo-Assyria

outlook.zh: 在公元前800年前后，犹大前景总体稳定且具韧性，只要王位继承顺畅、边境压力可控、圣殿与宫廷关系维持平衡，便能继续作为一个小而持久的南方王国存在；但未来区域帝国竞争加剧将显著提高其生存成本
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, Judah’s outlook was generally stable and resilient; if succession remained orderly, border pressures stayed manageable, and the Temple-court relationship remained balanced, it could continue as a small but enduring southern kingdom. However, intensifying competition among regional empires would significantly raise the cost of its survival

description.zh: 耶路撒冷為中心的南方王國，規模較小但王朝延續穩定。
description.en: A smaller southern kingdom centered on Jerusalem with a relatively stable dynastic line.

name.zh: 阿拉伯北部與中部部族聯盟
name.en: North and Central Arabian Tribal Confederacies

governmentForm.zh: 以血缘与盟约为基础的部落联盟体制，各部落多保持自治，仅在贸易、牧场争端与对外防御时形成临时性联合
governmentForm.en: A tribal confederative system based on kinship and alliance, with most tribes remaining autonomous and forming temporary coalitions mainly for trade, pasture disputes, and external defense

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族与部落为核心，游牧群体、绿洲定居者、商旅中介与手工业者并存；荣誉、亲属关系、馈赠与报复义务构成社会运作的基础
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on clan, lineage, and tribe, combining nomadic groups, oasis settlers, caravan intermediaries, and artisans; honor, kinship, gift exchange, and obligations of vengeance formed the basis of social life

rulingClass.zh: 部落贵族、显赫家族长老、战士首领与掌控水源和绿洲贸易的家族
rulingClass.en: Tribal aristocrats, prominent lineage elders, war leaders, and families controlling water sources and oasis trade

succession.zh: 酋长通常由显赫家族内部推举产生，继承并非严格长子制，往往取决于年龄、声望、财富、战功与部落协商
succession.en: Chiefs were usually selected from leading lineages; succession was not strictly primogeniture and often depended on age, prestige, wealth, martial reputation, and tribal consensus

structure.zh: 无统一国家官僚机构，各主要部落由酋长、长老会议、战时首领和祭祀人物共同治理；绿洲聚落可能设有管理水井、农地和商路的地方首领
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; major tribes were governed by chiefs, councils of elders, wartime leaders, and ritual figures, while oasis settlements could have local headmen managing wells, fields, and trade routes

function.zh: 调解部族纠纷、认可酋长权威、决定结盟、赔偿与迁徙事项
function.en: Mediated tribal disputes, recognized chiefly authority, and decided alliance, compensation, and migration matters

function.zh: 组织商队护送、征收过路费、分配水源并维持集市秩序
function.en: Organized caravan escorts, collected transit dues, allocated water, and maintained market order

function.zh: 在冲突时期召集战士、分配坐骑与武器并协调防御
function.en: Mobilized warriors in times of conflict, assigned mounts and weapons, and coordinated defense

function.zh: 看护地方圣所、主持誓约、祭礼与神谕活动
function.en: Maintained local sanctuaries and supervised oaths, rituals, and divinatory practices

function.zh: 依据习惯法处理血仇、赔偿、婚约和财产争议
function.en: Handled blood-feuds, compensation, marriage agreements, and property disputes according to customary law

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理高度分散，草原营地由族长管理，绿洲由掌井家族、耕地长和集市头人控制；权力来自亲族支持而非成文任命
localAdmin.en: Local administration was highly decentralized: pasture camps were overseen by lineage heads, while oases were controlled by well-holding families, field supervisors, and market headmen; authority came from kin support rather than written appointment

legalSystem.zh: 法律以口传习惯法为主，核心包括血亲复仇、赎罪赔偿、庇护义务、盟誓约束与财产共享规则；在贸易节点上也存在较稳定的商贸惯例
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily oral customary law, centered on blood vengeance, compensatory payments, obligations of protection, binding oaths, and shared property rules; relatively stable commercial customs also existed at trade nodes

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，收入主要来自绿洲农产分成、商队过路费、市场抽成、贡礼、战利品分配及附庸小群体的定期馈赠
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified tax system; revenues came mainly from shares of oasis produce, caravan tolls, market fees, tribute gifts, distribution of war booty, and regular offerings from dependent groups

religion.zh: 以多神信仰、祖先崇敬、地方圣石、神木、泉井和圣所崇拜为主，不同部落拥有各自守护神，同时也重视誓言与神判
religion.en: Religion was predominantly polytheistic, with ancestor veneration and the worship of sacred stones, trees, springs, and sanctuaries; different tribes had their own patron deities, while oaths and divine judgment were also highly important

philosophy.zh: 尚无系统哲学传统，价值观主要体现为部落荣誉、勇武、慷慨、忠诚、诗歌记忆与对命运及神意的朴素理解
philosophy.en: There was not yet a formal philosophical tradition; core values were tribal honor, bravery, generosity, loyalty, poetic memory, and a practical understanding of fate and divine will

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯内陆多数部落仍以口述传统为主，书写使用有限；在北阿拉伯与商路接触地带，可能接触并零星采用原始字母性记写方式或借用邻近闪米特书写传统
writingSystem.en: Most tribes in the Arabian interior still relied primarily on oral tradition, with limited writing; in northern Arabia and trade-contact zones, early alphabetic notation or borrowed neighboring Semitic writing practices may have been used sporadically

culturalAchievements.zh: 口头诗歌、谱系记忆、沙漠导航知识、井泉管理、驯养骆驼与长距离商旅组织是其主要文化成就
culturalAchievements.en: Its main cultural achievements included oral poetry, genealogical memory, desert navigation knowledge, well and spring management, camel pastoralism, and the organization of long-distance caravan travel

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语族，主要为早期阿拉伯方言，并与阿拉米语及南阿拉伯相关语言圈存在接触
languageFamily.en: Semitic language family, mainly early Arabian dialects, with contact with Aramaic and South Arabian-related linguistic spheres

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总体规模不大但适应性很强，以游牧畜产、绿洲农业和商道中介收入为支柱。财富分布极不均衡，控制水源、驼群和商队的家族明显更富裕
gdpDescription.en: The economy was not large overall but was highly adaptive, resting on pastoral livestock, oasis agriculture, and intermediary income from trade routes. Wealth was very unevenly distributed, with families controlling water, camel herds, and caravans being much richer

mainIndustries.zh: 骆驼与羊山羊牧养、椰枣和谷物种植、皮革加工、驮运服务、香料与乳香转运、盐与铜的小规模交换
mainIndustries.en: Camel and sheep-goat herding, date and grain cultivation, leatherworking, transport services, transshipment of aromatics and frankincense, and small-scale exchange of salt and copper

tradeGoods.zh: 骆驼、羊毛、皮革、奶制品、椰枣、盐、乳香、没药、铜制品、外来纺织品、酒、珠饰与武器
tradeGoods.en: Camels, wool, leather, dairy products, dates, salt, frankincense, myrrh, copper goods, imported textiles, wine, beads, and weapons

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以牲畜、武器、帐幕、奴仆、井泉使用权和亲族关系体现，而非铸币储蓄
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through livestock, weapons, tents, servants, access rights to wells, and kin connections rather than coined savings

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以部落共享、家族控制与市场交换并存的混合经济为主，生产更多围绕生计保障和互惠关系，而非持续的国家性征收
economicSystem.en: The economy was a mixed system of tribal sharing, lineage control, and market exchange, with production oriented more toward subsistence security and reciprocal relations than continuous state extraction

source.zh: 商队过路费与护送收入
市场抽成与交易仲介费

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库，储备分散在各强势部落、绿洲首领和圣所手中，形式包括称量银、牲畜、粮食和奢侈品
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury; reserves were dispersed among powerful tribes, oasis leaders, and sanctuaries in the form of weighed silver, livestock, grain, and prestige goods

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持联盟、分配馈赠和保障商路为核心，征收弹性很大，重在维系人际忠诚而非建立固定预算制度
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on maintaining alliances, distributing gifts, and securing trade routes; extraction was highly flexible and aimed more at preserving personal loyalty than establishing a fixed budgetary system

description.zh: 以长矛、短剑、投枪和圆盾为主，多用于营地防御、劫掠和近距离冲突
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, daggers, javelins, and round shields, they were used for camp defense, raiding, and close-range fighting

description.zh: 擅长长距离机动、侦察、护送商队和沙漠突袭，是阿拉伯内陆最具特色的武装力量
description.en: Skilled in long-range mobility, scouting, caravan escort, and desert raiding, they were the most distinctive armed force of the Arabian interior

description.zh: 由精英战士与酋长随从组成，负责边界巡察、使者护送和快速增援
description.en: Composed of elite warriors and chiefly retainers, they handled frontier patrols, escort of envoys, and rapid reinforcement

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各部落战时首领协商统兵
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; wartime leadership was coordinated among tribal war chiefs

technology.zh: 处于铁器时代早期至中期水平，常见铁制矛头、刀剑、皮甲、木盾与简易复合或单体弓；依赖地形认知与补水点控制胜于攻城器械
technology.en: Military technology was at an early to middle Iron Age level, with iron spearheads, blades, leather armor, wooden shields, and simple composite or self bows; success depended more on terrain knowledge and control of water points than on siege equipment

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部落间争夺牧场与水源、北方边缘地带受亚述势力波及的压力，以及商路控制权竞争
threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal competition over pasture and water, pressure in northern fringe zones affected by Assyrian power, and competition for control of caravan routes

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于阿拉伯半岛内陆草原、沙漠边缘和绿洲地带，绝大多数为流动性较高的牧民与半定居群体，定居聚落规模普遍较小
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Arabian Peninsula’s interior steppe, desert margins, and oasis zones; the great majority were mobile pastoralists and semi-settled groups, and permanent settlements were generally small

name.zh: 亚特里卜早期绿洲聚落

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层包括部落贵族与显族、自由牧民与农民、商旅中介、工匠、依附者与奴仆；但身份受亲族和战功影响较大，界限不如官僚国家固定
socialClasses.en: Social strata included tribal aristocrats and leading lineages, free herders and cultivators, caravan intermediaries, artisans, dependents, and slaves; however, status was strongly shaped by kinship and martial reputation, making boundaries less rigid than in bureaucratic states

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟友体系，不同部落会与北阿拉米商邦、腓尼基商人、红海沿岸社群及邻近绿洲部落建立临时贸易和互保关系
allies.en: There was no lasting unified alliance system; different tribes formed temporary trade and mutual-protection ties with northern Aramaean polities, Phoenician merchants, Red Sea coastal societies, and neighboring oasis tribes

enemies.zh: 敌对关系多为局部性，主要是竞争水源和商道的邻近部落；在北部边缘则可能与受亚述影响的势力发生冲突
enemies.en: Hostilities were usually local, mainly with neighboring tribes competing for water and caravan routes; in the northern fringe there could also be conflict with powers under Assyrian influence

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以务实为主，优先保证商路畅通、维护放牧空间和通过婚姻、盟誓、馈赠与护送服务扩大影响
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic, prioritizing secure trade routes, preservation of grazing space, and the expansion of influence through marriage ties, oaths, gift exchange, and escort services

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制兵器与工具逐渐普及，骆驼驮运体系更加成熟，深井与绿洲灌溉管理经验发展，长距离沙漠导航和商旅组织能力显著增强
keyInnovations.en: Iron weapons and tools were gradually spreading, camel transport systems had become more mature, experience in deep-well and oasis irrigation management was developing, and long-distance desert navigation and caravan organization had significantly improved

strengths.zh: 机动性强，熟悉沙漠环境，社会韧性高，能灵活适应干旱气候并通过商路中介获得外部财富
strengths.en: They possessed strong mobility, deep knowledge of the desert environment, high social resilience, and the ability to adapt to arid conditions while drawing outside wealth through caravan intermediation

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一指挥和稳定征收能力，人口集中度低，难以支撑大型工程、常备军或长期对外扩张
weaknesses.en: They were politically highly fragmented, lacked unified command and stable extraction capacity, and had low population concentration, making it difficult to sustain major public works, large standing forces, or prolonged external expansion

outlook.zh: 在公元前800年前后总体保持稳定，只要绿洲与商路未遭严重破坏，部落联盟将继续存在并在近东贸易网络中发挥中介作用；但若北方帝国势力深入或区域气候恶化，内部整合与冲突都可能加剧
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE they were broadly stable; as long as oases and caravan routes were not severely disrupted, tribal confederacies would continue to exist and play an intermediary role in Near Eastern trade networks. However, deeper intervention by northern empires or worsening regional climate could intensify both internal consolidation and conflict

description.zh: 阿拉伯半島內陸由多個遊牧與綠洲部族聯盟構成，活躍於商路間。
description.en: The Arabian interior was inhabited by nomadic and oasis-based tribal confederacies active along caravan routes.

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心的区域性君主制，结合宫廷、神庙与地方贵族网络
governmentForm.en: A regional monarchy centered on royal authority, combining court, temple, and local noble networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族与军事首领、神庙祭司、书记官与工匠、自由农民、依附民及奴隶构成；山区部族首领在边地治理中仍有重要影响
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, nobles and military leaders, temple priests, scribes and artisans, free farmers, dependents, and slaves; highland tribal chiefs still held important influence in frontier administration

rulingClass.zh: 苏萨宫廷王族、低地大贵族、神庙高级祭司以及掌握军事与地方征收权的显贵家族
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the royal family at Susa, major lowland nobles, senior temple priests, and elite families controlling military and local extraction rights

succession.zh: 以王族世袭为原则，但王位继承常受宫廷派系、宗族关系与地方权贵支持所影响
succession.en: Succession was in principle dynastic and hereditary, but actual accession was often shaped by court factions, kinship ties, and support from regional magnates

structure.zh: 政权以苏萨王廷为中枢，下辖低地农业区、通往扎格罗斯山地的要道城镇及若干附属地区。王室依靠宫廷官员、地方总督、城邑长官与神庙管理体系共同治理。
structure.en: The state was centered on the royal court at Susa, overseeing lowland agricultural districts, key towns on routes into the Zagros highlands, and several subordinate territories. The crown governed through court officials, provincial governors, city administrators, and temple management structures.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保存泥板档案、记录贡赋、条约、土地与劳役事务
function.en: Drafted royal orders, preserved tablet archives, and recorded tribute, treaties, land, and labor matters

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、金属与手工业贡赋，管理仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, metals, and craft tribute, and managed storage and redistribution

function.zh: 协调国家与主要神庙关系，监督祭祀供给、神庙土地与祭司登记
function.en: Coordinated relations between the state and major temples, overseeing cult supplies, temple lands, and priestly registration

function.zh: 管理地方城邑、河道、道路驿传与边地联络
function.en: Managed provincial towns, waterways, roads, courier routes, and frontier communication

function.zh: 组织王家近卫、征召部队、军需、边防与战时动员
function.en: Organized the royal guard, levied troops, supplied the army, maintained frontier defense, and handled wartime mobilization

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以苏萨、安善及若干城镇和灌溉区为核心，由王室任命或认可的总督、城长、税吏和部族中介共同运作；在山地边缘地区，王权常通过盟约与人质制度维持影响。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was centered on Susa, Anshan, and a number of towns and irrigation districts, operating through royal governors or approved local authorities, city heads, tax collectors, and tribal intermediaries; in upland fringe zones, royal influence was often maintained through alliances and hostage arrangements.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法、契约文书和神庙见证为基础。土地、婚姻、债务、劳役与继承多通过泥板文书确认，重大案件由地方官、长老与王廷共同裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on royal command, customary practice, contractual documents, and temple witnessing. Land, marriage, debt, labor, and inheritance were commonly confirmed by tablet documents, while major cases were judged by local officials, elders, and the royal court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以实物征收为主，包括谷物、枣椰、牲畜、纺织品、金属、木材与劳役；附属地区和边地首领则以贡赋、军役和道路供给履行义务。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily in kind, including grain, dates, livestock, textiles, metals, timber, and labor service; subordinate districts and frontier chiefs fulfilled obligations through tribute, military service, and road provisioning.

religion.zh: 埃兰宗教为多神信仰，重视地方神与王权保护神，因舒希纳克在苏萨尤其重要，胡班等神灵亦具崇高地位；祭祀与神庙经济在政治生活中占据核心位置。
religion.en: Elamite religion was polytheistic, emphasizing local deities and royal patron gods; Inshushinak was especially important at Susa, while deities such as Humban also held high status. Cult and temple economy occupied a central place in political life.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成后世意义上的系统哲学，思想主要体现为王权神授、祖先传统、契约伦理、祭祀秩序与对天命和神意的重视。
philosophy.en: There was no systematic philosophy in the later sense; thought was expressed mainly through divine kingship, ancestral tradition, contractual ethics, ritual order, and concern for divine will and fate.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用埃兰语楔形文字记事，并在外交、贸易和接触巴比伦地区时采用阿卡德语楔形书写传统。
writingSystem.en: Elamite cuneiform was the principal writing system for administration, while Akkadian cuneiform conventions were also used in diplomacy, trade, and contacts with Babylonia.

culturalAchievements.zh: 埃兰在苏萨形成悠久的宫廷与神庙文化传统，擅长金属工艺、印章雕刻、彩釉与建筑装饰，并在跨两河与伊朗高原交流中发展出独特的混合艺术风格。
culturalAchievements.en: Elam developed a long-standing court and temple culture at Susa, excelling in metalwork, seal carving, glazing, and architectural decoration, and produced a distinctive hybrid artistic style through exchange between Mesopotamia and the Iranian plateau.

languageFamily.zh: 埃兰语，通常被视为孤立语言
languageFamily.en: Elamite, generally regarded as a language isolate

gdpDescription.zh: 埃兰经济属中等发展水平，以灌溉农业、枣椰园、畜牧、手工业与区域转运贸易为基础。苏萨位于两河平原与伊朗高原之间，使其兼具农业产区与商路节点双重优势。
gdpDescription.en: Elam's economy stood at a moderate level of development, based on irrigated agriculture, date cultivation, pastoralism, craft production, and regional transit trade. Susa's position between Mesopotamia and the Iranian plateau gave it the dual advantage of an agrarian base and a trade-route hub.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、枣椰种植、畜牧、青铜与铁器加工、陶器、纺织、建筑、印章与奢侈品制作
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, date cultivation, pastoralism, bronze and iron working, pottery, textiles, construction, seal carving, and luxury craft production

tradeGoods.zh: 大麦、小麦、枣椰、羊毛织物、牲畜、沥青、木材、铜、锡、青铜制品、铁器、石材、半宝石与奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Barley, wheat, dates, wool textiles, livestock, bitumen, timber, copper, tin, bronze goods, iron tools, stone, semi-precious materials, and slaves

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在粮食储备、牲畜、农具、织物、枣椰园使用权和家族劳动力上；城市精英则拥有土地、仓储、金属器、印章和跨区域贸易联系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly held in grain stores, livestock, tools, textiles, usufruct rights in date groves, and family labor; urban elites held land, storage capacity, metal goods, seals, and long-distance trade connections.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为王室—神庙再分配、地方庄园生产与市场交换并存的混合模式；金属按重量计值，实物支付仍占主导。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a mixed system combining royal-temple redistribution, production on local estates, and market exchange; metals were valued by weight, while payment in kind remained dominant.

source.en: Agricultural taxes and irrigation district levies

source.zh: 战利品、罚金与杂项收入

category.zh: 王室赏赐、外交与应急储备

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由贵金属、谷物储备、牲畜、武器、纺织品与可再分配贡品构成。实际财富并非全部以银储存，而是广泛分散在宫廷仓储、神庙库房与地方节点之中。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metals, grain reserves, livestock, weapons, textiles, and redistributable tribute goods. Much of its real wealth was not stored purely as silver, but dispersed across palace stores, temple treasuries, and provincial depots.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体谨慎，优先维持军备、灌溉和宫廷稳定；在外部压力上升时，王廷会加强对附属地区贡赋和过境贸易的征取。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly cautious, prioritizing military readiness, irrigation, and court stability; when external pressure rose, the crown tended to intensify tribute extraction from subordinate areas and transit trade.

description.zh: 以长矛、盾牌、短剑和弓箭装备的核心地面力量，负责守城、野战和护卫补给线。
description.en: The core land force, equipped with spears, shields, short swords, and bows, used for city defense, field combat, and supply-line protection.

description.zh: 由低地城邑与山地人口提供，擅长远射、伏击和复杂地形作战。
description.en: Drawn from lowland towns and upland populations, skilled in ranged combat, ambush, and fighting in difficult terrain.

description.zh: 主要为贵族与王室精英使用，用于冲击、指挥机动和展示威望，数量有限但政治象征意义强。
description.en: Used mainly by nobles and royal elites for shock, command mobility, and prestige; limited in number but politically significant.

name.zh: 边地征召军与部族辅助兵

description.zh: 来自附属地区和山地首领麾下，适合边境巡防、袭扰和临时增援。
description.en: Raised from subordinate territories and upland chiefs, suitable for frontier patrol, raiding, and temporary reinforcement.

commanderInChief.zh: 胡班-尼卡什一世兼王家统帅
commanderInChief.en: Humban-nikash I as royal commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军队处于铁器时代早期至中期水平，普遍使用青铜与逐渐增多的铁制兵器，拥有战车、复合弓、城防工事和基本攻城手段，但整体不如同时期新亚述军制系统化。
technology.en: The army stood at an early-to-middle Iron Age level, using bronze weapons alongside increasing numbers of iron arms, with chariots, composite bows, fortifications, and basic siege methods, though it was less systematized than the contemporary Neo-Assyrian military.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自亚述扩张、与巴比伦关系波动导致的边境冲突，以及扎格罗斯山地诸部族和地方附属势力可能出现的离心倾向。
threats.en: Primary threats came from Assyrian expansion, border conflict arising from fluctuating relations with Babylonia, and centrifugal tendencies among Zagros tribes and subordinate local powers.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约七十万，主体分布于苏萨平原及其灌溉农业区，另有相当人口居于安善及通往伊朗高原的丘陵、山麓和边地据点。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 700,000, concentrated mainly in the Susiana plain and its irrigated agricultural zones, with substantial populations also in Anshan and in foothill, upland, and frontier settlements leading into the Iranian plateau.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、贵族和高级祭司；中层包括书记官、商人、工匠与城镇自由民；下层为农民、佃附民、劳役人口与奴隶。山地部族成员在国家体系内既可能是盟友、征召兵来源，也可能保持半独立地位。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal family, nobles, and senior priests; the middle layers included scribes, merchants, artisans, and free townspeople; the lower strata comprised peasants, dependent cultivators, laborers, and slaves. Highland tribal groups could function within the state as allies and levy sources while also retaining semi-independent status.

allies.zh: 无持久而制度化的强盟，更多依赖与巴比伦的阶段性合作、与部分扎格罗斯山地首领的盟约，以及对附属地区的宗主关系
allies.en: There were no durable and institutionalized great-power alliances; Elam relied more on periodic cooperation with Babylonia, treaties with some Zagros chiefs, and suzerain ties over subordinate territories

enemies.zh: 亚述是最主要的战略对手；在特定时期，巴比伦也可能因边境、继承或地区控制问题与埃兰发生敌对
enemies.en: Assyria was the principal strategic rival; at certain times Babylonia could also become hostile over borderlands, succession issues, or regional control

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保持西部边境缓冲、阻止亚述过度扩张、维护通往两河与高原商路为核心。埃兰常通过扶植盟友、介入巴比伦事务、支持边地附庸与灵活结盟来维护安全。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving buffer zones on the western frontier, checking excessive Assyrian expansion, and safeguarding trade routes to Mesopotamia and the plateau. Elam often pursued security by backing allies, intervening in Babylonian affairs, supporting frontier clients, and forming flexible alliances.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制工具与兵器的扩大使用、成熟的灌溉农业、楔形文字行政、复合弓、战车运用、砖土台基建筑以及跨地区仓储与再分配管理能力
keyInnovations.en: Expanded use of iron tools and weapons, mature irrigation agriculture, cuneiform administration, composite bows, chariot warfare, mudbrick platform architecture, and cross-regional storage and redistribution management

strengths.zh: 埃兰的优势在于苏萨平原的农业基础、连接两河与伊朗高原的地理位置、深厚的宫廷与神庙传统，以及能够联合低地城邑与山地部族的政治弹性。
strengths.en: Elam's strengths lay in the agricultural base of Susiana, its geographic position linking Mesopotamia and the Iranian plateau, its deep court and temple traditions, and its political flexibility in combining lowland cities with upland tribal forces.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点包括政权整合度有限、地方与部族势力较强、继承与宫廷斗争隐患、军制不及亚述集中高效，以及对商路与边疆缓冲地带的依赖较大。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included limited state integration, strong local and tribal power, potential succession and court conflict, a military system less centralized and efficient than Assyria's, and heavy dependence on trade routes and frontier buffer zones.

outlook.zh: 至公元前800年前后，埃兰总体稳定，仍是近东重要区域强国之一。但随着亚述势力进一步增强，若不能稳固对附属地区和巴比伦方向的影响，其长期安全将面临更大挑战。
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, Elam was broadly stable and remained one of the important regional powers of the Near East. Yet as Assyrian power continued to grow, its long-term security would face greater strain if it failed to secure its subordinate territories and influence toward Babylonia.

description.zh: 伊朗西南古老王國，與巴比倫及亞述保持複雜互動。
description.en: An ancient southwestern Iranian kingdom engaged in complex relations with Babylonia and Assyria.

ruler.zh: 推罗、西顿、比布鲁斯等城之诸王
ruler.en: Kings of Tyre, Sidon, Byblos, and other cities

capital.en: Tyre and other major port cities

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭君主为首、由商人贵族与城邦议事机构辅佐的海上城邦政体
governmentForm.en: Maritime city-state monarchies led by hereditary kings and assisted by merchant aristocracies and civic councils

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、祭司、商人贵族、工匠、船员、农民与奴隶构成；沿海城市居民与海外殖民者联系紧密，商业财富在社会地位中极为重要。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of royal households, priests, merchant aristocrats, artisans, sailors, farmers, and slaves; coastal urban communities were closely tied to overseas settlers, and commercial wealth strongly shaped social status.

rulingClass.zh: 王室家族、神庙高阶祭司以及掌握航运、染料、木材与金属贸易的商人贵族
rulingClass.en: Royal families, high temple priests, and merchant aristocrats controlling shipping, purple dye, timber, and metal trade

succession.zh: 通常为王室世袭，多为父子相承，但在王位争议时，王族支系、贵族与神庙势力亦会影响继承
succession.en: Usually hereditary within royal houses, often from father to son, though collateral royal branches, nobles, and temple interests could influence succession during disputes

structure.zh: 腓尼基并非统一帝国，而是由推罗、西顿、比布鲁斯、阿尔瓦德等自治城邦组成。每城由国王统治，并依赖宫廷书记、神庙机构、港务官员、税吏与商人议事团处理政务。
structure.en: Phoenicia was not a unified empire but a group of autonomous city-states such as Tyre, Sidon, Byblos, and Arwad. Each city was ruled by a king and relied on court scribes, temple institutions, harbor officials, tax collectors, and merchant councils for administration.

function.zh: 管理国王近臣、外交接待、王室地产、宫廷档案与王命传达
function.en: Managed royal attendants, diplomatic receptions, crown estates, palace archives, and the transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 监管港口泊位、船只登记、海关征收、航线安全与船队组织
function.en: Oversaw berths, vessel registration, customs duties, sea-route security, and fleet organization

function.zh: 负责神庙土地、供奉、节庆、祭司编制与部分公共储藏
function.en: Handled temple lands, offerings, festivals, priestly staffs, and part of public storage

function.zh: 征收关税、贡赋、港口费与农业地产收入，并管理仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected customs, tribute, harbor dues, and agricultural estate income, and managed storage and redistribution

function.zh: 监督紫染、木材、金属加工、造船与王室工匠作坊
function.en: Supervised purple dyeing, timber, metalworking, shipbuilding, and royal workshops

function.zh: 统筹城墙守备、舰船武装、民兵征召、军械储备与雇佣兵支付
function.en: Coordinated wall defense, armed ships, militia levies, weapons storage, and mercenary payments

localAdmin.zh: 各城邦直接管理本城、周边耕地、附属村庄与若干海外贸易站；地方治理多依赖城门官、港务官、税吏、神庙管理者与氏族长老。
localAdmin.en: Each city-state directly governed its urban center, nearby farmland, dependent villages, and some overseas trading posts; local administration relied on gate officials, harbor officers, tax collectors, temple managers, and clan elders.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断、商业契约与宗教誓约为基础。贸易、债务、继承、海运风险与奴隶买卖常以书面契约和见证人确认，神庙亦具有仲裁作用。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, royal judgment, commercial contracts, and sacred oaths. Trade, debt, inheritance, maritime risk, and slave sales were often formalized in written contracts and witnessed agreements, while temples also played an arbitral role.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自港口关税、航运费、王室地产、工坊收益、附属地区贡赋及对贸易利润的征收；农业直接税相对次要。
taxationSystem.en: Public revenue came mainly from port customs, shipping fees, crown estates, workshop income, tribute from dependent areas, and levies on trade profits; direct agricultural taxation was comparatively secondary.

religion.zh: 多神信仰，各城有主神与城邦保护神。常见神祇包括巴力、埃尔、阿斯塔特、梅尔卡特、埃什蒙与塔尼特前身性女神传统。神庙在政治、经济与社会生活中居核心地位。
religion.en: Polytheistic religion with patron deities specific to each city. Common gods included Baal, El, Astarte, Melqart, Eshmun, and earlier goddess traditions that would later inform Punic cults such as Tanit. Temples stood at the center of political, economic, and social life.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成如后世希腊式系统哲学，思想核心更偏向实用主义、神意观、契约伦理、航海经验与家族—城邦荣誉。
philosophy.en: They had not yet developed systematic philosophy in the later Greek sense; their intellectual outlook was more pragmatic, centered on divine favor, contractual ethics, seafaring knowledge, and family-city honor.

writingSystem.zh: 使用辅音字母文字，即腓尼基字母，书写方向通常自右向左。其简洁性极利于商业记录与跨区域传播，并深刻影响希腊字母及后世多种字母体系。
writingSystem.en: They used a consonantal alphabet, the Phoenician script, usually written right to left. Its simplicity strongly favored commercial record-keeping and long-distance transmission, and it profoundly influenced the Greek alphabet and many later alphabetic systems.

culturalAchievements.zh: 腓尼基人在字母传播、远洋航海、殖民网络、紫色染料制作、象牙与金属工艺、木雕、神庙建筑和跨文化贸易中成就卓著，是东地中海最重要的中介文明之一。
culturalAchievements.en: Phoenicians excelled in the diffusion of alphabetic writing, long-distance seafaring, colonial networking, purple dye production, ivory and metal craftsmanship, wood carving, temple construction, and intercultural trade, making them one of the foremost intermediary civilizations of the eastern Mediterranean.

languageFamily.zh: 西北闪米特语族中的迦南语支
languageFamily.en: Canaanite branch of the Northwest Semitic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 以800年前后的标准衡量，腓尼基城邦人均经济产出较高，原因在于高度城市化、发达的海运与中转贸易、手工业精品制造以及海外殖民收益。其财富总量虽不及大型陆上帝国，但商业流通能力极强。
gdpDescription.en: By the standards of around 800 BCE, the Phoenician city-states had relatively high per-capita output due to urbanization, advanced maritime and transit trade, luxury manufacturing, and returns from overseas colonies. Their total wealth did not match large land empires, but their commercial circulation was exceptionally strong.

mainIndustries.zh: 远洋贸易、造船、紫色染料生产、木材出口、金属加工、玻璃与珠饰、象牙雕刻、纺织、橄榄油与葡萄酒贸易
mainIndustries.en: Long-distance trade, shipbuilding, purple dye production, timber export, metalworking, glass and bead making, ivory carving, textiles, and the trade in olive oil and wine

tradeGoods.zh: 出口雪松木、紫染织物、金属器、玻璃器、珠饰、香料转口品、象牙工艺品与奢侈纺织品；进口银、铜、锡、谷物、金、马匹、奴隶与异域奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: They exported cedar timber, purple-dyed textiles, metal goods, glassware, beads, spice transshipments, ivory crafts, and luxury fabrics; they imported silver, copper, tin, grain, gold, horses, slaves, and exotic luxury goods.

name.zh: 称量银与实物支付体系
name.en: Weighed Silver and Commodity Payment System

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人与船主家庭财富显著高于普通工匠与农户。富裕家庭可持有仓库、船只、海外股份与贵金属存储，而普通家庭财富多体现为房屋、工具、牲畜与有限银锭。
