gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence agriculture, pastoralism, river-crossing trade, and supplementary war booty; small in scale, but with growth potential due to control of a Tiber transport node

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与橄榄的早期栽培、畜牧、制陶、简易金工、木工、盐与河运交换
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, early vine and olive growing, animal husbandry, pottery, simple metalworking, woodworking, and salt and riverine exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、皮革、陶器、木材、盐、金属器、纺织品与少量输入的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, hides, pottery, timber, salt, metal goods, textiles, and small amounts of imported prestige goods

unitName.en: Head of livestock and bronze weight

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以土地、牲畜、谷物储备、家族劳力和武器装备衡量，贵族家庭拥有更大农地和依附人口
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured chiefly in land, livestock, grain stores, family labor, and arms, with aristocratic households controlling larger estates and dependent populations

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、公地利用、贡赋再分配和有限区域交换为特征的早期宫廷—部族混合经济
economicSystem.en: An early palace-tribal mixed economy characterized by household production, use of public land, tributary redistribution, and limited regional exchange

source.en: Public land and royal domain income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要不是铸币金银，而是谷物、牲畜、青铜器、战利品与可调用劳役；储备规模有限但足以支持短期征战与祭祀
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not mainly coin or bullion, but grain, livestock, bronze objects, booty, and callable labor; reserves were limited but adequate for short campaigns and major rites

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守储备与战时动员，平时依赖实物积累，危机时向氏族与附属社区追加征发
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored conservative reserve-holding and wartime mobilization, relying on in-kind accumulation in normal times and extra levies on clans and dependent communities in crises

description.zh: 由国王近臣和精选武士组成，负责城内警卫、王命执行与战时核心作战
description.en: Composed of close retainers and selected warriors, responsible for urban security, enforcing royal authority, and serving as the core in battle

description.zh: 由有资格持械的自由男性构成，是军队主体，装备以矛、盾、短剑和少量甲具为主
description.en: Drawn from free males able to bear arms, this was the main military body, equipped chiefly with spears, shields, daggers, and limited armor

description.zh: 数量很少，主要由贵族与其随从提供，用于侦察、追击和传令
description.en: Very limited in number, provided mainly by aristocrats and their followers for scouting, pursuit, and dispatch carrying

commanderInChief.zh: 国王罗慕路斯
commanderInChief.en: King Romulus

technology.zh: 处于早期铁器时代，兵器包括铁矛头、青铜与木制盾、短剑与投枪；防御依赖丘陵据点、木土工事和聚落壁垒
technology.en: In the early Iron Age, arms included iron spearheads, bronze-and-wood shields, daggers, and javelins; defense relied on hilltop positions, timber-and-earthworks, and settlement barriers

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边拉丁与萨宾社群的争地冲突、伊特鲁里亚势力扩张、河道控制竞争以及小规模袭扰战
threats.en: Main threats came from land conflicts with neighboring Latin and Sabine communities, Etruscan expansion, competition over river control, and small-scale raiding warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口由罗马丘陵聚落居民、周边农民、牧民、依附人口与少量外来工匠商贩构成；处于吸纳移民与整合邻近社群的增长阶段
populationDescription.en: The population consisted of inhabitants of the Roman hill settlements, nearby farmers, herders, dependents, and a small number of immigrant craftsmen and traders; it was in a growth phase marked by migrant absorption and integration of neighboring groups

name.zh: 雅尼库卢姆周边聚居点

socialClasses.zh: 上层为帕特里基贵族与祭司家族，其次是持地平民与武装自由民，再下为客户、佃耕者、仆役及战俘奴婢
socialClasses.en: At the top stood patrician aristocrats and priestly families, followed by landholding commoners and armed free men, then clients, tenant cultivators, servants, and war-captive slaves

allies.zh: 与部分拉丁与萨宾邻社存在婚盟、祭祀联盟和临时军事合作，但关系流动且不稳定
allies.en: Rome maintained marriage alliances, cultic ties, and temporary military cooperation with some neighboring Latin and Sabine communities, though relations were fluid and unstable

enemies.zh: 潜在敌手包括竞争台伯河贸易与土地的拉丁城镇、萨宾集团以及日益强势的伊特鲁里亚中心
enemies.en: Potential enemies included Latin settlements competing for Tiber trade and land, Sabine groups, and increasingly powerful Etruscan centers

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以巩固河渡控制、吸纳邻近人口、通过联姻和祭祀扩大影响，并在必要时以小规模战争获取土地和威望
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to secure control of the ford, absorb nearby populations, expand influence through intermarriage and cult, and when necessary use small-scale warfare to gain land and prestige

era.zh: 铁器时代早期

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具与兵器的逐步采用、丘陵防御聚落、早期排水与土木工事、制陶与青铜加工技术提升，以及字母书写传统的初步接触
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included gradual adoption of iron tools and weapons, defensible hilltop settlement patterns, early drainage and earthwork construction, improved pottery and bronze working, and initial contact with alphabetic writing traditions

strengths.zh: 地处台伯河交通要冲，具有整合拉丁—萨宾人口的能力；社会组织富于军事动员性，贵族网络和宗教仪式有助于塑造共同体认同
strengths.en: Rome’s strengths lay in its position at a strategic Tiber crossing, its capacity to integrate Latin-Sabine populations, its socially embedded military mobilization, and the role of aristocratic networks and cult in forging communal identity

weaknesses.zh: 规模仍小，制度高度依赖个人权威与氏族妥协；经济基础脆弱，书写与行政能力有限，易受邻近强势中心压制
weaknesses.en: It remained small, with institutions highly dependent on personal authority and clan compromise; its economic base was fragile, writing and administration were limited, and it was vulnerable to stronger neighboring centers

outlook.zh: 若能持续控制台伯河渡口、整合周边村社并吸收伊特鲁里亚与希腊世界技术影响，罗马有望由地方性聚落联盟成长为拉丁地区的重要城邦
outlook.en: If it could continue to control the Tiber ford, integrate surrounding communities, and absorb technological influences from the Etruscan and Greek worlds, Rome could grow from a local settlement league into a major city-state in Latium

description.zh: 台伯河畔的新興小城邦，仍處傳說中的王政初期階段。
description.en: A small emerging city-state on the Tiber, in the legendary early phase of the Roman monarchy.

ruler.zh: 诸城镇首领与部族酋长

capital.zh: 阿尔巴隆加、罗马及其他早期中心
capital.en: Alba Longa, Rome, and other early centers

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族和村落联盟为基础的分散部族酋邦体系
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal chiefdom system based on clans and village leagues

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族和村社为核心，贵族家系、自由农民、依附者与奴隶构成分层；宗教与亲族纽带在政治中极为重要。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on clans, extended families, and village communities, with aristocratic lineages, free farmers, dependents, and slaves forming a layered order; religious and kinship ties were highly important in politics.

rulingClass.zh: 地方贵族氏族首领、武士随从集团与祭司性家长
rulingClass.en: Local aristocratic clan chiefs, warrior retinues, and priestly patriarchs

succession.zh: 通常由氏族内部世袭并结合长老认可，部分城镇首领需经贵族与战士集会拥立
succession.en: Usually hereditary within leading clans with elder approval; in some towns chiefs were affirmed by assemblies of nobles and warriors

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家政府；拉丁人与萨宾人分布于多个山城、村社与圣所联盟之中。政治由家族首领会议、长老会、战士集会和宗教祭祀中心共同维系，部分聚落已出现早期王权或首领职位。
structure.en: There was no unified state government; Latin and Sabine communities were spread across multiple hilltowns, villages, and sanctuary-based leagues. Politics was maintained jointly by councils of household heads, elders, warrior assemblies, and religious cult centers, while some settlements had begun to develop early kingship or chiefship.

function.zh: 裁决氏族争端、认可继承、主持对外关系与重大公共决定
function.en: Settled clan disputes, approved succession, and oversaw diplomacy and major communal decisions

function.zh: 管理祭祀、历法、誓约、节庆与跨部落圣所财物
function.en: Managed rites, calendars, oaths, festivals, and property of inter-tribal sanctuaries

function.zh: 组织战时征召、守卫山寨、分配武器与协调盟友支援
function.en: Organized wartime levies, guarded hillforts, distributed arms, and coordinated allied support

function.zh: 记录耕地、牧场、公共地使用以及实物贡赋与劳役义务
function.en: Tracked arable land, pasture, use of common lands, and obligations in kind and labor

function.zh: 监管交换场所、牲畜交易、谷物储备与危机时期分配
function.en: Oversaw exchange sites, livestock trade, grain storage, and redistribution in times of crisis

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由村社首领、氏族家长和山城统治者执行；圣林、山丘据点和公共集会场是行政与司法中心。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out mainly by village chiefs, clan patriarchs, and hilltown rulers; sacred groves, hilltop strongholds, and communal meeting places served as centers of administration and justice.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、祖先传统、誓约和赔偿原则为基础，尚无成熟成文法；杀伤、盗窃、越界与婚姻纠纷通常由长老和祭司调解。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, ancestral tradition, oaths, and compensation, with no mature written code; homicide, theft, boundary disputes, and marriage conflicts were typically mediated by elders and priests.

taxationSystem.zh: 缺乏统一税制，贡赋多为谷物、牲畜、酒、金属制品和劳役；战时另加军事征发与临时供给。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system; dues were commonly paid in grain, livestock, wine, metal goods, and labor service, with additional wartime levies and emergency provisions.

religion.zh: 以印欧传统多神信仰、祖先崇拜和自然圣所崇拜为主，重视天空神、火、泉水、树林、边界与战争神灵；地方神祇后来构成罗马宗教的早期基础。
religion.en: Religion was primarily an Indo-European polytheistic tradition with ancestor worship and the cult of natural sanctuaries, emphasizing sky gods, fire, springs, groves, boundaries, and war deities; local divinities later formed part of the early basis of Roman religion.

philosophy.zh: 尚无系统哲学，价值观以祖训、荣誉、勇武、敬神、守誓、家族责任和农业节律为核心。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophy; values centered on ancestral custom, honor, martial courage, piety, oath-keeping, family duty, and the rhythms of agriculture.

writingSystem.zh: 书写尚极不普及，本地社会主要依赖口传；在800年前后，部分地区可能开始接触经由伊特鲁里亚人与希腊殖民网络传入的字母书写，但尚未形成广泛行政用途。
writingSystem.en: Writing was still very uncommon, and local society relied mainly on oral transmission; around 800 BCE some areas may have begun to encounter alphabetic writing transmitted through Etruscan and Greek networks, but it had not yet developed broad administrative use.

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要成就包括山丘聚落与防御工事、村社圣所、葬俗与祖先纪念、铁器使用扩展、拉丁节庆传统以及后来被罗马继承的氏族宗教和公共仪式雏形。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included hilltop settlements and defensive works, village sanctuaries, funerary and ancestral commemorative practices, the spread of iron use, Latin festival traditions, and the early forms of clan religion and public ritual later inherited by Rome.

languageFamily.zh: 意大利语族中的拉丁-法利斯坎语支与萨宾等奥斯坎-翁布里亚相关方言群
languageFamily.en: Italic languages, especially the Latino-Faliscan branch and Sabine-related Oscan-Umbrian dialect groups

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性农业和畜牧业为主，市场交换有限但在河谷、渡口和沿海接触点逐渐增长。财富主要体现为土地、牲畜、谷仓储备、武器和婚姻联盟，而非货币积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based chiefly on subsistence agriculture and pastoralism, with limited market exchange that was gradually increasing at river valleys, crossings, and coastal contact points. Wealth was expressed mainly in land, livestock, grain reserves, weapons, and marriage alliances rather than monetary accumulation.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与橄榄早期栽培、养羊养牛养猪、木工、陶器、纺织、铁铜工具制造、建筑与盐业交换
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, early viticulture and olive growing, sheep-, cattle-, and pig-rearing, woodworking, pottery, textiles, iron and bronze toolmaking, construction, and salt exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、羊毛、皮革、陶器、盐、木材、蜂蜜、酒、青铜器与少量铁器；进口品包括精美陶器、饰品与奢侈金属制品。
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, wool, hides, pottery, salt, timber, honey, wine, bronze goods, and limited iron tools; imports included fine ceramics, ornaments, and luxury metal goods.

unitName.en: bronze ingots and head of livestock

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富通常集中于耕地使用权、小型牲畜、农具、储粮与家屋；贵族家庭则拥有更大片土地、战马、武器和依附人口。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth usually consisted of access to fields, small livestock, tools, stored grain, and the dwelling itself; aristocratic households held larger lands, war animals, weapons, and dependent populations.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、氏族共有资源和地方再分配为主的部族经济，辅以集市交换和跨区域礼物贸易。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on household production, clan-managed resources, and local redistribution, supplemented by market exchange and interregional gift trade.

source.en: Labor service and public works obligations

category.en: Chiefly household and allied gift exchange

treasuryDescription.zh: 并不存在统一国库；财富分散储存在首领家产、圣所奉献物、牲畜群和谷仓中。危机时通过氏族动员和宗教中心重新分配资源。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no single state treasury; wealth was dispersed across chiefly households, sanctuary offerings, herds, and grain stores. In crises, resources were redistributed through clan mobilization and cult centers.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持首领威望、祭祀秩序、防务与灾荒储备，政策较为保守，依赖实物征收而非货币税。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining chiefly prestige, ritual order, defense, and famine reserves; policy was conservative and depended on levies in kind rather than monetary taxation.

description.zh: 由自由农民和氏族成员组成，装备长矛、圆盾、短剑与少量铁制兵器，是主要作战力量。
description.en: Composed of free farmers and clan members, armed with spears, round shields, short blades, and limited iron weaponry; this was the main fighting force.

description.zh: 围绕地方首领的精锐战士，装备较好，承担突击、护卫和首领象征性战斗任务。
description.en: Elite warriors attached to local chiefs, better equipped and used for shock action, guard duty, and the symbolic combat role of the chief.

description.zh: 负责山口、瞭望点和牧道巡逻，擅长本地地形中的警戒、伏击和快速集结。
description.en: Responsible for passes, lookout points, and herding tracks, skilled in warning, ambush, and rapid mustering in local terrain.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各部族首领和战时盟主分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; command rested with individual tribal chiefs and temporary wartime leaders

technology.zh: 处于早期铁器时代军事阶段，普遍使用矛、盾、短剑、投枪和青铜盔甲部件；防御依赖山丘聚落、壕沟、木栅和土石工事。
technology.en: Military technology was at an early Iron Age stage, with widespread use of spears, shields, short swords, javelins, and some bronze armor components; defense relied on hilltop settlements, ditches, palisades, and earth-and-stone works.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族冲突、伊特鲁里亚势力扩张、地方资源竞争、牲畜掠夺以及山地与平原通道控制争夺。
threats.en: Main threats came from nearby tribal conflict, Etruscan expansion, competition for local resources, livestock raiding, and struggles over control of routes between hills and plains.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于拉提姆平原、丘陵和亚平宁山前地带的村落、山城与小型聚落中；密度不高，但在渡口、圣所与肥沃农业地带较为集中。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across villages, hilltowns, and small settlements in the Latium plain, hill country, and the foothills of the Apennines; density was modest, but higher near crossings, sanctuaries, and fertile farming zones.

socialClasses.zh: 主要包括贵族氏族、自由农民、牧民、工匠、依附者、战俘奴隶与祭司性家族成员；阶层流动有限，但婚姻、战功和依附关系可改变地位。
socialClasses.en: The main social classes included aristocratic clans, free farmers, pastoralists, artisans, dependents, captive slaves, and members of priestly families; mobility was limited, but marriage, military success, and patronage could alter status.

allies.zh: 与部分拉丁邻邦、婚姻同盟氏族及若干萨宾和地方意大利社群保持松散联盟关系。
allies.en: They maintained loose alliances with some neighboring Latin communities, marriage-linked clans, and certain Sabine and other local Italic groups.

enemies.zh: 与争夺土地、牧场和贸易通道的邻近部族时常发生敌对，尤其需警惕较强的伊特鲁里亚城邦以及拉丁内部竞争者。
enemies.en: They were often hostile to neighboring tribes contesting land, pasture, and trade routes, especially stronger Etruscan centers and rival Latin communities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以地方均势、宗教联会、婚姻结盟、报复袭击与临时军事联盟为主，目标是保护耕地、牧道和圣所影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy relied on local balance-of-power politics, religious leagues, marriage alliances, retaliatory raiding, and temporary military coalitions, aiming to protect farmland, herding routes, and sanctuary influence.

era.zh: 早期铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具和武器逐步普及、山丘聚落工事改进、陶器生产提升、木石混合建筑发展、区域道路与渡口网络形成，以及与伊特鲁里亚和希腊世界接触带来的工艺影响。
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included the gradual spread of iron agricultural tools and weapons, improved hill-settlement fortifications, better pottery production, advances in mixed timber-and-stone construction, the formation of regional track and crossing networks, and craft influences from contact with the Etruscan and Greek worlds.

strengths.zh: 优势在于氏族凝聚力强、地形防御有利、农业与牧业互补、能迅速动员自由战士，并处于未来地中海交流网络的有利位置。
strengths.en: Strengths included strong clan cohesion, defensible terrain, complementary farming and herding, the ability to mobilize free warriors quickly, and a favorable position within emerging Mediterranean exchange networks.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是政治高度分散、缺乏统一财政与常备军、书写和行政能力有限、城镇规模较小，且易受更组织化邻邦影响。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses were extreme political fragmentation, the absence of unified finance and a standing state army, limited writing and administrative capacity, small settlement scale, and vulnerability to more organized neighbors.

outlook.zh: 整体局势稳定，但未来数代将面临整合与竞争并存的局面；若某些中心能整合宗教、军事和贸易资源，拉丁—萨宾世界可能演化为更强的城邦体系，其中罗马具备上升潜力。
outlook.en: The overall situation was stable, but the coming generations would likely see both consolidation and rivalry; if certain centers could unify religious, military, and trade resources, the Latin-Sabine world might evolve into stronger city-state systems, with Rome showing notable potential for ascent.

description.zh: 拉提姆與亞平寧中部由多個拉丁、薩賓與相關族群的小型政體組成。
description.en: Latium and central Italy consisted of many small Latin, Sabine, and related polities.

ruler.zh: 雅典、斯巴达、科林斯及其他城邦的统治者（国王、执政官或贵族领袖）
ruler.en: Rulers of Athens, Sparta, Corinth, and other poleis (kings, magistrates, or aristocratic leaders)

rulerTitle.zh: 巴西琉斯、执政官、普里塔尼斯或贵族长老
rulerTitle.en: Basileus, magistrate, prytanis, or aristocratic elder

dynasty.zh: 多个王族与贵族家系并存

capital.zh: 无统一首都；主要城邦包括雅典、斯巴达、科林斯、底比斯与优卑亚诸城
capital.en: No single capital; major poleis included Athens, Sparta, Corinth, Thebes, and the cities of Euboea

governmentForm.zh: 分散的城邦体制，以君主制残余、贵族寡头制与新兴公民机构并存
governmentForm.en: Decentralized polis system combining remnants of kingship, aristocratic oligarchy, and emerging civic institutions

socialStructure.zh: 社会以公民家户为核心，上有贵族与大地主，中有自由农民、工匠和商人，下有依附民、外来居民与奴隶；不同城邦差异显著
socialStructure.en: Society centered on citizen households, with nobles and large landholders above free farmers, artisans, and merchants, and dependent laborers, resident foreigners, and slaves below; patterns varied notably by polis

rulingClass.zh: 土地贵族、武士精英、氏族长老及掌握祭祀与政务的名门家族
rulingClass.en: Landholding aristocrats, warrior elites, clan elders, and prominent families controlling cult and office

succession.zh: 无统一继承制度；部分城邦保留世袭王位，更多城邦由贵族轮任、选举或氏族协商产生官职
succession.en: No unified succession system; some poleis retained hereditary kingship, while others filled offices through aristocratic rotation, election, or clan negotiation

structure.zh: 希腊政治结构高度分散，各城邦独立治理。多数城邦在公民大会尚未完全成熟前，由贵族议事会、氏族领袖会议和少数执政官共同执政；斯巴达保留双王传统与长老权威，雅典则处于贵族家族主导的早期政制阶段。
structure.en: Greek political organization was highly fragmented, with each polis governing itself. In most city-states, before the full maturation of citizen assemblies, power was shared among aristocratic councils, clan leaders, and a small number of magistrates; Sparta retained dual kingship and elder authority, while Athens remained in an early aristocratic phase.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、战争指挥、裁断重大争议，并象征城邦共同体的合法性
function.en: Oversaw cult, war leadership, major adjudication, and the symbolic legitimacy of the polis

function.zh: 审议政策、协调氏族利益、监督官员并在许多城邦掌握实际决策权
function.en: Debated policy, coordinated clan interests, supervised officials, and in many poleis exercised real decision-making power

function.zh: 管理祭典、圣所财物、神谕往来与宗教土地
function.en: Managed festivals, sanctuary property, oracular contacts, and sacred lands

function.zh: 处理血仇、财产、婚姻、债务与跨氏族争端，多依习惯法与长老裁决
function.en: Handled feuds, property, marriage, debt, and inter-clan disputes, largely through customary law and elder judgment

function.zh: 管理港埠贸易、公共粮储、关税与临时征敛
function.en: Managed harbor trade, public grain storage, customs dues, and extraordinary levies

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以各城邦本身及其周边乡村为单位，乡里、氏族和村社在税赋、征兵与土地纠纷中发挥重要作用；殖民地则通常保留与母邦的宗教和亲族联系，但行政上相对独立。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized around each polis and its surrounding countryside, with villages, clans, and rural communities playing major roles in taxation, levy obligations, and land disputes; colonies typically retained religious and kin ties to mother cities but were relatively autonomous in administration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以口传习惯、宗教禁忌、氏族传统和贵族裁断为基础，成文法尚不普遍；血亲复仇、赔偿、誓言与仲裁是主要司法方式。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on oral custom, religious taboo, clan tradition, and aristocratic judgment, with written law not yet widespread; kin vengeance, compensation, oath-taking, and arbitration were major judicial mechanisms.

taxationSystem.zh: 缺乏统一常设税制。城邦主要依赖王室或公地收入、贡献、战时摊派、港口关税、市场收费、祭祀捐献与贵族承担公共义务。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified permanent tax system. Poleis relied chiefly on royal or public land income, contributions, wartime levies, harbor dues, market fees, cult donations, and public burdens borne by aristocrats.

religion.zh: 希腊多神教占主导，敬奉宙斯、赫拉、雅典娜、阿波罗、阿耳忒弥斯、波塞冬等神明；宗教生活以城邦祭祀、家族祖先崇拜、地方英雄崇拜和泛希腊圣所为中心。
religion.en: Greek polytheism predominated, honoring Zeus, Hera, Athena, Apollo, Artemis, Poseidon, and other deities; religious life centered on polis cults, family ancestral rites, local hero worship, and Panhellenic sanctuaries.

philosophy.zh: 尚处于前哲学阶段，价值观主要由史诗伦理、贵族荣誉、神话传统和审慎政治经验塑造；关于宇宙与秩序的系统哲学尚未形成。
philosophy.en: Thought remained in a pre-philosophical stage, shaped chiefly by epic ethics, aristocratic honor, mythic tradition, and pragmatic political experience; systematic philosophy about cosmos and order had not yet emerged.

writingSystem.zh: 希腊字母正在形成和传播，源自腓尼基字母并加入元音表示；在公元前8世纪主要用于铭文、标记和逐步记录诗歌与献辞。
writingSystem.en: The Greek alphabet was taking shape and spreading, derived from the Phoenician script with the addition of vowel notation; in the 8th century BCE it was used mainly for inscriptions, marks, and the gradual recording of poetry and dedications.

culturalAchievements.zh: 荷马史诗传统成形，几何风格陶器达到高峰，城邦祭典和竞技活动日益重要，神庙建筑从木构和泥砖结构向更持久形式发展，海外殖民促进了希腊共同文化认同的扩展。
culturalAchievements.en: The Homeric epic tradition was taking shape, Geometric pottery reached a high point, civic festivals and athletic contests grew in importance, temple architecture was developing from timber and mudbrick toward more durable forms, and overseas colonization broadened a shared Greek cultural identity.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系希腊语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Hellenic branch

gdpDescription.zh: 经济整体繁荣，建立在小农农业、畜牧、手工业、陶器生产、沿海航运与区域交换之上。虽然尚未形成统一市场，但爱琴海网络和新殖民活动显著扩大了资源流动与财富积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was broadly thriving, based on smallholder agriculture, pastoralism, craft production, pottery manufacture, coastal shipping, and regional exchange. Although no unified market existed, Aegean networks and new colonization significantly expanded resource flows and wealth accumulation.

mainIndustries.zh: 大麦与小麦种植、葡萄与橄榄栽培、牧羊牧山羊、陶器、青铜与铁器加工、造船、渔业、织造
mainIndustries.en: Barley and wheat cultivation, vine and olive growing, sheep and goat herding, pottery, bronze and ironworking, shipbuilding, fishing, and textile production

tradeGoods.zh: 陶器、酒、橄榄油、金属器、纺织品、盐、木材、奴隶、香料与奢侈品转运，以及从外部输入的铜、锡、象牙与精致工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Pottery, wine, olive oil, metal goods, textiles, salt, timber, slaves, re-exported aromatics and luxury items, along with imported copper, tin, ivory, and fine crafted goods

name.zh: 未统一铸币；以金属称量与实物交换为主
name.en: No unified coinage; metal by weight and commodity exchange

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分化明显。多数自由家庭拥有少量土地、牲畜和储粮，贵族家族则控制较大耕地、牧场、武器装备、宴飨资源与贸易联系；沿海城邦的手工业与航海收入高于内陆地区。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was unevenly distributed. Most free households held small plots, livestock, and grain stores, while aristocratic families controlled larger fields, pasture, weaponry, feasting resources, and trade connections; coastal poleis enjoyed stronger craft and maritime incomes than inland regions.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产和地方交换为主的分散经济，辅以贵族再分配、神庙储藏与海上贸易网络；殖民拓展正推动更复杂的跨区域商业联系。
economicSystem.en: A decentralized economy based on household production and local exchange, supplemented by aristocratic redistribution, temple storage, and maritime trade networks; colonial expansion was driving more complex interregional commerce.

source.en: Public lands, royal income, and aristocratic contributions

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备分散存在于各城邦的神庙库藏、贵族掌控的公共仓储、金属储备和粮食积存中，并无统一的泛希腊国库。
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves were dispersed among temple treasuries, public stores controlled by aristocrats, metal reserves, and grain accumulations in individual poleis; there was no unified Panhellenic treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持祭祀、战争能力与公共声望，平时征敛较轻，遇到战争、饥荒或殖民行动时则依赖临时摊派与精英资助。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining cult, war capacity, and civic prestige, with relatively light normal exactions but heavy reliance on temporary levies and elite sponsorship during war, famine, or colonial ventures.

name.zh: 贵族步兵与早期重装战士
name.en: Aristocratic infantry and early heavy warriors

description.zh: 由贵族及较富裕自由民组成，装备长矛、盾牌、头盔与护甲，尚处于重装步兵体系的形成阶段。
description.en: Composed of aristocrats and wealthier freemen, equipped with spears, shields, helmets, and armor, representing an early stage in the development of hoplite warfare.

description.zh: 主要为普通农民和随军民众，使用标枪、短矛、弓或投石器，适合山地与机动作战。
description.en: Drawn mainly from ordinary farmers and camp followers, armed with javelins, short spears, bows, or slings, suitable for rough terrain and mobile fighting.

description.zh: 骑兵规模有限，多为贵族侦察和追击用途；战车在实战中作用已下降，更多保留于仪式和少数精英战斗传统。
description.en: Cavalry remained limited, used mainly by aristocrats for scouting and pursuit; chariots had declined in battlefield importance and survived more in ritual and elite martial tradition.

description.zh: 沿海城邦可征集武装船只和船员，用于护航、海盗袭击、殖民远航和近海战斗，但尚未形成后世大型三列桨舰海军。
description.en: Coastal poleis could muster armed vessels and crews for escort, raiding, colonial voyages, and coastal combat, though they had not yet developed the later large trireme navies.

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦国王、执政官或选定将领分别统军
commanderInChief.en: Each polis was commanded separately by its kings, magistrates, or appointed war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术属于铁器时代早期水平，广泛使用铁制长矛、刀剑、木盾、青铜盔甲部件和小型海船；阵列作战逐步规范化，但尚未完全形成经典重装步兵密集方阵。
technology.en: Military technology was at an early Iron Age level, with widespread use of iron spearheads, swords, wooden shields, bronze armor elements, and small warships; ordered formation fighting was becoming more regular, but the classic dense hoplite phalanx had not yet fully matured.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自城邦间竞争、土地与边界争端、海盗活动、意大利与爱琴海殖民冲突，以及与腓尼基、安纳托利亚沿海势力和巴尔干部族的接触摩擦。
threats.en: Primary threats came from inter-polis rivalry, land and border disputes, piracy, colonial conflicts in Italy and the Aegean, and friction with Phoenician, Anatolian coastal powers, and Balkan tribal groups.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于希腊本土、爱琴海岛屿和部分新殖民据点，聚落规模普遍不大，但多个核心城邦和港口正逐步扩大。人口增长推动了海外殖民、土地竞争与政治组织创新。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across mainland Greece, the Aegean islands, and some new colonial settlements, with most communities still modest in size though several core poleis and ports were expanding. Population growth encouraged overseas colonization, land competition, and political innovation.

socialClasses.zh: 主要包括贵族家族、自由公民农民、手工业者与商人、依附劳动者、外来居民以及奴隶；在斯巴达等地，征服地居民和附属人口的地位尤其重要。
socialClasses.en: Major classes included aristocratic families, free citizen farmers, artisans and merchants, dependent laborers, resident outsiders, and slaves; in places such as Sparta, conquered and subordinate populations were especially important.

allies.zh: 无统一盟友体系；各城邦以亲族、殖民关系、宗教圣所联系和临时军事同盟维持合作，优卑亚诸城、科林斯及部分多利亚城邦之间常有阶段性结盟。
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; poleis cooperated through kinship, colonial ties, religious sanctuary connections, and temporary military leagues, with Euboean cities, Corinth, and some Dorian poleis often forming situational partnerships.

enemies.zh: 无统一国家敌人；主要对手是彼此竞争的希腊城邦、海盗集团，以及在西地中海和爱琴海贸易中发生摩擦的腓尼基势力与地方部族。
enemies.en: There was no single state enemy; principal rivals were competing Greek poleis, pirate groups, and Phoenician interests and local tribes encountered in western Mediterranean and Aegean trade.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护航路、获取耕地与金属资源、建立殖民据点和争夺地区声望为核心。宗教节会与神谕中心也为跨城邦外交提供调停平台。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on securing sea routes, acquiring farmland and metal resources, founding colonial outposts, and competing for regional prestige. Religious festivals and oracular centers also provided platforms for inter-polis diplomacy and mediation.

era.zh: 铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器工具与武器更加普及，希腊字母开始使用，远洋航海与殖民组织能力提升，几何风格陶器工艺成熟，城邦公共空间和早期制度化集会逐渐发展。
keyInnovations.en: Iron tools and weapons became more common, the Greek alphabet began to be used, long-distance seafaring and colonial organization improved, Geometric pottery techniques matured, and civic public spaces and early institutional assemblies developed.

strengths.zh: 地中海航海能力强，区域贸易活跃，文化认同逐渐形成，城邦制度具有灵活性，殖民活动为人口压力和资源短缺提供出口，手工业与陶器生产极具竞争力。
strengths.en: Strong Mediterranean seafaring, active regional trade, a gradually forming shared cultural identity, flexible polis institutions, colonial activity that relieved population pressure and resource scarcity, and highly competitive craft and pottery production.

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分裂，城邦间冲突频繁，农业基础脆弱且受地形限制，法律与行政制度尚不统一，贫富差距和氏族竞争可能引发内部动荡。
weaknesses.en: Highly fragmented politics, frequent inter-polis conflict, a fragile agricultural base constrained by terrain, still uneven legal and administrative development, and social tensions from inequality and clan rivalry.

outlook.zh: 整体前景乐观。若殖民扩张、文字传播和城邦制度继续发展，希腊世界将在未来数世纪形成更成熟的政治共同体、军事组织与文化影响力；但持续的内部竞争也将长期塑造其历史轨迹。
outlook.en: The overall outlook was favorable. If colonial expansion, literacy, and polis institutions continued to develop, the Greek world would form more mature political communities, military organization, and cultural influence over the coming centuries; however, persistent internal rivalry would continue to shape its trajectory.

description.zh: 愛琴海與希臘本土出現眾多城邦，殖民與公民政治傳統正逐步形成。
description.en: The Aegean and Greek mainland contained many emerging city-states as colonization and civic institutions developed.

governmentForm.zh: 由王室、贵族家族与商人寡头共同主导的腓尼基殖民城邦君主制
governmentForm.en: A Phoenician colonial city-state monarchy shaped jointly by the royal house, aristocratic lineages, and mercantile oligarchs

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与建城贵族居上，其下为富裕商人、船主、祭司与书记，再下为工匠、水手、小农、码头工人，以及数量有限的奴仆与依附民；城外与周边则与利比亚-柏柏尔社群保持贸易与婚姻联系。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the royal household and founding aristocrats, followed by wealthy merchants, shipowners, priests, and scribes, then artisans, sailors, small cultivators, and dock workers, with a limited number of servants and dependents; outside the city, trade and marriage links connected Carthage with Libyan-Berber communities.

rulingClass.zh: 泰尔出身的殖民贵族、王室亲族、大神庙祭司集团与控制远洋贸易的富商家族
rulingClass.en: Tyrian colonial aristocrats, royal kin, major temple priesthoods, and wealthy merchant families controlling overseas trade

succession.zh: 早期以王室世袭为原则，但须获得建城贵族、神庙祭司与核心家族的承认，属于半世袭、半协商式继承。
succession.en: Succession was principally dynastic in the early period, but required recognition by founding aristocrats, temple priesthoods, and leading families, making it semi-hereditary and semi-consensual.

structure.zh: 800年前后的迦太基仍处建城初期，政体较为紧凑：女王与宫廷为最高权力中心，下设神庙、港务、仓储、税收、城防与对外贸易管理机构；重要决策常在王室与显贵家族会议中形成。
structure.en: Around 800 BCE, Carthage was still in its formative stage and had a compact political structure: the queen and court formed the supreme authority, beneath which stood temple, harbor, storage, taxation, defense, and external trade administrations; major decisions were often shaped through consultation between the royal house and leading families.

function.zh: 处理女王诏令、宫廷仪礼、贵族协调与建城土地分配
function.en: Handled royal decrees, court ceremony, aristocratic coordination, and allotment of settlement lands

function.zh: 管理巴力与塔尼特前身性质的地方神祇崇拜、祭品、圣产与宗教节庆
function.en: Managed the worship of Baal and local cults ancestral to later Punic religion, along with offerings, sacred property, and religious festivals

function.zh: 监督泊位、船只登记、装卸秩序、航海时令与外来商队接待
function.en: Oversaw berths, vessel registration, loading order, sailing seasons, and reception of foreign merchants

function.zh: 管理粮仓、油酒储备、金属与木材库藏，并负责应急配给
function.en: Managed granaries, oil and wine reserves, metal and timber storage, and emergency provisioning

function.zh: 征收港税、市场费与贸易抽成，校准衡器并监督银锭称量
function.en: Collected harbor dues, market fees, and trade shares, calibrated measures, and supervised weighing of silver bullion

function.zh: 负责城墙、守备、武库、瞭望与民兵动员
function.en: Responsible for walls, garrison duties, arsenals, watch posts, and militia mobilization

localAdmin.zh: 作为单一城邦，迦太基的地方行政主要围绕城内街区、港区、神庙区与近郊农地展开，由王室任命的管理人、祭司与贵族代理协同治理；对周边利比亚聚落尚无成熟直接统治，更多依赖盟约、贸易和保护关系。
localAdmin.en: As a single city-state, Carthage’s local administration centered on urban quarters, the harbor zone, temple precincts, and nearby agricultural lands, governed cooperatively by royal appointees, priests, and aristocratic agents; over surrounding Libyan settlements it had not yet developed mature direct rule, relying more on alliance, trade, and protective ties.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以腓尼基习惯法、王室裁断、商贸契约和宗教誓约为基础。财产、债务、海运和婚姻事务常由家族长老、祭司与宫廷官员共同裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on Phoenician customary practice, royal judgment, mercantile contracts, and religious oaths. Property, debt, maritime, and marriage matters were often judged jointly by family elders, priests, and court officials.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自港口关税、市场交易费、船舶停泊费、王室地产收入、祭祀奉献与对附属农业社群征收的实物贡纳。税制较轻但重视贸易环节抽收。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from harbor customs, market transaction fees, docking charges, income from royal lands, temple offerings, and produce tribute from dependent agricultural communities. Taxation was relatively light but strongly focused on commerce.

religion.zh: 宗教属西闪米特传统，核心是来自泰尔的城邦神祇崇拜，重视巴力、阿斯塔特等神明的祭祀，并逐步形成具有地方特征的布匿宗教体系。
religion.en: Religion belonged to the West Semitic tradition, centered on city-god worship brought from Tyre, especially reverence for deities such as Baal and Astarte, gradually developing into a locally distinctive Punic religious system.

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系哲学学派，思想以神意、祖先传统、航海经验、契约伦理与城邦实用主义为主。
philosophy.en: There was not yet a formal philosophical school; thought was dominated by divine order, ancestral tradition, seafaring experience, contractual ethics, and civic pragmatism.

writingSystem.zh: 使用腓尼基字母文字，以辅音字母书写，广泛用于碑铭、贸易记号、神庙记录和库存清单，是后世布匿文字的直接前身。
writingSystem.en: The Phoenician alphabet was used, a consonantal script widely employed for inscriptions, trade marks, temple records, and inventory lists; it was the direct ancestor of later Punic writing.

culturalAchievements.zh: 建城时期最显著的成就是将泰尔式海洋贸易文化移植到北非：规划港口与仓储、建立多族群商业社区、推广字母书写、染料与金属工艺，并把黎凡特宗教和建筑传统带入西地中海。
culturalAchievements.en: The chief cultural achievement of the founding period was the transplantation of Tyrian maritime-commercial culture into North Africa: planned harbor and storage facilities, a multiethnic merchant community, spread of alphabetic writing, dye and metalworking craft, and the transfer of Levantine religious and architectural traditions into the western Mediterranean.

languageFamily.zh: 西北闪米特语族中的腓尼基语，逐步演变为早期布匿语
languageFamily.en: Phoenician of the Northwest Semitic family, gradually developing toward early Punic

gdpDescription.zh: 以3万人口的早期港口殖民城而言，迦太基的经济异常活跃。其财富不主要来自大片内陆农业，而来自转口贸易、海运、仓储、金属加工、紫染与港口服务，因此人均产出高于同时期多数普通聚落。
gdpDescription.en: For an early port colony of only 30,000 people, Carthage’s economy was exceptionally dynamic. Its wealth came not mainly from broad inland agriculture, but from entrepôt trade, shipping, storage, metalworking, purple-dye production, and harbor services, giving it a higher per-capita output than most ordinary settlements of the time.

mainIndustries.zh: 海运贸易、港口装卸、造船与修船、金属制品、陶器、纺织、染料加工、橄榄油与葡萄酒转运、近郊农业与渔业
mainIndustries.en: Maritime trade, harbor handling, shipbuilding and repair, metal goods, pottery, textiles, dye processing, redistribution of olive oil and wine, peri-urban agriculture, and fishing

tradeGoods.zh: 输出与转运品包括紫染织物、精制陶器、金属器、玻璃器、木材制品、油酒与奢侈品；输入品包括北非谷物、牲畜、皮革、象牙、金属矿物、木材、奴仆及来自伊比利亚、西西里、撒丁与黎凡特的各类商品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports and re-exports included purple-dyed textiles, fine pottery, metal goods, glassware, worked timber, oil, wine, and luxury items; imports included North African grain, livestock, hides, ivory, mineral resources, timber, servants, and varied goods from Iberia, Sicily, Sardinia, and the Levant.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距明显。商人和船主可积累银锭、库存货物、奴仆和海外债权；普通家庭多拥有住房、渔具、作坊器具、少量牲畜和谷物储备。整体生活水平在西地中海新兴城镇中属较高。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was unevenly distributed. Merchants and shipowners could accumulate bullion, inventories, servants, and overseas claims, while ordinary households typically held a dwelling, fishing gear, workshop tools, small livestock, and grain stores. Overall living standards were relatively high for an emerging western Mediterranean town.

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换和海上商业为核心、由王室与贵族把控关键港口资源的混合型商业经济；私营商队活跃，但重要贸易路线与仓储设施受政治权力保护和调节。
economicSystem.en: A mixed commercial economy centered on market exchange and maritime enterprise, with the royal house and aristocracy controlling key harbor resources; private merchant ventures were active, but major trade routes and storage facilities were protected and regulated by political authority.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模对一座新建殖民城而言相当可观，但构成并非纯金银，还包括金属锭、粮食储备、船具、木材和可迅速变卖的库存商品。财政弹性取决于航线安全与贸易季节。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was considerable for a newly founded colony, though it consisted not only of precious metal but also bullion, grain reserves, ship gear, timber, and inventories that could be liquidated quickly. Fiscal flexibility depended heavily on route security and the sailing season.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健积累：保持港税和贸易抽成的稳定，优先投入港口、仓储和城防，并通过保留银锭与实物储备来应对歉收、海损或区域冲突。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored prudent accumulation: maintaining stable customs and trade dues, prioritizing harbor, storage, and defense spending, and preserving bullion and material reserves against crop failure, maritime loss, or regional conflict.

description.zh: 由殖民者、公民和部分依附居民组成，装备长矛、圆盾、短剑与皮甲，主要用于守城和近郊巡防。
description.en: Composed of settlers, citizens, and some dependents, armed with spears, round shields, daggers, and leather armor, mainly used for city defense and patrol of nearby lands.

description.zh: 负责宫廷、神庙与国库安全，成员多为忠于王室的精锐亲兵。
description.en: Responsible for the security of the court, temples, and treasury, consisting of elite retainers loyal to the royal house.

description.zh: 平时服务于商船和轻型船只，战时可转为海上护航、登船作战和沿岸警戒力量。
description.en: In peacetime they served aboard merchantmen and light craft; in war they could be turned into convoy escorts, boarding parties, and coastal patrol forces.

description.zh: 来自周边北非社群的盟友战士，善于地形熟悉、侦察与快速支援。
description.en: Allied fighters from nearby North African communities, useful for terrain knowledge, scouting, and rapid support.

commanderInChief.zh: 女王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The Queen as Commander-in-Chief

technology.zh: 军备处于早期铁器时代水平，使用铁质矛头、短剑、弓箭、木盾和简易护甲；海上力量以商船改装和轻型桨帆船为主，尚未形成后世迦太基的成熟海军体系。
technology.en: Military equipment stood at an early Iron Age level, using iron spearheads, daggers, bows, wooden shields, and simple armor; naval strength relied mainly on adapted merchant vessels and light oared-sailing craft, not yet the mature navy of later Carthage.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边利比亚-柏柏尔部族关系失衡、与其他腓尼基殖民点的商业竞争、海盗活动，以及西西里与撒丁周边日益活跃的希腊与意大利诸社群。
threats.en: Main threats came from unstable relations with surrounding Libyan-Berber tribes, commercial rivalry with other Phoenician colonies, piracy, and the increasing activity of Greek and Italic communities around Sicily and Sardinia.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约3万，已是一座兴盛的新殖民港城。居民由来自泰尔及其他腓尼基城邦的移民、本地北非居民、通婚家庭、工匠、水手和商旅构成，人口仍在快速增长。
populationDescription.en: With about 30,000 inhabitants, Carthage was already a thriving new colonial port city. Its population included migrants from Tyre and other Phoenician cities, local North African residents, intermarried families, artisans, sailors, and itinerant merchants, and it was still growing rapidly.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与建城贵族、富商与船主、祭司和书记、工匠与商贩、海员与码头劳工、小农与渔民，以及依附民和奴仆。族群与身份并非完全重合，部分本地人可通过贸易、婚姻与军事服务上升。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included the royal house and founding aristocracy, wealthy merchants and shipowners, priests and scribes, artisans and traders, sailors and dock laborers, small farmers and fishers, and dependents and servants. Ethnicity and status did not fully overlap, and some locals could rise through trade, marriage, or military service.

allies.zh: 最重要的外部联系对象是母邦泰尔及其他腓尼基城市，同时与周边利比亚-柏柏尔首领维持互利关系，并可能与乌提卡等既有殖民据点合作。
allies.en: Its most important external ties were with the mother-city Tyre and other Phoenician cities, while mutually beneficial relations were maintained with nearby Libyan-Berber chiefs, and cooperation likely existed with established colonial sites such as Utica.

enemies.zh: 800年前后尚无固定意义上的头号宿敌，但潜在敌对者包括敌视殖民扩张的地方部族、争夺航线的海盗，以及未来在西地中海扩张的希腊势力。
enemies.en: Around 800 BCE Carthage had no single permanent arch-enemy, but potential opponents included local tribes hostile to colonial expansion, pirates competing for sea lanes, and the Greek powers that would later expand in the western Mediterranean.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以生存与贸易优先：通过馈赠、婚盟、宗教纽带和商业互惠稳固周边关系，同时谨慎扩展航线节点，避免过早卷入大规模陆战。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy prioritized survival and commerce: using gifts, marriage alliances, religious ties, and commercial reciprocity to stabilize surrounding relations while cautiously expanding maritime nodes and avoiding premature involvement in large-scale land warfare.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期

keyInnovations.zh: 字母文字行政记录、远洋航海技术、港口仓储组织、紫染与玻璃工艺、铁器使用、标准化衡量体系、腓尼基式造船与木工技术
keyInnovations.en: Alphabetic administrative record-keeping, long-distance seafaring techniques, organized harbor storage, purple-dye and glass craft, use of iron tools, standardized weight systems, and Phoenician shipbuilding and carpentry

strengths.zh: 迦太基最大的优势在于地理位置优越、港口条件良好、与泰尔相连的海贸网络、灵活的寡头—王权合作结构，以及能够吸纳本地人口与资源的殖民适应力。
strengths.en: Carthage’s greatest strengths lay in its favorable geographic position, good harbor conditions, sea-trade networks tied to Tyre, a flexible oligarchic-monarchical partnership, and colonial adaptability in absorbing local people and resources.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于建城时间尚短、内陆控制有限、粮食与资源基础仍不稳固、军事规模偏小，并且对海上贸易和母邦联系依赖较深。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were its recent foundation, limited inland control, still insecure food and resource base, relatively small military scale, and strong dependence on maritime trade and links with the mother-city.

outlook.zh: 若能持续控制港口、整合周边北非社群并扩展至西西里、撒丁和伊比利亚贸易圈，迦太基极可能由殖民城成长为西地中海的主导海上强权。
outlook.en: If it could continue controlling its harbor, integrating surrounding North African communities, and extending into the trade circuits of Sicily, Sardinia, and Iberia, Carthage was highly likely to grow from a colony into the dominant maritime power of the western Mediterranean.

description.zh: 北非新興的腓尼基殖民城邦，日後將成為西地中海霸權。
description.en: A newly founded Phoenician colonial city in North Africa that would later dominate the western Mediterranean.

name.en: Berber Tribal Polities of North Africa

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族酋邦联盟与氏族统治，没有统一国家机构，各地由部族会议、长老与酋长共同治理。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of tribal chiefdoms and clan-based rule, without a unified state apparatus; local governance was shared by tribal assemblies, elders, and chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族与部族为核心，按血缘、牧场控制权、战士声望与宗教威望分层。定居农民、半游牧牧民与跨区域商旅并存。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on clans, lineages, and tribes, stratified by kinship, control of pastureland, warrior prestige, and religious standing. Settled farmers, semi-nomadic pastoralists, and long-distance caravan traders coexisted.

rulingClass.zh: 部族贵族、酋长家族、战士首领与长老会议成员构成统治阶层。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of tribal nobility, chiefly families, warrior leaders, and members of councils of elders.

succession.zh: 继承多依部族习惯法而定，通常在酋长家族内世袭，但常需长老与武士支持确认，有时由旁支或最具威望者继任。
succession.en: Succession was governed by tribal customary law, usually hereditary within chiefly families, but often required confirmation by elders and warriors; at times a collateral kinsman or the most prestigious candidate succeeded.

structure.zh: 柏柏尔地区在公元前800年前后并无统一中央政府。政治结构以地方部族、山地共同体、绿洲聚落和草原酋邦为单位。重大事务由酋长召集长老会议、家族首领和武士议决，涉及放牧权、迁徙路线、婚姻联盟、复仇调解与对外贸易。
structure.en: Around 800 BCE, the Berber world had no unified central government. Its political structure was organized through local tribes, mountain communities, oasis settlements, and steppe chiefdoms. Major matters were decided by chiefs convening councils of elders, lineage heads, and warriors, especially concerning grazing rights, migration routes, marriage alliances, feud settlement, and external trade.

function.zh: 裁决争端、确认继承、监督习惯法与部族盟约。
function.en: Adjudicated disputes, confirmed succession, and oversaw customary law and tribal agreements.

function.zh: 执行酋长命令、联络附属氏族、组织集会与征召战士。
function.en: Carried out chiefly orders, liaised with subordinate clans, organized assemblies, and summoned warriors.

function.zh: 协调季节性迁徙、分配井泉与牧地、处理边界摩擦。
function.en: Coordinated seasonal movements, allocated wells and grazing lands, and handled boundary frictions.

function.zh: 管理绿洲集市、商队通行费、对外交换和向酋长缴纳的牲畜与谷物。
function.en: Managed oasis markets, caravan tolls, external exchange, and livestock and grain rendered to chiefs.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、祖灵崇拜、圣地维护与誓约见证。
function.en: Conducted rites, ancestor veneration, shrine maintenance, and oath witnessing.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖氏族首领、村社长老、绿洲头人和山地部落会议。行政权与司法权并未严格分离，更多体现为习惯法秩序与个人威望统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on clan heads, village elders, oasis headmen, and mountain tribal assemblies. Administrative and judicial powers were not sharply separated, being expressed more through customary order and personal authority.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以口传习惯法为主，核心包括血亲责任、赔偿、誓约、庇护、婚姻、牧场使用与复仇限制。许多争端通过赔偿牲畜、婚姻安排或仲裁和解，而非成文法庭裁判。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily oral customary law, centered on kin responsibility, compensation, oath-taking, protection, marriage, pasture use, and limits on feud. Many disputes were settled through livestock compensation, marriage arrangements, or arbitration rather than written courts.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制。酋长通过贡纳、战利品分配、集市通行费、商队保护费以及从附属氏族征收牲畜、谷物、皮革和劳役获得资源。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system. Chiefs drew resources through tribute, distribution of booty, market tolls, caravan protection payments, and levies in livestock, grain, hides, and labor from dependent clans.

religion.zh: 宗教以祖灵崇拜、地方神灵、圣石、山岳、泉水和天体崇拜为主，不同地区受埃及与腓尼基宗教接触影响程度不一。祭祀多与家族、墓葬、丰产、牲畜和战争誓约相关。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, local deities, sacred stones, mountains, springs, and celestial worship, with varying degrees of Egyptian and Phoenician religious contact in different regions. Rituals were closely tied to family, burial, fertility, livestock, and war oaths.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成系统化哲学传统，价值观主要体现为荣誉、血缘义务、待客之道、勇武、口头记忆和对土地与祖先的忠诚。
philosophy.en: No formal philosophical tradition had yet emerged; values were expressed through honor, kin obligation, hospitality, martial virtue, oral memory, and loyalty to land and ancestors.

writingSystem.zh: 大多数地区仍以口传文化为主，尚无广泛普及的本土文字行政体系。部分沿海与贸易接触地区可能接触腓尼基书写传统，但内陆柏柏尔社会总体仍以记忆、歌谣、纹饰和纪念碑式标志传承信息。
writingSystem.en: Most regions remained predominantly oral in culture, without a widely used indigenous writing system for administration. Some coastal and trade-contact zones may have encountered Phoenician writing traditions, but inland Berber societies overall transmitted information through memory, song, ornament, and monument-like markers.

culturalAchievements.zh: 主要成就包括适应北非多样生态的游牧与农牧复合生活方式、石制与土坯聚落、墓葬与巨石纪念传统、精于马术和畜群管理、以及连接地中海沿岸、沙漠边缘与内陆的商路网络。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included a pastoral and agro-pastoral way of life adapted to North Africa’s diverse environments, stone and mudbrick settlements, funerary and megalithic commemorative traditions, skill in horsemanship and herd management, and trade networks linking the Mediterranean coast, desert margins, and interior.

languageFamily.zh: 阿非罗-亚细亚语系中的柏柏尔语支
languageFamily.en: Berber branch of the Afro-Asiatic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总体为低到中等复杂度的部族农牧经济。财富主要以牲畜、谷物、盐、皮革、金属器物和人际依附关系体现，而非统一货币。沿海接触区与绿洲节点的交换较活跃，但广大地区仍以自给性生产为主。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was a low- to medium-complexity tribal agro-pastoral economy. Wealth was expressed chiefly in livestock, grain, salt, hides, metal goods, and networks of dependency rather than in unified currency. Exchange was more active in coastal contact zones and oasis nodes, but much of the region remained primarily self-sufficient.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、谷物农业、橄榄与果树种植（局部地区）、皮革加工、织物制作、陶器、石工、铜铁器使用与修造、盐与地方矿产交换。
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, grain farming, olive and fruit cultivation in some areas, leatherworking, textile production, pottery, stoneworking, the use and repair of copper and iron tools, and exchange in salt and local mineral resources.

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、羊毛、皮革、谷物、盐、蜂蜜、蜡、象牙转运品、羽毛、金属器、染料原料、木材和地方手工制品。
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, wool, hides, grain, salt, honey, wax, transit ivory, feathers, metal tools, dye materials, timber, and local handicrafts.

unitName.en: head of livestock and silver weight

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异较大。普通家庭的核心资产是羊群、山羊、少量牛马、粮食储备、工具和亲族关系；富裕酋长家族则掌握更大畜群、奴仆或依附者、武器和贸易联系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied widely. The core assets of ordinary households were flocks of sheep and goats, a few cattle or horses, grain stores, tools, and kin ties; wealthy chiefly families controlled larger herds, retainers or dependents, weapons, and trade connections.

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、氏族控制资源、贡纳关系和区域交换并存的部族经济。生产组织主要依赖亲属劳动与季节迁徙，市场作用有限但在绿洲、沿海与外来贸易接触区逐渐增强。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy combining household production, clan control of resources, tribute relations, and regional exchange. Production was organized mainly through kin labor and seasonal movement, while market mechanisms remained limited but were growing in oases, coastal zones, and areas of external trade contact.

source.zh: 商队通行费与市场收费

category.en: Maintenance of Chiefly Retinues and Armed Followers

category.zh: 贸易护送与道路水源维护

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一国库。此处为各主要部族酋长、圣所储备、谷仓、牲畜储备与贵重金属存量的合计估算。财富多以牲畜、粮食、武器和礼品形式保存，而非集中现金。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury. This figure estimates the combined reserves held by major tribal chiefs, sanctuaries, granaries, livestock stores, and precious metal holdings. Wealth was preserved largely as herds, grain, weapons, and prestige goods rather than centralized cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持酋长威望、平衡部族关系、支持战时动员和应对旱灾风险为核心。资源再分配通过宴飨、赠礼、赔偿和危机时的粮畜调拨实现。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on maintaining chiefly prestige, balancing tribal relationships, supporting wartime mobilization, and coping with drought risk. Redistribution occurred through feasting, gifts, compensations, and emergency allocation of grain and livestock.

description.zh: 由氏族武士与成年男子组成，装备长矛、短剑、木盾、投枪和部分铁制武器，适合山地、丘陵和近距离突袭作战。
description.en: Composed of clan warriors and adult men, equipped with spears, daggers, wooden shields, javelins, and some iron weapons, suited for fighting in mountains, hills, and close-range raids.

description.zh: 由富裕首领和贵族战士组成，使用马匹与部分轻型战车，在开阔地带用于侦察、追击、威慑和贵族作战展示。
description.en: Made up of wealthy chiefs and noble warriors using horses and some light chariots, employed in open terrain for scouting, pursuit, intimidation, and aristocratic combat display.

name.en: Missile Troops and Light Raiding Bands

description.zh: 善于使用投枪、石索和弓，机动性高，适合伏击、掠袭商路与边地防御。
description.en: Skilled with javelins, slings, and bows, highly mobile, and effective in ambushes, route raiding, and frontier defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各部族酋长在战时分别统军或推举盟主
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; tribal chiefs led separately in war or selected a temporary alliance leader

technology.zh: 军备处于铁器时代早期北非水平，广泛使用长矛、投枪、短剑、盾牌、皮甲和少量铁制兵器；战法强调地形熟悉、机动袭扰、部族集结和短期作战，而非长期围城。
technology.en: Military equipment was at an early Iron Age North African level, with widespread use of spears, javelins, daggers, shields, leather protection, and limited iron weaponry; warfare emphasized terrain familiarity, mobile raiding, tribal mustering, and short campaigns rather than prolonged siege operations.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间争斗、牧场与水源冲突、沿海外来势力渗透、与埃及边地接触摩擦，以及西部与南部商路控制权竞争。
threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal conflict, disputes over pasture and water, encroachment by foreign coastal powers, friction along zones of contact with Egypt, and competition over western and southern trade routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于马格里布沿海腹地、阿特拉斯山地、草原地带、绿洲节点与部分沙漠边缘。人口密度差异很大，较多居民为小型村社、帐幕群或季节性迁徙共同体。
populationDescription.en: The population was widely distributed across the Maghrebi hinterland, Atlas mountain zones, steppe regions, oasis nodes, and parts of the desert fringe. Population density varied greatly, with many people living in small villages, tent clusters, or seasonally mobile communities.

name.zh: 无统一大型城市，主要为绿洲聚落与山地集镇
name.en: No Unified Large Cities; Mainly Oasis Settlements and Highland Market Centers

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致可分为酋长与贵族家族、武士与亲随、自由氏族成员、农牧民、工匠、商旅与依附者。部分地区可能存在战俘或债务性依附人口，但规模通常小于后世国家社会。
socialClasses.en: Social classes can be broadly divided into chiefly and noble families, warriors and retainers, free clan members, farmers and pastoralists, artisans, traders, and dependents. Some regions may have had captives or debt-bound dependents, though on a smaller scale than in later state societies.

allies.zh: 无统一盟国，通常以邻近氏族联盟、山地部落互助关系以及与部分腓尼基贸易者的务实合作为主。
allies.en: There were no unified allies; diplomacy usually took the form of neighboring clan alliances, mutual aid among mountain tribes, and pragmatic cooperation with some Phoenician traders.

enemies.zh: 无统一国家级宿敌，但常与竞争部族、边地掠袭者及试图控制沿海贸易据点的外来势力发生冲突。
enemies.en: There was no single state-level enemy, but conflict was frequent with rival tribes, frontier raiders, and foreign powers seeking to control coastal trade points.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护部族自治、控制牧道和水源、争取贸易利益、通过婚姻和赠礼稳固联盟为主。沿海地区倾向与腓尼基商人接触交换，内陆地区更重视路线安全与部族均势。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving tribal autonomy, controlling pasture routes and water sources, securing trade benefits, and stabilizing alliances through marriage and gift exchange. Coastal areas tended toward exchange with Phoenician merchants, while inland groups placed greater emphasis on route security and intertribal balance.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制工具和武器的逐步扩散、改良畜群管理、旱地农耕与梯田经验、土坯与石构建筑、储粮技术、区域马术发展以及沙漠边缘路线利用能力。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included the gradual spread of iron tools and weapons, improved herd management, dry farming and terracing knowledge, mudbrick and stone construction, grain storage techniques, regional developments in horsemanship, and effective use of desert-margin routes.

strengths.zh: 优势在于对北非山地、草原与半干旱环境的高度适应；社会韧性强；机动能力高；地方自治传统稳固；能够连接沿海、内陆与沙漠边缘贸易网络。
strengths.en: Strengths included high adaptation to North African mountains, steppes, and semi-arid environments; strong social resilience; high mobility; durable traditions of local autonomy; and the ability to connect coastal, interior, and desert-margin trade networks.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于缺乏统一政权、税制与常备军，难以集中资源进行大规模工程或长期战争；政治碎片化使部族争斗频繁，也使沿海外来势力更易逐步建立影响。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included the absence of a unified state, tax system, and standing army, making it difficult to concentrate resources for large-scale works or long wars; political fragmentation encouraged frequent tribal conflict and made it easier for foreign coastal powers to build influence gradually.

outlook.zh: 总体前景稳定。短期内柏柏尔社会将继续以部族与酋邦形式存在，并在与腓尼基—布匿沿海网络、埃及西境及撒哈拉边缘交换中逐步增强复杂性。但若无统一整合，其发展仍将以区域差异和地方政治为主。
outlook.en: The overall outlook was stable. In the near term, Berber society would continue in tribal and chiefly forms, gradually increasing in complexity through interaction with Phoenician-Punic coastal networks, Egypt’s western frontier, and Saharan exchange. Without political unification, however, its development would remain regionally varied and locally driven.

description.zh: 北非內陸與沿海腹地由多個柏柏爾部族與酋邦組成。
description.en: The interior and hinterlands of North Africa were inhabited by numerous Berber tribes and chiefdoms.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族酋邦体系，由地方亲族集团和仪式中心共同构成
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal chiefdom system composed of local kin-based groups and ceremonial centers

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村社、扩大家族和地方酋邦为基础，上层为世袭首领与祭司，下层为普通农民、工匠与劳役者
socialStructure.en: Society was based on villages, extended families, and local chiefdoms, with hereditary leaders and ritual specialists above common farmers, artisans, and laborers

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、祭司性领袖及控制仪式与土地分配的显贵
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly families, ritual leaders, and notables who controlled ceremonies and land allocation

succession.zh: 通常为家族内世袭，但具体继承方式因地区而异，常结合长幼、能力与地方支持
succession.en: Usually hereditary within leading families, though exact succession varied by region and often depended on seniority, ability, and local support

structure.zh: 无统一国家官僚机构，各地区由酋长、家族长老、祭司与村社领袖共同治理；大型礼仪中心对周边聚落具有号召与协调作用
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; regions were governed by chiefs, lineage elders, ritual specialists, and village leaders, while larger ceremonial centers exercised influence and coordination over nearby settlements

function.zh: 组织劳役、储存农产、分配礼物与管理地方贡纳
function.en: Organized labor, stored agricultural produce, distributed gifts, and managed local tribute

function.zh: 主持祭祀、维持圣所、记录时间周期并安排公共仪式
function.en: Conducted rituals, maintained sacred places, tracked time cycles, and organized public ceremonies

function.zh: 组织修筑平台、土丘、道路、排水设施及协调播种与收获劳力
function.en: Organized the building of platforms, mounds, paths, drainage works, and coordinated labor for planting and harvest

function.zh: 在冲突时召集成年男子守卫聚落、护送商旅并进行小规模袭击或报复
function.en: Mobilized adult men in times of conflict to defend settlements, escort traders, and conduct small-scale raids or reprisals

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村社和酋邦为单位，通常由酋长家族、长老会议和祭司共同处置土地、水源、劳役与纠纷
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through villages and chiefdoms, usually with chiefly families, councils of elders, and ritual leaders jointly handling land, water, labor, and disputes

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依靠习惯法、家族仲裁与酋长裁决，重视赔偿、和解、婚姻与土地使用规范
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on customary practice, lineage arbitration, and chiefly judgment, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, and rules for marriage and land use

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制，酋长主要以劳役、农产、手工品和礼仪性贡纳形式获取资源
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; chiefs drew resources mainly through labor dues, agricultural produce, craft goods, and ritual tribute

religion.zh: 早期玛雅宗教以祖先崇拜、自然神灵、山川洞穴圣地和农业祭祀为核心，玉米、雨水与丰产观念尤为重要
religion.en: Early Maya religion centered on ancestor veneration, nature spirits, sacred mountains and caves, and agricultural rites, with maize, rain, and fertility holding particular importance

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系哲学传统，但宇宙观强调时间循环、祖先合法性、人与自然及神圣空间之间的秩序关系
philosophy.en: No formal philosophical school existed, but their worldview stressed cyclical time, ancestral legitimacy, and ordered relations among people, nature, and sacred space

writingSystem.zh: 约公元前800年尚未普遍使用成熟的玛雅文字，主要依赖口传传统、象征图像与仪式记忆；部分地区可能开始出现原始记号与图像表达
writingSystem.en: Around 800 BCE, a fully developed Maya script was not yet in widespread use; communication relied chiefly on oral tradition, symbolic imagery, and ritual memory, though some regions may have begun using proto-symbolic markings and iconographic expression

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出早期礼仪中心、土台与平台建筑、区域性祭祀空间、精细陶器、玉石与贝饰加工，以及与天象和季节相关的时间观念
culturalAchievements.en: They developed early ceremonial centers, earthen and platform architecture, regional ritual spaces, fine pottery, jade and shell ornament working, and time concepts tied to celestial observation and seasonal cycles

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以玉米、豆类、南瓜等农业为基础，辅以狩猎、渔猎、采集、陶作和区域交换。由于缺乏统一市场与国家征收体系，整体产出主要体现在家庭自给、酋长再分配和礼仪性消费中
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture including maize, beans, and squash, supplemented by hunting, fishing, gathering, pottery production, and regional exchange. In the absence of a unified market and state revenue system, most output appeared in household subsistence, chiefly redistribution, and ceremonial consumption

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、陶器制造、石器加工、玉石与贝饰制作、建筑劳役、狩猎渔猎
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pottery making, stone working, jade and shell ornament production, construction labor, and hunting and fishing

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、玉石、贝壳、陶器、盐、可可的早期交换品、羽毛、燧石工具、农产品
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, jade, shells, pottery, salt, early exchanged cacao in some areas, feathers, flint tools, and agricultural produce

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭拥有房屋、耕地使用权、陶器、石器和少量装饰品；精英家庭则积累玉饰、珍稀贝壳、祭器及更多劳动力支配权
householdWealth.en: Most households possessed dwellings, land-use rights, pottery, stone tools, and a few ornaments; elite households accumulated jade items, rare shells, ritual objects, and greater command over labor

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业为核心、辅以酋长再分配和长距离珍贵物资交换的部族经济
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on village agriculture, supplemented by chiefly redistribution and long-distance exchange in prestige goods

category.zh: 酋长家族维持与再分配
category.en: Maintenance of chiefly households and redistribution

treasuryDescription.zh: 并不存在统一国库，财富主要分散储存在各酋长中心的粮仓、祭祀器物、玉饰、贝壳和可调配劳动力中
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; wealth was mainly dispersed among chiefly centers in the form of granaries, ritual objects, jade ornaments, shells, and command over mobilizable labor

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持礼仪权威、工程组织和精英再分配为主，通常避免对基层生产造成持续过重压榨，以保持地方稳定
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on sustaining ritual authority, organizing construction, and elite redistribution, generally avoiding constant excessive extraction from local producers in order to preserve stability

description.zh: 由地方成年男子和酋长亲随组成，负责守卫礼仪中心、村社与储藏设施
description.en: Made up of local adult men and chiefly retainers, responsible for guarding ceremonial centers, villages, and storage facilities

description.zh: 主要为临时召集的步兵，使用木矛、石刃武器、投射器和木盾，适于伏击、袭扰和小规模冲突
description.en: These were mainly temporarily mobilized infantry using wooden spears, stone-bladed weapons, projectiles, and wooden shields, suited for ambushes, raiding, and small-scale conflict

description.zh: 直属酋长的核心武装，兼具护卫、执法与战时指挥作用
description.en: The core force directly attached to chiefs, serving as bodyguards, enforcers, and wartime leaders

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各地酋长分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; each chief directed his own forces

technology.zh: 军事技术处于石器晚期与早期复杂酋邦阶段，尚未使用铁器；武器包括矛、投枪、棍棒、石刃刀、投石器和木盾，防御依靠地形、栅栏和土台
technology.en: Military technology remained at a Late Stone Age to early complex-chiefdom stage and did not use iron; weapons included spears, javelins, clubs, stone-bladed knives, slings, and wooden shields, while defense relied on terrain, palisades, and earth platforms

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦间的土地、水源、贸易路线和声望竞争，而非跨区域大帝国入侵
threats.en: The main threats came from competition among neighboring chiefdoms over land, water, trade routes, and prestige rather than invasion by any large external empire

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于玛雅低地和高地的众多村社、农业聚落及少数早期礼仪中心，整体较分散但已有局部区域性集中趋势
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across many villages, farming communities, and a small number of early ceremonial centers in the Maya lowlands and highlands, remaining generally dispersed but showing localized trends toward concentration

name.zh: 米拉多尔盆地早期中心

socialClasses.zh: 社会大致可分为酋长与显贵家族、祭司与仪式专职者、工匠与商旅中介、普通农民，以及在战俘或债务背景下处于从属地位者
socialClasses.en: Society can be broadly divided into chiefs and noble families, ritual specialists, artisans and exchange intermediaries, common farmers, and dependents of subordinate status such as captives or debt-bound persons

allies.zh: 无统一对外联盟；各地区酋邦通过婚姻、礼物交换、共同祭祀和贸易联系形成临时盟友关系，并与奥尔梅克文化圈保持间接或局部接触
allies.en: There was no unified foreign alliance; regional chiefdoms formed temporary partnerships through marriage, gift exchange, shared rituals, and trade ties, with indirect or localized contact with the Olmec cultural sphere

enemies.zh: 主要对手是周边竞争酋邦和争夺资源、通道与声望的邻近社群
enemies.en: The principal rivals were neighboring competing chiefdoms and nearby communities contesting resources, routes, and prestige

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以区域交换、婚姻结盟和礼仪互访为主，战争多为局部冲突，目的常在于维护地位与控制交换网络
foreignPolicy.en: External relations were based mainly on regional exchange, marriage alliances, and ritual visits, while warfare was usually local and aimed at preserving status and controlling exchange networks

era.zh: 前古典早期的晚期石器—早期复杂社会阶段
era.en: Late Stone Age to early complex-society phase of the Early Preclassic

keyInnovations.zh: 高产玉米农业、土台与平台建筑、区域性仪式中心规划、黑曜石与燧石工具网络、早期天象观察与历时性记忆传统
keyInnovations.en: Productive maize agriculture, earthen and platform architecture, planning of regional ceremonial centers, obsidian and flint tool exchange networks, and early celestial observation with long-term ritual memory traditions

strengths.zh: 农业基础稳固、人口规模可观、区域交换活跃、礼仪权威有助于整合劳力，且多样生态带提升了资源弹性
strengths.en: The Maya possessed a solid agricultural base, a substantial population, active regional exchange, ritual authority that helped integrate labor, and diverse ecological zones that improved resource resilience

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散、缺乏统一军政机构、文字和国家行政尚未成熟，面对长期冲突或环境波动时协调能力有限
weaknesses.en: They were politically highly fragmented, lacked unified military and administrative institutions, and had not yet developed mature writing or state bureaucracy, limiting coordination in prolonged conflict or environmental stress

outlook.zh: 整体前景稳定且具有发展潜力，未来数百年内可能在若干礼仪中心周围形成更复杂的酋邦和早期城邦，为后来的古典玛雅文明奠定基础
outlook.en: The general outlook was stable and promising; over the coming centuries, more complex chiefdoms and early city-polities were likely to emerge around certain ceremonial centers, laying the foundations for the later Classic Maya civilization

description.zh: 瑪雅低地與高地散布多個農業村落與早期儀式中心，尚未形成古典城邦。
description.en: The Maya lowlands and highlands contained many agricultural communities and early ceremonial centers, before the Classic city-states.

ruler.zh: 拉文塔等仪式中心的不具名统治者
ruler.en: Unknown rulers of ceremonial centers such as La Venta

governmentForm.zh: 以神权为核心的区域性酋邦联盟，由若干大型仪式中心主导周边聚落与农业区。
governmentForm.en: A theocratic network of regional chiefly polities, with major ceremonial centers dominating surrounding settlements and farming zones.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分层明显，上层为祭司、世袭首领与贵族家族，中层包括工匠、长途商人和附属地方头人，下层为农民、渔民、劳役者与普通村社成员。
socialStructure.en: Society was clearly stratified, with priests, hereditary chiefs, and noble lineages at the top; artisans, long-distance traders, and subordinate local headmen in the middle; and farmers, fishers, laborers, and ordinary village members at the base.

rulingClass.zh: 掌控仪式权威、贡赋分配和大型工程组织的祭司贵族宗族。
rulingClass.en: Priestly noble lineages who controlled ritual authority, tribute distribution, and the organization of large public works.

succession.zh: 以世袭为主，可能在显赫宗族内部按血缘与宗教合法性传承，并伴随仪式确认。
succession.en: Primarily hereditary, likely passing within prominent lineages according to kinship and religious legitimacy, with succession affirmed through ritual.

structure.zh: 政权并非统一帝国官僚制，而是以拉文塔为首的多中心神权体系；核心仪式中心通过宗教威望、贡赋网络和工程动员统辖周边村落与次级中心。
structure.en: The polity was not a unified imperial bureaucracy but a multicentered theocratic system led by La Venta; core ceremonial centers governed surrounding villages and secondary centers through religious prestige, tribute networks, and labor mobilization.

function.zh: 主持历法仪式、祖先崇拜、圣地管理和统治合法性宣示。
function.en: Oversaw calendrical rites, ancestor worship, sacred precinct management, and proclamations of ruling legitimacy.

function.zh: 征集玉石、粮食、可可、盐、羽毛、渔猎产物及劳役，并管理储藏设施。
function.en: Collected jade, foodstuffs, cacao, salt, feathers, fishery and hunting products, and labor service, while managing storage facilities.

function.zh: 组织土台、广场、堤道、排水设施和巨石头像等公共工程建设。
function.en: Organized public works such as earthen platforms, plazas, causeways, drainage systems, and colossal stone heads.

function.zh: 协调附属聚落、地方首领、婚姻联盟与贡赋义务。
function.en: Coordinated subordinate settlements, local chiefs, marriage alliances, and tribute obligations.

name.zh: 手工业与远程交换管理

function.zh: 监督精英工坊、玉器加工、黑曜石与异域奢侈品流通。
function.en: Supervised elite workshops, jade working, and the circulation of obsidian and exotic prestige goods.

function.zh: 保护圣地、维持集会秩序、执行首领命令并组织小规模武装行动。
function.en: Protected sacred precincts, maintained order at gatherings, enforced elite directives, and organized small-scale armed actions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托村社首领、亲属网络和附属仪式节点，中央中心通过定期集会、贡赋征收和劳役征调发挥影响。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village headmen, kinship networks, and subordinate ritual nodes, while the central center exerted influence through periodic gatherings, tribute collection, and labor drafts.

legalSystem.zh: 缺乏成文法典，主要以习惯法、宗教禁忌、祭司裁决和首领仲裁维系秩序，处罚多为赔偿、劳役、驱逐或宗教性惩戒。
legalSystem.en: There was no known written law code; order was maintained through customary law, religious taboo, priestly judgment, and chiefly arbitration, with punishments often involving compensation, labor, expulsion, or ritual sanction.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以实物贡赋和徭役为主，而非货币征税；各地向中心输送玉石、粮食、鱼盐、橡胶、羽毛、陶器及建筑劳动力。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based primarily on in-kind tribute and labor service rather than money; communities supplied centers with jade, food, fish and salt, rubber, feathers, pottery, and construction labor.

religion.zh: 多神信仰与祖先崇拜相结合，强调山岳、洞穴、水域、玉石、玉米和美洲豹等神圣象征，祭司通过占卜、献祭与公共仪式沟通超自然力量。
religion.en: Religion combined polytheistic belief with ancestor veneration, emphasizing sacred symbols such as mountains, caves, water, jade, maize, and the jaguar; priests communicated with supernatural forces through divination, offerings, and public ritual.

philosophy.zh: 尚无后世意义上的系统哲学，世界观以宇宙层级、王权神授、祖灵庇佑和人与自然循环关系为核心。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophy in the later sense; the worldview centered on cosmic hierarchy, divinely sanctioned rulership, ancestral protection, and cyclical relations between humans and nature.

writingSystem.zh: 可能处于前文字或早期符号记事阶段，已出现象征性图像、标记和原始铭刻传统，但尚未形成被充分证实的成熟完整文字体系。
writingSystem.en: The civilization likely stood at a proto-writing or early symbolic recording stage, using emblematic imagery, signs, and incipient inscriptional traditions, but no fully confirmed mature writing system is known for this date.

culturalAchievements.zh: 修建大型土筑仪式中心、广场与金字塔式土台，雕刻巨型石头像与精美玉器，形成影响后世玛雅与中部美洲文明的图像母题、宗教观念和礼仪传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included large earthen ceremonial centers, plazas, and pyramid-like mounds, colossal stone heads and finely worked jade objects, and iconographic, religious, and ceremonial traditions that influenced later Maya and broader Mesoamerican civilizations.

languageFamily.zh: 可能与米赫-索克语系有关，但仍存学术争议。
languageFamily.en: Possibly associated with the Mixe-Zoque language family, though this remains debated.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业剩余、渔猎采集、手工业和礼仪性再分配为基础。拉文塔等中心能够集中劳动力和奢侈品资源，显示出高于一般村社联盟的组织能力，但整体仍属于前货币、低商业化的区域复杂经济。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agricultural surplus, fishing and foraging, craft production, and ritual redistribution. Centers such as La Venta could concentrate labor and prestige goods, indicating organizational capacity above that of ordinary village alliances, though the system remained largely pre-monetary and only lightly commercialized.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米、豆类、南瓜与块根作物种植，渔业与河湖资源利用，玉器雕刻，陶器制造，玄武岩与巨石运输，橡胶加工，羽毛与贝壳制品生产。
mainIndustries.en: Cultivation of maize, beans, squash, and root crops; fishing and riverine resource use; jade carving; pottery production; basalt and megalith transport; rubber processing; and manufacture of feather and shell goods.

tradeGoods.zh: 玉石、蛇纹石、黑曜石、玄武岩制品、陶器、盐、可可、羽毛、贝壳、橡胶、动物皮和精英礼器。
tradeGoods.en: Jade, serpentine, obsidian, basalt goods, pottery, salt, cacao, feathers, shell, rubber, animal skins, and elite ritual objects.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在农地使用权、住房、陶器、渔猎工具和储粮；精英财富则表现为玉器、异域奢侈品、劳动力支配权和靠近仪式中心的居住权。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in access to fields, houses, pottery, fishing and farming tools, and stored food; elite wealth was expressed in jade, exotic prestige goods, control over labor, and residence near ceremonial centers.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业为基础、由神权精英通过贡赋和仪式再分配整合的区域经济，并辅以长距离奢侈品交换网络。
economicSystem.en: A regional economy based on village agriculture and integrated by theocratic elites through tribute and ritual redistribution, supplemented by long-distance prestige-goods exchange.

category.zh: 手工业、运输与交换组织
category.en: Craft production, transport, and exchange organization

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非铸币金库，而是由储粮、玉器、异域礼品、仪式用品和可动员劳动力构成的综合性储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a coin reserve but a composite stock of stored food, jade, exotic gifts, ritual goods, and mobilizable labor.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持仪式权威、土木工程和精英再分配为核心，依靠稳定征收实物贡赋与季节性劳役，在丰收年份积累储备以应对洪水、歉收和政治竞争。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on sustaining ritual authority, earthworks, and elite redistribution, relying on regular in-kind tribute and seasonal labor while building reserves in good harvest years to face floods, shortages, and political rivalry.

description.zh: 驻守拉文塔等中心，保护统治者、神庙和大型集会，兼具威慑与执法功能。
description.en: Stationed at centers such as La Venta to protect rulers, temples, and major gatherings, serving both deterrent and policing functions.

description.zh: 由各村社和附属聚落临时征调，负责防御、护送和小规模冲突。
description.en: Temporarily levied from villages and subordinate settlements for defense, escort duty, and small-scale conflicts.

description.zh: 附属于首领和贵族宗族的小规模战士群体，执行突袭、护卫和权力展示任务。
description.en: Small warrior groups attached to chiefs and noble lineages, used for raids, protection, and displays of power.

commanderInChief.zh: 祭司王及其首席贵族军事随从
commanderInChief.en: The Priest-King and his senior noble military retainers

technology.zh: 未使用铁器，武器以木矛、投枪、石刃武器、棍棒和盾牌为主；依靠地形、水网和仪式中心防御，而非大规模正规会战。
technology.en: Iron was not used; weapons consisted mainly of wooden spears, darts, stone-bladed weapons, clubs, and shields. Defense relied on terrain, waterways, and ceremonial center security rather than large formal pitched battles.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自区域内部竞争中心、对贸易路线与资源点的争夺，以及环境压力引发的社会动荡；不存在跨洋接触下的旧大陆军事威胁。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival regional centers, competition over trade routes and resource zones, and social instability driven by environmental stress; there was no Old World military threat due to the absence of transoceanic contact.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于墨西哥湾沿岸低地与河流湿地环境，集中在拉文塔、圣洛伦索余脉地区及众多村社农业聚落中。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Gulf Coast lowlands and riverine wetland environments, especially around La Venta, the broader San Lorenzo sphere, and numerous agricultural village settlements.

name.zh: 特雷斯萨波特斯地区中心

socialClasses.zh: 祭司王与贵族宗族、祭司与占卜者、地方首领、专业工匠与商旅、普通农民渔民、劳役者与依附者构成主要社会层级。
socialClasses.en: The main social layers were priest-kings and noble lineages, priests and diviners, local chiefs, specialized artisans and traders, ordinary farmers and fishers, and laborers and dependents.

allies.zh: 与周边墨西哥湾沿岸及内陆附属或友好酋邦保持婚姻、宗教与交换联盟；与早期玛雅地区存在文化和贸易联系。
allies.en: Maintained marriage, religious, and exchange alliances with neighboring Gulf Coast and inland chiefdoms; also had cultural and trade links with early Maya regions.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是中美洲内部争夺 prestige goods、交通节点和宗教影响力的竞争中心，而非题述中的欧亚非诸文明。
enemies.en: Its principal rivals were competing Mesoamerican centers contesting prestige goods, transport nodes, and religious influence, not the Afro-Eurasian civilizations listed in the prompt.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以宗教威望扩散、精英礼物交换、婚姻联盟和控制长距离奢侈品网络为核心，倾向间接支配而非领土征服。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on spreading religious prestige, exchanging elite gifts, forging marriage alliances, and controlling long-distance prestige networks, favoring indirect influence over territorial conquest.

era.zh: 中美洲前古典期早期；虽题设称“铁器时代”，但奥尔梅克在公元前800年并未使用铁器，技术上仍属石器、陶器与土木工程高度发展的阶段。
era.en: Early Formative Mesoamerica; although the prompt labels the era 'Iron Age,' the Olmec in 800 BCE did not use iron, and technologically remained in an advanced stone, ceramic, and earthwork-building phase.

keyInnovations.zh: 大型土木工程规划、复杂祭祀建筑布局、远距离巨石运输、精细玉器加工、橡胶利用、区域性图像系统与礼仪标准化。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included large-scale earthwork planning, complex ceremonial architectural layouts, long-distance transport of massive stone, fine jade working, use of rubber, and regional standardization of iconographic and ritual systems.

strengths.zh: 拥有强大的宗教号召力、成熟的仪式中心组织能力、广泛的奢侈品交换网络和对后世中美洲文化深远的象征影响。
strengths.en: Its strengths were strong religious charisma, mature organizational capacity at ceremonial centers, extensive prestige-goods exchange networks, and a symbolic legacy that deeply influenced later Mesoamerican cultures.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度有限，缺乏统一成文行政体系和稳定军事机器，对环境变化、河道迁移、地方离心和精英竞争较为脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included limited political integration, the absence of a unified written administrative system or stable military machine, and vulnerability to environmental change, shifting river courses, local fragmentation, and elite rivalry.

outlook.zh: 至前800年仍处于繁盛阶段，但未来数世纪可能因生态变化、贸易网络重组和政治中心转移而逐渐衰落，其文化母题将被后继的玛雅及其他中美洲文明继承与转化。
outlook.en: By 800 BCE it remained in a thriving phase, but over the coming centuries it could decline through ecological change, reorganization of exchange networks, and shifts in political centers, while its cultural motifs would be inherited and transformed by the Maya and other later Mesoamerican civilizations.

description.zh: 中美洲最早的重要複合文明之一，以巨型石頭像與儀式中心聞名。
description.en: One of Mesoamerica's earliest major complex civilizations, known for colossal heads and ceremonial centers.

governmentForm.zh: 以宗教仪式权威为核心的神权中心体制，政治控制通过圣所、祭司网络与朝圣关系间接实施
governmentForm.en: A temple-centered theocratic system in which political authority rested on ritual prestige, with control exercised indirectly through sanctuaries, priestly networks, and pilgrimage ties

socialStructure.zh: 社会由祭司与仪式专家居于顶层，其下为地方首领、工匠、长途交换中介、农牧民与服役劳工；不同山谷社区保持地方自治，但在宗教与象征秩序上服从查文圣中心
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by priests and ritual specialists, followed by local chiefs, artisans, long-distance exchange intermediaries, farmers, herders, and laborers obligated to communal service; valley communities retained local autonomy but were subordinated to the Chavin sacred center in religious and symbolic terms

rulingClass.zh: 掌握圣所、历法、祭仪、神谕与珍贵物资分配权的祭司—仪式贵族
rulingClass.en: A priestly-ritual aristocracy controlling sanctuaries, calendrical knowledge, ceremonies, oracles, and the distribution of prestige goods

succession.zh: 无固定世袭君主制；高阶祭司职位大体由祭司家族、资深仪式专家与圣所内部共识推举产生
succession.en: There was no fixed hereditary monarchy; senior priestly offices were likely filled through selection among priestly lineages, experienced ritual specialists, and internal sanctuary consensus

structure.zh: 中央圣所为核心，外围由受影响的区域祭祀中心、山谷社群首领与附属工坊组成；行政功能与宗教功能高度合一，没有成熟的文书官僚国家
structure.en: The central sanctuary stood at the core, surrounded by influenced regional cult centers, valley-community chiefs, and dependent workshops; administrative and religious functions were tightly fused, and there was no fully developed scribal bureaucratic state

function.zh: 主持大型祭祀、神谕、朝圣接待与宗教秩序维持
function.en: Oversaw major ceremonies, oracular functions, pilgrimage reception, and maintenance of sacred order

function.zh: 维护石建筑、地下甬道、排水系统、广场与仪式空间
function.en: Maintained stone architecture, subterranean galleries, drainage systems, plazas, and ritual spaces

function.zh: 接收地方贡品、管理玉米、块根、纺织品、贝壳、金属与祭品储藏
function.en: Received local tribute and managed stores of maize, tubers, textiles, shells, metals, and ritual offerings

function.zh: 组织石雕、骨器、纺织、金工与仪式器物生产
function.en: Organized the production of stone carvings, bone tools, textiles, metalwork, and ceremonial objects

function.zh: 协调与周边山谷首领、朝圣团体及交换路线的联系
function.en: Coordinated relations with surrounding valley chiefs, pilgrimage groups, and exchange routes

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由各山谷社群首领、宗族长者与地方祭司负责，查文中心通过宗教威望、贡赋要求与仪式网络进行间接统合
localAdmin.en: Local governance was handled mainly by valley-community chiefs, lineage elders, and local priests, while the Chavin center integrated them indirectly through religious prestige, tribute expectations, and ritual networks
