legalSystem.zh: 无成文法典；秩序以习惯法、宗族调解、祭司裁断与对神圣禁忌的遵守为基础，重大冲突通常通过仪式性和解、补偿或劳役解决
legalSystem.en: There was no written law code; order rested on customary law, kin mediation, priestly judgment, and observance of sacred taboos, with major disputes usually settled by ritual reconciliation, compensation, or labor obligations

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋主要采取贡纳与劳役形式，而非标准化货币税；各地向圣所输送粮食、纺织品、骆马类产品、工艺材料、珍异物品及季节性劳工
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form chiefly of tribute and labor service rather than standardized monetary taxation; communities sent food, textiles, camelid products, craft materials, exotic goods, and seasonal labor to the sanctuary

religion.zh: 以多重神灵、祖先崇拜、山岳与水体神圣性为核心的安第斯宗教，查文神殿强调豹、鹰、蛇等复合神性象征，并通过致幻仪式、音响效果与地下空间制造神圣体验
religion.en: An Andean religion centered on multiple deities, ancestors, and the sacredness of mountains and waters; the Chavin temple emphasized composite sacred imagery of felines, raptors, and serpents, using hallucinogenic rites, sound effects, and subterranean spaces to create powerful religious experiences

philosophy.zh: 无成体系哲学文本；其核心思想可概括为宇宙秩序、人与自然神力的互通、祭司对神意的诠释权，以及通过仪式维持生态与社会平衡
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical literature; its core worldview may be summarized as cosmic order, interaction between humans and sacred natural forces, priestly authority to interpret divine will, and the maintenance of ecological and social balance through ritual

writingSystem.zh: 无已知成熟文字系统；信息主要通过口传、图像符号、建筑布局、纪念性雕刻与仪式表演传递
writingSystem.en: No mature writing system is known; information was conveyed primarily through oral transmission, symbolic imagery, architectural layout, monumental carving, and ritual performance

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括高度成熟的宗教建筑群、精细石雕与浮雕、复杂地下甬道与水力设计、跨区域传播的图像艺术风格，以及在纺织、骨雕和早期金工中的高水平工艺
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included highly developed ceremonial architecture, refined stone carving and reliefs, complex subterranean galleries and hydraulic engineering, an iconographic style spread across regions, and advanced craftsmanship in textiles, bone carving, and early metallurgy

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不明，可能涉及多种安第斯语言社群；查文影响圈并非单一语言国家
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is uncertain and probably involved multiple Andean language communities; the Chavin sphere was not a single-language state

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高地农业、牧业、区域交换和宗教中心再分配为基础。财富主要体现为粮食、纺织品、牲畜、劳力与祭仪珍品，而非货币化市场总量。查文之繁荣来自其作为朝圣中心和声望物资集散地的地位
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on highland farming, pastoralism, regional exchange, and temple-centered redistribution. Wealth was embodied mainly in food, textiles, herds, labor, and ceremonial prestige goods rather than monetized market output. Chavin's prosperity derived from its role as a pilgrimage center and hub for prestige goods

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米与块根作物农业、骆马类牧养、纺织、石雕、骨角器制作、早期金属加工、建筑工程与宗教服务
mainIndustries.en: Maize and tuber agriculture, camelid herding, textile production, stone carving, bone and antler working, early metalworking, construction, and religious services

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、贝壳尤其是棘螺壳、纺织品、羽毛、药用与致幻植物、石材、金属原料、盐、干肉、羊毛与仪式器物
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, shells especially spondylus, textiles, feathers, medicinal and hallucinogenic plants, stone, metal raw materials, salt, dried meat, wool, and ceremonial objects

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要由耕地使用权、亲属劳力、储粮、纺织工具、骆马类牲畜及礼仪关系构成；精英财富则体现在控制圣所、工匠产品与稀有供品
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of access to fields, kin labor, food stores, weaving tools, camelid herds, and ritual ties; elite wealth lay in control over sanctuaries, artisan goods, and rare offerings

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、社区互助、精英控制的声望物资交换与圣所再分配并存的混合型经济体系
economicSystem.en: A mixed economic system combining household production, communal reciprocity, elite-controlled prestige exchange, and sanctuary redistribution

source.zh: 工艺品与声望物资交换

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要是圣所仓储、纺织品储备、牲畜、工具、建材和稀有祭品的综合储藏，而非铸币金库
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' consisted mainly of sanctuary storehouses holding food, textiles, livestock, tools, building materials, and rare offerings rather than a coin reserve

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健储备与仪式性再分配，在丰年扩大庆典与建筑，在歉年依靠仓储和地方互助减轻风险
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored prudent storage and ritual redistribution, expanding ceremonies and building in good years while relying on reserves and local reciprocity to reduce risk in poor years

description.zh: 负责查文圣所、防御要道、仓储和祭司人身安全的常备护卫力量，装备以木矛、投石器、棍棒和简易护具为主
description.en: A standing guard force responsible for the Chavin sanctuary, key approaches, storehouses, and priestly security, equipped mainly with wooden spears, slings, clubs, and simple protection

description.zh: 由附属或受影响社群在危机时征集，用于防御山谷、护送贡品和抵御地方冲突
description.en: Raised from subordinate or allied communities in times of danger, used to defend valleys, escort tribute, and respond to local conflict

description.zh: 由地方首领随从和山道巡护者组成，承担交通线巡查、朝圣队伍保护与紧急集结任务
description.en: Composed of local chiefly retainers and mountain-route patrols, tasked with checking routes, protecting pilgrim parties, and rapid assembly in emergencies

commanderInChief.zh: 大祭司及其指定的仪式—军事护卫首领
commanderInChief.en: The high priest and his appointed ritual-military guard chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术处于前铁器时代安第斯水平，依赖投石器、矛、木棍、石刃武器与地形防御；组织上更适合护卫与局部冲突，而非远程征服战争
technology.en: Military technology was at a pre-iron Andean level, relying on slings, spears, clubs, stone-edged weapons, and terrain defense; organizationally it was better suited to guarding and local conflict than to long-range wars of conquest

threats.zh: 主要威胁并非大型帝国入侵，而是区域山谷冲突、交换路线受阻、生态灾害引发的资源竞争，以及宗教影响力衰退导致的离心化
threats.en: The main threats were not invasions by large empires but regional valley conflict, disruption of exchange routes, resource competition caused by ecological stress, and centrifugal tendencies if religious influence weakened

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于安第斯高地与相关山谷网络中，真正居住在查文·德·万塔尔核心圣所附近者仅占少数；该数字代表查文影响圈及相关社群总人口，而非单一城市人口
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across Andean highlands and connected valley networks, with only a small fraction living near the core sanctuary of Chavin de Huantar; this figure represents the broader Chavin sphere and associated communities rather than a single urban population

socialClasses.zh: 祭司与仪式贵族、地方首领与家族长者、专业工匠与交换中介、农民与牧民、劳役人口与依附者构成主要社会层次
socialClasses.en: The main social layers were priests and ritual elites, local chiefs and lineage elders, specialist artisans and exchange intermediaries, farmers and herders, and laboring dependents

allies.zh: 无近代意义上的固定盟国；主要依靠与区域安第斯文化、山谷首领和朝圣网络维持合作关系
allies.en: There were no fixed allies in the modern sense; cooperation depended primarily on relations with regional Andean cultures, valley chiefs, and pilgrimage networks

enemies.zh: 无明确常年宿敌；潜在对手主要是拒绝贡纳或争夺交换路线与宗教声望的区域社群
enemies.en: There were no clearly defined permanent enemies; likely rivals were regional groups that resisted tribute demands or competed over exchange routes and religious prestige

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以宗教传播、象征整合、精英馈赠和交换网络扩展为核心，而非军事扩张；通过共享图像、圣物、祭仪和朝圣关系扩大影响力
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on religious diffusion, symbolic integration, elite gift exchange, and the expansion of exchange networks rather than military conquest; influence spread through shared imagery, sacred objects, rituals, and pilgrimage ties

era.zh: 安第斯早期地平线前期，属旧大陆分期中的铁器时代同时期但不使用铁器
era.en: Early Horizon formative phase in the Andes, contemporaneous with the Old World's Iron Age but without iron use

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括大型石构建筑、复杂地下通道、先进排水与水声设计、成熟纺织工艺、精细石雕图像系统，以及金、银、铜早期装饰性加工
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included monumental stone architecture, complex underground galleries, advanced drainage and hydraulic-acoustic design, mature textile production, a refined carved iconographic system, and early decorative working of gold, silver, and copper

strengths.zh: 查文的优势在于强大的宗教号召力、跨区域文化影响、象征体系统一能力、杰出的建筑与艺术水准，以及能将分散山谷社群纳入共同祭仪网络的整合力
strengths.en: Chavin's strengths lay in its powerful religious appeal, broad cultural influence, capacity for symbolic unification, outstanding architecture and art, and its ability to integrate scattered valley communities into a common ritual network

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于缺乏成熟文字与官僚体制、政治控制间接、军事力量有限、经济高度依赖宗教威望与交换网络，并易受气候波动和地方离心趋势影响
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were the absence of mature writing and bureaucracy, indirect political control, limited military power, a strong dependence on religious prestige and exchange networks, and vulnerability to climatic fluctuation and local centrifugal tendencies

outlook.zh: 在公元前800年前后，查文处于兴盛上升期，极可能继续扩大其宗教艺术影响并强化区域朝圣体系；但若生态压力、地方竞争或圣所权威衰退加剧，其影响圈未来可能分化为多个后继地方传统
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE, Chavin was in a thriving and ascending phase, likely to expand its religious-artistic influence and strengthen regional pilgrimage systems; however, if ecological stress, local competition, or weakening sanctuary authority intensified, its sphere would likely fragment into multiple successor regional traditions

description.zh: 安第斯高地的宗教與藝術中心，對廣泛區域施加文化影響。
description.en: A major Andean religious and artistic center exerting influence across a wide cultural sphere.

name.zh: 安第斯前期形成諸政體

capital.zh: 帕拉卡斯、库皮斯尼克等区域中心
capital.en: Regional centers such as Paracas and Cupisnique

governmentForm.zh: 由多个区域性酋邦、祭祀中心与亲族联盟组成的分散部落政治体制
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal political system composed of multiple regional chiefdoms, ceremonial centers, and kin-based alliances

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、村社与地方祭祀中心为核心，上层为酋长与祭司，中层为工匠、战士与专业劳作者，下层为普通农民、渔民与牧民
socialStructure.en: Society centered on kin groups, village communities, and local ceremonial centers, with chiefs and priests at the top, artisans, warriors, and specialist laborers in the middle, and common farmers, fishers, and pastoralists below

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、祭司集团与控制灌溉、劳役和交换网络的显贵
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly families, priestly groups, and elites who controlled irrigation, labor mobilization, and exchange networks

succession.zh: 多以世袭为主，但常需获得氏族长老、祭司及地方精英认可
succession.en: Primarily hereditary, though succession often required acceptance by clan elders, priests, and local elites

structure.zh: 不存在统一的中央国家；各谷地、高地盆地和海岸聚落由酋长统治，围绕神庙、祖先崇拜地与公共工程组织权力
structure.en: There was no unified central state; valleys, highland basins, and coastal settlements were ruled by chiefs whose authority was organized around temples, ancestor shrines, and public works

function.zh: 主持祭祀、历法仪式、祖先崇拜与跨社群集会，维系首领合法性
function.en: Oversaw ritual observances, calendrical ceremonies, ancestor worship, and inter-community gatherings, sustaining chiefly legitimacy

function.zh: 组织水渠维护、梯田修筑、土台和公共建筑建设
function.en: Organized canal maintenance, terrace construction, and building of platform mounds and public architecture

function.zh: 征集剩余产品、管理仓储、协调节庆与灾荒时的再分配
function.en: Collected surplus produce, managed storage, and coordinated redistribution during festivals and shortages

function.zh: 管理纺织、陶器、石器、贝壳与金属初级制品的生产和区域交换
function.en: Managed production and regional exchange of textiles, pottery, stone goods, shell valuables, and early metal items

function.zh: 召集战士、守卫聚落、组织劳役与应对邻近竞争者
function.en: Summoned warriors, guarded settlements, organized communal labor, and responded to nearby rivals

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托村社首领、氏族长老与神庙管理者运作，权力在谷地和聚落层级分散
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through village headmen, clan elders, and temple managers, with authority dispersed at valley and settlement levels

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，由亲族规范、祭祀禁忌、酋长裁决和补偿性调解构成，严重纠纷可能以驱逐、赔偿或血亲和解处理
legalSystem.en: Law was mainly customary, based on kinship norms, ritual taboos, chiefly judgment, and compensatory mediation; serious disputes could be handled through expulsion, compensation, or kin-based reconciliation

taxationSystem.zh: 无标准化货币税制，主要以劳役、农渔产品、纺织品和手工业品形式向酋长与祭祀中心输纳
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized monetary tax system; obligations were rendered chiefly in labor, agricultural and fishing produce, textiles, and craft goods to chiefs and ceremonial centers

religion.zh: 以多神信仰、祖先崇拜和自然崇拜为核心，重视山岳、海洋、猛禽、猫科动物与蛇等神圣象征，并深受沙文宗教艺术圈影响
religion.en: Religion centered on polytheism, ancestor worship, and nature cults, emphasizing sacred mountains, the sea, birds of prey, felines, and serpents, with strong influence from the Chavín religious-art sphere

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系哲学传统，但宇宙观强调人与祖先、地景、季节循环和祭祀秩序之间的平衡
philosophy.en: No formal philosophical tradition had emerged, but worldview emphasized balance among people, ancestors, landscape, seasonal cycles, and ritual order

writingSystem.zh: 尚无已知成熟文字系统，信息主要通过口述传统、图像符号、纺织图案和仪式记忆传承
writingSystem.en: There was no known mature writing system; information was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, visual symbolism, textile patterns, and ritual memory

culturalAchievements.zh: 区域文化在精细纺织、彩绘与刻纹陶器、宗教图像、地下与土台建筑、海岸渔业技术以及早期冶金与宝石、贝壳装饰方面成就突出
culturalAchievements.en: Regional cultures excelled in fine textiles, painted and incised ceramics, religious iconography, subterranean and platform architecture, coastal fishing technology, and early metallurgy and ornamentation in stone and shell

languageFamily.zh: 语言高度多样，包含已消失或未明分类的安第斯本地语言，部分地区可能存在早期克丘亚或其他高地语群前身
languageFamily.en: Languages were highly diverse, including local Andean tongues now lost or unclassified; some regions may have contained early predecessors of Quechuan or other highland language groups

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、渔业、牧业和祭祀中心主导的再分配为基础，生产力在前国家社会中较高，但区域差异显著，沿海与高地互补明显
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, fishing, pastoralism, and ceremonial-center redistribution, with relatively strong productivity for a pre-state society but marked regional variation and clear coast-highland complementarity

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、渔业、骆驼科放牧、纺织、陶器制作、石器加工、建筑劳役与贝壳及矿石装饰品生产
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, fishing, camelid herding, textile production, pottery making, stoneworking, construction labor, and manufacture of shell and mineral ornaments

tradeGoods.zh: 棉花、渔产品、玉米、豆类、葫芦、辣椒、纺织品、黑曜石、青金石替代矿石、羽毛、海贝、仙人掌致幻物、金属小件与宗教器物
tradeGoods.en: Cotton, fish products, maize, beans, gourds, chili peppers, textiles, obsidian, decorative minerals, feathers, marine shell, psychoactive cactus products, small metal items, and ritual objects

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、灌溉水权、羊驼和美洲驼数量、渔具、纺织品储备及与祭祀中心的关系
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through land use rights, irrigation access, numbers of llamas and alpacas, fishing gear, textile stores, and ties to ceremonial centers

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、亲族合作、酋长再分配和跨生态区交换为特征的区域性互补经济
economicSystem.en: A regionally complementary economy characterized by community production, kin cooperation, chiefly redistribution, and exchange across ecological zones

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非统一金库，而是分散在各祭祀中心和酋长仓储中的粮食、纺织品、贝壳珍物和可供再分配的劳动力义务
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a single vault but a dispersed stock of food, textiles, shell valuables, and claimable labor obligations held in ceremonial centers and chiefly storehouses

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持祭祀权威、灌溉设施与再分配功能，审慎积储以应对厄尔尼诺式气候波动和局部歉收
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining ritual authority, irrigation infrastructure, and redistributive functions, while storing prudent reserves against El Niño-type climatic shocks and local crop shortfalls

description.zh: 由酋长直属亲兵、随从和仪式武士组成，负责守卫中心聚落、护卫精英并在冲突中充当核心力量
description.en: Composed of chiefs' personal guards, retainers, and ceremonial warriors, serving as settlement guards, elite escorts, and the core force in conflict

description.zh: 战时由各村社和氏族征调，使用木棒、投石索、矛和石刃武器，适合短期地方冲突
description.en: Raised in wartime from villages and clans, using clubs, slings, spears, and stone-bladed weapons, suited for short local conflicts

description.zh: 来自高地牧业社群，具备山地行军和运输能力，负责侦察、突袭和道路控制
description.en: Drawn from highland pastoral communities, possessing mountain mobility and transport capacity for scouting, raiding, and route control

commanderInChief.zh: 各地酋长及其指定战首
commanderInChief.en: Local chiefs and their appointed war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术处于前铁器阶段，主要装备投石索、长矛、木棒、石斧、棉甲和简易盾牌；依靠地形、防御工事和集体动员作战
technology.en: Military technology remained pre-iron, relying mainly on slings, spears, clubs, stone axes, quilted cotton armor, and simple shields; warfare depended on terrain, fortification, and communal mobilization

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近谷地和高地群体间对水源、耕地、贸易路线和祭祀声望的竞争，而非大规模帝国战争
threats.en: The chief threats came from competition among neighboring valleys and highland groups over water, farmland, trade routes, and ritual prestige rather than large imperial wars

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于安第斯海岸河谷、高地盆地与山麓地带，沿海聚落密度较高，高地则依赖农业与牧业复合生计
populationDescription.en: Population was distributed across Andean coastal river valleys, highland basins, and foothill zones, with denser settlement on some coasts and mixed farming-pastoral lifeways in the highlands

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括酋长与家族显贵、祭司、战士与随从、工匠、农民、渔民、牧民及在大型工程中被征调的普通劳作者
socialClasses.en: Social strata included chiefs and noble kin groups, priests, warriors and retainers, artisans, farmers, fishers, pastoralists, and common laborers mobilized for major works

allies.zh: 以邻近亲族群体、互市伙伴和受共同宗教影响的区域酋邦为主，联盟多短期且依婚姻、祭祀和互惠建立
allies.en: Allies consisted mainly of neighboring kin groups, exchange partners, and regional chiefdoms sharing religious ties; alliances were often short-term and built through marriage, ritual, and reciprocity

enemies.zh: 敌对者主要是相邻竞争酋邦和争夺水源、耕地及贸易节点的地方群体
enemies.en: Enemies were chiefly neighboring rival chiefdoms and local groups competing for water, farmland, and trade nodes

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持区域平衡、扩展宗教声望、保障跨生态交换通道和避免持续性毁灭战争为目标
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve regional balance, extend ritual prestige, secure inter-ecological exchange routes, and avoid sustained destructive warfare

era.zh: 前铁器时代晚期安第斯区域文化阶段
era.en: Late pre-iron regional Andean cultural stage

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括灌溉渠系、梯田雏形、精密纺织、投石索战术、复杂陶器烧制、棉纤维加工、芦苇与木材海岸舟筏技术以及少量金属冷加工与锤揲工艺
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included irrigation canal systems, early terraces, refined textiles, sling warfare, complex ceramic firing, cotton processing, reed and wooden coastal watercraft, and limited cold-hammered and beaten metalworking

strengths.zh: 优势在于多生态区资源互补、强韧的村社组织、发达的祭祀整合能力、优秀纺织和手工业传统，以及在干旱环境中管理水利的经验
strengths.en: Strengths included complementary resources across ecological zones, resilient community organization, strong ritual integration, highly developed textile and craft traditions, and experience managing water in arid environments

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、缺乏统一指挥、对气候异常和灌溉失效高度敏感，以及地区冲突容易破坏交换网络
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included political fragmentation, lack of unified command, high vulnerability to climatic anomalies and irrigation failure, and the ease with which local conflicts could disrupt exchange networks

outlook.zh: 800年前后整体局势稳定，区域文化将在与沙文宗教网络的互动中继续发展，并可能孕育更强的礼仪中心和更复杂的酋邦结构
outlook.en: Around 800 BCE the overall situation was stable, and these regional cultures were likely to continue developing through interaction with the Chavín religious network, potentially giving rise to stronger ceremonial centers and more complex chiefdoms

description.zh: 安第斯沿海與高地由多個地方文化與酋邦構成，與查文文化互有交流。
description.en: The Andean coast and highlands contained many regional cultures and chiefdoms interacting with the Chavin sphere.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，以部族长老传统为主
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; organized through tribal elder traditions

governmentForm.zh: 由村落与地域群体构成的部族性社会，权威主要来自长老威望、狩猎能力与祭仪地位。
governmentForm.en: A tribal society of villages and regional groups, where authority derived chiefly from elder prestige, hunting ability, and ritual status.

socialStructure.zh: 社会基本单位是以亲族关系为核心的聚落群体。内部区分不强，但有更受尊重的长老、猎手、祭仪主持者和熟练工匠。社会协作高度依赖互赠、共同狩猎和季节性资源分配。
socialStructure.en: The basic social unit was the settlement group organized around kinship ties. Internal stratification was limited, though elders, skilled hunters, ritual specialists, and craftsmen enjoyed higher esteem. Social cooperation depended heavily on gift exchange, collective hunting, and seasonal resource sharing.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在固定的世袭贵族阶层，但部分家族因掌握渔猎资源、婚姻联系与祭祀权威而居于优势。长老的影响力通常大于强制权力。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed hereditary aristocracy, but some families held advantage through access to hunting and fishing resources, marriage ties, and ritual authority. Elder influence usually outweighed coercive power.

succession.zh: 领导权多由聚落内部推举经验丰富者承继，可在同一家族内延续，但并非严格王位继承。若领袖失去威望，群体可转而支持其他长老。
succession.en: Leadership was usually passed to an experienced figure chosen within the settlement, sometimes continuing within the same family but not as strict royal succession. If a leader lost prestige, the group could shift support to another elder.

structure.zh: 阿伊努文化在前8世纪并非统一国家，而是由北海道及邻近区域的多个聚落网络组成。治理依靠聚落会议、长老协调和祭仪共识。跨聚落事务通常通过婚姻、互访与交换关系解决。
structure.en: In the 8th century BCE, Ainu culture was not a unified state but a network of settlements across Hokkaido and nearby regions. Governance relied on settlement councils, elder mediation, and ritual consensus. Inter-settlement matters were usually handled through marriage ties, visits, and exchange relations.

function.zh: 协调食物分配、季节迁徙和纠纷调解。
function.en: Coordinated food distribution, seasonal movement, and dispute mediation.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、维系盟友互赠与聚落间联络。
function.en: Conducted rituals and maintained gift ties and communication between settlements.

localAdmin.zh: 不存在正式官僚地方行政，各聚落由长老和家族首领协商管理。资源丰富的河口、沿海和内陆猎场往往形成较稳定的地域性核心。
localAdmin.en: There was no formal bureaucratic local administration; settlements were managed by elders and household heads through consultation. Resource-rich estuaries, coasts, and inland hunting grounds often formed relatively stable regional cores.

legalSystem.zh: 规范主要来自习俗、禁忌、口传传统与赔偿原则。冲突通常通过长老调停、礼物赔偿与关系修复来解决。
legalSystem.en: Norms came mainly from custom, taboo, oral tradition, and principles of compensation. Conflicts were usually resolved through elder mediation, gift compensation, and restoration of relationships.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有国家税制，公共需要主要通过共同劳动、共享猎获和首领家庭的再分配满足。对外交换中的贡礼性质互赠比固定税更重要。
taxationSystem.en: There was no state taxation; common needs were met mainly through communal labor, shared catches, and redistribution by leading households. In external exchange, tribute-like gifting mattered more than fixed taxes.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 这一时期形成了以渔猎、木工、编织、骨角器和仪礼器物为特色的北方文化传统。口头叙事、熊祭原型与精致的日用器具反映出高度适应寒冷生态环境的生活方式。
culturalAchievements.en: This period developed a northern cultural tradition marked by fishing, hunting, woodworking, weaving, and bone-antler implements. Oral narrative, early bear-ritual forms, and finely made everyday objects reflect a way of life highly adapted to cold environments.

languageFamily.zh: 阿伊努语系（通常视为孤立语言）
languageFamily.en: Ainu language family (commonly treated as a language isolate)

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、人与自然互惠和祭仪维系秩序的宇宙观为核心。
philosophy.en: A worldview centered on animism, reciprocity between humans and nature, and ritual maintenance of cosmic order.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土书写系统，知识以口传、歌谣、仪式叙事与技艺传授保存。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; knowledge was preserved through oral transmission, song, ritual narrative, and craft instruction.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以渔猎、采集、有限农业和家庭手工业为主，市场化程度很低。财富更适合以食物安全、器具、舟船和人际互助网络衡量，而非单纯金属货币。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on fishing, hunting, gathering, limited agriculture, and household crafts, with very low marketization. Wealth is better measured in food security, tools, boats, and networks of mutual support than in metallic money alone.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、狩猎、采集、木工、编织、骨角器制作
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, hunting, gathering, woodworking, weaving, bone and antler craft

tradeGoods.zh: 鱼干、海兽制品、兽皮、骨角器、木器、黑曜石与地方特产
tradeGoods.en: Dried fish, marine animal products, furs, bone-antler goods, wooden goods, obsidian, and local specialties

name.zh: 无统一货币，以实物交换为主
name.en: No unified currency; barter in goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在鱼干储备、皮毛、舟船、狩猎工具与冬季居所品质。拥有稳定鲑鱼河段和良好交换关系的家庭通常更富足。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in stores of dried fish, furs, boats, hunting tools, and the quality of winter housing. Families controlling reliable salmon stretches and strong exchange ties were usually better off.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以亲族共享、季节性采集渔猎和互赠交换为核心的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on kin-based sharing, seasonal harvesting, and gift-based exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“公库”主要是分散式储备，包括干鱼、皮毛、武器、木舟和祭仪用品，而非集中金属库存。其价值依季节与资源丰歉而波动很大。
treasuryDescription.en: The effective 'treasury' was a dispersed reserve of dried fish, furs, weapons, boats, and ritual items rather than concentrated metal stocks. Its value fluctuated strongly with season and resource abundance.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践重在保障冬季生存、稳定互助网络并维持祭仪秩序。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on winter survival, stable mutual-aid networks, and the maintenance of ritual order.

description.zh: 由成年猎手组成，平时用于狩猎，冲突时转为近战与伏击力量。
description.en: Made up of adult hunters, they served in hunting during peace and became close-combat and ambush forces in conflict.

description.zh: 依靠小型舟船进行沿岸巡查、运输和小规模袭击或防御。
description.en: Using small boats, they carried out coastal watch, transport, and small-scale raiding or defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 资深部族长老与首席猎手协同指挥
commanderInChief.en: Senior tribal elders and leading hunters acting jointly

technology.zh: 武备以弓箭、矛、木盾、骨角器和少量铁器或青铜外来品为主。战斗方式偏重伏击、地形利用和近距离突袭。
technology.en: Arms centered on bows, spears, wooden shields, bone-antler equipment, and a limited number of imported iron or bronze items. Combat emphasized ambush, terrain use, and close-range raids.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要风险来自聚落间资源竞争、寒冷环境导致的生存压力，以及与邻近文化群体围绕渔猎地带的摩擦。
threats.en: Main risks came from inter-settlement competition for resources, survival pressure from the harsh climate, and friction with neighboring cultural groups over fishing and hunting zones.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约三万，分散于北海道及附近岛屿、沿海、河谷与内陆猎场。聚落规模较小，季节性移动明显，人口密度极低。
populationDescription.en: The total population of roughly 30,000 was dispersed across Hokkaido and nearby islands, coasts, river valleys, and inland hunting grounds. Settlements were small, mobility was strongly seasonal, and population density was very low.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与首领家庭、熟练猎手与祭仪主持者、普通家庭成员、依附亲族与战俘性从属者（规模有限）。
socialClasses.en: Elders and leading households, skilled hunters and ritual specialists, ordinary family members, and dependent kin or captive subordinates on a limited scale.

allies.zh: 以相邻聚落、婚姻联盟和交换伙伴为主要盟友形式。
allies.en: Alliances took the form mainly of neighboring settlements, marriage ties, and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为争夺河口、渔场和狩猎地的邻近群体，而非远程国家。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually nearby groups contesting estuaries, fishing sites, and hunting grounds rather than distant states.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外策略以维持资源边界、建立互赠关系和避免长期消耗性冲突为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy aimed mainly to preserve resource boundaries, build gift relations, and avoid prolonged costly conflict.

era.zh: 北方铁器接触期与后绳文—续绳文文化背景
era.en: Northern Iron-contact phase within the post-Jomon to Epi-Jomon cultural background

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器零星输入、改良渔猎工具、木舟与冬季居住技术、骨角器精制和复杂口传知识体系。
keyInnovations.en: Limited import of iron tools, improved fishing and hunting gear, boat and winter dwelling technology, fine bone-antler craftsmanship, and complex oral knowledge systems.

strengths.zh: 生态适应能力强、机动性高、共同体互助紧密、渔猎资源利用成熟。
strengths.en: It possessed strong ecological adaptation, high mobility, tight communal support, and mature use of fishing and hunting resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、政治整合有限、粮食剩余低、难以长期组织大规模战争。
weaknesses.en: Population was sparse, political integration limited, food surplus low, and large-scale sustained warfare difficult to organize.

outlook.zh: 只要生态条件稳定，社会可长期延续；但一旦外来人口、疾病或贸易结构变化加剧，其自主空间将受压缩。
outlook.en: The society could endure for long periods so long as ecological conditions remained stable, but intensified outside migration, disease, or trade change would narrow its autonomy.

description.zh: 前8世纪的阿伊努文化是北海道及周边地区以亲族聚落、渔猎经济和万物有灵信仰为核心的北方部族社会。
description.en: The Ainu culture of the 8th century BCE was a northern tribal society in Hokkaido and surrounding areas centered on kin settlements, fishing-hunting economies, and animist belief.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，以诸岛酋邦与部族首领传统为主
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; organized through island chiefdoms and tribal leadership traditions

governmentForm.zh: 由海岛酋长、亲族领袖和仪式权威共同构成的分散型部族—酋邦体制。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal-chiefdom system shaped by island chiefs, kin leaders, and ritual authorities.

socialStructure.zh: 南岛语族各群体社会形态差异较大，但普遍以家族、氏族与村社为基础。多数地区存在酋长家族、自由民、依附者与战俘从属者等层次。沿海聚落通常比内陆更开放，更依赖航海、交换与联盟婚姻。
socialStructure.en: Austronesian groups varied widely in social form, but were generally based on households, clans, and village communities. Many areas had layers such as chiefly families, free commoners, dependents, and captive subordinates. Coastal settlements were often more outward-looking, relying more on seafaring, exchange, and alliance marriage than inland communities.

rulingClass.zh: 统治集团主要是酋长家族、航海首领、祭司与战士精英。其地位建立在血缘合法性、财富再分配和对舟船与外部交换网络的控制上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted mainly of chiefly families, maritime leaders, priests, and warrior elites. Their status rested on lineage legitimacy, redistribution of wealth, and control over boats and external exchange networks.

succession.zh: 首领职位多带世袭色彩，通常在酋长家族内部传承，但也需获得长老、战士与祭仪共同体认可。若竞争激烈，分支迁徙与另立新聚落是常见解决方式。
succession.en: Chiefly office often had a hereditary character, usually passing within the chiefly family, but it also required recognition from elders, warriors, and ritual communities. When rivalry intensified, branch migration and the founding of new settlements were common solutions.

structure.zh: 南岛语族在前8世纪不是统一政体，而是分布于台湾、菲律宾、印尼群岛及更广阔海域的众多酋邦与部落网络。治理核心是村社议事、酋长再分配和祭仪协调。跨岛权威有限，但海上联系能够形成短期同盟与贸易圈。
structure.en: In the 8th century BCE, the Austronesian peoples did not form a single polity but a wide array of chiefdoms and tribal networks across Taiwan, the Philippines, the Indonesian archipelago, and broader seas. Governance centered on village deliberation, chiefly redistribution, and ritual coordination. Supra-island authority was limited, but maritime links could create temporary alliances and trade spheres.

function.zh: 组织村社决策、粮食分配、公共劳作与婚盟安排。
function.en: Handled village decisions, food redistribution, communal labor, and marriage alliances.

function.zh: 统筹远航、物资交换、祭海仪式与对外联络。
function.en: Managed voyaging, exchange of goods, sea rituals, and external contacts.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落与海湾共同体为单位，岛屿之间高度分散。个别大聚落能对周边小村施加影响，但通常依靠威望、再分配与结盟，而非正式官僚命令。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated at the level of villages and bay communities, with strong fragmentation between islands. Some larger settlements could influence surrounding hamlets, but usually through prestige, redistribution, and alliance rather than formal bureaucratic command.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法、禁忌、祖灵规范与酋长裁决。涉及婚姻、航海事故、土地使用和赔偿的事务多由长老会或酋长主持解决。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted mainly of custom, taboo, ancestral norms, and chiefly judgment. Matters involving marriage, voyaging accidents, land use, and compensation were typically settled by councils of elders or chiefs.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有统一税收制度，公共资源主要来自贡礼、共同劳动、渔获分成与酋长主导的再分配。贸易中也可抽取部分交换利润或港湾停泊礼物。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified tax system; public resources came mainly from tribute gifts, communal labor, shares of catches, and chiefly redistribution. Trade could also yield portions of exchange profit or harbor gifts.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 其重要成就在于远洋与近海航海技术、独木舟与外伸浮具船、园圃农业和广泛的语言扩散。精细编织、木雕、贝饰与口传传统体现出高度发展的海岛文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Their major achievements lay in oceanic and coastal navigation, dugout and outrigger craft, horticulture, and broad linguistic expansion. Fine weaving, wood carving, shell ornament, and oral traditions reflect highly developed island cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系
languageFamily.en: Austronesian language family

philosophy.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、万物有灵、航海宇宙观和人与海洋环境互惠关系为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor veneration, animism, a maritime cosmology, and reciprocal relations between people and the sea environment.

writingSystem.zh: 前8世纪多数南岛群体尚无成熟本土文字，知识依靠口传史诗、谱系记忆、歌谣与航海传统保存。
writingSystem.en: By the 8th century BCE most Austronesian groups lacked mature indigenous writing systems; knowledge was preserved through oral epics, genealogical memory, song, and navigational tradition.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础是园圃农业、渔业、椰子与块根作物生产、家畜饲养和岛际交换。部分沿海群体通过航海贸易获得额外财富，使其经济活力高于典型内陆部族社会。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on horticulture, fishing, coconut and root-crop production, animal husbandry, and inter-island exchange. Some coastal groups gained additional wealth through maritime trade, giving them greater dynamism than typical inland tribal societies.

mainIndustries.zh: 园圃农业、渔业、造船、编织、木工、贝壳与石器工艺
mainIndustries.en: Horticulture, fishing, boatbuilding, weaving, woodworking, shell and stone craft

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、贝饰、陶器、纺织品、盐、鱼干、椰制品、木材、树脂、香料性植物
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, shell ornaments, pottery, textiles, salt, dried fish, coconut products, timber, resins, and aromatic plants

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为船只、果园与园圃、猪鸡等家畜、陶器、纺织品和交换伙伴。拥有大船和远航能力的家族通常更具威望与富裕程度。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in boats, orchards and garden plots, pigs and fowl, pottery, textiles, and exchange partners. Families with large vessels and voyaging capacity usually enjoyed greater prestige and prosperity.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、酋长再分配和岛际互赠交换相结合的海岛经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An island economic system combining household production, chiefly redistribution, and inter-island gift exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Chiefly tribute gifts and crop shares

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Shares of catches and salt production

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共财富主要分散在船只、储粮、贝饰、陶器、家畜和祭仪器物之中，而不是集中金属库存。富裕酋长能通过大型宴飨与远航组织表现其“财政”实力。
treasuryDescription.en: Public wealth was held mainly in boats, food stores, shell valuables, pottery, livestock, and ritual items rather than in concentrated metal reserves. Wealthy chiefs displayed fiscal strength through large feasts and the organization of long voyages.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践强调再分配、维持航海能力和通过礼物巩固联盟关系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized redistribution, the maintenance of voyaging capacity, and the strengthening of alliances through gifts.

description.zh: 由成年男性组成，负责村落防卫、袭击和冲突中的主力作战。
description.en: Composed of adult men, they handled village defense, raiding, and the main fighting role in conflict.

description.zh: 依托独木舟和外伸浮具船进行沿海作战、掠袭与护送贸易航行。
description.en: Using dugouts and outrigger craft, they conducted coastal fighting, raiding, and protection of trading voyages.

commanderInChief.zh: 部族酋长与航海首领
commanderInChief.en: Tribal chief and maritime leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以木矛、弓箭、棍棒、藤甲或木盾为主，部分地区开始接触铁器。真正优势在于造船、航海、沿岸机动和对岛屿地形与海流的熟悉。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wooden spears, bows, clubs, rattan armor or wooden shields, with some regions beginning to encounter iron. The real advantage lay in boatbuilding, navigation, coastal mobility, and familiarity with island terrain and currents.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 威胁主要来自岛际掠袭、资源争夺、风暴造成的航海损失，以及与其他海岛群体竞争港湾与贸易节点。
threats.en: Threats came mainly from inter-island raiding, competition for resources, storm-related maritime losses, and rivalry with other island groups over harbors and trade nodes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约六万，分散在多个岛屿、海湾和河口聚落，分布极不均匀。大型聚落通常靠近良港、淡水、渔场和适宜园圃农业的地带。
populationDescription.en: The population of about 60,000 was spread across many islands, bays, and estuarine settlements, with very uneven distribution. Larger settlements typically stood near good harbors, fresh water, fishing grounds, and land suitable for horticulture.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族与祭司、航海与战士精英、自由村民、依附者、战俘或奴属者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families and priests, maritime and warrior elites, free villagers, dependents, and captives or slave-like subordinates.

allies.zh: 盟友多由婚盟岛屿、贸易伙伴港湾和共同祖源的邻近群体构成。
allies.en: Allies were usually marriage-linked islands, trading harbors, and nearby groups claiming common ancestry.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是竞争航道、渔场和港湾控制权的其他岛群与酋邦。
enemies.en: Enemies were mainly other island groups and chiefdoms competing for sea lanes, fishing grounds, and harbor control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策偏向以贸易、婚盟和仪式结盟扩大影响，必要时辅以海上掠袭和报复性行动。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy tended to expand influence through trade, marriage alliances, and ritual compacts, with sea raiding and retaliatory action used when necessary.

era.zh: 海岛东南亚铁器接触初期与晚期新石器—金属转型阶段
era.en: Early Iron-contact phase in Island Southeast Asia during the late Neolithic-to-metal transition

keyInnovations.zh: 外伸浮具船技术、星象与海流导航、园圃农业体系、陶器制作、岛际交换网络和局部铁器采用。
keyInnovations.en: Outrigger vessel technology, navigation by stars and currents, horticultural systems, pottery making, inter-island exchange networks, and localized adoption of iron.

strengths.zh: 航海能力强、流动性高、文化扩散力大、适应多岛生态环境并能建立广泛交换网络。
strengths.en: They possessed strong seafaring ability, high mobility, broad cultural diffusion, and the capacity to adapt to multi-island ecologies while building wide exchange networks.

weaknesses.zh: 政治统一程度低、资源分散、易受气候和风暴影响，难以形成长期集权国家。
weaknesses.en: Political unity was low, resources were dispersed, climatic and storm risks were high, and long-term centralized states were difficult to form.

outlook.zh: 随着航海与交换网络扩张，这些群体将持续向更复杂的酋邦和区域海上体系发展，但仍会保持高度地方性。
outlook.en: As voyaging and exchange networks expanded, these groups were likely to develop toward more complex chiefdoms and regional maritime systems while remaining highly local in character.

description.zh: 前8世纪的南岛语族诸群体是分布于海岛东亚与东南亚、以航海、园圃农业和酋邦网络为特征的活跃海洋社会。
description.en: The Austronesian peoples of the 8th century BCE were dynamic maritime societies across island East and Southeast Asia, marked by seafaring, horticulture, and chiefdom networks.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族与营地为基础的游牧部族联盟，由牧民首领通过协商与威望进行统治。
governmentForm.en: A camp- and clan-based nomadic tribal confederacy ruled by a pastoralist headman through consensus and prestige.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以扩展家族、氏族和临时结盟的部落为核心，财富主要以牛、羊、山羊与骆驼等牲畜数量衡量。成年男子多负责放牧、护卫与远途迁徙，妇女承担帐幕管理、乳制品加工与家庭手工业，奴婢和依附者数量有限但在部分群体中存在。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on extended families, clans, and temporary tribal alliances, with wealth measured chiefly in cattle, sheep, goats, and camels. Adult men mainly handled herding, protection, and long-distance migration, while women managed tents, dairy processing, and household crafts; small numbers of dependents and slaves likely existed in some groups.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层主要由声望最高的氏族长老、富裕牧群拥有者和掌握水源路线的首领组成。他们的权威依赖慷慨分配、调解纠纷与在危机时组织武装随从的能力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted mainly of senior clan elders, wealthy herd owners, and leaders who controlled access to water routes. Their authority rested on generosity, dispute mediation, and the ability to organize armed followers in times of crisis.

succession.zh: 继承通常不完全固定，往往在首领家族内部择贤而立，并需得到主要氏族长老承认。若继承人缺乏威望，联盟可迅速分裂为多个独立营地。
succession.en: Succession was not entirely fixed, usually favoring a capable man within the headman’s family who also required recognition from major clan elders. If an heir lacked prestige, the confederacy could quickly fragment into separate camps.

structure.zh: 政务规模很小，核心是首领会议与长老协商。日常管理围绕迁徙路线、水井分配、牲畜纠纷和对外交换展开，没有常设官僚体系。
structure.en: Government was very small in scale, centered on the headman’s council and elder consultation. Daily administration focused on migration routes, well allocation, herd disputes, and external exchange, with no permanent bureaucracy.

function.zh: 协调季节迁徙、分配井泉使用次序并处理营地间争端。
function.en: Coordinated seasonal migration, allocated access to wells and springs, and handled disputes between camps.

function.zh: 统计贡纳牲畜、组织与绿洲居民的交换，并筹措首领馈赠。
function.en: Recorded livestock tribute, organized exchange with oasis dwellers, and assembled goods for the headman’s gift distribution.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由各氏族长、牧群领队和营地主母共同维系。距离核心首领较远的营地通常高度自治，只在战争、迁徙和祭仪时接受统一协调。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was maintained by clan heads, herd leaders, and camp matrons. Camps far from the chief center were usually highly autonomous, accepting unified coordination mainly during war, migration, and ritual gatherings.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和长老裁决为主，重视血偿、牲畜赔偿和婚姻协议。严重冲突往往通过调解、赠礼或暂时分营来化解。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom and elder arbitration, emphasizing blood compensation, livestock restitution, and marriage agreements. Serious conflicts were often resolved through mediation, gift exchange, or temporary camp separation.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有统一定额税，首领主要通过贡纳牲畜、战利品分配和商路保护回报取得资源。遇到干旱或战争时，各氏族需按畜群规模提供额外供给。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform fixed tax; the headman drew resources mainly from livestock tribute, shares of booty, and returns for route protection. In drought or war, clans had to provide additional support according to herd size.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 他们发展出适应干旱环境的放牧知识、井泉记忆体系和长距离导航传统。歌谣、家族谱系和仪式性身体装饰构成文化传承的重要载体。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed pastoral knowledge suited to arid environments, systems of remembering wells, and traditions of long-distance navigation. Songs, genealogies, and ritual body decoration were key vehicles of cultural transmission.

languageFamily.zh: 阿非罗-亚细亚语系诸方言与地方混合语
languageFamily.en: Afroasiatic dialects and local mixed speech forms

philosophy.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、自然神灵敬畏和迁徙生存经验为核心的口传世界观。
philosophy.en: An oral worldview centered on ancestor reverence, respect for natural spirits, and the lived knowledge of migratory survival.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土成文文字，信息主要依靠口述、记忆和标记性符号传递。
writingSystem.en: No native fully developed writing system; information was transmitted mainly through oral memory and simple marks.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总量较小，以牲畜、乳制品、皮革和有限商路服务为主。财富高度分散于畜群之中，实际产出常受降雨和草场条件波动影响。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was modest in total size, based on livestock, dairy products, hides, and limited caravan-route services. Wealth was heavily stored in herds, and actual output fluctuated strongly with rainfall and pasture conditions.

mainIndustries.zh: 游牧放牧、乳制品加工、皮革制作、绿洲交换与商旅引导
mainIndustries.en: Nomadic pastoralism, dairy processing, leatherwork, oasis exchange, and caravan guiding

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、皮张、酥乳、盐、羽毛、石料与少量铜器
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, hides, clarified dairy products, salt, feathers, stone goods, and small amounts of copperware

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为小型羊群、少量牛只和帐幕器具，富裕家庭则控制较大的牲畜群与通往可靠水源的优先权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly represented by small flocks, a few cattle, and tent equipment, while wealthy families controlled larger herds and priority access to dependable water sources.

economicSystem.zh: 以自给性牧业为基础，辅以礼物交换、互助分配和小规模跨区域贸易的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on subsistence pastoralism, supplemented by gift exchange, mutual redistribution, and small-scale interregional trade.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非固定金库，而是以首领家族畜群、储藏皮革和盟友所欠人情债构成的流动财富。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a fixed vault but a mobile store of wealth consisting of the headman’s herds, stored hides, and obligations owed by allies.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守，优先维持首领威望、旱灾缓冲和部族团结，而非长期积累贵金属。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, prioritizing the headman’s prestige, drought buffering, and tribal cohesion rather than long-term accumulation of precious metal.

description.zh: 依靠骆驼与驴进行远距侦察、护送和追踪。
description.en: Used camels and donkeys for long-range scouting, escort, and tracking.

description.zh: 以长矛、棍棒和简易皮盾作战，主要承担营地防御和突袭。
description.en: Fought with spears, clubs, and simple hide shields, mainly for camp defense and raids.

commanderInChief.zh: 牧民首领
commanderInChief.en: Pastoralist Headman

technology.zh: 军事技术较低，主要装备为木杆长矛、投枪、皮盾和少量铜铁刀具，优势在机动与地形熟悉。
technology.en: Military technology was limited, with wooden spears, javelins, hide shields, and small numbers of copper or iron blades; strengths lay in mobility and knowledge of terrain.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自争夺水源的邻近部落、绿洲定居者的报复，以及长期干旱引发的人口迁徙冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribes competing for water, reprisals from oasis settlers, and migration conflicts triggered by prolonged drought.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于多个季节性迁徙营地，人口密度极低。婴幼儿死亡率较高，人口增长缓慢且高度受气候制约。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed among multiple seasonal camps with extremely low density. Infant mortality was high, and population growth was slow and strongly constrained by climate.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族与富牧户、普通牧民、依附者与少量奴婢。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families and wealthy herd owners, common pastoralists, dependents, and a small number of slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分绿洲居民及相邻牧民群体保持临时互市和婚姻联盟。
allies.en: Maintained temporary trade and marriage alliances with some oasis communities and neighboring pastoral groups.

enemies.zh: 与竞争井泉和草场的部族常有敌对关系，也可能袭击缺乏保护的商旅。
enemies.en: Frequently hostile to tribes competing for wells and pasture, and they could also raid poorly protected caravans.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在维持迁徙通道、获取交换资源并避免被更强定居政体长期压制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving migration corridors, obtaining exchange goods, and avoiding sustained domination by stronger settled polities.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期的撒哈拉游牧文化
era.en: Early Iron Age Saharan nomadic culture

keyInnovations.zh: 井泉路线记忆、干旱地带畜群管理、简易金属刀具使用与长程沙漠导航。
keyInnovations.en: Memorized well routes, arid-zone herd management, use of simple metal blades, and long-range desert navigation.

strengths.zh: 机动性强、适应干旱环境、对商路和水源有深入认知。
strengths.en: Highly mobile, adapted to arid conditions, and deeply knowledgeable about routes and water sources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、组织松散、财政与军力都依赖畜群和气候条件。
weaknesses.en: Sparse population, loose organization, and finances and military strength both depended on herds and climate.

outlook.zh: 若降雨稳定并扩大与绿洲的交换，其联盟可保持延续；若旱灾加剧，则易分裂和外迁。
outlook.en: If rainfall remained stable and exchange with oases expanded, the confederacy could endure; if drought worsened, fragmentation and migration were likely.

description.zh: 这是一个以牲畜、井泉和季节迁徙为生命线的撒哈拉早期铁器时代游牧部族联盟。
description.en: This was an Early Iron Age Saharan nomadic tribal confederacy whose lifeline was livestock, wells, and seasonal migration.

governmentForm.zh: 由长老主持的松散部落社会，以亲族关系和集体协商维持秩序。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal society led by elders, with order maintained through kinship ties and collective discussion.

socialStructure.zh: 社会单位主要是小型采猎群与季节性聚会营地，成员按血缘和婚姻关系组织。劳动分工以年龄和性别为基础，男性多负责狩猎与远程搜索，女性多负责采集、育儿和轻型器物制作。
socialStructure.en: The basic social unit was the small foraging band and the seasonal gathering camp, organized through kinship and marriage. Labor was divided mainly by age and gender, with men often handling hunting and long-range searching, while women focused on gathering, childrearing, and light craft production.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在严格意义上的世袭贵族，影响力集中在经验丰富的长老、猎手首领和祭仪主持者手中。其权威更多来自知识、口才和分配公正，而非强制力。
rulingClass.en: There was no true hereditary aristocracy; influence lay with experienced elders, leading hunters, and ritual specialists. Their authority came more from knowledge, eloquence, and fair distribution than from coercion.

succession.zh: 长老地位通常由年长且受尊敬者自然取得，不存在正式王位继承。若意见不合，部落可分裂为较小群体各自活动。
succession.en: Elder status usually fell naturally to respected senior figures, with no formal throne succession. When disputes arose, bands could split into smaller groups and move separately.

structure.zh: 治理方式极为简约，依赖营地会议和长者劝导。公共事务主要包括水源共享、觅食区域安排、婚姻协调和对外危险预警。
structure.en: Governance was extremely simple, relying on camp meetings and elder persuasion. Public matters mainly involved water sharing, foraging-zone coordination, marriage arrangements, and warnings about external dangers.

function.zh: 安排季节性驻地、协调泉眼使用并调停群体间矛盾。
function.en: Arranged seasonal camps, coordinated use of springs, and mediated tensions between groups.

function.zh: 主持治病、占兆、成年礼与口述传统传承。
function.en: Oversaw healing, divination, initiation rites, and preservation of oral tradition.

localAdmin.zh: 地方层面几乎完全自治，每个采猎群自行决定活动范围。只有在干季集中时，多个群体才会接受长老会议的共同安排。
localAdmin.en: At the local level, bands were almost fully autonomous, each deciding its own range of movement. Only during dry-season concentrations did multiple groups accept joint decisions from an elder council.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法强调共享、互惠与避免公开冲突，争端通常通过赔礼、婚姻调解或短期分离解决。暴力报复受到群体舆论抑制。
legalSystem.en: Customary law emphasized sharing, reciprocity, and avoidance of open conflict, with disputes usually settled through apology, marriage mediation, or temporary separation. Violent revenge was restrained by group opinion.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税收，公共需要通过集体提供食物、皮张和劳力满足。长老在季节性集会时可获得象征性馈赠。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; communal needs were met through collective provision of food, hides, and labor. Elders could receive symbolic gifts during seasonal gatherings.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 他们在追踪猎物、辨识植物和利用零散水源方面拥有高度发达的生态知识。岩画、身体彩绘和仪式舞蹈是其重要文化表达。
culturalAchievements.en: They possessed highly developed ecological knowledge in tracking game, identifying plants, and exploiting scattered water sources. Rock art, body painting, and ritual dance were important forms of cultural expression.

languageFamily.zh: 地方采猎者语言群，可能包含早期科伊桑或其他已失传语支成分
languageFamily.en: Local hunter-gatherer language groups, possibly including early Khoisan-related or otherwise lost branches

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、祖先沟通和人与荒漠环境平衡相处的观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animistic and shamanic ideas of spirit-filled nature, ancestral contact, and balance between people and the desert environment.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，依赖口传故事、岩画符号和地景记忆。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was preserved through oral storytelling, rock art symbols, and remembered landscapes.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性狩猎采集为绝对主导，仅有少量与邻近牧民或绿洲居民的交换。物质积累有限，但环境知识本身是最重要的生计资本。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was overwhelmingly based on subsistence hunting and gathering, with only limited exchange with neighboring pastoralists or oasis peoples. Material accumulation was low, but ecological knowledge itself was the most important livelihood asset.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、皮张处理、骨石器制作与少量交换
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, hide processing, bone-and-stone tool production, and small-scale exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、羽毛、药草、赭石、蛋壳珠与少量野产物
tradeGoods.en: Animal hides, feathers, medicinal herbs, ochre, eggshell beads, and small quantities of wild products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常只有携行工具、皮袋、装饰物和少量储备食物，较少形成可长期积累的大宗财产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth usually consisted only of portable tools, skin bags, ornaments, and small food reserves, with little capacity for long-term accumulation of bulk property.

economicSystem.zh: 以共享、互赠和直接取用自然资源为特征的采猎经济。
economicSystem.en: A foraging economy characterized by sharing, gift exchange, and direct use of natural resources.

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓国库仅是长老群体掌握的仪式物品、珠饰、皮张和少量储藏食物，并无固定仓库。
treasuryDescription.en: The so-called treasury consisted only of ritual objects, beads, hides, and modest food stores held by the elders, with no permanent treasury building.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 资源使用强调即时分享与风险分散，尽量避免少数人长期垄断储备。
fiscalPolicy.en: Resource use emphasized immediate sharing and risk spreading, minimizing long-term monopolization of stores by a few people.

description.zh: 使用轻型弓箭与毒箭，擅长伏击、追踪和小规模冲突。
description.en: Used light bows and often poisoned arrows, excelling in ambush, tracking, and small-scale fighting.

description.zh: 以短矛和棍棒保护营地与水源点。
description.en: Protected camps and water points with short spears and clubs.

commanderInChief.zh: 长老会议推举的猎手首领
commanderInChief.en: Hunter leader chosen by the elder council

technology.zh: 军事技术非常基础，但弓箭、毒素应用、追踪术和地形隐蔽能力相当出色。
technology.en: Military technology was very basic, but archery, poison use, tracking skills, and concealment in terrain were highly effective.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自扩张中的牧民群体、对水源的竞争，以及更有组织的袭击者进入采猎区域。
threats.en: Main threats came from expanding pastoral groups, competition over water sources, and more organized raiders entering foraging zones.

populationDescription.zh: 人口由许多小规模流动群体组成，分布在沙漠边缘与稀疏水源地带。人口恢复能力有限，一旦遭遇连年旱灾或疫病便会迅速下降。
populationDescription.en: The population was made up of many small mobile groups spread across desert margins and sparse water points. Its capacity for recovery was limited, and repeated drought or disease could quickly reduce numbers.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与祭仪者、资深猎手、普通采集家庭、少年与依附者。
socialClasses.en: Elders and ritual specialists, senior hunters, ordinary foraging families, youths, and dependents.

allies.zh: 与部分牧民和邻近采猎群保持低强度互助与婚姻联系。
allies.en: Maintained low-intensity mutual aid and marriage ties with some pastoralists and neighboring forager groups.

enemies.zh: 通常避免长期敌对，但会与侵入水源点的外来群体发生冲突。
enemies.en: Usually avoided long-term enmity, but clashed with outsiders who intruded on water points.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外以回避冲突、保护觅食路线和维持交换关系为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy focused on avoiding conflict, protecting foraging routes, and preserving exchange relations.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期的沙漠采猎文化
era.en: Early Iron Age desert foraging culture

keyInnovations.zh: 毒箭技术、岩画记录、精细追踪术、可携带储水与沙漠植物利用知识。
keyInnovations.en: Poison-arrow technology, rock-art recording, refined tracking, portable water storage, and knowledge of desert plants.

strengths.zh: 生态适应力强、行动隐蔽、共享机制有助于在贫瘠环境中生存。
strengths.en: Strong ecological adaptation, stealthy movement, and sharing mechanisms that helped survival in poor environments.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、缺乏集中权力、难以抵御持续扩张的牧民或国家力量。
weaknesses.en: Small population, lack of centralized power, and difficulty resisting sustained expansion by pastoralists or states.

outlook.zh: 未来多半会维持边缘生存状态，但若周边牧业扩张加剧，其活动范围可能进一步缩小。
outlook.en: They were likely to continue in a marginal survival pattern, but expanding pastoralism around them could further shrink their range.

description.zh: 这是一个依靠狩猎、采集与口传传统在荒漠边缘维持生存的早期铁器时代部落社会。
description.en: This was an Early Iron Age tribal society surviving on desert margins through hunting, gathering, and oral tradition.

governmentForm.zh: 以亲族群体和地方长老为基础的分散部族体制，通过共识而非强制进行治理。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal order based on kin groups and local elders, governed through consensus rather than coercion.

socialStructure.zh: 科伊桑社会由若干采猎群与部分早期牧养群体构成，社会规模普遍较小，流动性较强。人与人之间总体较为平等，但资深猎手、治病者和牛群拥有者在部分地区享有更高声望。
socialStructure.en: Khoisan society consisted of several foraging bands and some early herding groups, generally small in scale and highly mobile. Social relations were broadly egalitarian, though senior hunters, healers, and cattle owners enjoyed higher prestige in some areas.

rulingClass.zh: 没有严格封闭的统治阶层，领导权由长老、知名猎手、疗愈者与富有牲畜的家族分享。领导者必须持续通过慷慨、调停和仪式能力来维系地位。
rulingClass.en: There was no rigid closed ruling class; leadership was shared among elders, renowned hunters, healers, and families wealthy in livestock. Leaders had to maintain status continually through generosity, mediation, and ritual skill.

succession.zh: 地位通常在家族与营地内部延续，但并非绝对世袭。若后继者缺乏威望，群体往往转而追随更能干的长者。
succession.en: Status often continued within families and camps but was not absolutely hereditary. If a successor lacked prestige, people often shifted allegiance to a more capable elder.

structure.zh: 治理结构以地方营地会议为主，重大事务由多个群体临时集会讨论。行政重点在于猎场与水源协调、婚姻网络维系以及处理群体间赔偿。
structure.en: Governance rested mainly on local camp meetings, while major matters were discussed in temporary gatherings of several groups. Administrative priorities included coordinating hunting grounds and water access, maintaining marriage networks, and settling compensation between groups.

function.zh: 安排驻地、调解亲族争端并组织季节性集会。
function.en: Organized camps, mediated kin disputes, and arranged seasonal gatherings.

function.zh: 在有牧养成分的群体中管理牛羊分配，并主持与邻近部落的交换。
function.en: Managed cattle and sheep allocation among groups with herding elements and oversaw exchange with neighboring peoples.

function.zh: 主持舞蹈疗愈、成年礼和祖灵相关仪式。
function.en: Oversaw trance healing dances, initiations, and rituals associated with ancestral spirits.

localAdmin.zh: 地方事务由营地长者和家族代表处理，不存在固定官署。各地群体在资源压力下可迅速分散或重组。
localAdmin.en: Local affairs were handled by camp elders and family representatives, with no permanent offices. Under resource pressure, local groups could disperse or reorganize rapidly.

legalSystem.zh: 法律基础是习惯、调解与补偿，尤其重视婚姻义务、伤害赔偿和共享规则。公开暴力虽存在，但通常被集体舆论和亲族网络限制。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on custom, mediation, and compensation, especially concerning marriage obligations, injury payments, and rules of sharing. Open violence did occur, but it was generally constrained by communal opinion and kinship networks.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税制，首领和长老依靠礼物、牲畜贡献与集体劳作获得支持。规模较大的集会时，参与者按能力分担食物与祭仪成本。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; leaders and elders were supported through gifts, livestock contributions, and communal labor. During larger gatherings, participants shared food and ritual costs according to ability.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 科伊桑群体在岩画艺术、追踪术、弓箭毒药使用和疗愈舞蹈传统方面具有突出成就。其口述故事与点击音语言传统也反映出高度复杂的文化记忆。
culturalAchievements.en: Khoisan groups achieved distinction in rock art, tracking, poison-arrow use, and traditions of healing dance. Their oral narratives and click-language traditions also reflected highly complex cultural memory.

languageFamily.zh: 科伊桑语群诸语言
languageFamily.en: Khoisan language groups

philosophy.zh: 强调人与动物、祖灵和土地之间的灵性联系，并通过舞蹈、故事和疗愈仪式理解世界。
philosophy.en: It emphasized spiritual connections among people, animals, ancestors, and the land, with dance, storytelling, and healing ritual as ways of understanding the world.

writingSystem.zh: 无成文文字，依赖口头叙事、岩画和象征性装饰传递知识。
writingSystem.en: There was no formal writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral narrative, rock art, and symbolic ornament.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济主要建立在采猎、渔猎、野生植物利用和部分地区的早期牧养之上。由于储存能力有限，财富多表现为社会关系、牲畜和环境知识，而非集中物资。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested mainly on foraging, fishing and hunting, use of wild plants, and early herding in some areas. Because storage capacity was limited, wealth appeared more in social ties, livestock, and ecological knowledge than in concentrated material stocks.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎采集、渔猎、早期牧养、皮革工艺、蛋壳珠制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting and gathering, fishing, early herding, leathercraft, and eggshell bead production

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、干肉、蛋壳珠、赭石、骨器、药材与少量牲畜
tradeGoods.en: Hides, dried meat, eggshell beads, ochre, bone tools, medicinal materials, and small numbers of livestock

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭通常拥有便携工具、装饰珠串和少量食物储备；部分牧养家庭另拥有少数牛羊，显著提升其社会地位。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households usually held portable tools, ornamental bead strings, and small food reserves; some herding families also possessed small numbers of cattle and sheep, which significantly raised their status.

economicSystem.zh: 以采猎共享为基础、兼具礼物交换和局部牧养成分的混合部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed tribal economy based on shared foraging, supplemented by gift exchange and localized herding.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备主要以珠饰、皮张、少量牲畜和由首领家族保管的礼品网络体现，并非集中金属库存。
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves were represented mainly by bead wealth, hides, small livestock holdings, and gift networks guarded by leading families rather than by concentrated metal stocks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政倾向维持共享网络与社会和解，优先保障集会、疗愈和旱季互助。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to sustain networks of sharing and social reconciliation, giving priority to gatherings, healing, and dry-season mutual aid.

description.zh: 使用轻弓与毒箭，适合追踪、袭扰和猎战结合的作战方式。
description.en: Used light bows and poison arrows, suited to tracking, harassment, and fighting closely linked to hunting practice.

description.zh: 装备短矛、投掷棒和轻盾，在水源或营地周边作战。
description.en: Equipped with short spears, throwing clubs, and light shields, fighting around camps and water points.

commanderInChief.zh: 长老推举的战时领袖
commanderInChief.en: Wartime leader selected by the elders

technology.zh: 以石骨木器和少量金属器并用，弓箭、毒药和追踪技能比重甲或阵列战更重要。
technology.en: Stone, bone, and wood tools remained important alongside limited metal items; bows, poison, and tracking mattered far more than armor or formation warfare.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括邻近牧民争夺牧场和水源、群体间报复循环，以及更北方或东部人口扩张带来的压力。
threats.en: Main threats included neighboring pastoralists competing for grazing and water, cycles of intergroup revenge, and pressure from demographic expansion farther north or east.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在南部非洲的干旱草原、半荒漠和海岸边缘地区，聚落规模普遍很小。不同群体之间存在语言与生计差异，但共享若干相近的仪式、追踪和交换传统。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across southern African dry grasslands, semi-deserts, and coastal margins, with settlements generally very small. Different groups varied in language and subsistence, but shared related traditions of ritual, tracking, and exchange.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与疗愈者、资深猎手或富牧户、普通家庭成员、青年与依附者。
socialClasses.en: Elders and healers, senior hunters or wealthy herders, ordinary family members, youths, and dependents.

allies.zh: 与邻近科伊桑群体维持婚姻与礼物网络，部分地区也与牧民进行和平交换。
allies.en: Maintained marriage and gift networks with neighboring Khoisan groups, and in some areas traded peacefully with pastoralists.

enemies.zh: 与争夺水源、猎场或牲畜的外来群体时有冲突。
enemies.en: They periodically clashed with outsiders competing for water, hunting grounds, or livestock.

foreignPolicy.zh: 总体上以分散求存、避免被卷入长期战争并维持跨群体交换为主要方针。
foreignPolicy.en: Their general foreign policy was one of dispersed survival, avoidance of prolonged war, and maintenance of intergroup exchange.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期南部非洲科伊桑文化
era.en: Early Iron Age southern African Khoisan culture

keyInnovations.zh: 毒箭狩猎、精密追踪、蛋壳珠制作、岩画传统与部分地区的早期牧养融合。
keyInnovations.en: Poison-arrow hunting, fine tracking, eggshell bead production, rock-art traditions, and in some regions integration with early herding.

strengths.zh: 对环境适应极强，追踪与狩猎技术高超，社会弹性大且共享网络广泛。
strengths.en: They were highly adapted to their environment, excelled in tracking and hunting, and possessed flexible social structures and broad sharing networks.

weaknesses.zh: 人口规模小、缺乏集中政权、面对持续外来扩张时难以形成统一抵抗。
weaknesses.en: They had a small population, no centralized state, and difficulty organizing unified resistance against sustained outside expansion.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍能在边缘生态区维持独特文化与生计，但未来将面临牧业和农耕扩张的长期压力。
outlook.en: In the short term they could preserve distinctive culture and livelihood in marginal ecological zones, but they would face long-term pressure from expanding herding and farming societies.

description.zh: 这是一个以采猎传统为核心、在部分地区结合早期牧养的南部非洲科伊桑部族社会。
description.en: This was a southern African Khoisan tribal society centered on foraging traditions, with early herding incorporated in some regions.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族与营地为基础的松散部落联盟，由长老会议在共识下决定迁徙、狩猎与纠纷事务。
governmentForm.en: A loose clan-and-camp-based tribal confederation in which a council of elders decides migration, hunting, and dispute matters by consensus.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以扩大家族、氏族和季节性营地为核心，人口分散于森林、苔原与河谷之间。成年猎手、驯鹿管理者与萨满在日常生活中具有较高威望，但财富积累有限，整体差异不大。婚姻、互赠与共同狩猎构成维系群体关系的主要机制。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on extended families, clans, and seasonal camps dispersed across forests, tundra, and river valleys. Adult hunters, reindeer handlers, and shamans held higher prestige in daily life, but wealth accumulation was limited and inequality remained modest. Marriage ties, gift exchange, and collective hunting were the main mechanisms binding communities together.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层并非固定贵族，而是由资深氏族长老、著名猎手与有影响力的萨满共同构成。其权威主要来自经验、声望与调解能力，而非正式官职。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum was not a fixed nobility but a combination of senior clan elders, renowned hunters, and influential shamans. Their authority derived chiefly from experience, prestige, and mediating ability rather than formal office.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常在同一氏族内由最有威望的成年成员继承，兼顾年龄、狩猎能力与亲属支持。若候选者声望不足，长老会议可推举旁支亲族或另一营地领袖。
succession.en: Leadership usually passed within the same clan to the most respected adult member, taking into account age, hunting ability, and kin support. If a candidate lacked prestige, the council of elders could elevate a collateral kinsman or another camp leader.

structure.zh: 政体高度分散，没有固定首都与常设官僚机构。重大事务如季节迁徙、狩猎区域分配、与邻近群体的和解或报复，通常由营地长老和萨满共同商议。日常管理多在家庭与氏族层面完成。
structure.en: The polity was highly decentralized, with no fixed capital and no standing bureaucracy. Major matters such as seasonal migration, allocation of hunting grounds, and reconciliation or retaliation toward neighboring groups were typically discussed jointly by camp elders and shamans. Everyday administration was handled largely at the family and clan level.

function.zh: 协调迁徙路线、狩猎区域、婚姻联盟与内部纠纷。
function.en: Coordinated migration routes, hunting grounds, marriage alliances, and internal disputes.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、口述传统、治疗仪式与禁忌裁定。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, oral tradition, healing ceremonies, and rulings on taboos.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依季节性营地展开，各营地由一至数名长者主持分工。河谷、林地与猎场的使用权多凭惯例和亲属网络维持。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through seasonal camps, each guided by one or several senior figures. Rights to river valleys, woodlands, and hunting grounds were maintained largely by custom and kinship networks.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和禁忌为主，重视赔偿、调解与血亲之间的和解。严重冒犯如偷猎、破坏祭仪或伤害同族，可能导致驱逐或复仇。
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on custom and taboo, emphasizing compensation, mediation, and reconciliation among kin. Serious offenses such as poaching, violating ritual practice, or harming a clansman could lead to expulsion or vengeance.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有固定税制，首领与祭司所得主要来自猎获分配、礼物与集体祭祀供奉。战时或灾年会临时征集食物、皮毛和劳力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed tax system; chiefs and ritual specialists received shares from hunting distributions, gifts, and offerings during communal rites. In wartime or years of scarcity, food, furs, and labor could be requisitioned temporarily.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 在极寒环境中发展出成熟的骨角器、皮革缝制、雪地交通与季节性营地组织方式。口头史诗、狩猎禁忌与萨满仪式构成文化核心。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed sophisticated bone and antler tools, leather sewing, snow travel, and seasonal camp organization for life in extreme cold. Oral epic traditions, hunting taboos, and shamanic ritual formed the core of cultural life.

languageFamily.zh: 古西伯利亚诸语群（前文字、分化显著）
languageFamily.en: Paleo-Siberian language groups (pre-literate and highly diverse)

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、祖灵庇护与人与动物关系的平衡观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, ancestral protection, and a worldview of balance between humans and animals.

writingSystem.zh: 无成文文字，依靠口述传统、歌谣、纹饰与仪式记忆传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No formal writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, ornament, and ritual memory.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、渔猎、采集和小规模驯鹿利用为基础，市场交换极其有限。产出多以自给消费形式存在，仅少量高价值皮毛进入长距离交换网络。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on hunting, fishing, gathering, and limited use of reindeer, with very little market exchange. Most output was consumed directly, and only small volumes of high-value furs entered long-distance exchange networks.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、渔业、采集、皮毛加工、骨角器制作、少量驯鹿利用
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, gathering, fur processing, bone and antler tool production, limited reindeer use

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、兽牙、骨角器、干鱼、皮革、琥珀与石料交换品
tradeGoods.en: Furs, animal teeth, bone and antler goods, dried fish, hides, amber, and exchanged stone materials

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为皮帐、衣物、猎具、雪橇、犬只与储存食物，流动性强但积累有限。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in skin tents, clothing, hunting gear, sleds, dogs, and stored food, making it mobile but limited in accumulation.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给、亲族再分配和礼物交换为主的采猎经济。
economicSystem.en: A foraging economy centered on household subsistence, kin redistribution, and gift exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent in reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金属储藏，而是由皮毛、干鱼、驯鹿产品、工具与礼器构成的分散储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” was not a centralized hoard of metal but a dispersed reserve of furs, dried fish, reindeer products, tools, and ritual items.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政取向偏保守，重在维持冬季生存储备与氏族间互助，而非集中积累财富。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice was conservative, prioritizing winter survival reserves and inter-clan mutual support rather than concentrated wealth accumulation.

description.zh: 由成年猎手组成，兼具狩猎与作战职能，使用矛、弓与骨角装备。
description.en: Composed of adult hunters serving both hunting and fighting roles, armed with spears, bows, and bone-antler equipment.

description.zh: 擅长追踪、警戒与冬季机动，多依靠雪橇、滑行器具和熟悉地形取胜。
description.en: Specialized in tracking, warning, and winter mobility, relying on sleds, gliding devices, and terrain knowledge.

commanderInChief.zh: 诸氏族共推战时长老
commanderInChief.en: War elder chosen by the clans

technology.zh: 处于早期铁器边缘，主体仍为石、骨、木与少量输入金属工具；善用皮革、防寒衣物和环境机动。
technology.en: On the margins of the Early Iron Age, relying mainly on stone, bone, and wood with small amounts of imported metal tools; strong in leatherwork, cold-weather clothing, and environmental mobility.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部落争夺猎场、极端气候与食物波动，而非大规模国家战争。
threats.en: The main threats came from neighboring tribes competing over hunting grounds, extreme climate, and fluctuations in food supply rather than large state warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏，沿大河、森林边缘与苔原过渡地带分散居住。出生率与死亡率均高，饥荒、寒冷与疾病对人口波动影响显著。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse, spread along major rivers, forest margins, and tundra transition zones. Both birth and death rates were high, and famine, cold, and disease strongly affected demographic fluctuation.

socialClasses.zh: 氏族长老、萨满、资深猎手、普通家庭成员与依附亲族。
socialClasses.en: Clan elders, shamans, veteran hunters, ordinary household members, and dependent kin.

allies.zh: 与邻近采猎群体维持临时婚姻联盟和季节性互市关系。
allies.en: Maintained temporary marriage alliances and seasonal barter ties with neighboring foraging groups.

enemies.zh: 没有固定宿敌，但与争夺渔场、兽群通道的部落常发生冲突。
enemies.en: Had no permanent enemy, but often clashed with tribes competing for fisheries and animal migration routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以避免长期流血冲突、维持迁徙通道和交换网络为主，偏向实用与灵活。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on avoiding prolonged bloodshed, preserving migration corridors, and sustaining exchange networks, favoring pragmatism and flexibility.

era.zh: 铁器时代边缘采猎文化

keyInnovations.zh: 复合皮衣、雪橇运输、骨角复合工具、冷地储藏与长距离追踪技术。
keyInnovations.en: Composite fur clothing, sled transport, bone-antler composite tools, cold-region storage methods, and long-distance tracking techniques.

strengths.zh: 对严寒生态适应力极强，机动性高，社区互助稳固，生存知识丰富。
strengths.en: Exceptionally adapted to severe cold, highly mobile, supported by strong communal mutual aid, and rich in survival knowledge.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、技术积累有限、难以集中资源，且对气候与猎物变动十分敏感。
weaknesses.en: Small population, limited technological accumulation, difficulty concentrating resources, and high sensitivity to climate and game fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 短期内可保持稳定延续，但若气候恶化或外来人群进入加剧，既有平衡将受到明显压力。
outlook.en: In the short term it could remain stable, but worsening climate or increased intrusion by outside peoples would place major strain on the existing balance.

description.zh: 这是一个依靠狩猎、渔猎与氏族协商在西伯利亚严寒地带维持生存的分散部落联盟。
description.en: This was a dispersed tribal confederation surviving in the Siberian cold through hunting, fishing, and clan-based consensus.

governmentForm.zh: 以海岸营地和亲族船组为核心的部族共同体，由长者与祭仪人物共同主持公共决策。
governmentForm.en: A tribal community centered on coastal camps and kin-based boat groups, with public decisions led jointly by elders and ritual specialists.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构围绕家庭船组、季节性海岸聚落和冬季共同体展开。擅长海兽捕猎的成年男性、加工皮革与维系储粮的女性，以及主持禁忌仪式的萨满都拥有重要地位。整体分层较弱，但声望差异明显。
socialStructure.en: Social structure revolved around household boat crews, seasonal coastal settlements, and winter communities. Adult male marine hunters, women responsible for skin processing and food storage, and shamans who upheld ritual taboos all held important positions. Stratification was weak overall, though differences in prestige were clear.

rulingClass.zh: 并无世袭贵族，首领层主要由年长猎手、营地长者和能调解分配的家族首脑组成。优秀的海猎技能和在饥荒时组织共享的能力，是取得权威的关键。
rulingClass.en: There was no hereditary nobility; leadership consisted mainly of senior hunters, camp elders, and household heads able to manage distribution. Mastery of marine hunting and the ability to organize sharing during scarcity were key sources of authority.

succession.zh: 首领通常在近亲家族中推举，但不绝对父子相承。若继承者缺乏航海、捕猎或调解能力，营地可改推更具威望的长者。
succession.en: Leadership was usually selected within close kin groups, but not strictly from father to son. If an heir lacked seafaring, hunting, or mediating ability, the camp could choose a more respected elder instead.

structure.zh: 政治组织以小型海岸营地为单位，缺乏固定都城和正式官署。重要决策通常在冬季集会时作出，包括分配海象、海豹与鲸类猎获、安排迁徙时间以及处理跨家庭纠纷。统治的核心是共同生存而非抽象统治权。
structure.en: Political organization was based on small coastal camps, lacking a fixed capital or formal offices. Important decisions were usually made at winter gatherings, including allocation of walrus, seal, and whale catches, timing of migrations, and settlement of disputes across households. The core of rule was collective survival rather than abstract sovereignty.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、猎场使用、食物共享与家庭纠纷。
function.en: Coordinated migration, use of hunting grounds, food sharing, and household disputes.

function.zh: 监督海猎禁忌、出航准备、祭仪与冬储管理。
function.en: Supervised marine-hunting taboos, launch preparations, ritual observance, and winter storage.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即营地治理，通常以若干相互通婚的家庭组成。最重要的管理事项是船只协作、肉脂分配和冰况观察。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was essentially camp administration, usually composed of several intermarrying households. The most important administrative matters were boat cooperation, distribution of meat and blubber, and observation of ice conditions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要表现为习俗、禁忌和共同体舆论，对偷窃储粮、破坏分配秩序或违反海猎仪式者会施加赔偿、羞辱或驱逐。杀人多通过复仇或调解赔偿处理。
legalSystem.en: Law took the form of custom, taboo, and community opinion; theft of stored food, disruption of distribution, or violation of marine-hunting ritual could bring compensation, public shaming, or expulsion. Homicide was commonly addressed through revenge or negotiated compensation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税收，公共资源来自大型猎获后的分配份额、节庆供献和危机时期的集中储备。首领权威取决于分享能力，因此征取通常温和。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; public resources came from shares of large catches, festive offerings, and pooled reserves in times of crisis. Since leadership depended on generosity, extraction was usually mild.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 在海冰环境中形成高水平的皮船、骨叉、鱼叉头、油灯与保温衣物技术。与海兽相关的歌谣、面具与祭仪体现了高度适应性的海洋文化。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed advanced skin boats, bone toggling harpoons, lamps, and insulated clothing suited to sea-ice environments. Songs, masks, and rituals connected to marine mammals expressed a highly adaptive maritime culture.

languageFamily.zh: 古北极沿岸语言群（前文字）
languageFamily.en: Ancient Arctic coastal language groups (pre-literate)

philosophy.zh: 以海兽灵性、祖先庇佑和遵守禁忌以维持人与海洋平衡的观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on the spiritual power of sea animals, ancestral protection, and maintaining balance between humans and the sea through observance of taboos.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，依靠口述传承、歌唱、雕刻纹样与仪式叙事保存记忆。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; memory was preserved through oral transmission, song, carved motifs, and ritual storytelling.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济核心是海豹、海象、鱼类及少量鲸类利用，辅以采集与内陆驯鹿、鸟类资源。产出虽低，但海兽油脂、皮革与象牙具有较高交换价值。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on seals, walrus, fish, and some whaling, supplemented by gathering and occasional inland reindeer and bird resources. Output was modest, but marine oil, hides, and ivory had relatively high exchange value.

mainIndustries.zh: 海兽捕猎、渔业、皮革加工、骨牙雕制、油脂储存与皮船制造
mainIndustries.en: Marine hunting, fishing, leather processing, bone and ivory carving, oil storage, and skin-boat construction

tradeGoods.zh: 海象牙、海豹皮、鲸骨、鱼干、油脂、骨制工具与装饰品
tradeGoods.en: Walrus ivory, seal skins, whale bone, dried fish, oil, bone tools, and ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为皮船、鱼叉、油灯、保暖衣物、储藏油脂与犬只，具高度生存导向。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth mainly took the form of skin boats, harpoons, lamps, warm clothing, stored oil, and dogs, all strongly oriented toward survival.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以海猎自给、群体共享和边缘物物交换为特征的极地生计经济。
economicSystem.en: A polar subsistence economy characterized by marine-hunting self-provisioning, communal sharing, and marginal barter exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent in reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备主要为油脂、干鱼、皮革、象牙和备用舟具，并无集中金属货币库藏。
treasuryDescription.en: Communal reserves consisted mainly of oil, dried fish, hides, ivory, and spare boat gear, not a centralized metallic treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政上优先保证越冬安全与共享机制，避免对任何家庭施加过重征取。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal priorities aimed first at secure overwintering and preserving sharing mechanisms, avoiding excessive extraction from any household.

description.zh: 由熟悉海岸冰缘地形的猎手组成，以鱼叉、短矛和弓为主。
description.en: Composed of hunters familiar with coastal ice-edge terrain, armed mainly with harpoons, short spears, and bows.

description.zh: 以皮船执行近岸侦察、追踪和小规模袭扰，适合夏季海面行动。
description.en: Used skin boats for coastal scouting, tracking, and small-scale raiding, suitable for summer sea operations.

commanderInChief.zh: 冬季集会推举的海猎领袖
commanderInChief.en: Marine-hunting leader chosen at winter assembly

technology.zh: 金属稀少，兵器以骨、石、木为主，少量输入金属用于刀具；海冰航行、保暖与鱼叉技术极为成熟。
technology.en: Metal was scarce; weapons were mainly of bone, stone, and wood, with small imported metal pieces used for knives. Sea-ice travel, thermal clothing, and harpoon technology were highly developed.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要风险是海冰变化、食物短缺、局部营地冲突和在猎场边界上的报复性袭击。
threats.en: The principal risks were changing sea ice, food shortages, local camp conflicts, and retaliatory attacks along hunting-frontier boundaries.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于海岸与河口地带，季节变化导致聚落规模波动明显。由于生态承载力有限，人口增长缓慢且易受灾年影响。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated along coasts and estuaries, with settlement size shifting markedly by season. Because ecological carrying capacity was limited, population growth was slow and vulnerable to bad years.

socialClasses.zh: 营地长者、萨满、资深海猎者、普通家庭成员与寄居亲属。
socialClasses.en: Camp elders, shamans, veteran marine hunters, ordinary household members, and resident kin dependents.

allies.zh: 与部分内陆采猎群体保持皮革、油脂和骨材交换关系。
allies.en: Maintained exchange relations with some inland foraging groups in hides, oil, and bone materials.

enemies.zh: 没有稳定国家级敌人，但与邻近海岸营地会因猎场和漂流物资源起冲突。
enemies.en: Had no stable state-level enemies, but could clash with neighboring coastal camps over hunting grounds and drift resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以确保海岸迁徙通道、维持互市和避免冬季封锁为首要目标。
foreignPolicy.en: Its main foreign policy goals were securing coastal migration routes, maintaining barter ties, and avoiding winter isolation.

era.zh: 北极边缘铁器时代海猎文化

keyInnovations.zh: 皮船、复合鱼叉、海兽油灯、冰缘导航、保温服装与象牙骨器精加工。
keyInnovations.en: Skin boats, composite harpoons, marine-oil lamps, ice-edge navigation, insulated clothing, and refined ivory-bone craftwork.

strengths.zh: 海猎技能高超，集体分享制度强，适应极端环境能力突出。
