era.zh: 中美洲前古典早期铁器时代对应阶段
era.en: Mesoamerican Early Preclassic stage, contemporary to the Iron Age elsewhere

keyInnovations.zh: 玉米密集种植、土台礼仪建筑、陶器烧制、投矛器与区域性黑曜石交换。
keyInnovations.en: Intensive maize cultivation, earthen ceremonial platforms, fired pottery, the atlatl, and regional obsidian exchange.

strengths.zh: 农业基础稳固、人口较多、具备组织劳役和建设公共设施的能力。
strengths.en: Strong agricultural base, comparatively large population, and the ability to organize labor for public works.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合有限、缺乏成熟文字和金属武器，对气候波动较敏感。
weaknesses.en: Limited political integration, no mature writing or metal weapons, and high sensitivity to climate fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 若农业剩余和区域交换持续扩大，未来有可能发展出更复杂的祭祀中心和早期国家结构。
outlook.en: If agricultural surplus and regional exchange continued to expand, these societies could develop more complex ceremonial centers and early state structures.

description.zh: 这是一个依托玉米农业、村社聚居和酋长祭祀权威而兴盛的中美洲早期酋邦社会。
description.en: This was a flourishing early Mesoamerican chiefly society built on maize farming, village settlement, and ritual authority.

governmentForm.zh: 由多个亲缘群体组成的松散草原部落体系，以长老与战士首领的临时协商统治为主。
governmentForm.en: A loose grassland tribal system composed of multiple kin groups, ruled mainly through temporary consultation among elders and warrior leaders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会组织以家族营地、季节性迁徙群体和地方部落为基础，依靠采集、渔猎和初步园圃农业维生。长老、勇士和善于协调交换的首领更有威望，但整体分层不深。沿河与湿地聚落比内陆草原营地更稳定。
socialStructure.en: Social organization rested on family camps, seasonal migrating groups, and local tribes, sustained by gathering, hunting, fishing, and limited horticulture. Elders, warriors, and leaders skilled in exchange held greater prestige, though stratification remained shallow. Riverine and wetland settlements were more stable than inland grassland camps.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层主要是长老、战时首领和祭仪人物，他们通过声望、亲缘和馈赠维系追随者。缺乏世袭贵族阶层和常设官僚。
rulingClass.en: The ruling layer consisted mainly of elders, wartime leaders, and ritual figures who maintained followers through prestige, kinship, and gift giving. There was no hereditary nobility or permanent bureaucracy.

succession.zh: 首领通常由有威望家族中最能服众者担任，可受长老会议制约。若出现分歧，部落常通过分营或迁徙化解。
succession.en: A leader was usually selected from a prestigious family based on personal standing and remained subject to the council of elders. In case of disagreement, tribes often resolved tensions by splitting camps or migrating apart.

structure.zh: 没有固定都城，最重要的政治场所是季节性会盟营地和河谷聚落。公共决策围绕迁徙路线、渔猎区域、婚盟与复仇安排展开。
structure.en: There was no fixed capital; the main political venues were seasonal assembly camps and river-valley settlements. Public decisions focused on migration routes, fishing and hunting zones, marriage ties, and revenge arrangements.

function.zh: 裁决部落事务、安排迁徙与调解跨家族冲突。
function.en: Adjudicated tribal affairs, arranged migrations, and mediated inter-family conflicts.

function.zh: 组织大型狩猎、边界巡查和战时防御。
function.en: Organized large hunts, border patrols, and wartime defense.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由营地长者和家族头人负责，按传统使用水源、草地和捕鱼点。较大的河畔聚落在旱季对周围营地具有一定协调权。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by camp elders and family heads, who regulated use of water sources, grassland, and fishing spots by tradition. Larger riverside settlements exercised some coordinating authority over surrounding camps in dry seasons.

legalSystem.zh: 依赖口头习惯法，重视血亲责任、赔礼与公开和解。报复性冲突并不少见，但可通过婚盟和馈赠缓和。
legalSystem.en: It relied on oral customary law, emphasizing kin responsibility, compensation, and public reconciliation. Feud-like retaliation was not uncommon, but could be eased through marriage alliances and gifts.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税制，首领的资源来自猎获分配、集体劳作和附庸家庭献纳。公共事务多通过互助义务而非强制征收维持。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; leaders drew resources from shares of the hunt, communal labor, and offerings from dependent families. Public affairs were sustained more by mutual obligation than by compulsory taxation.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化擅长草原与湿地环境的复合生计，包括渔猎、编织和陶器制作。营地仪式、羽饰与身体彩绘在身份认同和部族交流中具有重要作用。
culturalAchievements.en: Their culture excelled in mixed subsistence across grassland and wetland environments, including fishing, hunting, weaving, and pottery making. Camp rituals, feather adornment, and body painting played important roles in identity and intertribal exchange.

languageFamily.zh: 南锥体草原地区多样语言群，可能含前查鲁亚及相关语言
languageFamily.en: Diverse Southern Cone grassland language groups, possibly including pre-Charruan-related languages

philosophy.zh: 泛灵信仰与祖灵崇拜并存，重视草原动物、河流湿地和天象的神圣力量。
philosophy.en: Animistic beliefs coexisted with ancestor reverence, placing sacred importance on grassland animals, rivers, wetlands, and celestial signs.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，知识依靠口述传承、身体彩绘、装饰图案和仪式表演保存。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was preserved through oral transmission, body painting, decorative motifs, and ritual performance.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以渔猎、采集、局部园圃农业和手工制品为基础，自给性很强。沿河交换使贝壳饰品、石器和陶器在不同群体间流通。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on fishing, hunting, gathering, limited horticulture, and handicrafts, with a strong subsistence orientation. Riverine exchange circulated shell ornaments, stone tools, and pottery among different groups.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔猎、采集、编织、陶器、小规模园圃农业、皮革加工
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, hunting, gathering, weaving, pottery, small-scale horticulture, leatherworking

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、鱼干、羽毛、贝壳饰品、陶器、石器
tradeGoods.en: Hides, dried fish, feathers, shell ornaments, pottery, stone tools

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为猎具、渔网、陶器储备、皮革、装饰品和亲族关系。河畔家庭通常比内陆营地更容易积累剩余。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed through hunting gear, nets, pottery stores, hides, ornaments, and kin connections. Riverine households generally accumulated more surplus than inland camps.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以自给生计、季节性迁徙和礼物交换为核心的草原部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A grassland tribal economy centered on subsistence production, seasonal mobility, and gift exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent in reserve

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备主要是干鱼、皮革、陶器和装饰品，通常分散保存在多个首领营地而非集中金库。少量稀有贝饰在外交馈赠中尤为重要。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves consisted mainly of dried fish, hides, pottery, and ornaments, typically dispersed among several leader camps rather than held in a central treasury. Small quantities of rare shell adornments were especially important in diplomacy.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政倾向低度征集和高比例再分配，以保持部落凝聚与季节性生存安全。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice favored light extraction and high redistribution in order to preserve tribal cohesion and seasonal survival security.

description.zh: 持矛、棍棒、投掷器和简易盾牌，适合近距离突袭和防守营地。
description.en: Armed with spears, clubs, throwing weapons, and simple shields, suited for close raids and camp defense.

description.zh: 兼具狩猎与侦察功能，熟悉河道、湿地和草原过渡地带。
description.en: Combined hunting and scouting roles, familiar with rivers, wetlands, and grassland transition zones.

commanderInChief.zh: 战时首领
commanderInChief.en: Wartime leader

technology.zh: 军事技术较为基础，以石骨木器为主，依靠机动、地形熟悉和短促袭击，而非阵地决战。
technology.en: Military technology was basic, relying mainly on stone, bone, and wood weapons; success depended on mobility, terrain knowledge, and short raids rather than pitched battle.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括邻近草原群体的报复性袭击、河谷资源争夺和气候波动造成的食物短缺。
threats.en: Main threats included retaliatory raids by neighboring grassland groups, competition over river-valley resources, and food shortages caused by climatic fluctuation.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散在潘帕斯草原、河谷和湿地边缘，多为小型营地与半定居聚落。资源季节性变化使人口密度始终较低。
populationDescription.en: Population was dispersed across the Pampas grasslands, river valleys, and wetland margins, mostly in small camps and semi-sedentary settlements. Seasonal shifts in resources kept population density low.

name.en: Río de la Plata Wetland Settlement

socialClasses.zh: 长老与祭仪人物、勇士与猎手、普通家庭成员、依附者与少量俘虏。
socialClasses.en: Elders and ritual figures, warriors and hunters, ordinary family members, dependents, and a small number of captives.

allies.zh: 同盟多为临时性的邻近部落合作，通常围绕婚盟、共同狩猎或共同防御形成。
allies.en: Alliances were mostly temporary cooperation with neighboring tribes, usually formed around marriage ties, joint hunting, or mutual defense.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系多因复仇循环、河道控制与猎场竞争而起，少有长期统一敌人。
enemies.en: Hostilities usually arose from revenge cycles, river control, and competition over hunting grounds, with few permanent enemies.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策偏向灵活平衡，通过迁徙、联姻和礼物交换避免被单一敌手压制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy favored flexible balancing, using migration, intermarriage, and gift exchange to avoid domination by any single rival.

era.zh: 南美南锥体早期金属接触前后阶段
era.en: Southern Cone pre-metal to early-contact stage contemporary with the Iron Age elsewhere

keyInnovations.zh: 湿地渔猎技术、编织与陶器、季节性营地组织、沿河交换网络。
keyInnovations.en: Wetland fishing-hunting techniques, weaving and pottery, seasonal camp organization, and riverine exchange networks.

strengths.zh: 机动性高、适应草原与湿地环境、社会结构灵活且抗压性较强。
strengths.en: High mobility, strong adaptation to grassland and wetland environments, and a flexible resilient social structure.

weaknesses.zh: 人口和资源集中度不足、政治整合低、缺乏强大工事与金属武器。
weaknesses.en: Insufficient population and resource concentration, low political integration, and lack of major fortifications or metal weapons.

outlook.zh: 其社会可长期维持地方自主与生态适应，但若面对更集中的农耕政体，往往难以形成统一抵抗。
outlook.en: These societies could maintain local autonomy and ecological adaptation for long periods, but would struggle to mount unified resistance against more centralized farming polities.

description.zh: 这是一个分布于潘帕斯草原与河湿地之间、以季节性迁徙和渔猎采集为主的部落文化群。
description.en: This was a tribal cultural complex spread between the Pampas grasslands and river wetlands, centered on seasonal mobility, fishing, hunting, and gathering.

dynasty.zh: 东部林地部族联盟前期

governmentForm.zh: 以村落与氏族为核心的松散部族酋邦，由酋长在长老与宗族代表协商下行使权威。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal chiefdom centered on villages and clans, with authority exercised by chiefs in consultation with elders and kin representatives.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、氏族和村落共同体为基本单位，身份多由血缘、婚姻与狩猎或农业贡献决定。多数地区已出现半定居村落，妇女常在农业与家户分配中具有重要地位，男性则更多承担狩猎、远行与战争职责。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around kin groups, clans, and village communities, with status shaped by ancestry, marriage, and contributions in hunting or food production. Many areas had semi-sedentary villages, where women often held important roles in agriculture and household distribution, while men more often handled hunting, travel, and warfare.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层主要由世系显赫的酋长家族、长老和有威望的战士或祭仪主持者构成。其权威依赖馈赠、调停纠纷和组织公共仪式，而非严格官僚命令。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted mainly of prominent chiefly lineages, elders, and respected warriors or ritual leaders. Their authority depended on gift-giving, dispute mediation, and organizing communal rites rather than strict bureaucratic command.

succession.zh: 酋长职位通常在特定氏族或家族内继承，但须获得长老与主要家户认可。若继承人缺乏能力，部族可改推更有威望的亲族成员。
succession.en: Chiefly office usually passed within specific clans or families, but required recognition from elders and major households. If an heir proved incapable, the tribe could elevate another more respected kinsman.

structure.zh: 政治结构较为简单，以村落议事会和部族酋长为核心。重大事项如迁徙、战争、狩猎分配和祭祀时间，通常通过协商达成共识。
structure.en: The political structure was simple, centered on village councils and a tribal chief. Major matters such as migration, warfare, hunting distribution, and ritual timing were usually settled through consensus.

function.zh: 协调村落事务、调解纠纷并决定季节性迁徙与资源使用。
function.en: Coordinated village affairs, mediated disputes, and decided seasonal movements and resource use.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、葬礼、节庆及酋长向各家户再分配物资。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, funerals, festivals, and the chief's redistribution of goods to households.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由各聚落长者、家族首领和酋长亲属共同承担。没有固定层级官署，管理依靠面对面协商与习惯权威。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by settlement elders, lineage heads, and relatives of the chief. There were no fixed bureaucratic offices; governance relied on face-to-face negotiation and customary authority.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是口传习惯法，重视赔偿、和解和维持氏族间平衡。严重暴力冲突可能引发报复，但长老常尝试通过赠礼和婚姻安排化解。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted mainly of oral customary norms emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, and balance between clans. Serious violence could trigger revenge, but elders often sought settlement through gifts and marriage arrangements.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税制，酋长通过贡礼、猎获分成和节庆馈赠集中少量资源。公共负担通常以劳役、食物贡献或战时随从形式体现。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; chiefs gathered modest resources through tribute gifts, shares of hunting yields, and ceremonial offerings. Public burdens usually took the form of labor, food contributions, or wartime service.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 东部林地居民发展了土丘传统、精致陶器和适应森林河流环境的季节性聚落组织。其口头史诗、葬俗和交换仪式为后来的复杂区域文化奠定了基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Eastern Woodlands peoples developed mound traditions, fine pottery, and seasonal settlement organization adapted to forest and riverine environments. Their oral histories, mortuary customs, and exchange rituals laid the groundwork for later complex regional cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 以阿尔冈昆语系、易洛魁语系及部分苏语族前身语言为主
languageFamily.en: Mainly early Algonquian, Iroquoian, and some Siouan-related languages

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵、祖灵敬奉与人与土地互惠的世界观
philosophy.en: Animist and ancestral worldview stressing reciprocity between people, spirits, and land

writingSystem.zh: 无成体系文字，知识依靠口述传统、纹饰与仪式记忆传承
writingSystem.en: No formal writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, motifs, and ritual memory

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集、渔业和初级园圃农业混合构成，市场化程度很低。财富主要表现为食物储备、工具、兽皮和社会关系网络，而非可积累货币。
gdpDescription.en: The economy combined hunting, gathering, fishing, and early horticulture, with very low market integration. Wealth was expressed mainly in food stores, tools, hides, and social exchange networks rather than accumulable money.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、渔业、采集、玉米与葫芦园圃、制陶、木骨器制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, gathering, maize and gourd horticulture, pottery, wood and bone craftwork

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、燧石、铜器原料、贝珠、陶器、烟草、干鱼
tradeGoods.en: Hides, flint, native copper materials, shell beads, pottery, tobacco, dried fish

householdWealth.zh: 普通家户财富有限，多以季节性粮食、肉干、皮毛、陶器和亲族支援衡量。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was modest, measured largely in seasonal food stores, dried meat, furs, pottery, and kin support.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、氏族共享和礼物再分配为主的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on household production, clan sharing, and gift redistribution.

source.en: Shares of hunting and fishing yields

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非集中金属库藏，而是酋长控制下的粮食、皮毛、器物和礼品储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a centralized metal reserve, but stores of food, hides, tools, and gifts under chiefly control.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持声望和再分配为核心，强调灾年互助与节庆集体消费。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on maintaining prestige and redistribution, emphasizing mutual aid in lean years and collective ceremonial consumption.

description.zh: 由成年男子组成，使用木矛、石刃武器、棍棒和弓箭，适于伏击和突袭。
description.en: Formed by adult men using wooden spears, stone-edged weapons, clubs, and bows, suited for ambushes and raids.

description.zh: 负责聚落外围警戒、木栅防御和紧急集结。
description.en: Handled perimeter watch, palisade defense, and emergency musters for settlements.

commanderInChief.zh: 大酋长或首席战酋
commanderInChief.en: High Chief or Principal War Chief

technology.zh: 军事技术处于弓箭、木栅寨与轻装步战阶段，缺乏金属甲胄和攻城能力。
technology.en: Military technology was at the stage of bows, palisaded villages, and light infantry fighting, lacking metal armor and siege capabilities.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族争夺狩猎地、河道资源和复仇性袭击。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribes competing for hunting grounds, river resources, and retaliatory raiding.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于森林、河谷和湖区，以小型村落和季节性营地为主。整体密度较低，但沿主要水道的聚落联系较为频繁。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across forests, river valleys, and lake regions, living mostly in small villages and seasonal camps. Density was low overall, but settlements along major waterways interacted more frequently.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族、长老与祭仪主持者、普通自由家户、依附亲族与战俘。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders and ritual specialists, ordinary free households, dependent kin, and captives.

allies.zh: 以婚姻、交换和共同祭仪联系的邻近村落与同语族部落。
allies.en: Nearby villages and related-language tribes linked by marriage, exchange, and shared rituals.

enemies.zh: 与其竞争猎场、渔场和贸易通道的周边部族。
enemies.en: Surrounding tribes competing for hunting grounds, fishing sites, and trade routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持区域平衡、交换网络和有限复仇威慑为主，倾向局部结盟而非长期统一。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining regional balance, exchange networks, and limited retaliatory deterrence, favoring local alliances over long-term unification.

era.zh: 北美东部晚期古风至早期林地过渡阶段
era.en: Late Archaic to Early Woodland transition in Eastern North America

keyInnovations.zh: 早期园圃农业、制陶、土丘营建、木栅聚落和区域性交换网络。
keyInnovations.en: Early horticulture, pottery, mound construction, palisaded settlements, and regional exchange networks.

strengths.zh: 生态适应力强，食物来源多样，亲族网络稳固，适合在广阔森林环境中生存。
strengths.en: Highly adaptive to local ecology, with diverse food sources and strong kin networks suited to broad forest environments.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度低，人口分散，储备与军事动员能力有限。
weaknesses.en: Political integration was low, the population was dispersed, and reserve storage and military mobilization were limited.

outlook.zh: 若农业和仪式中心继续发展，未来可能形成更稳定的区域酋邦与远距交换体系。
outlook.en: If agriculture and ceremonial centers continue to develop, more stable regional chiefdoms and longer-distance exchange systems may emerge.

description.zh: 北美东部诸部族在公元前800年前后处于以亲族村落、混合生计和口传传统为核心的早期酋邦阶段。
description.en: Around 800 BCE, the peoples of Eastern North America lived in early chiefdoms centered on kin-based villages, mixed subsistence, and oral tradition.

dynasty.zh: 澳洲原住民诸族群长老传统

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族、语言群和仪式长者为核心的分散部落治理体系，没有统一国家机构。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal order based on clans, language groups, and ritual elders, without a unified state apparatus.

socialStructure.zh: 社会组织围绕亲属关系、图腾归属和年龄等级展开，不同地区在婚姻规则和土地使用上各有传统。长者掌握圣地知识、仪式权和调解权，成年仪式决定个人在共同体中的责任。
socialStructure.en: Social organization revolved around kinship, totemic affiliation, and age grades, with regional variation in marriage rules and land use. Elders held sacred knowledge, ritual authority, and mediating roles, while initiation ceremonies defined personal responsibilities within the community.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在固定贵族阶层，但资深长者、仪式主持者和著名猎手在决策中影响最大。权威主要来自知识、血缘资历和对梦创时代传统的掌握。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed aristocratic class, but senior elders, ritual leaders, and renowned hunters had the greatest influence in decision-making. Authority came primarily from knowledge, lineage seniority, and mastery of Dreaming traditions.

succession.zh: 领导地位通常不以单一世袭决定，而由年资、仪式资格和群体认可逐步形成。某些职责可在亲族中传承，但长者资格必须经共同体承认。
succession.en: Leadership was usually not determined by simple hereditary succession, but developed through seniority, ritual qualification, and communal recognition. Some responsibilities could pass within kin lines, but elder status had to be acknowledged by the community.

structure.zh: 治理以营地长者会议和跨家族协商为主，权力高度分散。不同地域群体在旱季、雨季和仪式集会时会临时扩大政治合作。
structure.en: Governance relied on camp elder councils and cross-family consultation, with power highly decentralized. In different regions, political cooperation temporarily expanded during dry-season, wet-season, or ceremonial gatherings.

function.zh: 处理婚姻、迁徙、狩猎地分配与纠纷调解。
function.en: Handled marriage matters, movement, hunting-ground allocation, and dispute mediation.

function.zh: 维护梦创传说、主持成年礼并管理圣地访问规范。
function.en: Preserved Dreaming narratives, conducted initiation rites, and regulated access to sacred sites.

localAdmin.zh: 地方事务由各地营地长者、家族首领和知识持有者管理。由于缺乏定居都城，行政重心随季节性移动而变化。
localAdmin.en: Local matters were managed by camp elders, family heads, and knowledge holders. Since there was no permanent capital, the administrative focus shifted with seasonal movement.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体现为口传习俗、梦创法则和婚姻禁忌，违规常以赔偿、仪式惩戒或群体隔离处理。重罪也可能导致复仇性暴力，但通常先由长者调停。
legalSystem.en: Law took the form of oral custom, Dreaming-based norms, and marriage taboos, with violations addressed through compensation, ritual punishment, or communal exclusion. Serious offenses could also provoke revenge violence, though elders usually mediated first.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有常设税收，资源通过共享义务、仪式供奉和集体劳动分担。大型仪式期间，各群体会贡献食物、器具和劳力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no standing taxation; resources were shared through reciprocal obligations, ritual offerings, and communal labor. During major ceremonies, groups contributed food, tools, and labor.

religion.zh: 梦时代信仰
religion.en: Dreamtime

culturalAchievements.zh: 澳洲原住民在岩画、歌线记忆体系、环境火管理和复杂亲属分类上成就卓著。其文化能够在沙漠、海岸、热带与温带多种生态中长期稳定延续。
culturalAchievements.en: Aboriginal Australians achieved remarkable sophistication in rock art, songline memory systems, environmental fire management, and complex kin classification. Their culture sustained long-term continuity across desert, coastal, tropical, and temperate ecologies.

languageFamily.zh: 以帕马-恩雍甘语系为主，另有北部和阿纳姆地等多种非帕马-恩雍甘语言
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Pama-Nyungan languages, alongside various non-Pama-Nyungan languages in the north and Arnhem Land

philosophy.zh: 梦创时代宇宙观，强调祖灵、地景、歌线与人群责任的连续性
philosophy.en: Dreaming cosmology emphasizing continuity among ancestral beings, landscape, songlines, and human obligation

writingSystem.zh: 无文字体系，以口传、歌线、身体绘饰、岩画和器物符号传递知识
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songlines, body painting, rock art, and symbolic objects

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以采集、狩猎、渔猎和地区性资源管理为核心，部分地区形成相当高效的季节性剩余。整体上不存在货币市场，但跨群体交换和仪式分配十分重要。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on gathering, hunting, fishing, and regional resource management, with some areas producing quite efficient seasonal surpluses. There was no monetary market overall, but intergroup exchange and ceremonial distribution were highly important.

mainIndustries.zh: 采集、狩猎、渔业、贝类采集、石器制造、木器与编织、火耕式生态管理
mainIndustries.en: Gathering, hunting, fishing, shellfish collecting, stone toolmaking, woodworking and weaving, and fire-based ecological management

tradeGoods.zh: 石斧坯料、赭石、贝壳、树脂、木矛、编织物、装饰品、仪式物品
tradeGoods.en: Stone axe blanks, ochre, shells, resin, wooden spears, woven goods, ornaments, and ritual items

householdWealth.zh: 家户财富主要体现为工具、武器、采集知识、亲属网络和对特定土地资源的使用权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in tools, weapons, ecological knowledge, kin ties, and rights to use particular lands and resources.

economicSystem.zh: 以共享、互惠和仪式性交换为核心的狩猎采集经济。
economicSystem.en: A hunter-gatherer economy centered on sharing, reciprocity, and ceremonial exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备主要是食物、石料、赭石、仪式器物和人际义务，不是集中金属财富。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves consisted mainly of food, stone material, ochre, ritual objects, and interpersonal obligations rather than concentrated metal wealth.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政行为服从共享伦理，重视在仪式和资源紧缺时进行再分配。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal behavior followed an ethic of sharing, with emphasis on redistribution during ceremonies and resource scarcity.

description.zh: 主要使用矛、投矛器、棍棒和盾牌，作战多为短促袭击与仪式化冲突。
description.en: Primarily used spears, spear-throwers, clubs, and shields, with warfare often taking the form of short raids and ritualized conflict.

description.zh: 负责警戒水源、圣地和营地周边，战时迅速集结。
description.en: Guarded water sources, sacred sites, and camp perimeters, assembling quickly in times of danger.

commanderInChief.zh: 资深长者或战斗领袖
commanderInChief.en: Senior Elder or War Leader

technology.zh: 军事技术以木石武器、投矛器和地域熟悉为优势，不具备金属兵器与攻城技术。
technology.en: Military technology relied on wood-and-stone weaponry, spear-throwers, and local terrain knowledge, without metal arms or siege techniques.

threats.zh: 主要威胁是邻近族群间因水源、婚姻纠纷、复仇和资源通道引发的冲突。
threats.en: Main threats arose from conflicts with neighboring groups over water sources, marriage disputes, revenge, and access to resource routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于海岸、内陆草原、沙漠边缘和热带北部，多为小规模流动营地。季节性集会时，分散群体会短期汇聚形成较大仪式中心。
populationDescription.en: The population was widely spread across coasts, inland grasslands, desert margins, and the tropical north, mostly in small mobile camps. During seasonal gatherings, dispersed groups temporarily assembled into larger ceremonial centers.

socialClasses.zh: 长者与仪式知识持有者、成年自由成员、青年受训者、姻亲附属者与个别俘获者。
socialClasses.en: Elders and ritual knowledge holders, adult free members, initiated youths, affinal dependents, and occasional captives.

allies.zh: 通过婚姻、歌线、共同圣地和交换会联系的邻近语言群。
allies.en: Neighboring language groups connected through marriage, songlines, shared sacred sites, and exchange gatherings.

enemies.zh: 因资源边界或复仇关系而敌对的近邻群体。
enemies.en: Nearby groups hostile over resource boundaries or cycles of revenge.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持土地使用平衡、仪式互访和跨地区信息传递为主，冲突多局部且可通过长者调停暂时缓和。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on balancing land use, ritual visitation, and transmitting information across regions, with conflicts usually local and temporarily eased through elder mediation.

era.zh: 澳洲全新世后期原住民技术传统
era.en: Late Holocene Aboriginal Australian technological tradition

keyInnovations.zh: 投矛器、精细石器、岩画体系、歌线导航和火管理景观塑造。
keyInnovations.en: Spear-throwers, refined stone tools, rock-art systems, songline navigation, and fire-based landscape management.

strengths.zh: 对生态环境认知极深，社会网络灵活，适应干旱与多变气候能力强。
strengths.en: They possessed deep ecological knowledge, flexible social networks, and strong ability to adapt to arid and variable climates.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏集中政治组织与大规模储粮体系，地区间协同行动能力有限。
weaknesses.en: They lacked centralized political organization and large-scale grain storage systems, limiting coordinated action across regions.

outlook.zh: 在没有外来大规模压力的条件下，此类社会结构能够长期稳定延续，并持续优化区域资源管理。
outlook.en: In the absence of large external pressures, this social structure could remain stable for long periods while continuing to refine regional resource management.

description.zh: 公元前800年前后的澳大利亚原住民社会是以梦创信仰、长者权威和高适应性狩猎采集经济为核心的分散族群体系。
description.en: Around 800 BCE, Aboriginal Australian society was a decentralized cultural system centered on Dreaming beliefs, elder authority, and a highly adaptive hunter-gatherer economy.

dynasty.zh: 伊洛瓦底上游先缅与骠周缘部族
dynasty.en: Early Irrawaddy Proto-Burmese and Pyu-Frontier Tribes

governmentForm.zh: 以河谷聚落和山地部族为基础的早期酋邦政治，酋长依靠宗族、祭司和战士维系统治。
governmentForm.en: An early chiefdom polity based on riverine settlements and upland tribes, where chiefs ruled through kin groups, ritual specialists, and warriors.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由河谷农耕居民、手工业者、祭仪人员与周边山地部族共同构成，亲族关系仍是组织核心。沿伊洛瓦底和萨尔温流域的聚落已出现较稳定的农业社群，并与更复杂的骠文化圈接触。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of river-valley farmers, craftspeople, ritual personnel, and surrounding upland tribes, with kinship still the main organizing principle. Along the Irrawaddy and Salween basins, relatively stable agricultural communities had emerged and were in contact with the more complex Pyu cultural sphere.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层包括酋长家族、部落长老、祭司以及掌握武力的战士首领。其地位来自耕地控制、灌溉组织和对交换通道的影响。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum included chiefly families, tribal elders, priests, and warrior leaders. Their position derived from control of farmland, irrigation organization, and influence over exchange routes.

succession.zh: 首领多在强势家族内部继承，但仍需获得长老和主要战士支持。若部族分裂，分支家族可另立聚落与新酋长。
succession.en: Leadership usually passed within powerful families, but still required support from elders and leading warriors. If a tribe split, cadet branches could establish new settlements and chiefs.

structure.zh: 政体尚未形成成熟国家官僚，核心是酋长庭院、村社首领和宗教权威的联合治理。灌溉、水利、耕作时令和防御是主要公共事务。
structure.en: The polity had not yet developed a mature state bureaucracy; its core was joint governance by the chief's household, village headmen, and religious authorities. Irrigation, water management, farming seasons, and defense were the main public affairs.

function.zh: 组织农时、维护水渠与协调谷地聚落之间的劳役。
function.en: Organized agricultural timing, maintained canals, and coordinated labor among valley settlements.

function.zh: 主持祖灵祭祀，管理公共粮仓和酋长再分配储备。
function.en: Conducted ancestral rites and managed communal granaries and chiefly redistribution stores.

function.zh: 组织守卫、护送商旅并监控山谷通道安全。
function.en: Organized guards, escorted traders, and monitored the security of valley routes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由村社首领和家族长者管理，向酋长提供劳役、粮食和兵员。距离中心较远的山地群体自治程度更高。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was managed by village headmen and lineage elders, who supplied labor, grain, and fighters to the chief. Upland groups farther from the center enjoyed greater autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、部族调解和祭仪誓约为主，重点在土地、水源和婚姻纠纷。惩罚通常是赔偿、服役或被排除出共同体。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, tribal mediation, and ritual oaths, focusing on disputes over land, water, and marriage. Punishments usually took the form of compensation, service, or exclusion from the community.

taxationSystem.zh: 税负主要表现为谷物贡纳、灌溉劳役、手工业供给和战时徭役。酋长对贸易通道也可能征收通行贡礼。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form of grain tribute, irrigation labor, craft supplies, and wartime corvée. Chiefs could also collect transit tribute on trade routes.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 缅甸先民在稻作、水利协作、青铜与铁器并用及河谷聚落发展方面已有明显进展。其文化处于南亚、云贵高地与东南亚内陆交流的交汇地带。
culturalAchievements.en: Early Burmese populations had made clear advances in rice cultivation, water management cooperation, and the combined use of bronze and iron tools in river-valley settlements. Their culture stood at a crossroads between South Asia, the Yunnan uplands, and mainland Southeast Asia.

languageFamily.zh: 以藏缅语族早期诸语为主，并与骠语等区域语言接触
languageFamily.en: Mainly early Tibeto-Burman languages, with contact with Pyu and other regional tongues

philosophy.zh: 祖灵崇拜、万物有灵信仰与早期南亚文化接触并存
philosophy.en: Ancestral and animist beliefs coexisting with early influences from South Asian cultural contacts

writingSystem.zh: 本地尚无成熟文字，重要信息依靠口传；邻近骠文化圈可能已接触早期印度书写传统
writingSystem.en: There was no mature local writing system; important knowledge was transmitted orally, though neighboring Pyu spheres may already have encountered early Indian writing traditions

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础是稻作农业、河谷渔业、林产采集和区域交换，生产水平高于典型狩猎采集部族。部分富裕聚落已能形成公共仓储和有限专业手工业。
gdpDescription.en: The economic base was rice agriculture, riverine fishing, forest products, and regional exchange, with productivity above that of typical hunter-gatherer tribes. Some richer settlements could already sustain communal granaries and limited craft specialization.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作、粟作、渔业、林产、制陶、纺织、青铜与早期铁器加工
mainIndustries.en: Rice farming, millet cultivation, fishing, forest extraction, pottery, weaving, and bronze and early iron working

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、盐、木材、漆、象牙、珠饰、陶器、金属工具
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, timber, lacquer, ivory, beads, pottery, and metal tools

unitName.en: grain measure or tael of silver

householdWealth.zh: 家户财富主要体现为耕地、水牛或役畜、粮仓余量、金属工具和亲族劳力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in farmland, buffalo or draft animals, granary surplus, metal tools, and kin labor.

economicSystem.zh: 以农耕村社为基础、由酋长再分配和区域交换补充的早期部族经济。
economicSystem.en: An early tribal economy based on agrarian village communities, supplemented by chiefly redistribution and regional exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备主要保存在公共粮仓、金属器库存和酋长控制的贡物中，而非纯银金库存。
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves were held mainly in communal granaries, stocks of metal goods, and tribute under chiefly control rather than in pure silver or gold hoards.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政强调维持灌溉、储粮和战时动员，在丰年积累、歉年赈济。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized sustaining irrigation, grain storage, and wartime mobilization, accumulating in good years and providing relief in poor ones.

description.zh: 装备矛、盾、弓箭和少量铁刃武器，负责守卫村寨与田地。
description.en: Equipped with spears, shields, bows, and some iron-edged weapons, they defended villages and fields.

description.zh: 熟悉丘陵与丛林地形，适合侦察、伏击和护路。
description.en: Familiar with hills and jungle terrain, they were suited to scouting, ambushes, and route protection.

description.zh: 由酋长直属战士组成，承担护卫、征收贡物和战时核心突击任务。
description.en: Composed of the chief's direct warriors, they served as guards, tribute enforcers, and the core assault force in war.

commanderInChief.zh: 大酋长
commanderInChief.en: High Chief

technology.zh: 军事技术处于青铜晚期向铁器早期过渡，具备简单木寨、防御工事和有限金属兵器。
technology.en: Military technology was in a transition from late bronze to early iron use, with simple stockades, defensive works, and limited metal weaponry.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自山地部族冲突、河谷控制竞争以及与邻近更强聚落集团的摩擦。
threats.en: Main threats came from upland tribal conflict, competition for river-valley control, and friction with stronger neighboring settlement clusters.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在伊洛瓦底及支流河谷的小型农耕聚落，山区人口更分散。聚落形态以村寨和围栅社区为主，尚未出现真正大型城市。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in small farming settlements along the Irrawaddy and its tributaries, while upland populations were more dispersed. Settlements took the form of villages and stockaded communities rather than true large cities.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族、祭司与长老、自由农户与工匠、依附部众、战俘与奴役者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, priests and elders, free farmers and artisans, dependent followers, and captives or slaves.

allies.zh: 与邻近河谷村社、通婚部族及部分骠边缘聚落保持合作。
allies.en: Maintained cooperation with nearby river-valley villages, intermarrying tribes, and some Pyu frontier settlements.

enemies.zh: 争夺灌溉土地、盐源和通道控制权的山地或河谷竞争群体。
enemies.en: Upland or riverine rival groups competing for irrigable land, salt sources, and route control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以控制谷地资源、维持交换网络和避免被更强区域势力吞并为目标，常通过婚盟与贡礼达成暂时和平。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to control valley resources, preserve exchange networks, and avoid absorption by stronger regional powers, often using marriage alliances and tribute to secure temporary peace.

era.zh: 缅甸铁器时代早期

keyInnovations.zh: 湿稻农业扩展、简单灌溉、水牛役用、青铜晚期至早期铁器工具并用、围栅聚落。
keyInnovations.en: Expansion of wet-rice agriculture, simple irrigation, buffalo traction, combined late-bronze and early-iron tools, and stockaded settlements.

strengths.zh: 农业潜力较高，河谷交通便利，能逐步支持储粮、手工业和更强政治整合。
strengths.en: Agricultural potential was relatively high, river valleys offered good communications, and the society could gradually support grain storage, craft specialization, and stronger political integration.

weaknesses.zh: 政治仍较分散，文字与行政能力薄弱，易受地形分割与周边势力影响。
weaknesses.en: Politics remained fragmented, literacy and administration were weak, and the region was vulnerable to terrain fragmentation and neighboring powers.

outlook.zh: 若灌溉和河谷中心继续发展，未来有可能形成更稳定的城邦或早期王国结构。
outlook.en: If irrigation and river-valley centers continue to develop, more stable city-states or early kingdom structures may eventually emerge.

description.zh: 公元前800年前后的缅甸先民处于由部族酋邦向河谷农耕政治体过渡的早期铁器时代阶段。
description.en: Around 800 BCE, the early Burmese were in an Early Iron Age transition from tribal chiefdoms toward river-valley agrarian polities.

governmentForm.zh: 以蜀王为核心的神权—世俗混合君主制，王权与祭祀权高度统一，统治成都平原及周边山地河谷，具有强烈的地方性和独立性，与中原周室几乎无臣属关系。
governmentForm.en: A theocratic-secular hybrid monarchy centered on the King of Shu, in which royal authority and ritual power were closely unified. The polity controlled the Chengdu Plain and surrounding mountain valleys, maintaining strong local independence with virtually no subordination to the Zhou royal house.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由蜀王与王族、祭司—巫觋阶层、贵族武士、青铜工匠与玉石匠人、农民与山地部族构成。祭祀阶层的社会地位极高，反映古蜀独特的神权色彩。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the King and royal clan, a priest-shaman stratum, noble warriors, bronze and jade artisans, peasant farmers, and mountain tribal peoples. The ritual specialist class held exceptionally high status, reflecting the distinctive theocratic character of Ancient Shu.

rulingClass.zh: 以蜀王族和世袭祭司贵族为统治核心，掌控大型青铜祭祀、玉石生产、粮食征收和对外贸易权。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the Shu royal house and hereditary priestly aristocracy, controlling major bronze ritual production, jade manufacture, grain collection, and external trade.

succession.zh: 王位世袭，但在杜宇时代可能受宗教合法性与贵族支持影响，传承细节因文献匮乏而不甚清晰。
succession.en: Succession was hereditary, though during the Duyu period it may have been influenced by religious legitimacy and aristocratic backing. Details remain uncertain due to the scarcity of written records.

structure.zh: 古蜀政治体制以蜀王为最高权威，王廷兼具行政与宗教功能。中央权力集中于金沙—成都平原核心区，周边山地和河谷地区通过首领纳贡和军事联盟维持松散联系。官僚体系远不如中原发达，更依赖血缘、祭祀纽带和部族关系。
structure.en: The Ancient Shu political system placed the King as supreme authority, with the royal court serving both administrative and religious functions. Central power was concentrated in the Jinsha–Chengdu Plain core, while surrounding mountain and valley areas maintained loose ties through chiefly tribute and military alliances. The bureaucratic apparatus was far less developed than in the Central Plains, relying more on kinship, ritual bonds, and tribal relations.

function.zh: 管理蜀王宫室、近臣侍从、宝器保管与王族事务。
function.en: Managed the royal palace, personal attendants, custodianship of sacred objects, and royal clan affairs.

function.zh: 主持大型祭祀仪式、青铜面具与神树祭品的制作与焚烧、占卜和天文观测。
function.en: Directed major ritual ceremonies, production and burning of bronze masks and sacred tree offerings, divination, and astronomical observation.

function.zh: 统管官营青铜冶铸工坊、原料采集、大型礼器与兵器的制造。
function.en: Oversaw state bronze smelting and casting workshops, raw material procurement, and manufacture of large ritual vessels and weapons.

function.zh: 管理成都平原的稻作与粮食征收、灌溉水利、仓储和人口编户。
function.en: Managed rice cultivation and grain levies on the Chengdu Plain, irrigation works, granary storage, and population registration.

function.zh: 负责兵员征发、边地山口防御、武器储备和战时调度。
function.en: Handled troop conscription, defense of mountain passes, weapons stockpiles, and wartime coordination.

function.zh: 管理与巴、楚、滇、氐羌部族的贸易往来、通道维护和使节接待。
function.en: Managed trade relations with Ba, Chu, Dian, and Di-Qiang peoples, maintained pass routes, and received envoys.

localAdmin.zh: 成都平原核心区由蜀王直辖，周边山地河谷则通过附庸首领、贡赋义务和季节性朝觐维系联系。部族首领保留较大自治权，以定期贡献物资和出兵服务换取蜀王庇护与祭祀物品。
localAdmin.en: The Chengdu Plain core was under the king's direct administration, while surrounding mountain and valley areas were linked through subordinate chiefs, tribute obligations, and seasonal attendance. Tribal leaders retained significant autonomy, exchanging periodic resource contributions and military service for royal patronage and ritual goods.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁决和宗教禁忌为主，无成文法典。祭祀违规、盗窃祭器和对王权的不敬被视为最严重的罪行。争端多由部族长老或王廷裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on customary law, royal verdicts, and religious taboos without codified statutes. Ritual violations, theft of sacred objects, and disrespect toward royal authority were treated as the gravest offenses. Disputes were typically resolved by tribal elders or the royal court.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入来自平原稻作区的谷物田赋、山地部族的贡品（包括铜料、兽皮、象牙、玉石原料）、劳役征发和青铜作坊的生产剩余。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue derived from grain levies on Chengdu Plain rice lands, tribute from mountain communities (including copper ore, hides, ivory, and raw jade), corvée labor, and surpluses from bronze workshops.

religion.zh: 古蜀宗教极具特色，以太阳崇拜、神树信仰、纵目面具祭祀和大规模青铜祭品焚烧为核心。祖先崇拜与自然神灵崇拜并存，巫觋通过迷狂仪式沟通人神，宗教色彩远浓于中原同期文明。
religion.en: Ancient Shu religion was highly distinctive, centered on solar worship, sacred tree cults, protruding-eye mask rituals, and large-scale burning of bronze offerings. Ancestor veneration and nature spirit worship coexisted, with shamans communicating between humans and deities through ecstatic ceremonies. The religious atmosphere was far more intense than contemporary Central Plains civilizations.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成系统哲学思想，精神生活围绕对超自然力量的敬畏、天象观测、祖先灵魂信仰和王权神圣性展开。其思维模式偏重象征与仪式，与中原理性化趋向不同。
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophy had developed. Intellectual life revolved around awe of supernatural forces, celestial observation, ancestral spirit beliefs, and the sacredness of kingship. The mode of thinking emphasized symbolism and ritual, diverging from the rationalizing tendencies emerging in the Central Plains.

writingSystem.zh: 古蜀是否拥有独立文字系统至今存疑。三星堆与金沙遗址出土部分刻画符号，但尚无法确认为成熟文字。行政与祭祀记录可能主要依赖口头传统和器物符号。
writingSystem.en: Whether Ancient Shu possessed an independent writing system remains debated. Sanxingdui and Jinsha sites have yielded incised symbols, but these cannot yet be confirmed as a mature script. Administrative and ritual records likely relied primarily on oral tradition and object-based symbolism.

culturalAchievements.zh: 古蜀文化在大型青铜雕塑（纵目面具、青铜神树、青铜立人像）、金器制作、玉器琮璧、象牙祭品和独特宗教美学方面达到极高水平，是同时期东亚最具创造力的青铜文化之一。
culturalAchievements.en: Ancient Shu culture achieved extraordinary heights in large-scale bronze sculpture (protruding-eye masks, sacred bronze trees, standing bronze figures), gold craftsmanship, jade cong and bi, ivory offerings, and a distinctive religious aesthetic. It was one of the most creative bronze cultures in contemporary East Asia.

languageFamily.zh: 古蜀语言归属不明，可能属于藏缅语族或独立语支。与中原古汉语存在交流但差异明显，境内可能存在多种部族语言。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic affiliation of Ancient Shu remains uncertain, possibly belonging to the Tibeto-Burman family or an independent branch. It had contacts with Old Chinese of the Central Plains but was clearly distinct, and multiple tribal languages likely coexisted within its territory.

gdpDescription.zh: 古蜀经济依托成都平原肥沃的稻作农业，辅以山地资源（铜、锡、玉石、木材）和发达的青铜手工业。虽然贸易网络不如中原广泛，但盆地封闭地形内自给能力强，加上高度发达的青铜产业，使整体经济规模在西南地区首屈一指。
gdpDescription.en: The Ancient Shu economy relied on the fertile rice agriculture of the Chengdu Plain, supplemented by mountain resources (copper, tin, jade, timber) and an advanced bronze craft industry. Although its trade networks were less extensive than those of the Central Plains, the basin's self-sufficiency and highly developed bronze production made its total economic scale preeminent in the southwest.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、青铜冶铸、玉石加工、金器制作、畜牧、林业、象牙贸易和盐业。
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, bronze smelting and casting, jade working, gold craftsmanship, animal husbandry, forestry, ivory trade, and salt production.

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、青铜礼器与兵器、玉石制品、金箔金器、象牙、漆器、兽皮、木材、丹砂和盐。
tradeGoods.en: Rice, bronze ritual vessels and weapons, jade objects, gold foil and gold artifacts, ivory, lacquerware, hides, timber, cinnabar, and salt.

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户以耕地、粮储、家畜和竹木器具为主要财富；贵族与祭司阶层则拥有大量青铜器、金器、玉器、象牙和祭祀权力。财富分化明显，祭祀物品具有超越物质的精神价值。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households held wealth chiefly in farmland, grain stores, livestock, and bamboo and wooden tools. The aristocratic and priestly classes possessed large quantities of bronze, gold, jade, ivory, and ritual authority. Wealth stratification was pronounced, with sacrificial objects carrying spiritual value beyond their material worth.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室与祭司阶层主导的再分配经济为核心。平原农业产出通过贡赋集中于王廷，再以祭祀物品、军需和贵族封赏形式分配。与外部的交换主要通过贡品和长途贸易品实现。
economicSystem.en: A redistributive economy dominated by the royal house and priestly class. Agricultural output from the plains was concentrated at court through tribute, then distributed as ritual goods, military supplies, and aristocratic rewards. External exchange operated chiefly through tribute and long-distance trade goods.

source.en: Plain Land Tax and Grain Collection

source.zh: 青铜作坊与官营手工业产出
source.en: Bronze Workshop and State Craft Output

category.zh: 青铜作坊与手工业投入

treasuryDescription.zh: 蜀国国库以谷仓、青铜器储备、金器、玉器、象牙和铜锡原料构成。金沙遗址显示的大量金器和青铜埋藏表明王室积累了相当财富，但大量资源通过祭祀仪式被'消费'（焚烧、掩埋）。
treasuryDescription.en: The Shu treasury consisted of grain stores, bronze reserves, gold artifacts, jade, ivory, and raw copper and tin. The large deposits of gold and bronze found at the Jinsha site indicate substantial royal accumulation, though significant resources were 'consumed' through ritual burning and burial.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策注重维持祭祀体系的物资需求、保障青铜生产链条和储备战略粮食。对山地部族征收适度贡赋以维系忠诚，同时通过长途贸易获取盆地内缺乏的战略物资。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining material needs for the ritual system, securing the bronze production chain, and stockpiling strategic grain reserves. Moderate tribute was extracted from mountain tribes to maintain loyalty, while long-distance trade obtained strategic materials unavailable within the basin.

description.zh: 蜀王直属精锐武士，负责王城安全、祭祀护卫和战时中军，装备精良的青铜兵器。
description.en: Elite warriors directly attached to the king, responsible for capital security, ritual protection, and the central battle line, equipped with fine bronze weapons.

description.zh: 从成都平原农业人口中征发的主力步兵，装备青铜矛、戈、弓箭和竹盾。
description.en: Main infantry levied from the Chengdu Plain farming population, armed with bronze spears, ge-daggers, bows, and bamboo shields.

description.zh: 来自盆地周边山地的辅助部队，擅长山林地形作战、伏击和小规模突袭。
description.en: Auxiliary forces from the mountains surrounding the basin, skilled in rough-terrain warfare, ambush, and small-scale raids.

description.zh: 控制岷江、沱江等水路的船队武力，负责水上运输、渡口防御和江上巡逻。
description.en: Boat-based forces controlling the Min and Tuo rivers, responsible for waterborne transport, ferry defense, and river patrol.

commanderInChief.zh: 蜀王兼最高统帅，军事指挥由王族将领和资深武士首领分掌
commanderInChief.en: The King of Shu served as commander-in-chief, with military command shared among royal clan generals and senior warrior chiefs

technology.zh: 军备以青铜兵器为主，包括独具蜀地风格的三角形青铜剑、矛头、戈、箭镞和青铜盾饰。缺乏战车（盆地地形限制），但在山地战和水路作战方面有独到优势。铁器尚未进入军事领域。
technology.en: Military equipment was predominantly bronze-based, including distinctively Shu-style triangular bronze swords, spearheads, ge-daggers, arrowheads, and bronze shield fittings. Chariots were absent due to basin terrain, but the military held unique advantages in mountain warfare and riverine operations. Iron had not yet entered the military sphere.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方秦岭以北的周系诸侯侵扰、东部巴国的竞争、西部和南部氐羌及西南部族的袭扰，以及盆地内部可能的部族离心。地理屏障提供天然防护，但也限制了战略纵深。
threats.en: Primary threats included pressure from Zhou-affiliated states north of the Qinling Mountains, competition with the Ba polity to the east, raids from Di-Qiang and southwestern tribal peoples to the west and south, and potential tribal defections within the basin. Geographic barriers offered natural protection but also limited strategic depth.

populationDescription.zh: 古蜀人口约五十万，集中于成都平原的灌溉稻作区，周边山地河谷有零散部族聚落。人口密度在平原区较高，但山地区域极为稀疏。
populationDescription.en: Ancient Shu had a population of roughly 500,000, concentrated in the irrigated rice-growing areas of the Chengdu Plain, with scattered tribal settlements in surrounding mountain valleys. Population density was relatively high on the plain but extremely sparse in mountainous areas.

socialClasses.zh: 最高层为蜀王与王族，其次为祭司—巫觋贵族，再次为军事贵族与武士，然后是青铜与玉石工匠、普通农民和山地依附部族。祭祀阶层的社会地位之高在同期东亚文明中极为罕见。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the king and royal house, followed by the priestly-shamanic aristocracy, then military nobles and warriors, bronze and jade artisans, ordinary farmers, and dependent mountain tribes. The exceptionally high status of the ritual specialist class was rare among contemporary East Asian civilizations.

allies.zh: 古蜀与周边关系以贸易和贡纳为主，无现代意义上的盟友。与巴国既有竞争又有贸易往来，与长江中游楚国有间接商品交换，与西北氐羌部族维持不稳定的和平。
allies.en: Ancient Shu's external relations were primarily based on trade and tribute, with no allies in the modern sense. It had both competitive and commercial ties with Ba, indirect goods exchange with Chu in the middle Yangtze, and an unstable peace with the Di-Qiang tribes to the northwest.

enemies.zh: 与巴国在川东地区存在领土竞争，与氐羌部族在西部山区时有冲突。来自北方秦岭方向的威胁潜在但尚不直接。
enemies.en: Territorial rivalry existed with Ba in eastern Sichuan, and periodic conflicts occurred with Di-Qiang groups in the western mountains. The threat from the north across the Qinling was latent but not yet direct.

foreignPolicy.zh: 古蜀外交以维持盆地独立、确保贸易通道畅通、控制铜锡盐等战略资源来源为核心。对周王室体系保持距离，既不臣服也不主动对抗，依靠地理屏障维持事实独立。
foreignPolicy.en: Shu foreign policy centered on maintaining basin independence, ensuring open trade routes, and controlling access to strategic copper, tin, and salt resources. It kept its distance from the Zhou royal system, neither submitting nor actively opposing it, relying on geographic barriers to maintain de facto independence.

era.zh: 青铜时代高峰

keyInnovations.zh: 世界级大型青铜雕塑铸造技术（纵目面具高达数十公分）、金箔锤揲术、复杂玉器加工、水稻灌溉与水利管理、金沙太阳鸟金饰制作工艺、象牙精加工。铁器尚未传入。
keyInnovations.en: World-class large-scale bronze sculpture casting (protruding-eye masks reaching tens of centimeters), gold foil hammering techniques, complex jade processing, rice irrigation and water management, the Jinsha sun-bird gold ornament craftsmanship, and refined ivory working. Iron had not yet arrived.

strengths.zh: 古蜀拥有成都平原高产农业基础、独步东亚的青铜艺术与冶铸技术、天然的盆地地理屏障、丰富的铜锡玉石资源，以及强大的宗教凝聚力和文化认同。
strengths.en: Ancient Shu possessed the highly productive agricultural base of the Chengdu Plain, bronze art and casting technology unmatched in East Asia, natural basin geographic barriers, abundant copper, tin, and jade resources, and powerful religious cohesion and cultural identity.

weaknesses.zh: 地理封闭限制了对外交流与战略机动性，缺乏文字系统使行政效率受限，人口规模相对中原大国偏小，过度依赖祭祀消耗大量社会资源，且铁器技术滞后。
weaknesses.en: Geographic enclosure limited external exchange and strategic mobility, the lack of a writing system constrained administrative efficiency, population was relatively small compared to major Central Plains states, heavy investment in ritual consumed substantial social resources, and iron technology lagged behind.

outlook.zh: 前800年的古蜀正处于金沙文化的繁盛期。成都平原的农业潜力和独特的青铜文化使其在西南地区独占鳌头，但长期而言，若不能适应铁器时代的技术变革和扩大对外联系，将面临被更大政治体吸收的风险。
outlook.en: In 800 BCE Ancient Shu was in the flourishing period of Jinsha culture. The agricultural potential of the Chengdu Plain and its unique bronze culture made it preeminent in the southwest, but in the long term, failure to adapt to Iron Age technological changes and expand external connections risked absorption by larger polities.

description.zh: 四川盆地的古蜀王國，以三星堆─金沙系統的獨特青銅文明聞名，擁有強大祭祀傳統。
description.en: The Ancient Shu kingdom in the Sichuan Basin, renowned for the distinctive Sanxingdui–Jinsha bronze civilization and powerful ritual traditions.

ruler.zh: 各部族首领（无统一君主）

capital.zh: 无统一都城（各部有区域中心）
capital.en: No unified capital (each group had regional centers)

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族联盟与酋邦体制。各越族群体独立运作，以血缘氏族和地域为基础组织社会，部分沿海和河谷地区形成较大的酋邦，但从未形成统一政权。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed system of tribal confederacies and chiefdoms. Each Yue group operated independently, organizing society on the basis of kinship clans and territory. Some coastal and river valley areas formed larger chiefdoms, but no unified polity ever emerged.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由部族首领与长老、战士与猎手、稻农与渔民、工匠（尤其是陶器和纺织）构成。氏族血缘纽带为基本社会组织，无鲜明的贵族世袭制。
socialStructure.en: Society comprised tribal chiefs and elders, warriors and hunters, rice farmers and fishermen, and artisans (especially potters and weavers). Clan kinship ties formed the basic social organization, without a distinct hereditary aristocratic system.

rulingClass.zh: 各部首领通过军事威望、长老推举和宗族地位获取权力，无跨越部族界限的统一统治阶层。
rulingClass.en: Individual chiefs derived power through military prestige, elder selection, and clan standing. No unified ruling class existed across tribal boundaries.

succession.zh: 首领继承多在同族内由长老推选或以军功为据，各部做法不一，无固定制度。
succession.en: Succession within each tribe typically involved elder selection or recognition of military merit within the clan, with practices varying by group and no fixed system.

structure.zh: 百越无中央政府。各部族以首领—长老会议为决策核心，大型酋邦可能设有专职战争首领、祭祀主持者和贸易管理者，但不存在正规官僚体系。部族间通过季节性集会、婚姻联盟和贸易关系维持松散联系。
structure.en: The Baiyue had no central government. Each tribe was governed by a chief and council of elders. Larger chiefdoms may have had specialized war leaders, ritual directors, and trade managers, but no formal bureaucracy existed. Inter-tribal relations were maintained through seasonal gatherings, marriage alliances, and trade ties.

function.zh: 管理首领家族事务、食物分配、贡品接收与族内纠纷调解。
function.en: Managed chiefly family affairs, food distribution, receipt of contributions, and intra-clan dispute resolution.

function.zh: 集体议决部族重大事务，包括战争、迁徙、祭祀和对外关系。
function.en: Collectively decided major tribal affairs including warfare, migration, ritual, and external relations.

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、自然神灵崇拜、农事祈福和占卜活动。
function.en: Conducted ancestor worship, nature spirit cults, agricultural blessings, and divination activities.

function.zh: 组织部族武力、领土防御、掠夺远征和渔猎活动。
function.en: Organized tribal fighting forces, territorial defense, raiding expeditions, and hunting-fishing activities.

function.zh: 管理部族间物品交换、海上和河流贸易以及与北方华夏族群的往来。
function.en: Managed inter-tribal goods exchange, maritime and riverine trade, and interactions with northern Huaxia peoples.

localAdmin.zh: 各部族自治，村寨由氏族长老管理，较大的酋邦由首领通过血缘网络和附庸关系统辖周边小部。无超越部族的统一行政系统，各区域以方言、习俗和地理为界。
localAdmin.en: Each tribe was self-governing, with villages managed by clan elders and larger chiefdoms overseeing surrounding smaller groups through kinship networks and dependent relationships. No unified administrative system existed beyond tribal level, with regions defined by dialect, custom, and geography.

legalSystem.zh: 以口头习惯法、长老裁决和部族惯例为主。严重罪行包括杀人、盗窃、违反禁忌和叛族，处罚包括放逐、赔偿、身体惩罚和在极端情况下的处死。
legalSystem.en: Based on oral customary law, elder adjudication, and tribal convention. Serious offenses included homicide, theft, taboo violation, and tribal betrayal, with punishments including exile, compensation, corporal punishment, and in extreme cases, execution.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正规税制。首领通过社群劳动义务、渔获分享、集体猎猎成果分配和部族成员自愿献纳获取资源。较大酋邦可能向附庸小部征收谷物和物资。
taxationSystem.en: No formal tax system existed. Chiefs obtained resources through communal labor obligations, sharing of fishing catches, distribution of collective hunting yields, and voluntary contributions from tribal members. Larger chiefdoms may have levied grain and goods from subordinate groups.

religion.zh: 百越宗教以自然崇拜为核心，包括蛇崇拜（蛇图腾广泛存在）、山川河海祭祀、稻神崇拜、祖先灵魂崇拜和各种动物图腾。断发纹身具有宗教与身份标识双重意义。
religion.en: Baiyue religion centered on nature worship, including serpent veneration (snake totems were widespread), mountain-river-sea rituals, rice deity cults, ancestral spirit worship, and various animal totems. Hair cropping and body tattooing carried both religious and identity significance.

philosophy.zh: 无系统哲学思想，精神世界围绕万物有灵论、祖先灵魂信仰、自然界的超自然力量和部族起源神话展开。
philosophy.en: No systematic philosophy existed. The spiritual world revolved around animism, ancestral spirit beliefs, supernatural forces in nature, and tribal origin myths.

writingSystem.zh: 百越无已知文字系统，知识和历史通过口头传统、刻画符号和纹身图案传承。
writingSystem.en: The Baiyue had no known writing system. Knowledge and history were transmitted through oral tradition, incised symbols, and tattoo patterns.

culturalAchievements.zh: 百越文化在几何印纹硬陶、稻作技术、船舶建造、干栏式建筑、纺织（树皮布、葛布）和龙舟传统方面有突出成就。其航海技术在东亚沿海地区居领先地位。
culturalAchievements.en: Baiyue culture achieved distinction in geometric stamped hard pottery, rice cultivation technology, boat building, pile-dwelling architecture, weaving (bark cloth, kudzu fiber), and the dragon boat tradition. Their maritime skills were leading among East Asian coastal peoples.

languageFamily.zh: 百越语言属于侗台语系、南亚语系等多个语族的前身，各地差异极大。与现代壮、侗、傣、越南语等语言有源流关系。
languageFamily.en: Baiyue languages were ancestral to multiple language families including Kra-Dai and Austroasiatic, with enormous regional variation. They are ancestral to modern Zhuang, Dong, Dai, Vietnamese, and related languages.

gdpDescription.zh: 百越经济总量因人口众多而规模较大，但人均产出偏低。以水稻种植为基础，辅以渔猎、采集、制陶和沿海贸易。各地经济发展水平参差不齐，沿海和大河谷地区较为富裕，山地部族更依赖采集和游耕。
gdpDescription.en: The total Baiyue economy was sizeable due to large population but per capita output was modest. It was based on rice cultivation, supplemented by fishing, hunting, gathering, pottery production, and coastal trade. Economic development varied greatly, with coastal and major valley areas more prosperous while mountain tribes relied more on gathering and swidden farming.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻种植、渔业、狩猎采集、制陶（几何印纹硬陶）、纺织、造船、盐业、采矿（铜、锡）和沿海贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, fishing, hunting-gathering, pottery (geometric stamped hard ware), weaving, boat building, salt production, mining (copper, tin), and coastal trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、海产品、贝壳、珍珠、龟甲、犀角、象牙、硬陶器、树皮布、铜锡矿料、香料植物和药草。
tradeGoods.en: Rice, seafood, shells, pearls, tortoiseshells, rhinoceros horn, ivory, hard pottery, bark cloth, copper and tin ore, aromatic plants, and medicinal herbs.

name.zh: 以物易物（贝壳与谷物为主要交换媒介）
name.en: Barter (shells and grain as main exchange media)

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭以稻田、渔具、陶器、家畜（猪、犬、鸡）和竹木器具为主要财富。首领家族可能拥有较多青铜器、贝壳饰品和贸易获取的奢侈品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households held wealth mainly in rice paddies, fishing equipment, pottery, livestock (pigs, dogs, chickens), and bamboo and wooden implements. Chiefly families may have possessed more bronze goods, shell ornaments, and luxury items obtained through trade.

economicSystem.zh: 以自给自足的稻作—渔猎经济为基础，辅以部族间物物交换和沿海—河流贸易网络。无统一市场体系，但沿海地区的贸易活动已具有相当规模。
economicSystem.en: Based on a self-sufficient rice-farming and fishing-hunting economy, supplemented by inter-tribal barter and coastal-riverine trade networks. No unified market system existed, but trade activity in coastal areas had already reached considerable scale.

source.zh: 部族间贸易与沿海交换

treasuryDescription.zh: 百越各部无集中国库概念。储备分散于各部族首领的仓储中，以谷物、干鱼、盐、贝壳、铜器和陶器为主要形式。沿海部族积累的贸易品和贝壳饰品相对丰厚。
treasuryDescription.en: The Baiyue had no centralized treasury concept. Reserves were distributed among individual chiefly stores, primarily in the form of grain, dried fish, salt, shells, bronze goods, and pottery. Coastal tribes accumulated relatively abundant trade goods and shell ornaments.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 无统一财政政策。各部族按需动员社群资源，以互助、分享和礼物交换为原则。较大酋邦的首领可能通过征收贡品和控制贸易来积累超出基本需要的资源。
fiscalPolicy.en: No unified fiscal policy existed. Each tribe mobilized communal resources as needed, based on principles of mutual aid, sharing, and gift exchange. Chiefs of larger chiefdoms may have accumulated resources beyond basic needs through tribute collection and trade control.

description.zh: 各部落全民皆兵式的步兵力量，装备矛、弓、竹盾和石器铜器混合武器，擅长丛林和山地战。
description.en: Universal militia-type infantry from various tribes, armed with spears, bows, bamboo shields, and a mix of stone and bronze weapons, skilled in jungle and mountain warfare.

description.zh: 沿海和河流部族的水上武力，使用独木舟和竹筏进行水上突袭、河口防御和沿海劫掠。
description.en: Waterborne fighters from coastal and riverine tribes, using dugout canoes and bamboo rafts for aquatic raids, estuary defense, and coastal raiding.

description.zh: 内陆山地部族的精锐猎手，擅长伏击、毒箭使用和密林中的游击战。
description.en: Elite hunters from inland mountain tribes, skilled in ambush, poisoned arrow use, and guerrilla warfare in dense forests.

description.zh: 利用渔船和近海航行能力执行沿海巡逻、侦察和贸易护航任务。
description.en: Leveraged fishing vessels and coastal navigation skills for patrols, reconnaissance, and trade escort missions.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一指挥官，各部族首领各自统率本部武力，战时可能推举联盟战争首领
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander; each tribal chief commanded their own forces, with a temporary alliance war leader potentially elected during major conflicts

technology.zh: 军备以青铜矛头、石器、弓箭（含毒箭）、竹矛、竹盾为主。造船技术先进，独木舟可载数十人。部分地区已出现早期青铜兵器，但石器和竹木器仍广泛使用。无战车。
technology.en: Military equipment consisted primarily of bronze spearheads, stone tools, bows and arrows (including poisoned arrows), bamboo spears, and bamboo shields. Boat-building was advanced, with dugout canoes capable of carrying dozens. Some areas had early bronze weapons, but stone and bamboo-wood implements remained widely used. No chariots.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方华夏诸侯（楚国等）的南进压力、部族间的领土争夺和劫掠、以及自然灾害（台风、洪水）对沿海聚落的威胁。百越各部间的内部冲突也是持续性安全隐患。
threats.en: Primary threats included southward pressure from northern Huaxia states (especially Chu), inter-tribal territorial disputes and raiding, and natural disasters (typhoons, floods) threatening coastal settlements. Internal conflicts among Baiyue groups were also a persistent security concern.

populationDescription.zh: 百越总人口约两百万，分散于华南广大的沿海平原、河谷、丘陵和山地。人口密度沿海和大河三角洲地区较高，内陆山区极低。各部族规模从数百到数万人不等。
populationDescription.en: The total Baiyue population was roughly two million, dispersed across the vast coastal plains, river valleys, hills, and mountains of southern China. Population density was higher in coastal and major river delta areas and extremely low in inland mountains. Individual tribal groups ranged from hundreds to tens of thousands.

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层较中原浅平。首领与长老居顶层，战士与猎手享有较高地位，其次为稻农、渔民和工匠，最底层为战俘和部族间附庸。各部族内部相对平等，但部族间存在强弱差异。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was relatively flat compared to the Central Plains. Chiefs and elders stood at the top, warriors and hunters enjoyed elevated status, followed by rice farmers, fishermen, and artisans, with war captives and inter-tribal dependents at the bottom. Internal equality within tribes was relatively high, but significant power differences existed between groups.

allies.zh: 各部族间通过婚姻联盟和贸易伙伴关系维持合作。部分越族群体与北方楚国有边境贸易往来。无现代意义上的外交同盟。
allies.en: Cooperation among tribes was maintained through marriage alliances and trade partnerships. Some Yue groups had border trade contacts with Chu to the north. No diplomatic alliances existed in the modern sense.

enemies.zh: 各部族间的领土争夺和劫掠时有发生。北方楚国的扩张是最大的长期外部威胁。部分内陆山地部族与沿海部族也存在资源竞争。
enemies.en: Territorial disputes and raiding among tribes were frequent. Chu expansion from the north represented the greatest long-term external threat. Resource competition also existed between some inland mountain groups and coastal tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 各部族以维持领地完整、保障贸易通道和确保食物安全为外交核心。面对北方压力时，部分部族可能临时联合，但联盟往往短暂。沿海部族更倾向于海上贸易网络的维护。
foreignPolicy.en: Each tribe's foreign policy centered on maintaining territorial integrity, securing trade routes, and ensuring food security. Facing northern pressure, some tribes might temporarily unite, but alliances were typically short-lived. Coastal tribes focused more on maintaining maritime trade networks.

era.zh: 青铜时代（部分地区向铁器过渡）
era.en: Bronze Age (some areas transitioning to iron)

keyInnovations.zh: 先进的水稻种植与水田管理技术、几何印纹硬陶烧制、独木舟与竹筏造船、干栏式高脚建筑、树皮布与葛纤维纺织、毒箭与丛林生存技术、沿海航行与潮汐利用。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced rice cultivation and paddy management, geometric stamped hard pottery firing, dugout canoe and bamboo raft building, pile-dwelling elevated architecture, bark cloth and kudzu fiber weaving, poisoned arrows and jungle survival techniques, and coastal navigation with tidal exploitation.

strengths.zh: 百越拥有广大领土、众多人口、丰富的水产与农业资源、先进的航海与造船技术、密林山地带来的天然防御优势，以及极强的地方适应能力。
strengths.en: The Baiyue possessed a vast territory, large population, abundant aquatic and agricultural resources, advanced maritime and boat-building technology, natural defensive advantages from dense forests and mountains, and exceptional local adaptability.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏政治统一和有效的跨部族协调机制、无文字系统、军事技术落后于北方华夏诸国、部族间内部冲突消耗资源、且面对有组织的国家力量时难以形成有效抵抗。
weaknesses.en: They lacked political unity and effective cross-tribal coordination, had no writing system, possessed military technology inferior to northern Huaxia states, consumed resources through inter-tribal conflicts, and struggled to mount effective resistance against organized state forces.

outlook.zh: 前800年的百越诸部在华南拥有广大生存空间，但缺乏统一的政治意识和协调能力。随着楚国等北方强国南进加速，百越将长期面临被分割、同化和边缘化的压力。部分沿海部族可能通过海上贸易保持一定独立性。
outlook.en: In 800 BCE the Baiyue peoples occupied vast living space across southern China, but lacked unified political consciousness and coordination. As Chu and other northern powers accelerated southward expansion, the Baiyue would face long-term pressure of fragmentation, assimilation, and marginalization. Some coastal groups might maintain a degree of independence through maritime trade.

description.zh: 華南廣大區域的百越部族群體，以稻作、漁業和航海見長，文化多元但政治分散。
description.en: The diverse Baiyue tribal peoples across southern China, excelling in rice cultivation, fishing, and seafaring, culturally rich but politically fragmented.

dynasty.zh: 各邦世袭王族（如拘萨罗太阳族、迦尸王族等）
dynasty.en: Various hereditary royal lineages (e.g. Solar dynasty of Kosala, Kings of Kashi, etc.)

capital.zh: 各邦各有都城（阿约提亚、迦尸城、瞻波等）
capital.en: Each kingdom had its own capital (Ayodhya, Kashi, Champa, etc.)

governmentForm.zh: 晚期吠陀时代的北印度由多个从部落向君主国过渡的王国与部族共和体组成。罗阇（国王）权力正在扩大，但仍受萨巴（长老议会）和萨米提（民众大会）制约。婆罗门祭司阶层提供宗教合法性，刹帝利武士贵族掌控军事。
governmentForm.en: Late Vedic North India comprised multiple kingdoms and tribal republics transitioning from tribal to monarchical organization. The raja's power was expanding but remained constrained by the sabha (council of elders) and samiti (popular assembly). The Brahmin priestly class provided religious legitimacy while the Kshatriya warrior aristocracy controlled military affairs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以瓦尔纳（四种姓）制度为理想秩序：婆罗门（祭司学者）、刹帝利（武士贵族）、吠舍（农商平民）、首陀罗（服务者）。实际社会更为复杂，部落传统与种姓秩序并存，各邦情况不一。
socialStructure.en: Society was ideally organized according to the varna system: Brahmins (priests and scholars), Kshatriyas (warriors and nobles), Vaishyas (farmers and traders), and Shudras (servants). In practice society was more complex, with tribal traditions coexisting with caste order and conditions varying by kingdom.

rulingClass.zh: 刹帝利王族与武士贵族构成世俗统治核心，婆罗门祭司通过掌握祭祀权和宗教知识影响政治，两大阶层共同维系吠陀社会秩序。
rulingClass.en: The Kshatriya royal families and warrior nobility formed the secular ruling core, while Brahmin priests influenced politics through their monopoly on ritual authority and sacred knowledge. Together these two classes sustained the Vedic social order.

succession.zh: 王位通常父子相传，但长老议会、军事贵族支持和宗教仪式（如罗阇苏耶大祭）对继承合法性至关重要。部分共和体采用推选制。
succession.en: Kingship was generally patrilineal, but elder council backing, military aristocratic support, and religious rituals (such as the rajasuya sacrifice) were essential for succession legitimacy. Some republics used elective systems.

structure.zh: 各邦政体以国王为中心，辅以宫廷官员、婆罗门顾问和武士议会。大型王国如拘萨罗、迦尸开始出现常设行政机构，管理税收、军事和祭祀事务。较小部族仍以长老议会为主要决策机构。行政区域划分正在形成，但尚不成熟。
structure.en: Each polity centered on the king, assisted by court officials, Brahmin advisors, and warrior councils. Larger kingdoms like Kosala and Kashi were developing permanent administrative bodies for taxation, military, and ritual affairs. Smaller tribes still relied on elder councils as primary decision-making bodies. Territorial administrative divisions were emerging but not yet mature.

function.zh: 管理国王日常事务、宫室、王族婚姻与继承仪式、珍宝保管。
function.en: Managed the king's daily affairs, palace, royal marriages and succession rituals, and treasure custody.

function.zh: 统筹各邦军事力量，管理战象、战车、步兵征发、武器储备和边境防御。
function.en: Coordinated military forces across kingdoms, managed war elephants, chariots, infantry levies, weapons stores, and frontier defense.

function.zh: 主持国家祭祀（如马祭、罗阇苏耶）、吉凶占卜、维护吠陀传统和国王的宗教合法性。
function.en: Directed state sacrifices (such as ashvamedha and rajasuya), auspicious divination, maintenance of Vedic tradition, and the king's religious legitimacy.

function.zh: 征收粮税、牲畜税和手工业贡品，管理王室仓储与财物。
function.en: Collected grain taxes, livestock levies, and craft tribute, and managed royal granaries and treasures.

function.zh: 由高级婆罗门、刹帝利长老和有威望者组成，对国王政策提供建议、审理重大案件、参与外交决策。
function.en: Comprising senior Brahmins, Kshatriya elders, and persons of standing, it advised the king on policy, adjudicated major cases, and participated in diplomatic decisions.

function.zh: 管理恒河流域的灌溉渠道、农田分配、播种季节协调和储粮。
function.en: Managed irrigation channels in the Ganges basin, farmland allocation, sowing season coordination, and grain storage.

function.zh: 处理与邻邦的婚姻联盟、战争谈判、贡纳安排和商路事务。
function.en: Handled marriage alliances, war negotiations, tribute arrangements, and trade route matters with neighboring kingdoms.

localAdmin.zh: 各王国的地方治理以村落（格拉马）为基础单位，由村长和长老委员会管理。多个村落组成的区域由国王任命或认可的地方官管辖。吠舍和首陀罗负责具体生产，婆罗门负责宗教事务。偏远和部族地区仍保持较大自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was based on the village (grama) as the fundamental unit, managed by village headmen and elder committees. Clusters of villages were overseen by officials appointed or recognized by the king. Vaishyas and Shudras handled production, Brahmins managed religious affairs. Remote and tribal areas retained significant autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以吠陀经典中的达摩（法义）、国王裁决和婆罗门法典传统为基础。各种姓适用不同法律标准，婆罗门享有最高法律保护。刑罚包括罚金、肉刑、放逐和死刑。纠纷优先由种姓内部和村落长老解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on dharma as expressed in Vedic texts, royal decrees, and Brahmanical legal traditions. Different legal standards applied to each varna, with Brahmins enjoying the highest legal protections. Punishments included fines, corporal punishment, exile, and execution. Disputes were preferably resolved within caste and village elder systems.

taxationSystem.zh: 主要税源包括农产品的六分之一（巴里）、牲畜税、手工业贡品、商路关税和劳役。各邦税率不一，婆罗门通常免税。大型祭祀仪式需要征集大量财富和牲畜。
taxationSystem.en: Primary tax sources included one-sixth of agricultural produce (bali), livestock taxes, craft tribute, trade route tolls, and corvée labor. Tax rates varied by kingdom, with Brahmins typically exempt. Major sacrificial ceremonies required the mobilization of substantial wealth and cattle.

religion.zh: 晚期吠陀宗教以因陀罗、阿耆尼、婆楼那等吠陀诸神崇拜为核心，辅以盛大的火祭（雅吉尼亚）仪式。婆罗门经典正在编纂中，宇宙论和仪式哲学日趋复杂。早期奥义书思想开始萌芽，探索梵我关系。
religion.en: Late Vedic religion centered on worship of Vedic gods (Indra, Agni, Varuna, etc.) through elaborate fire sacrifice (yajna) rituals. Brahmana texts were being compiled, with increasingly complex cosmology and ritual philosophy. Early Upanishadic thought was beginning to emerge, exploring the relationship between Brahman and Atman.

philosophy.zh: 思想正处于从仪式主义向哲学思辨的过渡期。吠陀赞歌中的宇宙论和梵书中的祭祀哲学构成思想主体，同时对宇宙本源、灵魂轮回和解脱的早期追问已经出现。
philosophy.en: Thought was transitioning from ritualism toward philosophical speculation. Vedic hymn cosmology and Brahmana sacrificial philosophy constituted the intellectual mainstream, while early inquiries into cosmic origins, soul transmigration, and liberation were emerging.

writingSystem.zh: 晚期吠陀时代的知识传承主要依靠口头传统，婆罗门通过严格的口传体系保存吠陀经典。文字系统尚未在南亚次大陆广泛使用，虽然可能存在原始的标记和符号。
writingSystem.en: Knowledge transmission in the Late Vedic period relied primarily on oral tradition, with Brahmins preserving Vedic texts through rigorous oral transmission systems. Writing systems were not yet widely used on the Indian subcontinent, though primitive markings and symbols may have existed.

culturalAchievements.zh: 晚期吠陀文化在宗教经典编纂（梨俱吠陀后期赞歌、梵书、阿兰若书）、复杂祭祀仪式体系、音乐和吟诵传统、冶铁技术的引入和推广方面取得重要成就。种姓制度作为社会组织形式已开始固化。
culturalAchievements.en: Late Vedic culture achieved significant milestones in sacred text compilation (later Rigvedic hymns, Brahmanas, Aranyakas), elaborate sacrificial ritual systems, musical and chanting traditions, and the introduction and spread of iron-working technology. The caste system as a social organizational form was already solidifying.

languageFamily.zh: 梵语为宗教和上层交际语言，各地民众使用早期中古印度—雅利安语的地方变体（普拉克里特语前身）。达罗毗荼语系在南部和东部边缘仍有使用。
languageFamily.en: Sanskrit served as the language of religion and elite communication, while local populations spoke regional varieties of early Middle Indo-Aryan (proto-Prakrit). Dravidian languages persisted on the southern and eastern margins.

gdpDescription.zh: 晚期吠陀北印度经济以恒河中上游平原的农牧业为基础。铁器的引入正在扩大可耕地面积，推动从畜牧经济向定居农业的深度转型。牛是最重要的财富标准，谷物（大麦、小麦、水稻）产量不断增长。城市化萌芽和手工业专业化正在出现。
gdpDescription.en: The Late Vedic North Indian economy was based on agriculture and pastoralism on the upper-middle Ganges plain. The introduction of iron was expanding arable land and driving a deepening transition from pastoral to settled farming economies. Cattle remained the most important wealth standard, while grain production (barley, wheat, rice) was growing steadily. Urban germination and craft specialization were emerging.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业（大麦、小麦、水稻）、畜牧业（牛、马、羊）、冶铁、制陶、纺织（棉麻）、木工、珠宝加工和商路贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture (barley, wheat, rice), animal husbandry (cattle, horses, sheep), iron-working, pottery, textiles (cotton and hemp), woodworking, jewelry making, and overland trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、铁器、棉布、宝石、象牙、檀香木、香料、盐和金属。
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, iron goods, cotton cloth, gems, ivory, sandalwood, spices, salt, and metals.

householdWealth.zh: 吠舍农户财富主要体现在牛群、耕地、谷仓和农具；刹帝利贵族以土地、战车、武器、马匹和黄金为富；婆罗门财富以祭祀报酬（达克希那）和土地捐赠为来源。首陀罗几乎没有独立财产。
householdWealth.en: Vaishya farmer wealth consisted mainly of cattle herds, farmland, granaries, and tools. Kshatriya wealth was held in land, chariots, weapons, horses, and gold. Brahmin wealth came from sacrificial fees (dakshina) and land donations. Shudras held virtually no independent property.

economicSystem.zh: 以牛为中心的混合农牧再分配经济，祭祀活动是主要的财富再分配机制。国王通过大型祭典（如马祭）向婆罗门和武士分赐土地和财富。市场贸易正在兴起但尚非经济核心。
economicSystem.en: A cattle-centered mixed agro-pastoral redistributive economy in which sacrificial ceremonies served as the primary wealth redistribution mechanism. Kings distributed land and wealth to Brahmins and warriors through great sacrifices (such as the ashvamedha). Market trade was emerging but not yet central to economic life.

source.en: Trade Route Tolls and Market Levies

source.zh: 附庸邦国贡纳与战利品
source.en: Tributary State Contributions and War Booty

category.zh: 祭祀典礼与婆罗门供养

treasuryDescription.zh: 各邦国库以牛群、谷仓、黄金（尼什卡）、宝石、武器和马匹构成。大型祭祀仪式既是财富展示也是财富消耗的主要场合。拘萨罗和迦尸作为较大王国储备最为丰厚。
treasuryDescription.en: State treasuries consisted of cattle herds, granaries, gold (nishka), gems, weapons, and horses. Major sacrificial ceremonies were both occasions for wealth display and major expenditure. Kosala and Kashi, as larger kingdoms, held the most substantial reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持祭祀体系、供养军队和积累牛马为优先。大型祭典如马祭涉及巨额支出但也彰显王权。各邦之间财政独立，无统一政策。铁器推广正在提高农业产出和税基。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal priorities centered on sustaining the sacrificial system, maintaining armies, and accumulating cattle and horses. Great ceremonies like the ashvamedha involved enormous expenditure but also demonstrated royal power. Finances were independent across kingdoms with no unified policy. Iron adoption was improving agricultural output and the tax base.

description.zh: 刹帝利贵族与其驭手、弓手组成的战车编队，是战场上的核心打击力量和身份象征。
description.en: Chariot formations of Kshatriya nobles with their drivers and archers, serving as the core striking force and status symbol on the battlefield.

description.zh: 训练有素的战象部队，用于冲锋、攻城和心理震慑，在恒河流域战场上具有独特优势。
description.en: Trained war elephant units used for charging, siege warfare, and psychological intimidation, holding unique advantages on Ganges basin battlefields.

description.zh: 从吠舍和首陀罗中征发的主力步兵，装备铁矛、弓箭、木盾和简易甲胄。
description.en: Main infantry levied from Vaishya and Shudra classes, armed with iron spears, bows, wooden shields, and basic armor.

description.zh: 以刹帝利次子和边境部族勇士为主的骑兵力量，负责侦察、侧翼突击和追击。
description.en: Cavalry composed mainly of younger Kshatriya sons and frontier tribal warriors, responsible for scouting, flank attacks, and pursuit.

description.zh: 来自恒河流域边缘森林的部族弓手和轻步兵，擅长丛林战和伏击。
description.en: Tribal archers and light infantry from the forests fringing the Ganges basin, skilled in jungle warfare and ambush.

commanderInChief.zh: 各邦国王为本邦最高军事统帅，日常军务由塞纳帕提（将军）执掌
commanderInChief.en: Each kingdom's raja served as supreme military commander, with routine military affairs managed by the senapati (general)

technology.zh: 铁器正在进入军事领域，铁制矛头和箭镞逐渐取代青铜。战车仍为主力兵种，战象日益重要。弓箭为远程主武器，盾牌多为木制皮覆。甲胄多为皮甲和织物甲，铁甲尚不普遍。
technology.en: Iron was entering the military sphere, with iron spearheads and arrowheads gradually replacing bronze. Chariots remained the principal arm, while war elephants were growing in importance. Bows were the main ranged weapon, shields mostly wooden with leather covering. Armor was typically leather or textile, with iron armor not yet widespread.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自各邦之间的领土争夺和霸权竞争、西北方向游牧部族的侵扰、南方和东方未完全整合的部落地区的反抗，以及内部种姓矛盾和王位争夺可能引发的动荡。
threats.en: Primary threats included territorial disputes and hegemonic competition among kingdoms, raids from northwestern nomadic tribes, resistance from not fully integrated tribal areas to the south and east, and internal instability from caste tensions and succession struggles.

populationDescription.zh: 晚期吠陀北印度诸邦总人口约五百万，集中于恒河上中游平原和主要支流盆地。铁器的推广正在加速森林开垦和人口增长。城市化初期萌芽，少数地点开始形成早期城镇。
populationDescription.en: The total population of the Late Vedic North Indian states was roughly five million, concentrated on the upper-middle Ganges plain and major tributary basins. Iron adoption was accelerating forest clearance and population growth. Urbanization was in its earliest stages, with a few locations beginning to form proto-towns.

socialClasses.zh: 四种姓制度日益固化：婆罗门享有最高宗教地位和法律特权，刹帝利掌控军政权力，吠舍从事农商生产，首陀罗提供服务和劳动。种姓间流动性正在缩小，职业世袭化趋势加强。
socialClasses.en: The four-varna system was increasingly rigid: Brahmins held supreme religious status and legal privileges, Kshatriyas controlled military-political power, Vaishyas engaged in farming and commerce, and Shudras provided service and labor. Inter-caste mobility was shrinking and occupational heredity was strengthening.

allies.zh: 各邦之间通过婚姻联盟和季节性条约维持不稳定的合作关系。拘萨罗与迦尸经常结盟对抗共同威胁。婆罗门学者网络提供跨邦联系渠道。无超越区域的持久联盟。
allies.en: Kingdoms maintained unstable cooperative relationships through marriage alliances and seasonal treaties. Kosala and Kashi often allied against common threats. Brahmin scholarly networks provided cross-kingdom communication channels. No enduring supra-regional alliances existed.

enemies.zh: 各邦间霸权竞争激烈。拘萨罗、迦尸和鸯伽之间的领土争夺频繁。西北方向的甘陀罗和剑浮沙地区的部族群体也构成潜在威胁。
enemies.en: Hegemonic competition among kingdoms was intense. Territorial disputes between Kosala, Kashi, and Anga were frequent. Tribal groups in the Gandhara and Kamboja regions to the northwest also posed potential threats.

foreignPolicy.zh: 各邦外交以争夺恒河流域霸权、确保牲畜和贸易通道安全、通过大型祭祀昭示王权威严为核心。马祭（阿希瓦梅达）是最高的领土和霸权宣示手段。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy of each kingdom centered on competing for Ganges basin hegemony, securing cattle and trade routes, and demonstrating royal authority through grand sacrifices. The ashvamedha (horse sacrifice) was the supreme instrument of territorial and hegemonic assertion.

era.zh: 早期铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁冶炼与铁器制造（犁、斧、武器）正在推广、改良灌溉技术、战象训练与部署、水稻种植扩展至恒河东部、复杂祭祀仪式技术（火坛构筑）、口传知识体系（吠陀经典记忆术）。
keyInnovations.en: Iron smelting and tool-making (plows, axes, weapons) were spreading, improved irrigation technology, war elephant training and deployment, rice cultivation expanding into the eastern Ganges, complex sacrificial ritual technology (fire altar construction), and oral knowledge systems (Vedic memorization techniques).

strengths.zh: 恒河平原的肥沃农业基础、铁器带来的生产力提升、成熟的宗教与社会组织体系（瓦尔纳制度）、强大的战车和象兵传统、以及通过吠陀经典构建的跨地域文化认同。
strengths.en: The fertile agricultural base of the Ganges plain, productivity gains from iron adoption, a mature religious and social organization system (varna), strong chariot and war elephant traditions, and a trans-regional cultural identity constructed through Vedic texts.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂导致各邦相互消耗、种姓制度限制社会流动和人才选拔、婆罗门祭祀体系消耗大量社会资源、口头传统限制行政效率、东部和南部大量森林地区尚未开发。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation caused mutual exhaustion among kingdoms, the caste system restricted social mobility and talent selection, the Brahmanical sacrificial system consumed enormous social resources, oral tradition limited administrative efficiency, and vast forested areas to the east and south remained undeveloped.

outlook.zh: 前800年的北印度正处于深刻变革期。铁器的推广将加速城市化和国家整合，但各邦间的竞争也将加剧。在未来数百年内，这些晚期吠陀邦国将演化为十六大国（摩诃阇那波多）体系，最终孕育出世界性的宗教（佛教、耆那教）和帝国（摩揭陀─孔雀）。
outlook.en: In 800 BCE North India was undergoing profound transformation. Iron adoption would accelerate urbanization and state consolidation, but competition among kingdoms would also intensify. Over the coming centuries these Late Vedic states would evolve into the Mahajanapada system of sixteen great kingdoms, eventually giving birth to world religions (Buddhism, Jainism) and empires (Magadha–Maurya).

description.zh: 恆河流域晚期吠陀諸邦，鐵器時代正在開啟，多個王國正從部落體制向君主國過渡。
description.en: Late Vedic states across the Ganges basin entering the Iron Age, with multiple kingdoms transitioning from tribal to monarchical systems.

ruler.zh: 各部族首领（无统一君主）

capital.zh: 无固定都城（各部有季节性营地中心）
capital.en: No fixed capital (each group had seasonal camp centers)

governmentForm.zh: 以血缘氏族为基础的游牧—半农部族联盟。各部独立运作，首领权力来源于军事威望、牲畜财富和血统。在外部威胁下可能形成临时性大联盟，但联盟松散且短暂。
governmentForm.en: A nomadic-seminomadic tribal confederation based on kinship clans. Each group operated independently, with chiefly power derived from military prestige, livestock wealth, and lineage. Temporary grand alliances might form under external threats, but these were loose and short-lived.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由部族首领与其家族、长老、武士与牧人、工匠（冶金与皮革）和少量河谷农民构成。氏族血缘是基本社会纽带，等级分化不如定居文明鲜明。
socialStructure.en: Society comprised tribal chiefs and their families, elders, warriors and herders, artisans (metalworking and leatherworking), and a small number of valley farmers. Clan kinship was the fundamental social bond, with stratification less pronounced than in settled civilizations.

rulingClass.zh: 各部首领以拥有大量牦牛和马匹、卓越军事能力和尊贵血统为权威基础。长老在部族决策中拥有重要影响力。
