socialStructure.en: Society was rigidly divided into Spartan citizens (Spartiates), peripheral free inhabitants (Perioikoi), and conquered serfs (Helots); military training and communal life defined the citizen class.

rulingClass.zh: 双王家族与长老会议成员构成最高统治层。
rulingClass.en: The dual royal families and Gerousia members formed the supreme ruling stratum.

succession.zh: 双王位分别在阿基亚德和欧里庞提德家族内世袭。
succession.en: The dual thrones were hereditary within the Agiad and Eurypontid families respectively.

structure.zh: 斯巴达独特的双王制、28名长老加两王组成的革鲁西亚、全体公民参与的公民大会（阿佩拉）共同构成政治体系。监察官制度尚未正式确立。
structure.en: Sparta's distinctive dual kingship, the Gerousia of 28 elders plus two kings, and the full-citizen assembly (Apella) together formed the political system. The ephorate was not yet formally established.

function.zh: 管理阿果盖（教育训练体系）和军队编制。
function.en: Managed the agoge (education-training system) and army organization.

function.zh: 协助长老会议的议事与裁判工作。
function.en: Assisted the Gerousia in deliberation and judicial work.

function.zh: 监管黑劳士劳动、贡纳和安全控制。
function.en: Oversaw Helot labor, tribute, and security control.

localAdmin.zh: 拉科尼亚地区由斯巴达直接管辖，庇里阿西人的聚落保有一定自治。
localAdmin.en: Laconia was directly governed by Sparta; Perioikoi settlements retained some autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 以吕库古大律法为基础的习惯法体系，长老会议和公民大会共同裁决。
legalSystem.en: A customary-law system based on the Great Rhetra of Lycurgus, with the Gerousia and Apella jointly rendering decisions.

taxationSystem.zh: 公民的土地由黑劳士耕种并交纳定额贡赋，公民参加公共餐（西西提亚）需缴纳份额。
taxationSystem.en: Citizens' land was tilled by Helots paying fixed tribute; citizens contributed shares to public messes (syssitia).

religion.zh: 多神信仰（奥林匹斯诸神）
religion.en: Polytheism (Olympian gods)

culturalAchievements.zh: 早期斯巴达在音乐、诗歌和陶器方面并不落后。阿果盖军事教育制度正在形成，将塑造希腊世界最强悍的步兵传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Early Sparta was not backward in music, poetry, and pottery. The agoge military education system was taking shape, destined to create the Greek world's most formidable infantry tradition.

languageFamily.zh: 多利安希腊语。
languageFamily.en: Doric Greek.

philosophy.zh: 强调集体纪律、勇武荣誉和简朴生活方式，吕库古式理想正在形成。
philosophy.en: Emphasized collective discipline, martial honor, and austerity; the Lycurgan ideal was taking shape.

writingSystem.zh: 希腊字母。
writingSystem.en: Greek alphabet.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以黑劳士耕种的农业为基础，公民阶层不从事经济生产。对外贸易有限，自给自足为政策导向。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture tilled by Helots; the citizen class did not engage in economic production. Foreign trade was limited; self-sufficiency was the policy orientation.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业（黑劳士耕作）、畜牧、铁器生产、军械制造
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture (Helot labor), herding, iron production, arms manufacture

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、橄榄油、葡萄酒（少量）
tradeGoods.en: Ironware, olive oil, wine (limited)

householdWealth.zh: 斯巴达人名义上平等持有克莱罗斯（份地），由黑劳士耕种。
householdWealth.en: Spartiates nominally held equal kleroi (allotments), tilled by Helots.

economicSystem.zh: 以国家分配土地、黑劳士劳动为基础的集体经济体制，排斥商业。
economicSystem.en: A collectivist economy based on state-allocated land and Helot labor, hostile to commerce.

source.zh: 庇里阿西人贡赋与手工业

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备以谷物和铁制品形式为主，刻意避免贵金属积累。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves were mainly grain and iron goods, with deliberate avoidance of precious-metal accumulation.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持军事训练体系和公共餐制度为核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal priorities centered on maintaining the military training system and public mess institution.

description.zh: 受过阿果盖训练的斯巴达公民重装步兵，希腊世界最精锐的陆战力量。
description.en: Agoge-trained Spartan citizen hoplites, the most elite land force in the Greek world.

description.zh: 由边民组成的辅助步兵，执行侦察和侧翼任务。
description.en: Auxiliary infantry from Perioikoi, performing scouting and flanking duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 双王之一
commanderInChief.en: One of the Dual Kings

technology.zh: 方阵战术正在完善，铜盾、长矛和铁剑为标准装备。训练体系远超同时代其他城邦。
technology.en: Phalanx tactics were being refined; bronze shields, long spears, and iron swords were standard. The training system far surpassed contemporaries.

threats.zh: 黑劳士叛乱是最大内部威胁，美塞尼亚征服尚在进行中。
threats.en: Helot revolt was the greatest internal threat; the conquest of Messenia was still underway.

populationDescription.zh: 约四万，包括约八千斯巴达公民及其家庭、数万庇里阿西人和黑劳士。集中于拉科尼亚欧罗塔斯河谷。
populationDescription.en: About 40,000, including roughly 8,000 Spartiate citizens with families, tens of thousands of Perioikoi, and Helots. Concentrated in the Eurotas Valley of Laconia.

socialClasses.zh: 斯巴达公民（斯巴达人）、庇里阿西人（边民）、黑劳士（农奴）。
socialClasses.en: Spartiates (citizens), Perioikoi (peripheral freemen), Helots (serfs).

allies.zh: 与部分伯罗奔尼撒城邦有初步联盟关系。
allies.en: Had preliminary alliances with some Peloponnesian city-states.

enemies.zh: 美塞尼亚人是首要敌手，阿尔戈斯为潜在对手。
enemies.en: Messenians were the primary adversary; Argos was a potential rival.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以征服美塞尼亚和确保拉科尼亚安全为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on conquering Messenia and securing Laconia.

era.zh: 希腊古风时代早期

keyInnovations.zh: 重装步兵方阵战术、阿果盖军事训练制度、铁制武器。
keyInnovations.en: Hoplite phalanx tactics, agoge military training system, iron weaponry.

strengths.zh: 军事训练体系卓越，公民纪律严明，方阵战力正迅速成为希腊之冠。
strengths.en: Excellent military training, strict citizen discipline, and phalanx fighting power rapidly becoming the finest in Greece.

weaknesses.zh: 公民人口稀少，经济排斥商业，黑劳士制度是长期不稳定因素。
weaknesses.en: Citizen population was sparse, the economy rejected commerce, and the Helot system was a long-term instability factor.

outlook.zh: 军事力量将使其成为伯罗奔尼撒半岛霸主，但社会模式的内在矛盾将限制长期发展。
outlook.en: Military power would make it hegemon of the Peloponnese, but inherent contradictions in its social model would constrain long-term development.

description.zh: 早期斯巴达是希腊伯罗奔尼撒半岛上以双王制和军事训练著称的新兴军事城邦。
description.en: Early Sparta was an emerging military city-state on the Greek Peloponnese, known for its dual kingship and military training system.

governmentForm.zh: 由西卡尼人（西部）、西库利人（东部）和埃利米人（西北）组成的多民族岛屿部族体系。
governmentForm.en: A multi-ethnic island tribal system composed of Sicani (west), Siculi (east), and Elymians (northwest).

socialStructure.zh: 各部族内部分为首领家族、战士、农民和依附者。与腓尼基和希腊殖民者的接触正在加速社会变化。
socialStructure.en: Within each tribe, society was divided into chiefly families, warriors, farmers, and dependents. Contact with Phoenician and Greek colonists was accelerating social change.

rulingClass.zh: 各部族首领家族。
rulingClass.en: Chiefly families of each tribe.

succession.zh: 在首领家族内世袭。
succession.en: Hereditary within chiefly families.

structure.zh: 没有统一的岛屿政权。西卡尼、西库利和埃利米各有独立的部族组织，部分地区开始出现设防城镇。
structure.en: There was no unified island government. Sicani, Siculi, and Elymians each had independent tribal organizations, with fortified towns emerging in some areas.

function.zh: 协调部族防御和对外关系。
function.en: Coordinated tribal defense and external relations.

function.zh: 管理宗教仪式和农业协调。
function.en: Managed religious ceremonies and agricultural coordination.

localAdmin.zh: 各聚落由本地首领自治。
localAdmin.en: Each settlement was self-governed by its local chief.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法，由首领和长老裁决。
legalSystem.en: Customary law, adjudicated by chiefs and elders.

taxationSystem.zh: 以农产品和劳役形式征收贡赋。
taxationSystem.en: Tribute collected in agricultural produce and labor.

religion.zh: 万物有灵与多神信仰混合
religion.en: Animism and polytheism blended

culturalAchievements.zh: 西库利人和西卡尼人发展了独特的彩绘陶器和设防聚落传统。埃利米人的塞杰斯塔与埃里切是重要的文化中心。
culturalAchievements.en: The Siculi and Sicani developed distinctive painted pottery and fortified settlement traditions. The Elymian towns of Segesta and Eryx were important cultural centers.

languageFamily.zh: 西库利语（可能为印欧语）、西卡尼语（可能非印欧语）、埃利米语（争议中）。
languageFamily.en: Siculi (possibly Indo-European), Sicani (possibly non-Indo-European), Elymian (debated).

philosophy.zh: 以自然崇拜和祖先记忆为核心，与殖民者接触带来新的文化元素。
philosophy.en: Centered on nature worship and ancestral memory, with new cultural elements arriving through colonist contact.

writingSystem.zh: 西库利文字（受希腊字母影响）正在发展。
writingSystem.en: Siculi script (influenced by Greek alphabet) was developing.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以肥沃的农业和畜牧为基础。与希腊和腓尼基殖民者的贸易正在带来新技术和财富。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on fertile agriculture and herding. Trade with Greek and Phoenician colonists was bringing new technology and wealth.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、渔业、陶器制造、硫磺与盐
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, herding, fishing, pottery, sulfur, and salt

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、陶器、硫磺、盐、蜂蜜
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, pottery, sulfur, salt, honey

householdWealth.zh: 取决于田地和牲畜。与殖民者有贸易的沿海聚落较为富裕。
householdWealth.en: Depended on fields and livestock. Coastal settlements trading with colonists were wealthier.

economicSystem.zh: 部族自给经济正在向接触贸易经济过渡。
economicSystem.en: A tribal subsistence economy transitioning toward a contact-trade economy.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以谷物、牲畜和少量金属储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as grain, livestock, and a small amount of metal.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持防御和祭祀为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Defense and ritual maintenance took priority.

description.zh: 各部族首领的常备武装。
description.en: Standing fighters of each tribal chief.

description.zh: 战时征召的壮年男子。
description.en: Able-bodied men called up in wartime.

commanderInChief.zh: 联合首领
commanderInChief.en: Joint Chief

technology.zh: 铁器正在普及，以矛盾为主要装备。部分地区开始模仿希腊重装步兵战术。
technology.en: Iron was spreading; spear and shield were the main equipment. Some areas were beginning to imitate Greek hoplite tactics.

threats.zh: 希腊殖民城邦的扩张是最大威胁，腓尼基-迦太基势力也在西部施压。
threats.en: Greek colonial expansion was the greatest threat; Phoenician-Carthaginian forces also pressed from the west.

populationDescription.zh: 约二十万，分布于全岛内陆和沿海。东部的西库利人最多，西部为西卡尼人，西北角为埃利米人。
populationDescription.en: About 200,000, spread across the island's interior and coast. Siculi were most numerous in the east, Sicani in the west, and Elymians in the northwest.

socialClasses.zh: 部族首领与战士、祭司、自由农民、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Tribal chiefs and warriors, priests, free farmers, dependents.

allies.zh: 埃利米人倾向于与迦太基结盟，部分西库利人与希腊殖民者有合作。
allies.en: Elymians tended to ally with Carthage; some Siculi cooperated with Greek colonists.

enemies.zh: 希腊殖民城邦（叙拉古等）的扩张是共同威胁。
enemies.en: Expansion of Greek colonial cities (Syracuse etc.) was a shared threat.

foreignPolicy.zh: 各族策略不同：部分抵抗、部分合作、部分在希腊与迦太基之间保持平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: Strategies varied: some resisted, some cooperated, and some balanced between Greek and Carthaginian powers.

era.zh: 中地中海铁器时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器采用、设防城镇建设、与殖民文化的技术交流。
keyInnovations.en: Iron adoption, fortified town construction, and technological exchange with colonial cultures.

strengths.zh: 肥沃的农业基础，多民族的文化多样性，战略位置重要。
strengths.en: Fertile agricultural base, multicultural diversity, and strategic location.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政治组织，面对有组织的殖民势力处于劣势。
weaknesses.en: Lacking unified political organization, at a disadvantage against organized colonial powers.

outlook.zh: 原住民文化将逐渐被希腊化和腓尼基化进程所改变，但在内陆地区仍将长期保持影响。
outlook.en: Indigenous culture would gradually be transformed by Hellenization and Phoenicianization, though it would maintain influence in interior areas for a long time.

description.zh: 西西里诸族是地中海中心岛屿上西卡尼、西库利和埃利米三大原住民群体的统称。
description.en: The Sicilian Peoples was a collective term for the three main indigenous groups—Sicani, Siculi, and Elymians—on the central Mediterranean island.

dynasty.zh: 拉皮塔诸族群首领传统

governmentForm.zh: 以亲属关系和航海首领为核心的分散岛屿社会，分布于俾斯麦群岛至萨摩亚-汤加地区。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed island society organized around kinship and seafaring chiefs, spread from the Bismarck Archipelago to Samoa-Tonga.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以血缘氏族和岛屿聚落为单位，航海技术精湛的首领和仪式专家享有最高地位。陶器制作、远洋航行能力和贸易网络决定社会声望。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized by kin clans and island settlements, with skilled seafaring chiefs and ritual specialists holding the highest status. Pottery-making, oceanic voyaging ability, and trade networks determined social prestige.

rulingClass.zh: 航海首领和仪式长老。
rulingClass.en: Seafaring chiefs and ritual elders.

succession.zh: 在首领家系内传承，兼顾航海和仪式能力。
succession.en: Passed within chiefly lineages, with consideration for seafaring and ritual ability.

structure.zh: 没有统一国家，各岛屿聚落独立运行，通过远洋航行和贸易网络保持联系。大型仪式集会时形成临时政治协作。
structure.en: No unified state; each island settlement operated independently, connected through oceanic voyaging and trade networks. Temporary political cooperation formed during large ceremonial gatherings.

function.zh: 处理聚落事务、资源分配和航行规划。
function.en: Handled settlement affairs, resource allocation, and voyage planning.

function.zh: 维护航海知识、星象导航传统和陶器仪式。
function.en: Maintained seafaring knowledge, stellar navigation traditions, and pottery rituals.

localAdmin.zh: 各岛屿由地方首领和长老自治。
localAdmin.en: Each island was self-governed by local chiefs and elders.

legalSystem.zh: 口传习惯法与长老裁决。
legalSystem.en: Oral customary law and elder adjudication.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，以互惠交换和仪式供奉为基础。
taxationSystem.en: No formal taxation; based on reciprocal exchange and ritual offerings.

religion.zh: 南岛祖先崇拜与万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Austronesian ancestor worship and animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 拉皮塔陶器以独特的齿印纹饰闻名，是太平洋考古的标志性文化。其远洋航海技术使人类首次殖民遥远太平洋岛屿，从俾斯麦群岛扩展至萨摩亚和汤加。
culturalAchievements.en: Lapita pottery, famous for its distinctive dentate-stamped decoration, is an iconic Pacific archaeological culture. Their oceanic navigation technology enabled the first human colonization of Remote Oceania, expanding from the Bismarck Archipelago to Samoa and Tonga.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系（大洋洲语族）
languageFamily.en: Austronesian (Oceanic branch)

philosophy.zh: 以祖先崇拜、海洋精神和社群互惠为核心的宇宙观
philosophy.en: A cosmology centered on ancestor veneration, oceanic spirit, and communal reciprocity

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，以口传传统和陶器纹饰传递知识
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge transmitted through oral tradition and pottery decoration

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以园艺农业（芋头、山药）、渔业和远洋贸易为核心。黑曜石贸易网络横跨数千公里海域，拉皮塔陶器是重要交换物品。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on horticultural agriculture (taro, yam), fishing, and long-distance maritime trade. Obsidian trade networks spanned thousands of kilometers of ocean, with Lapita pottery as an important exchange item.

mainIndustries.zh: 芋头和山药种植、渔业、陶器制造、黑曜石开采与贸易、独木舟建造
mainIndustries.en: Taro and yam cultivation, fishing, pottery making, obsidian mining and trade, canoe building

tradeGoods.zh: 拉皮塔陶器、黑曜石、贝壳饰品、鱼干、芋头
tradeGoods.en: Lapita pottery, obsidian, shell ornaments, dried fish, taro

householdWealth.zh: 家户财富体现为独木舟、陶器、黑曜石工具、园艺地和贝壳饰品。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed in canoes, pottery, obsidian tools, garden plots, and shell ornaments.

economicSystem.zh: 以互惠交换、仪式分配和远洋贸易为核心的岛屿经济。
economicSystem.en: An island economy centered on reciprocal exchange, ceremonial distribution, and long-distance maritime trade.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以黑曜石、陶器和贝壳饰品形式储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as obsidian, pottery, and shell ornaments.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持航海能力和仪式网络为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Navigation capability and ritual networks took priority.

description.zh: 各岛屿聚落的常备武装青年。
description.en: Standing armed young men of each island settlement.

description.zh: 冲突时征召的壮年男子。
description.en: Able-bodied men called up during conflict.

commanderInChief.zh: 联合首领
commanderInChief.en: Joint Chief

technology.zh: 以石制和骨制武器为主（矛、棍棒、投石索），善于利用独木舟进行海上袭击和快速转移。
technology.en: Mainly stone and bone weapons (spears, clubs, slings), skilled at using canoes for maritime raids and rapid movement.

threats.zh: 岛屿间的部族冲突和与美拉尼西亚原住民的竞争。
threats.en: Inter-island tribal conflicts and competition with indigenous Melanesian peoples.

populationDescription.zh: 约五万人口分布于俾斯麦群岛至萨摩亚-汤加的广大岛屿地区。人口密度极低，但通过远洋航行保持联系。
populationDescription.en: About 50,000 people spread across the vast island region from the Bismarck Archipelago to Samoa-Tonga. Population density was extremely low, but connectivity was maintained through oceanic voyaging.

socialClasses.zh: 航海首领、仪式长老、园艺农民、渔民。
socialClasses.en: Seafaring chiefs, ritual elders, horticultural farmers, fishermen.

allies.zh: 通过远洋贸易网络与各岛屿聚落保持联系。
allies.en: Maintained connections with island settlements through long-distance maritime trade networks.

enemies.zh: 与部分美拉尼西亚原住民群体存在竞争。
enemies.en: Competition existed with some indigenous Melanesian groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以拓展新岛屿、建立贸易网络和维持远洋航路为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on expanding to new islands, establishing trade networks, and maintaining oceanic routes.

era.zh: 太平洋新石器时代晚期

keyInnovations.zh: 远洋航海独木舟（双体舟）、星象导航、齿印纹陶器制造、黑曜石加工、园艺农业。
keyInnovations.en: Oceanic voyaging canoes (outrigger/double-hulled), stellar navigation, dentate-stamped pottery making, obsidian working, and horticultural agriculture.

strengths.zh: 卓越的远洋航海能力，广泛的贸易网络，独特的陶器文化，适应多种岛屿环境的园艺技术。
strengths.en: Outstanding oceanic navigation, extensive trade networks, distinctive pottery culture, and horticultural techniques adapted to diverse island environments.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极度分散，缺乏统一政治组织，容易受到自然灾害（台风、火山）的影响。
weaknesses.en: Extremely dispersed population, lacking unified political organization, and vulnerable to natural disasters (typhoons, volcanoes).

outlook.zh: 拉皮塔文化将逐渐转化为早期波利尼西亚文化，其后裔将成为人类历史上最伟大的航海民族之一，殖民整个太平洋。
outlook.en: Lapita culture would gradually transform into early Polynesian culture, with its descendants becoming one of the greatest seafaring peoples in human history, colonizing the entire Pacific.

description.zh: 拉皮塔文化是公元前800年活跃于西太平洋的南岛语系航海社会，以独特的齿印纹陶器和卓越的远洋航海能力闻名，其扩张奠定了波利尼西亚文明的基础。
description.en: The Lapita Culture was an Austronesian seafaring society active in the western Pacific around 800 BCE, famous for its distinctive dentate-stamped pottery and outstanding oceanic navigation, whose expansion laid the foundation for Polynesian civilization.

dynasty.zh: 阿迪纳土丘建造者时期

governmentForm.zh: 以仪式中心为纽带的分散部族社会，由祭仪领袖与氏族首领联合治理。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed tribal society linked by ritual centers, governed jointly by ceremonial leaders and clan chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族为核心，祭仪领袖享有较高威望，普通成员从事农耕和采集，等级分化初显。
socialStructure.en: Society was clan-based with ritual leaders enjoying elevated prestige; common members engaged in farming and foraging; early social stratification was emerging.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 以仪式土丘为核心的松散聚落联合体，无统一政权。
structure.en: A loose confederation of settlements centered on ritual mounds, with no unified polity.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 建造大型土丘与环形土方工程，精美的管形烟斗雕刻、随葬品反映早期等级观念。
culturalAchievements.en: Built large earthen mounds and circular earthworks; produced fine tubular pipes and elaborate grave goods reflecting early social ranking.

languageFamily.zh: 可能属阿尔冈昆语系或苏语系（具体不详）
languageFamily.en: Possibly Algonquian or Siouan family (uncertain)

philosophy.zh: 祖灵崇拜、土丘仪式传统和与自然循环的深层联结。
philosophy.en: Ancestor veneration, mound ritual traditions, and deep connections to natural cycles.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以园圃农业（南瓜、向日葵）、采集和狩猎为主，辅以区域性交换网络。
gdpDescription.en: Economy relied on garden horticulture (squash, sunflower), foraging, and hunting, supplemented by regional exchange networks.

mainIndustries.zh: 园圃农业、狩猎、采集、土丘建造、管烟斗制作
mainIndustries.en: Garden horticulture, hunting, foraging, mound construction, pipe carving

tradeGoods.zh: 管烟斗、云母片、铜珠、贝壳、燧石
tradeGoods.en: Tubular pipes, mica sheets, copper beads, shells, flint

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现在园圃、储粮、工具与少量铜饰品上。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was embodied in gardens, grain stores, tools, and small copper ornaments.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"主要为首领控制的食物与工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 邻近部族的领地竞争、季节性食物短缺。
threats.en: Territorial competition with neighboring groups, seasonal food shortages.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于俄亥俄谷地多处聚落，以小型村庄和季节性营地为主。
populationDescription.en: Population was dispersed across Ohio Valley settlements in small villages and seasonal camps.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 土丘建造技术、铜冷锻、管烟斗雕刻、早期园圃农业。
keyInnovations.en: Mound construction, cold-hammered copper, pipe carving, early garden horticulture.

strengths.zh: 仪式传统强大，区域交换网络广泛，资源利用多样。
strengths.en: Strong ritual traditions, extensive regional exchange networks, diverse resource exploitation.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少，政治组织松散，无金属冶炼技术。
weaknesses.en: Sparse population, loose political organization, no metal smelting.

outlook.zh: 土丘建造传统将延续数百年，最终演化为霍普韦尔文化。
outlook.en: The mound-building tradition would continue for centuries, eventually evolving into the Hopewell culture.

description.zh: 公元前800年左右的阿迪纳文化是俄亥俄谷地的早期土丘建造者，以仪式土方工程和远距离交换闻名。
description.en: Around 800 BCE, the Adena Culture were early mound builders of the Ohio Valley, known for ritual earthworks and long-distance exchange.

governmentForm.zh: 以游猎营地为基础的分散部族社会，由经验丰富的猎人领袖与家族长者治理。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed tribal society based on mobile hunting camps, governed by experienced hunt leaders and family elders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度平等，以扩展家庭为基本单位，领导权依赖狩猎技能和分配公正性。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly egalitarian, organized around extended families, with leadership based on hunting skill and fair distribution.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 精通北方森林生存技能，独木舟与雪鞋制作工艺精湛，口述传统丰富。
culturalAchievements.en: Mastered boreal forest survival skills, excelled in canoe and snowshoe crafting, with rich oral traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 阿萨巴斯卡语系与阿尔冈昆语系
languageFamily.en: Athabaskan and Algonquian language families

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 以驯鹿狩猎、捕鱼、毛皮采集和季节性采集为经济基础。
gdpDescription.en: Economy based on caribou hunting, fishing, fur collecting, and seasonal foraging.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、捕鱼、毛皮加工、独木舟制造、雪鞋制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, fur processing, canoe building, snowshoe making

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、肉干、桦皮、骨角工具
tradeGoods.en: Furs, dried meat, birch bark, bone and antler tools

householdWealth.zh: 财富体现在独木舟、雪鞋、猎具、毛皮储备和家族互助网络上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was embodied in canoes, snowshoes, hunting gear, fur stores, and family support networks.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 严冬饥荒、猎物迁徙路线变化、与邻近群体的领地摩擦。
threats.en: Winter famine, shifts in caribou migration routes, territorial friction with neighboring groups.

populationDescription.zh: 人口极度分散在北美北部广袤的亚寒带森林中，以小型游猎营地为主。
populationDescription.en: Population was extremely dispersed across the vast subarctic forests of northern North America in small mobile hunting camps.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 雪鞋、桦皮独木舟、陷阱与圈套技术、冬季储存技术。
keyInnovations.en: Snowshoes, birch-bark canoes, trap and snare technology, winter storage techniques.

strengths.zh: 极端环境适应能力强，机动性高，毛皮资源丰富。
strengths.en: Excellent adaptation to extreme environments, high mobility, abundant fur resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口密度极低，政治组织松散，技术水平有限。
weaknesses.en: Extremely low population density, loose political organization, limited technology.

outlook.zh: 游猎生活方式将持续数千年，直到后来的毛皮贸易时代。
outlook.en: The mobile hunting lifestyle would persist for millennia until the later fur trade era.

description.zh: 亚北极森林猎人是北美北部寒带森林的游猎部族，以驯鹿狩猎和独木舟出行为生。
description.en: The Subarctic Forest Hunters were mobile hunting peoples of the boreal forests of northern North America, living by caribou hunting and canoe travel.

name.zh: 北美太平洋沿岸采集者
北美太平洋沿岸采集者

governmentForm.zh: 以村社和世袭首领为核心的复合采集社会，社会等级分明。
governmentForm.en: Complex foraging societies centered on villages and hereditary leaders, with marked social hierarchy.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为贵族、自由民和奴隶，等级由家族血统和财富决定，"夸富宴"强化威望分配。
socialStructure.en: Society was divided into nobles, commoners, and slaves, with rank determined by lineage and wealth; "potlatch" feasts reinforced prestige distribution.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 精美的图腾柱雕刻、独木舟工艺、夸富宴仪式和口述文学传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Fine totem pole carving, canoe craftsmanship, potlatch ceremonies, and rich oral literary traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 萨利希语系、瓦卡什语系、钦努克语系等
languageFamily.en: Salishan, Wakashan, Chinookan, and other families

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以丰富的鲑鱼捕捞、海洋资源和森林采集为基础，是北美最富裕的非农业社会。
gdpDescription.en: Economy based on abundant salmon fishing, marine resources, and forest foraging — among the wealthiest non-agricultural societies in North America.

mainIndustries.zh: 鲑鱼捕捞、海产采集、木材加工、独木舟制造、编织
mainIndustries.en: Salmon fishing, seafood gathering, woodworking, canoe building, weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 鲑鱼干、鲸油、贝壳、雪松制品、毛皮
tradeGoods.en: Dried salmon, whale oil, shells, cedar products, furs

householdWealth.zh: 财富集中体现在大型独木舟、铜器、毯子储备和仪式用品上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was concentrated in large canoes, coppers, blanket stores, and ceremonial goods.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

threats.zh: 邻近群体的掠夺、季节性食物波动、部族间战争。
threats.en: Raiding by neighboring groups, seasonal food fluctuations, intertribal warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 人口沿太平洋西北海岸河口和岛屿定居，以大型木屋村社为主。
populationDescription.en: Population settled along Pacific Northwest estuaries and islands in large plank-house villages.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 高级木工技术、鲑鱼烟熏保存、大型独木舟建造、编织工艺。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced woodworking, salmon smoking and preservation, large canoe construction, weaving techniques.

strengths.zh: 资源极为丰富，文化复杂度高，社会组织成熟。
strengths.en: Extremely abundant resources, high cultural complexity, mature social organization.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散，各村社独立，缺乏统一军事力量。
weaknesses.en: Politically fragmented, villages independent, lacking unified military force.

outlook.zh: 太平洋沿岸文化将持续繁荣至欧洲接触时期。
outlook.en: Pacific coast cultures would continue to flourish until European contact.

description.zh: 北美太平洋沿岸采集者是以鲑鱼捕捞为核心的复合社会，拥有丰富的物质文化和明确的社会等级。
description.en: The North American Pacific Foragers were complex societies centered on salmon fishing, with rich material culture and clear social hierarchy.

governmentForm.zh: 以季节性渔猎营地和半永久村社为基础的部族社会。
governmentForm.en: A tribal society based on seasonal fishing-hunting camps and semi-permanent villages.

socialStructure.zh: 社会相对平等，首领地位依赖捕鱼技能和馈赠能力。
socialStructure.en: Society was relatively egalitarian, with leadership based on fishing skill and generosity.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 精通河鱼捕捞技术、编篮工艺和岩画艺术。
culturalAchievements.en: Skilled in river fishing techniques, basket weaving, and rock art.

languageFamily.zh: 萨利希语系内陆支、萨哈普廷语系
languageFamily.en: Interior Salishan, Sahaptian

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以鲑鱼捕捞、根茎采集、狩猎和季节性贸易集会为基础。
gdpDescription.en: Economy based on salmon fishing, root gathering, hunting, and seasonal trade gatherings.

mainIndustries.zh: 鲑鱼捕捞、根茎采集、狩猎、编篮
mainIndustries.en: Salmon fishing, root gathering, hunting, basket weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 鲑鱼干、卡马斯根、毛皮、黑曜石
tradeGoods.en: Dried salmon, camas root, furs, obsidian

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现在鱼干储备、马匹（后期）、编篮和工具上。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was embodied in dried fish stores, horses (later), baskets, and tools.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"主要为首领控制的食物与工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 干旱影响鲑鱼洄游、邻近群体竞争。
threats.en: Droughts affecting salmon runs, competition with neighboring groups.

populationDescription.zh: 人口沿哥伦比亚高原河谷分布，兼有定居村社和季节性营地。
populationDescription.en: Population distributed along Columbia Plateau river valleys in both settled villages and seasonal camps.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 鲑鱼晾晒保存、编篮、半地穴住所。
keyInnovations.en: Salmon drying and preservation, basket weaving, semi-subterranean pit houses.

strengths.zh: 鲑鱼资源丰富，贸易网络活跃。
strengths.en: Abundant salmon resources, active trade networks.

weaknesses.zh: 人口分散，军事力量薄弱。
weaknesses.en: Dispersed population, weak military force.

outlook.zh: 高原渔猎生活方式将延续至马匹传入后发生变化。
outlook.en: The plateau fishing-hunting lifestyle would continue until transformed by the introduction of horses.

description.zh: 高原渔猎民是哥伦比亚高原地区依赖鲑鱼捕捞和根茎采集的部族群体。
description.en: The Plateau Fishers and Hunters were tribal peoples of the Columbia Plateau region dependent on salmon fishing and root gathering.

governmentForm.zh: 以游猎小群体为核心的高度分散部族社会。
governmentForm.en: A highly dispersed tribal society organized in small mobile hunting bands.

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度平等，以小型游猎群体为单位，首领地位不固定。
socialStructure.en: Highly egalitarian society organized in small hunting bands with fluid leadership.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 适应南美最南端的严苛环境，精通长距离追猎和皮革加工。
culturalAchievements.en: Adapted to the harsh environment of southernmost South America, skilled in long-distance pursuit hunting and leather working.

languageFamily.zh: 乔恩语系等
languageFamily.en: Chonan and related language families

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以猎取原驼和美洲鸵为主，辅以沿海采集。
gdpDescription.en: Economy based on hunting guanaco and rhea, supplemented by coastal gathering.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、皮革加工、石器制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, leather working, stone tool making

tradeGoods.zh: 皮革、鸵鸟羽毛、石器
tradeGoods.en: Leather, rhea feathers, stone tools

householdWealth.zh: 财富体现在皮革帐篷、猎具和毛皮储备上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was embodied in leather tents, hunting gear, and fur stores.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 严冬、猎物匮乏、与邻近群体的偶发冲突。
threats.en: Harsh winters, scarcity of game, occasional conflicts with neighboring groups.

populationDescription.zh: 人口极度分散在广袤的巴塔哥尼亚草原上。
populationDescription.en: Population was extremely dispersed across the vast Patagonian steppe.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 流星锤（"boleadoras"）狩猎工具、皮革帐篷、石器加工。
keyInnovations.en: Boleadoras hunting weapons, leather tents, stone tool manufacture.

strengths.zh: 极端环境适应力强，机动性高。
strengths.en: Excellent adaptation to extreme environments, high mobility.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极少，技术简单，无农业。
weaknesses.en: Very small population, simple technology, no agriculture.

outlook.zh: 游猎生活方式将延续至西班牙人到来。
outlook.en: The hunting lifestyle would persist until Spanish arrival.

description.zh: 巴塔哥尼亚猎人是南美最南端草原上的游猎部族，以追猎原驼为生。
description.en: The Patagonian Hunters were mobile hunting peoples of the southernmost South American steppe, living by guanaco pursuit.

governmentForm.zh: 以海岸贝丘聚落为核心的小型部族社会。
governmentForm.en: Small tribal societies centered on coastal shell midden settlements.

socialStructure.zh: 社会平等，以家庭为单位沿海岸季节性迁移和定居。
socialStructure.en: Egalitarian society organized in family units, with seasonal coastal migration and settlement.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 建造大型贝丘（sambaqui），展现了复杂的仪式葬俗和环境适应能力。
culturalAchievements.en: Built large shell middens (sambaquis), showing complex mortuary rituals and environmental adaptation.

languageFamily.zh: 未知（可能属杰语系或图皮语系前身）
languageFamily.en: Unknown (possibly proto-Jê or proto-Tupian)

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

gdpDescription.en: Economy based on shellfish gathering, nearshore fishing, and limited hunting-gathering.

mainIndustries.zh: 贝类采集、捕鱼、狩猎
mainIndustries.en: Shellfish gathering, fishing, hunting

tradeGoods.zh: 贝壳、鱼干、石器
tradeGoods.en: Shells, dried fish, stone tools

householdWealth.zh: 财富体现在渔具、储存食物和贝饰上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was embodied in fishing gear, stored food, and shell ornaments.

threats.zh: 风暴、海平面变化、与内陆群体的资源竞争。
threats.en: Storms, sea-level changes, resource competition with inland groups.

populationDescription.zh: 人口沿南美南部海岸线的贝丘遗址分布。
populationDescription.en: Population distributed along shell midden sites on the southern South American coastline.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 贝丘建造、近海捕鱼技术、贝壳加工。
keyInnovations.en: Shell midden construction, nearshore fishing techniques, shell processing.

strengths.zh: 海岸资源稳定，仪式传统有凝聚力。
strengths.en: Stable coastal resources, ritually cohesive traditions.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少，技术简单，无农业。
weaknesses.en: Small population, simple technology, no agriculture.

outlook.zh: 贝丘文化将逐渐融入或被后来的农业民族取代。
outlook.en: Shell midden cultures would gradually merge with or be replaced by later agricultural peoples.

description.zh: 南美贝丘采集者是沿巴西和智利海岸生活的贝类采集部族，建造了著名的贝丘遗址。
description.en: The South American Shellfish Gatherers were shellfish-collecting peoples living along the Brazilian and Chilean coasts, who built the famous shell midden (sambaqui) sites.

governmentForm.zh: 以海洋狩猎营地为核心的高度分散部族社会。
governmentForm.en: A highly dispersed tribal society based on marine hunting camps.

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度平等，以扩展家庭为单位，猎人地位由技能和分享意愿决定。
socialStructure.en: Highly egalitarian society organized in extended families, with hunter status determined by skill and willingness to share.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出精密的海洋狩猎技术、冰雪建筑（包括雪屋）和适应极寒的服饰工艺。
culturalAchievements.en: Developed sophisticated marine hunting technology, ice and snow architecture (including igloos), and cold-adapted clothing crafts.

languageFamily.zh: 古爱斯基摩-阿留申语系
languageFamily.en: Proto-Eskimo-Aleut language family

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

gdpDescription.en: Economy centered on seal, walrus, and whale hunting, supplemented by caribou hunting and fishing.

mainIndustries.zh: 海洋狩猎、驯鹿猎、捕鱼、皮革加工、骨角雕刻
mainIndustries.en: Marine hunting, caribou hunting, fishing, leather working, bone and ivory carving

tradeGoods.zh: 海象牙、海豹油、毛皮、骨角工具
tradeGoods.en: Walrus ivory, seal oil, furs, bone and antler tools

householdWealth.zh: 财富体现在皮划艇、猎具、毛皮衣物和海兽油脂储备上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was embodied in kayaks, hunting gear, fur clothing, and marine mammal oil stores.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 极端严寒、猎物迁徙变化、食物储备不足。
threats.en: Extreme cold, changes in prey migration, insufficient food stores.

populationDescription.zh: 人口沿北极海岸线分散分布于季节性营地。
populationDescription.en: Population dispersed along the Arctic coastline in seasonal camps.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 皮划艇与大型皮船、鱼叉技术、雪屋建造、防寒服饰制作。
keyInnovations.en: Kayaks and umiaks, harpoon technology, igloo construction, cold-weather clothing manufacture.

strengths.zh: 极端环境适应力卓越，海洋资源利用能力强。
strengths.en: Outstanding adaptation to extreme environments, strong marine resource exploitation.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极少，完全依赖动物蛋白，无农业基础。
weaknesses.en: Extremely small population, total dependence on animal protein, no agricultural base.

outlook.zh: 北极狩猎文化将延续数千年，适应不同气候波动。
outlook.en: Arctic hunting cultures would continue for millennia, adapting to various climatic fluctuations.

description.zh: 北极海洋猎人是北极沿海的专业海兽猎人，以精湛的海洋狩猎技术和极寒适应能力闻名。
description.en: The Arctic Marine Hunters were specialized sea mammal hunters of the Arctic coast, renowned for their marine hunting technology and cold adaptation.

governmentForm.zh: 以村社为基础的分散部族社会，由村长和家族长老联合治理。
governmentForm.en: Dispersed tribal societies based on villages, governed jointly by village headmen and family elders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村社和亲族集团为核心，村长通过馈赠和仪式维持威望，等级分化有限。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on villages and kin groups; headmen maintained prestige through gifts and ritual, with limited stratification.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展了木薯加工技术、陶器制作、羽饰艺术和丰富的口述神话体系。
culturalAchievements.en: Developed manioc processing technology, pottery making, featherwork art, and rich oral mythological traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 图皮语系、阿拉瓦克语系、加勒比语系等
languageFamily.en: Tupian, Arawakan, Cariban, and other families

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 以木薯种植为核心的热带园圃农业，辅以渔业、狩猎和森林采集。
gdpDescription.en: Tropical garden agriculture centered on manioc cultivation, supplemented by fishing, hunting, and forest gathering.

mainIndustries.zh: 木薯种植、渔业、狩猎、采集、陶器制作
mainIndustries.en: Manioc cultivation, fishing, hunting, gathering, pottery making

tradeGoods.zh: 木薯饼、陶器、羽饰、鱼干、树脂
tradeGoods.en: Manioc cakes, pottery, featherwork, dried fish, tree resins

householdWealth.zh: 财富体现在园圃、独木舟、陶器和仪式用品上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was embodied in gardens, canoes, pottery, and ritual goods.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"主要为首领控制的食物与工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 邻近部族的突袭、洪水、疫病。
threats.en: Raids from neighboring tribes, flooding, disease.

populationDescription.zh: 人口沿亚马逊河及支流分布，以河岸大型村社为主。
populationDescription.en: Population distributed along the Amazon and its tributaries, concentrated in large riverine villages.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 木薯毒素去除技术、热带园圃农业、独木舟建造、陶器烧制。
keyInnovations.en: Manioc detoxification, tropical garden agriculture, canoe building, pottery firing.

strengths.zh: 食物产出稳定，河流交通网络广泛，人口较多。
strengths.en: Stable food production, extensive river transport networks, relatively large population.

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一组织，无金属技术。
weaknesses.en: Highly fragmented politically, lacking unified organization, no metal technology.

outlook.zh: 亚马逊农业社会将持续扩展，在部分地区可能出现较大政体。
outlook.en: Amazonian agricultural societies would continue expanding, with larger polities potentially emerging in some areas.

description.zh: 亚马逊木薯农民是亚马逊盆地的热带园圃农业部族，以木薯种植和河流渔业为生计核心。
description.en: The Amazonian Manioc Farmers were tropical garden agricultural peoples of the Amazon basin, with manioc cultivation and river fishing at the core of their livelihood.

governmentForm.zh: 以村社和区域仪式中心为核心的农业部族社会，奥尔梅克核心区之外的广泛农村人口。
governmentForm.en: Agricultural tribal societies organized around villages and regional ritual centers, the broad rural population beyond the Olmec core area.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分化初显，仪式长老和成功农民享有较高地位，多数人从事玉米农耕。
socialStructure.en: Early social differentiation was emerging, with ritual elders and successful farmers enjoying higher status; most people engaged in maize farming.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 完善了玉米-豆类-南瓜"三姐妹"种植体系，发展了早期陶器和石碾技术。
culturalAchievements.en: Perfected the maize-bean-squash "Three Sisters" cultivation system, developed early pottery and stone grinding technology.

languageFamily.zh: 奥托-曼格语系、马雅语系、米赫-索克语系等
languageFamily.en: Oto-Manguean, Mayan, Mixe-Zoquean, and other families

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以玉米为核心的集约农业为基础，辅以可可、棉花和区域交换。
gdpDescription.en: Economy based on intensive maize-centered agriculture, supplemented by cacao, cotton, and regional exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米种植、陶器制作、棉纺织、石器加工、区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Maize cultivation, pottery making, cotton weaving, stone tool working, regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、可可、棉布、陶器、黑曜石
tradeGoods.en: Maize, cacao, cotton cloth, pottery, obsidian

householdWealth.zh: 财富体现在耕地、玉米储备、陶器和棉布上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was embodied in farmland, maize stores, pottery, and cotton cloth.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 干旱、洪水、邻近政体的扩张压力。
threats.en: Drought, flooding, expansion pressure from neighboring polities.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于中美洲和墨西哥南部的山谷和沿海平原，以村社聚落为主。
populationDescription.en: Population distributed across valleys and coastal plains of Central America and southern Mexico, primarily in village settlements.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 玉米品种改良、灌溉初步技术、黑曜石加工、早期历法知识。
keyInnovations.en: Maize variety improvement, early irrigation techniques, obsidian working, early calendrical knowledge.

strengths.zh: 农业产出高，人口较多，文化传统丰厚。
strengths.en: High agricultural output, relatively large population, rich cultural traditions.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散，易受气候波动影响。
weaknesses.en: Politically fragmented, vulnerable to climate fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 这些农业社区将为后来的玛雅和萨波特克等文明提供人口和文化基础。
outlook.en: These farming communities would provide the demographic and cultural foundation for later Maya and Zapotec civilizations.

description.zh: 中美洲玉米农民是奥尔梅克核心区以外的广泛农业人口，以玉米种植为生计核心。
description.en: The Mesoamerican Maize Farmers were the broad agricultural population beyond the Olmec heartland, with maize cultivation at the core of their livelihood.

governmentForm.zh: 以仪式中心为纽带的沿海聚落联合体，由祭仪精英治理。
governmentForm.en: A coastal settlement network linked by ritual centers, governed by ritual elites.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为仪式精英、工匠和普通农渔民，以精美纺织品随葬反映等级。
socialStructure.en: Society was divided into ritual elites, artisans, and common farmer-fishers, with fine textile burials reflecting hierarchy.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 以精美彩色纺织品、头骨环锯术和仪式葬俗闻名。
culturalAchievements.en: Renowned for exquisite polychrome textiles, cranial trepanation, and elaborate mortuary rituals.

languageFamily.zh: 可能属早期克丘亚语系前身
languageFamily.en: Possibly a proto-Quechuan language

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

mainIndustries.en: Fishing, irrigated agriculture, weaving, pottery

householdWealth.zh: 财富体现在纺织品、渔获储备和仪式用品上。

description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.
