technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 干旱、沿海厄尔尼诺灾害、邻近群体竞争。
threats.en: Drought, coastal El Niño events, competition with neighboring groups.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中在秘鲁南部海岸的伊卡河谷和帕拉卡斯半岛。
populationDescription.en: Population concentrated in the Ica Valley and Paracas Peninsula of southern coastal Peru.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 高级纺织技术、颅骨环锯术、沿海灌溉系统。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced textile techniques, cranial trepanation, coastal irrigation systems.

strengths.zh: 纺织工艺卓越，海陆资源兼备。
strengths.en: Outstanding textile craftsmanship, combined marine and agricultural resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口有限，依赖脆弱的沿海环境。
weaknesses.en: Limited population, dependent on fragile coastal environment.

outlook.zh: 帕拉卡斯文化将演化为后来的纳斯卡文明。
outlook.en: Paracas culture would evolve into the later Nazca civilization.

description.zh: 早期帕拉卡斯文化是秘鲁南部海岸的沿海文明，以精美纺织品和仪式葬俗闻名。
description.en: The Early Paracas Culture was a coastal civilization of southern Peru renowned for exquisite textiles and elaborate mortuary rituals.

capital.zh: 无统一都城（罗马仅为帕拉提诺山上的小聚落）
capital.en: No unified capital (Rome was merely a small Palatine Hill settlement)

governmentForm.zh: 以山丘聚落为核心的小型城邦与部族社会并存，各聚落由王或首领与长老会议共同治理，拉丁姆地区存在松散的宗教联盟。
governmentForm.en: Small city-states and tribal societies coexisted around hilltop settlements, each governed by a rex or chief alongside an elder council, with a loose religious league linking communities across Latium.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由世袭贵族家族（patricii的雏形）、自由农牧民、工匠和附属人口构成。氏族（gens）是基本社会单位，土地由氏族集体控制。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of hereditary noble families (proto-patricians), free agro-pastoral households, artisans, and dependent populations. The gens (clan) was the basic social unit, with land under collective clan control.

rulingClass.zh: 各聚落的贵族氏族首领、祭司和长老会成员
rulingClass.en: Noble clan heads, priests, and elder council members of each settlement

succession.zh: 王位或首领之位在贵族家族中传承，部分聚落可能采用长老推选制。
succession.en: Kingship or chieftainship was transmitted within noble families, with some settlements possibly using elder election.

structure.zh: 各聚落独立治理，拉丁联盟仅在共同祭祀（如阿尔巴山朱庇特祭典）和对外防御时发挥协调作用。内部政治以王、长老会议和公民集会的萌芽形式为主。
structure.en: Each settlement governed independently, with the Latin League functioning only for coordination during shared rituals (such as the Jupiter Latiaris festival on the Alban Mount) and external defense. Internal politics featured the rex, elder councils, and nascent citizen assemblies.

function.zh: 管理聚落政务、裁决争端、主持祭祀和组织防御。
function.en: Managed settlement affairs, adjudicated disputes, directed rituals, and organized defense.

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、占卜和节庆活动。
function.en: Oversaw religious ceremonies, augury, and festival activities.

function.zh: 负责战时公民征召、武器储备和边境巡防。
function.en: Handled wartime citizen levies, arms storage, and frontier patrol.

localAdmin.zh: 地方以各山丘聚落为自治单位，小型村庄依附于最近的设防聚落。氏族长老负责日常事务。
localAdmin.en: Local administration treated each hilltop settlement as autonomous, with small villages attached to the nearest fortified settlement. Clan elders managed daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法和宗教禁忌为基础，重视氏族间赔偿和长老仲裁。尚未形成成文法典。
legalSystem.en: Based on customary law and religious taboos, emphasizing inter-clan compensation and elder arbitration. No codified legal corpus yet existed.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，各聚落王通过田地收成分成、劳役、祭祀贡献和战利品分配获取资源。
taxationSystem.en: No uniform tax system; each settlement's rex drew resources from shares of field output, labor obligations, ritual contributions, and war-booty distribution.

religion.zh: 原始拉丁宗教，以朱庇特、玛尔斯、奎里努斯等神灵崇拜和农业祭祀为核心
religion.en: Proto-Latin religion centered on worship of Jupiter, Mars, Quirinus, and agricultural ritual cycles

culturalAchievements.zh: 拉丁姆地区正处于铁器时代早期文化形成阶段，维兰诺瓦文化影响下的陶器与金属器生产，阿尔巴朱庇特祭典成为拉丁诸邦共同认同的纽带。
culturalAchievements.en: Latium was in the early Iron Age cultural formation stage, with Villanovan-influenced pottery and metalwork production; the festival of Jupiter Latiaris on the Alban Mount served as a bond of shared Latin identity.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系意大利语族，原始拉丁语与萨宾方言
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Italic branch, proto-Latin and Sabine dialects

philosophy.zh: 以农耕仪式观、自然力崇拜、祖先敬畏和氏族伦理为思想基础。
philosophy.en: Based on agricultural ritual worldview, nature-power worship, ancestral reverence, and clan ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 尚未广泛使用文字，部分精英可能接触希腊字母的早期传入。
writingSystem.en: Writing was not yet widely used, though some elites may have encountered early transmission of the Greek alphabet.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以拉丁姆平原农牧业为基础，铁器逐渐取代青铜工具，台伯河渡口贸易使部分聚落获得额外商业收入。整体仍以自给自足为主。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on the agriculture and pastoralism of the Latin plain, with iron tools gradually replacing bronze; Tiber River ford trade provided some settlements with additional commercial income. The economy was still primarily self-sufficient.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄与葡萄试植、畜牧、铁器与青铜制造、制陶、盐业（台伯河口）
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, early olive and grape planting, animal husbandry, iron and bronze production, pottery, salt production (at the Tiber mouth)

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、盐、陶器、铁器、羊毛与皮革
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, salt, pottery, ironware, wool, and leather

householdWealth.zh: 普通家户财富以田地使用权、粮仓、牲畜和农具为主；贵族氏族财富集中于更大田地、武器、青铜器和仆从。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted of land-use rights, granaries, livestock, and tools; noble clan wealth was concentrated in larger fields, weapons, bronzeware, and retainers.

economicSystem.zh: 以氏族土地控制和农牧自给为基础的早期城邦经济，辅以台伯河渡口的区域贸易和伊特鲁里亚方向的交换。
economicSystem.en: An early city-state economy based on clan land control and agro-pastoral self-sufficiency, supplemented by regional trade at the Tiber ford and exchange toward Etruria.

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"以各聚落王控制的粮仓、金属储备和祭祀贡品为主，分散于拉丁姆各山丘聚落。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' consisted of granaries, metal stores, and ritual offerings under each settlement rex's control, dispersed across Latium's hilltop settlements.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持各聚落防御、祭祀正常运转和贵族氏族间的资源平衡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining each settlement's defenses, ritual functions, and resource balance among noble clans.

description.zh: 各聚落王和贵族家族的武装随从，装备铁矛、青铜甲和圆盾。
description.en: Armed retainers of settlement kings and noble families, equipped with iron spears, bronze armor, and round shields.

description.zh: 战时从自由农民中征召的步兵，使用矛、投枪和简易盾牌。
description.en: Infantry levied from free farmers in wartime, using spears, javelins, and simple shields.

description.zh: 负责监视伊特鲁里亚边界和萨宾山区通道的轻装武装。
description.en: Light-armed forces monitoring the Etruscan frontier and Sabine mountain passes.

commanderInChief.zh: 各聚落王在本邦领军，联合防御时由拉丁联盟推举指挥
commanderInChief.en: Each settlement rex commanded his own polity's forces, with the Latin League electing a commander for joint defense

technology.zh: 铁器逐渐进入军事领域，精英使用铁矛和青铜甲，普通士兵仍多用木矛和简易防具。防御依靠山丘聚落的自然地形和简易城墙。
technology.en: Iron was gradually entering the military sphere, with elites using iron spears and bronze armor while ordinary soldiers still relied on wooden spears and simple protection. Defense depended on hilltop settlement terrain and simple walls.

unit.en: shi of grain equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自伊特鲁里亚城邦的南扩、萨宾山地部族的侵扰以及沃尔西人等邻近意大利民族的竞争。
threats.en: Main threats came from the southward expansion of Etruscan city-states, raiding by Sabine highland tribes, and competition from neighboring Italic peoples such as the Volsci.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于拉丁姆平原的山丘聚落和台伯河谷农庄，帕拉提诺山上的罗马聚落只是众多小邦之一。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed across hilltop settlements on the Latin plain and Tiber valley farmsteads; the Palatine Hill settlement of Rome was merely one among many small polities.

socialClasses.zh: 贵族氏族首领、祭司、自由农牧民、工匠与附属人口
socialClasses.en: Noble clan heads, priests, free agro-pastoral households, artisans, and dependent populations

allies.zh: 拉丁联盟成员间的宗教和防御联系，以及与部分萨宾部族的姻亲关系。
allies.en: Religious and defensive ties among Latin League members, along with marriage connections to some Sabine tribes.

enemies.zh: 与伊特鲁里亚城邦存在领土竞争，与部分山地部族有边境摩擦。
enemies.en: Territorial competition with Etruscan city-states, and frontier friction with some highland tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以拉丁联盟共同祭祀为纽带维持松散团结，对伊特鲁里亚采取防御姿态，与希腊殖民者有零星接触。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintained loose unity through Latin League shared rituals, adopted a defensive posture toward Etruria, and had sporadic contact with Greek colonists.

era.zh: 意大利早期铁器时代（维兰诺瓦-拉丁文化期）
era.en: Early Italian Iron Age (Villanovan-Latin cultural period)

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器冶炼引入、改良农具、山丘聚落防御工事和陶器生产技术提升。
keyInnovations.en: Introduction of iron smelting, improved agricultural tools, hilltop settlement fortifications, and enhanced pottery production techniques.

strengths.zh: 占据拉丁姆肥沃平原和台伯河要津，拉丁联盟提供基本的集体安全框架，文化上正从伊特鲁里亚和希腊世界吸收先进技术。
strengths.en: Occupied the fertile Latin plain and strategic Tiber crossing, the Latin League provided a basic collective security framework, and culturally absorbed advanced techniques from Etruria and the Greek world.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分散，各聚落独立性强，军事力量弱小，面对伊特鲁里亚城邦处于明显劣势。
weaknesses.en: Extremely politically fragmented with strong settlement independence, weak military power, and at a clear disadvantage facing Etruscan city-states.

outlook.zh: 其中某些聚落（尤其是帕拉提诺山的罗马）可能通过吸收邻近人口和学习伊特鲁里亚技术而崛起，但在公元前800年，这一前景仍极不明朗。
outlook.en: Some settlements (especially Palatine Rome) could potentially rise by absorbing neighboring populations and learning Etruscan techniques, but in 800 BCE this prospect remained highly uncertain.

description.zh: 拉丁姆地区的拉丁-萨宾诸小邦处于铁器时代早期，罗马只是帕拉提诺山上的一个微小聚落。
description.en: The Latin-Sabine polities of Latium were in the early Iron Age; Rome was merely a tiny settlement on the Palatine Hill.

ruler.zh: 诸部族首领与战争领袖
ruler.en: Various tribal chiefs and war leaders

governmentForm.zh: 以部族王为核心的世袭军事酋邦群体，各部族独立治理，战时可能联合推举盟主。武士贵族和马术文化是政治权力的基础。
governmentForm.en: A cluster of hereditary military chiefdoms centered on tribal kings, with each tribe governing independently and possibly electing a confederate leader in wartime. Warrior aristocracy and equestrian culture formed the basis of political power.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由部族王与武士贵族、祭司与歌手、自由农牧民、金银工匠和附属人口构成。马匹拥有和武艺是地位核心标志。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of tribal kings and warrior aristocrats, priests and bards, free agro-pastoral households, gold-silver craftsmen, and dependent populations. Horse ownership and martial skill were central markers of status.

rulingClass.zh: 控制骑兵力量、金银冶炼和跨区域联盟的部族王家族
rulingClass.en: Tribal royal families controlling cavalry forces, gold-silver metallurgy, and inter-regional alliances

succession.zh: 王位在强势家族中继承，但武勇和军事追随者的支持往往决定最终继承者。
succession.en: Kingship was inherited within dominant families, but martial prowess and the support of military followers often determined the ultimate successor.

structure.zh: 各部族以王的庭帐为政治中心，行政依靠武士贵族和部族长老。跨部族事务通过宴饮、联姻和战争联盟协调。
structure.en: Each tribe had the king's court as its political center, with administration relying on warrior aristocrats and tribal elders. Inter-tribal affairs were coordinated through feasting, intermarriage, and war alliances.

function.zh: 处理政务、军事决策和对外交涉。
function.en: Handled governance, military decisions, and external negotiations.

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、占卜和节庆。
function.en: Oversaw religious rites, divination, and festivals.

function.zh: 征收贡品、管理金银工坊和跨区域贸易。
function.en: Collected tribute, managed gold-silver workshops, and oversaw interregional trade.

localAdmin.zh: 地方以各部族领地为单位，由附属首领和长老治理。边境地区依靠前哨骑兵巡逻。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized by tribal territories under sub-chiefs and elders. Frontier areas relied on mounted outpost patrols.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法和部族王裁决为主，武士荣誉法则和血仇制度并行。
legalSystem.en: Based on customary law and tribal royal adjudication, with warrior honor codes and blood-feud systems operating in parallel.

taxationSystem.zh: 部族王通过贡纳、战利品、金银矿收益和牲畜征收获取资源。武士贵族亦从附属人口获取劳役。
taxationSystem.en: Tribal kings obtained resources through tribute, war booty, gold-silver mining income, and livestock levies. Warrior aristocrats also drew labor from dependent populations.

religion.zh: 色雷斯多神教，以狄奥尼索斯式狂欢崇拜、骑士英雄崇拜和自然神灵祭祀为特色
religion.en: Thracian polytheism, featuring Dionysiac ecstatic worship, horseman hero cults, and nature deity rituals

culturalAchievements.zh: 以精湛的金银冶炼工艺闻名（尤其是金面具和银器），马术文化高度发达，色雷斯诗歌和音乐传统（奥尔菲斯传说）在希腊世界广为人知。
culturalAchievements.en: Renowned for exquisite gold and silver metalwork (especially gold masks and silverware), highly developed equestrian culture, and Thracian poetry and musical traditions (the Orpheus legend) known throughout the Greek world.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系色雷斯-弗里吉亚语族
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Thraco-Phrygian branch

philosophy.zh: 以武士荣誉、死后灵魂不朽信仰、骑士英雄崇拜和自然力量敬畏为思想核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on warrior honor, belief in the immortality of the soul after death, horseman hero veneration, and reverence for natural forces.

writingSystem.zh: 无广泛本地文字使用，部分地区可能接触希腊字母。信息以口传和象征纹饰传递。
writingSystem.en: No widespread indigenous writing; some areas may have encountered the Greek alphabet. Information was transmitted orally and through symbolic decoration.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农牧业为基础，金银冶炼是最突出的产业，与希腊殖民城邦和安纳托利亚的贸易提供额外收入。马匹养殖也是重要经济活动。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agro-pastoralism, with gold-silver metallurgy as the most distinctive industry; trade with Greek colonial cities and Anatolia provided additional income. Horse breeding was also a significant economic activity.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧与马匹养殖、金银冶炼、铁器生产、制陶、酿酒与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, animal husbandry and horse breeding, gold-silver metallurgy, iron production, pottery, winemaking, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 金银器、马匹、谷物、葡萄酒、蜂蜜、皮革、铁器与奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Gold-silver goods, horses, grain, wine, honey, leather, ironware, and slaves

name.zh: 实物交换与贵金属称量
name.en: Barter and precious metal by weight

householdWealth.zh: 普通家户以田地、牲畜和农具为主要财富；武士贵族则积累金银器、骏马和武器。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households held wealth in fields, livestock, and tools; warrior aristocrats accumulated gold-silverware, fine horses, and weapons.

economicSystem.zh: 以农牧自给、武士贵族控制的金银产业和与希腊城邦的贸易交换为特征的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy characterized by agro-pastoral self-sufficiency, warrior-aristocrat-controlled gold-silver industries, and trade exchange with Greek city-states.

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"以各部族王控制的金银储备、马群和粮仓为主，部分以豪华墓葬陪葬品形式固定化。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' consisted of gold-silver reserves, horse herds, and granaries under tribal kings' control, with some wealth fixed in the form of lavish burial goods.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持骑兵力量、王廷宴饮排场和金银工坊生产为重心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice centered on sustaining cavalry strength, royal court feasting grandeur, and gold-silver workshop production.

description.zh: 色雷斯武士贵族组成的精锐骑兵，以骑术和冲击战闻名于古代世界。
description.en: Elite cavalry composed of Thracian warrior aristocrats, renowned in the ancient world for horsemanship and shock combat.

description.zh: 战时征召的自由民步兵，使用投枪、弯刀和柳条盾。
description.en: Free-men infantry levied in wartime, using javelins, curved swords (sica), and wicker shields.

description.zh: 熟悉巴尔干山地的轻装游击战士。
description.en: Light guerrilla fighters familiar with Balkan mountain terrain.

commanderInChief.zh: 各部族王独立指挥本族军队，联合作战时由最强势的王担任盟主
commanderInChief.en: Each tribal king independently commanded his own forces, with the most powerful king leading in joint operations

technology.zh: 以骑兵战术见长，使用投枪、弯刀（rhomphaia/sica）和轻骑突击；铁器冶炼已普及，但大规模军事工程能力有限。
technology.en: Excelled in cavalry tactics, using javelins, curved swords (rhomphaia/sica), and light cavalry charges; iron smelting was widespread but large-scale military engineering capability was limited.

unit.en: shi of grain equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自希腊殖民城邦对沿海地区的争夺、斯基泰骑兵从黑海草原的侵入以及色雷斯诸部族间的内部战争。
threats.en: Main threats came from Greek colonial cities competing for coastal territory, Scythian cavalry incursions from the Black Sea steppe, and internal warfare among Thracian tribes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于巴尔干半岛内陆山地和河谷，以分散的部族领地和山丘聚落为主。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed across the Balkan interior highlands and river valleys, primarily in dispersed tribal territories and hilltop settlements.

socialClasses.zh: 部族王与武士贵族、祭司与歌手、金银工匠、自由农牧民、奴隶与附属人口
socialClasses.en: Tribal kings and warrior aristocrats, priests and bards, gold-silver artisans, free agro-pastoral households, slaves and dependent populations

allies.zh: 与安纳托利亚弗里吉亚王国存在文化和贸易联系，与部分希腊殖民城邦有商业往来。
allies.en: Maintained cultural and trade connections with the Phrygian kingdom in Anatolia, and commercial dealings with some Greek colonial cities.

enemies.zh: 与斯基泰游牧民和部分希腊沿海殖民地存在冲突，色雷斯诸部族间也频繁交战。
enemies.en: In conflict with Scythian nomads and some Greek coastal colonies, with frequent warfare also among Thracian tribes themselves.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外以维护领地、控制金银矿产和在希腊与草原世界间保持平衡为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on defending territory, controlling gold-silver resources, and maintaining balance between the Greek and steppe worlds.

era.zh: 巴尔干铁器时代早期

keyInnovations.zh: 精湛的金银冶金技术、骑兵战术发展、铁器冶炼普及和山地防御工事。
keyInnovations.en: Exquisite gold-silver metallurgical techniques, cavalry tactical development, widespread iron smelting, and mountain defensive works.

strengths.zh: 骑兵战力在古代世界名列前茅，金银冶金技术精湛，占据巴尔干战略要地，武士文化凝聚力强。
strengths.en: Cavalry combat power ranked among the highest in the ancient world, exquisite gold-silver metallurgy, occupation of strategic Balkan positions, and strong warrior cultural cohesion.

weaknesses.zh: 部族分裂严重，缺乏统一政治组织，内部战争消耗力量，面对希腊城邦方阵和斯基泰骑兵面临双重压力。
weaknesses.en: Severely fragmented tribally, lacking unified political organization, internal warfare draining strength, and facing dual pressure from Greek city-state phalanxes and Scythian cavalry.

outlook.zh: 若能实现更大范围的部族统一，色雷斯有潜力成为巴尔干主导力量；但持续的内部分裂更可能使其成为希腊和波斯扩张的目标。
outlook.en: If broader tribal unification could be achieved, Thrace had potential to become the dominant Balkan power; but persistent internal fragmentation more likely made it a target for Greek and Persian expansion.

description.zh: 巴尔干半岛的色雷斯-弗里吉亚部落以骑术、金银冶金和武士文化闻名，连接着希腊世界与安纳托利亚。
description.en: The Thraco-Phrygian communities of the Balkans were known for horsemanship, gold-silver metallurgy, and warrior culture, linking the Greek world with Anatolia.

description.zh: 新亚述帝国在阿淑尔纳西尔帕二世统治下向叙利亚和地中海沿岸推进，征服多个阿拉米人城邦。亚述军事机器以铁制武器和攻城战术闻名，重新确立了对近东的霸权。
description.en: Under Ashurnasirpal II, the Neo-Assyrian Empire pushed westward into Syria and the Mediterranean coast, subjugating several Aramaean city-states. The Assyrian war machine, renowned for iron weapons and siege techniques, reasserted hegemony over the Near East.

title.en: Rise of the Kingdom of Urartu

description.zh: 乌拉尔图王国在亚美尼亚高原整合势力，建造以图什帕城为中心的要塞体系，成为新亚述帝国在北方的主要对手。乌拉尔图以其精湛的水利灌溉工程和青铜冶金闻名。
description.en: The Kingdom of Urartu consolidated power on the Armenian Highlands, constructing a fortress system centered on Tushpa. It became the primary northern rival of the Neo-Assyrian Empire, renowned for its sophisticated irrigation engineering and bronze metallurgy.

title.en: Accession of King Xuan and Zhou Revival

description.zh: 周宣王即位后推行一系列改革，试图恢复西周王室的权威，派遣军队讨伐猃狁等北方游牧民族。这一时期被称为"宣王中兴"，但实际上诸侯势力已日益壮大。
description.en: King Xuan of Zhou ascended the throne and implemented reforms to restore royal authority, dispatching armies against the Xianyun nomads to the north. This period, known as the "Xuanwang Revival," saw temporary strengthening of the Zhou court, though vassal states were steadily growing in power.

description.zh: 希腊各城邦开始大规模海外殖民活动，在地中海和黑海沿岸建立殖民城市。人口增长和土地短缺促使希腊人远航至西西里、南意大利和北非，传播希腊文化与贸易网络。
description.en: Greek city-states launched large-scale overseas colonization, founding settlements across the Mediterranean and Black Sea coasts. Population growth and land scarcity drove Greeks to sail to Sicily, southern Italy, and North Africa, spreading Greek culture and trade networks.

description.zh: 来自推罗的腓尼基殖民者在北非建立迦太基城（传统纪年约公元前814年），该城迅速发展为西地中海最重要的贸易中心。迦太基控制了跨地中海的商业航线，与希腊殖民地展开竞争。
description.en: Phoenician colonists from Tyre founded the city of Carthage in North Africa (traditionally dated c. 814 BCE), which rapidly grew into the most important trading center in the western Mediterranean. Carthage controlled trans-Mediterranean commercial routes and competed with Greek colonies.

description.zh: 公元前776年，第一届有记录的古代奥运会在奥林匹亚举行，标志着泛希腊宗教文化传统的确立。各城邦在竞技期间实行神圣休战，促进了希腊世界的文化认同。
description.en: In 776 BCE, the first recorded ancient Olympic Games were held at Olympia, marking the establishment of a pan-Hellenic religious and cultural tradition. City-states observed a sacred truce during the games, fostering cultural identity across the Greek world.

title.zh: 犬戎攻破镐京，西周灭亡
title.en: Quanrong Sack Haojing, Fall of Western Zhou

description.zh: 犬戎联合申侯攻入西周都城镐京，杀周幽王，西周灭亡。周平王被迫东迁洛邑，开启东周时代。这一事件标志着周王室权威的急剧衰落和春秋争霸时代的序幕。
description.en: The Quanrong nomads, allied with the Marquis of Shen, sacked the Western Zhou capital Haojing and killed King You. King Ping was forced to relocate the capital east to Luoyi, inaugurating the Eastern Zhou period. This event marked the dramatic decline of Zhou royal authority and the prelude to the Spring and Autumn era.

description.zh: 秦襄公因护送周平王东迁有功，被正式册封为诸侯，获得周王室在关中的故地。这是秦国从边陲附庸跃升为正式诸侯国的关键转折，奠定了其日后统一中国的基础。
description.en: Duke Xiang of Qin was formally enfeoffed as a vassal lord for escorting King Ping eastward, receiving the former Zhou royal lands in the Wei River valley. This was a pivotal moment in Qin's rise from a frontier dependency to a full-fledged feudal state, laying the foundation for its eventual unification of China.

description.zh: 新亚述帝国对巴比伦尼亚发动军事行动，试图控制两河流域南部的富庶地区。亚述与巴比伦之间复杂的政治关系——既有征服也有文化尊重——贯穿整个铁器时代。
description.en: The Neo-Assyrian Empire launched military campaigns against Babylonia, seeking control over the wealthy southern Mesopotamian regions. The complex relationship between Assyria and Babylon—combining conquest with cultural reverence—characterized the entire Iron Age period.

title.en: Kingdom of Kush Establishes Capital at Napata

description.zh: 库什王国以纳帕塔为中心巩固努比亚地区的统治，继承了古埃及的诸多文化传统。库什国王修建金字塔和神庙，同时发展独立的铁器冶炼技术，成为非洲东北部的重要政权。
description.en: The Kingdom of Kush consolidated its rule over Nubia with Napata as its center, inheriting many cultural traditions from ancient Egypt. Kushite kings constructed pyramids and temples while developing independent iron-smelting technology, becoming a major power in northeastern Africa.

description.zh: 根据罗马传统，罗穆路斯于公元前753年在台伯河畔七丘之上建立罗马城。虽然这一日期带有传说色彩，但考古证据表明此时拉丁姆地区确实出现了日益复杂的定居点。
description.en: According to Roman tradition, Romulus founded the city of Rome on the seven hills beside the Tiber River in 753 BCE. While the date is legendary, archaeological evidence confirms increasingly complex settlements in the Latium region during this period.

title.zh: 辛梅里安人入侵安纳托利亚

description.zh: 辛梅里安人从黑海北部草原南下，入侵安纳托利亚，对弗里吉亚王国和吕底亚造成严重冲击。这次游牧民族迁徙打破了安纳托利亚的政治格局，加速了弗里吉亚的衰落。
description.en: The Cimmerians swept southward from the northern Black Sea steppe into Anatolia, devastating the Phrygian kingdom and threatening Lydia. This nomadic migration disrupted the political landscape of Anatolia and accelerated the decline of Phrygia.

title.zh: 提格拉特-帕拉萨尔三世改革亚述军制
title.en: Tiglath-Pileser III Reforms the Assyrian Military

description.zh: 提格拉特-帕拉萨尔三世推行深刻的军事与行政改革，建立常备职业军队取代征召兵制，将被征服地区改为直辖行省。这些改革使新亚述帝国成为古代近东最高效的军事强权。
description.en: Tiglath-Pileser III enacted sweeping military and administrative reforms, replacing the levy-based army with a standing professional force and converting conquered territories into directly governed provinces. These reforms made the Neo-Assyrian Empire the most efficient military power in the ancient Near East.

title.en: Crisis of the Kingdom of Israel

description.zh: 以色列王国在亚述帝国日益增大的压力下陷入政治动荡，内部频繁发生王位争夺和宫廷政变。提格拉特-帕拉萨尔三世的西征迫使以色列成为亚述的附庸国，预示着王国的覆亡。
description.en: The Kingdom of Israel fell into political turmoil under growing Assyrian pressure, with frequent throne seizures and court coups. Tiglath-Pileser III's western campaigns forced Israel into Assyrian vassalage, foreshadowing the kingdom's eventual destruction.

title.zh: 哈尔施塔特铁器文化扩展

description.zh: 中欧哈尔施塔特文化在铁器冶炼和盐矿开采的推动下持续扩张，凯尔特贵族通过控制贸易路线积累财富。精美的铁器、青铜器和黄金制品反映了与地中海世界的广泛贸易联系。
description.en: The Hallstatt culture of Central Europe continued to expand, driven by iron smelting and salt mining. Celtic elites accumulated wealth by controlling trade routes, with fine ironwork, bronze, and gold artifacts reflecting extensive trade connections with the Mediterranean world.

description.zh: 楚国利用其在长江中游的地理优势，不断向南方和东方扩张，兼并周边小国。楚国融合了中原华夏文化与南方本土传统，形成了独特的"楚文化"体系。
description.en: The State of Chu leveraged its geographic position in the middle Yangtze region to expand southward and eastward, annexing neighboring small states. Chu blended Central Plains Chinese culture with local southern traditions, creating the distinctive "Chu culture" system.

description.zh: 新亚述帝国对以色列首都撒马利亚发动围攻，最终于公元前722年攻陷该城，灭亡以色列王国。大批以色列人被强制迁移至帝国东部各地，即著名的"以色列十失落部族"事件。
description.en: The Neo-Assyrian Empire besieged Samaria, the capital of Israel, finally capturing it in 722 BCE and destroying the Kingdom of Israel. Large numbers of Israelites were forcibly deported to the eastern parts of the empire, giving rise to the famous "Ten Lost Tribes of Israel."

description.zh: 库什国王皮安基率军北上征服埃及，建立第二十五王朝（努比亚王朝）。库什法老统治尼罗河全流域，复兴了埃及的神庙建设和宗教传统，开创了努比亚-埃及统一时代。
description.en: Kushite king Piye led his armies northward to conquer Egypt, establishing the Twenty-Fifth Dynasty (Nubian Dynasty). Kushite pharaohs ruled the entire Nile Valley, reviving Egyptian temple construction and religious traditions, inaugurating an era of Nubian-Egyptian unity.

description.zh: 安纳托利亚西部的吕底亚王国率先使用天然金银合金铸造标准化货币，这是人类历史上最早的铸币之一。货币的发明极大地促进了商业交换，对整个地中海世界的经济发展产生了深远影响。
description.en: The Kingdom of Lydia in western Anatolia pioneered the minting of standardized coins from electrum, a natural gold-silver alloy—among the earliest coinage in human history. The invention of coinage dramatically facilitated commercial exchange and profoundly influenced economic development across the Mediterranean world.

description.zh: 西非的诺克文化发展出独立的铁器冶炼技术，同时以精美的赤陶雕塑闻名于世。诺克文化是撒哈拉以南非洲最早的铁器时代文明之一，对后来的西非文化发展有深远影响。
description.en: The Nok culture of West Africa developed independent iron-smelting technology while becoming famous for its exquisite terracotta sculptures. Nok was among the earliest Iron Age civilizations in sub-Saharan Africa and profoundly influenced later West African cultural development.

description.zh: 安第斯山区的查文·德万塔尔作为重要宗教中心，其独特的美洲豹崇拜和宗教艺术风格扩散至秘鲁广大地区。查文文化代表了南美洲最早的广域文化整合现象之一。
description.en: Chavín de Huántar, a major religious center in the Andes, spread its distinctive jaguar worship and religious art style across large parts of Peru. The Chavín culture represented one of the earliest wide-area cultural integrations in South America.

title.zh: 弥生时代稻作传入日本
title.en: Rice Agriculture Reaches Japan in Yayoi Transition

description.zh: 水稻种植技术从亚洲大陆经朝鲜半岛传入日本列岛北九州地区，标志着弥生文化过渡期的开始。随之传入的还有青铜器和铁器技术，日本社会开始从绳文采集经济向农耕社会转变。
description.en: Wet-rice agriculture spread from the Asian mainland via the Korean Peninsula to northern Kyushu in Japan, marking the beginning of the Yayoi transition. Bronze and iron technologies accompanied the agricultural package, initiating Japan's transformation from Jōmon foraging to an agrarian society.

title.zh: 塔尔特索斯的繁荣贸易

description.zh: 伊比利亚半岛西南部的塔尔特索斯王国凭借丰富的锡矿和银矿资源，与腓尼基和希腊商人建立了活跃的贸易网络。塔尔特索斯被古代地中海世界视为富饶的"远西之地"。
description.en: The kingdom of Tartessos in southwestern Iberia, rich in tin and silver resources, established active trade networks with Phoenician and Greek merchants. Tartessos was regarded by the ancient Mediterranean world as the wealthy "land of the far west."

title.zh: 奥尔梅克拉文塔中心兴盛

description.zh: 奥尔梅克文明的拉文塔中心在公元前8世纪进入繁盛期，建造了中美洲最早的大型金字塔和精美的巨石头像。奥尔梅克被视为中美洲文明的"母文化"，其历法和书写系统影响了后来的玛雅文明。
description.en: The Olmec center at La Venta entered its peak period around the 8th century BCE, constructing Mesoamerica's earliest large pyramids and exquisite colossal stone heads. The Olmec are regarded as the "mother culture" of Mesoamerican civilization, with their calendar and writing system influencing the later Maya.

title.zh: 后吠陀时代十六大国形成
title.en: Sixteen Mahajanapadas Begin Forming in Late Vedic India

description.zh: 南亚进入后吠陀时代晚期，恒河流域逐渐形成十六大国的雏形。以俱卢、般遮罗为代表的早期国家从部落联盟向领土国家转型，铁器农业的推广为国家形成提供了经济基础。
description.en: South Asia entered the late Vedic period, with the embryonic forms of the Sixteen Mahajanapadas gradually forming in the Ganges plain. Early states like Kuru and Panchala transitioned from tribal confederacies to territorial kingdoms, with the spread of iron-based agriculture providing the economic foundation for state formation.
